name: Sports and Entertainment Law Society
description: SELS is a law student-run organization with a mission to create opportunities for UGA Law students who may be interested in pursuing a career in the sports and entertainment law industries. We strive to expose law students to the dynamic industries of sports & entertainment via panels, round-table discussions, and various networking events.
summary: SELS is a student-run organization with a mission to create opportunities for UGA law students who may be interested in pursuing a career in the sports and entertainment law industries.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America at UGA
description:
The Public Relations Student Society of American (PRSSA) is a national organization for all students interested in public relations, marketing, advertising, communications and more. The Drewry chapter is the largest PRSSA chapter in the nation, hosting over 300 students at bi-weekly meetings at Grady College. PRSSA acts as an extension to the classroom by preparing students for the professional world both during and after graduation.

Members can expect an exciting lineup of meetings throughout the academic year, featuring real-world advice from real-world professionals. PRSSA invites current field experts to discuss their careers, offer tips and network with students. Members can build both hard and soft skills from workshops ranging from strategy development to conducting effective research. PRSSA provides opportunities for experience with graphic design, editing and social media on industry-essential platforms. PRSSA upholds its commitment to provide students with relevant meetings from engaging industry professionals. Members can gain leadership experience through PRSSA’s executive board and committee system. Students interested in learning more about the field, finding internships and kickstarting their career should try PRSSA.

The Drewry Chapter hosts a variety of events, activities and trips for its members throughout the year. Events include:

  • Bi-Weekly Speakers are invited to share about their careers in PR, advertising, marketing, and more. Members can enjoy learning from public relations professionals who work in varying industries and companies to gain a well-rounded perspective of the current job market.
  • ADPR Connection is an annual public relations and advertising conference where professionals from the Atlanta area connect with UGA students. The event features panel sessions, a career fair, resume critiques and a luncheon.
  • Classic City PR Tour is an opportunity offered to chapter members to visit PR agencies and network with professional in Athens!
  • Atlanta Agency Tour is an experience offered for members to visit Atlanta PR agencies and network with professionals.
  • Mentoring Event is organized by the mentoring committee and includes an informal panel discussion about professional relationships, networking, interview techniques, resume building and portfolio development.
  • Member Appreciation Night honors members for their achievements and involvement in PRSSA throughout the semester.
  • Pre-Professionalism Workshops offer hands-on training and insights tailored to prepare members for successful careers in public relations. These workshops cover a wide range of essential skills and topics, including brand management, resume writing, interview preparation, workplace conduct, networking etiquette and career advancement in the public relations industry.

Eager to soak in all the essential benefits provided exclusively at PRSSA? Don’t miss out – register here to join the most active PRSSA chapter in the nation. Follow PRSSA’s social media accounts below to get the latest updates.
summary:
PRSSA is the national student professional organization for students interested in pursuing careers in public relations.

We offer bi-weekly meetings with speakers from the communications industry, along with opportunities for peer networking.

name: Filipino Student Association
description:
FSA promotes the culture and customs of the Philippines at UGA and establishes a sense of family between our diverse members. Through events and gatherings, we learn more about the Filipino culture and share it with family and friends. Membership in FSA is extended to all students.

The Filipino culture places an emphasis on family, and that is one of our pillars. Through our Ate/Kuya/Ading Program (AKA), we promote a close knit community that serves as your family away from home.

We have several annual events where we work closely with organizations at UGA and the Athens-Clarke community. During these events, we work hard to give back to the community through charity work and showcasing of talents to name a few. We work hard, eat amazing Filipino food, and have fun throughout the year!
summary: The Filipino Student Association promotes the culture and traditions of the Philippines at UGA and fosters community among diverse members. Membership is open to all students. We host annual events with UGA, Athens-Clarke, and the state of Georgia.

name: MEDLIFE at UGA
shortName: MEDLIFE (Medicine, Education, and Development for Low-Income Families Everywhere)
description:
MEDLIFE at UGA is a local chapter of an international nonprofit organization based in Lima, Peru. Our mission is in our name: we seek to provide Medicine, Education, and Development for Low-Income Families Everywhere. We strive to help families achieve greater freedom from the constraints of poverty, empowering them to live healthier lives. In any initiative we pursue, we aim to help those experiencing the challenges of poverty by partnering with them, working together, and listening to what they have to say. In 2021, we were excited to be selected as the Organization of the Year at the UGA Student Organization Achievement and Recognition (SOAR) Awards for our dedication to alleviating poverty in the community. 

In our Athens community, MEDLIFE at UGA members have the opportunity to work with numerous local initiatives, such as Project Safe, the Food Bank of NEGA, Books for Keeps, and Keep Athens Clarke County Beautiful. Through fundraising initiatives, MEDLIFE at UGA works to finance specific projects and meet the needs of our international partner communities; every cent we raise goes directly to the communities we serve abroad. Notable fundraising events include the annual MEDLIFE Cornhole Tournament, percentage nights, and various suture and IV clinics. Every year, MEDLIFE at UGA offers 60+ service opportunities which benefit the Athens community and fundraises upwards of $5,000 dedicated to our international mission of empowering our partner communities in their fight for equal access to healthcare, education, and a safe home.

Our members also have the opportunity to join communities abroad (in Tanzania, Peru, and Ecuador) on our Mobile Clinic trips during winter, spring, and summer breaks. During these trips, students get in-field, global health experience. Students assist with stations pertaining to pharmacy, dentistry, gynecology, and education, and help teach healthy habits such as tooth brushing. Students also work with local villagers and community leaders on community development projects such as building stairs or painting schools. 

Overall, we provide a forum and network for students at the University of Georgia to build a solid global health foundation, combat poverty locally, and aid communities abroad in a sustainable manner. We also provide numerous opportunities for growth and advancement within our organization, such as our various committees and board positions.

Join on the Involvement Network to be added to our listserv. Additionally, see the video to learn more about the MEDLIFE movement!

https://vimeo.com/313448205
summary: MEDLIFE at UGA strives to efficiently and effectively alleviate the struggles of low-income families. We provide sustainable aid to impoverished communities abroad, serve local communities in Athens, and offer leadership opportunities for students at UGA

name: infUSion Magazine
description:
InfUSion Magazine serves to provide a journalistic medium where students can speak their minds, voice their opinions and exhibit their creativity. 

InfUSion Magazine was started in 1989 in the Office of Multicultural Services and Programs and was known as Kross Kultures Magazine. Today, the magazine has six editorial sections and 45 staff members. We are the university's first multicultural publication and continue to provide the latest coverage on culture, politics, fashion & style, entertainment and more!
summary: InfUSion Magazine is a semesterly, student produced publication that covers the latest campus, local, state and national news. InfUSion is the first and only multicultural  magazine on the University of Georgia's campus.

name: University Judiciary
description: University Judiciary is a student-run organization that administers and helps students through the conduct process who are charged with violating the Code of Conduct. UJ also proactively educates students about a variety topics that concern the conduct process and partners with the Office of Student Conduct to run the Georgia Resiliency and Integrity Training (GRIT) program where students have the opportunity to reflect on how they process negative experiences and the coping mechanisms they use to do so.
summary: University Judiciary is a student-run organization that helps students who are charged with violating the Code of Conduct in the Conduct Process. UJ also proactively educates students about the Conduct Process and provides support through sanctioning.

name: Undergraduate Moot Court Team at the University of Georgia
description: Moot Court models appellate court oral arguments, similar to what is used by lawyers arguing cases before the Supreme Court, cultivating a sphere of interest and knowledge in constitutional law while sharpening critical thinking and argumentation skills.
summary: Moot Court models appellate court oral arguments, similar to what is used by lawyers arguing cases before the Supreme Court, cultivating a sphere of interest and knowledge in constitutional law while sharpening critical thinking and argumentation skills.

name: Women's Lacrosse
description:
The purpose of the Women's Club Lacrosse team is to serve as UGA's competitive non-varsity lacrosse program. Along with competing regionally and nationally, the organization works to advance the growth of lacrosse in the state of Georgia and within the community. 

The Women's Lacrosse Club was started in 1994. It has continued to become a stronger, more competitive program throughout the years. In the Spring of 2007 the Women's Club Lacrosse team took its first trip to the national WCLA championship and has continued to receive a bid for all the following seasons. In the Spring of 2016 the team won the national championship tournament. We travel around the country to play some of the best teams in the nation. We have traveled to Los Angeles, CA, Boulder, CO, Phoenix, AZ, and Boston, MA in past seasons to represent UGA and play against the toughest competition to prepare for the national tournament.
summary: We are in the Southern Women's Lacrosse League (SWLL). We are very competitive and play against the top teams in the country. Our goal each year is to compete at the WCLA National Tournament, through either an automatic qualifier bid or an at-large bid.

name: Men's Water Polo Club
description: We are a group of students dedicated to the advancement of the sport of water polo in the state of Georgia. We compete in the CWPA Southeast Club Division yearly as well as travel to different schools to compete for fun in the offseason.
summary: Club Water Polo is a close-knit group of student-athletes who continue to develop their water polo skills by competing in various tournaments across the country. In the Fall semester, we compete in the Southeast Club Division of the CWPA.

name: Korean Undergraduate Student Association
description:
We are an organization under ISL (International Student Life). KUSA is here to spread awareness of the Korean culture not only on the UGA campus, but also to the entirety of Athens-Clarke county. We are here to serve both international students and students with an interest in Korean culture through various cultural and social events! We also host a Korean culture night every year and invite the Athens-Clarke community to celebrate Korean culture with us! 

 

kusageorgia@gmail.com
summary: The Korean Undergraduate Student Association serves to assist students interested in Korean culture. We work to preserve and promote the richness and diversity of the Korean culture, while providing our members with a secure and supportive environment.

name: Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy
description: The Academy of Managed Care Pharmacy, AMCP, is a student organization that encourages pharmacy students to explore nontraditional pharmacy careers, such as those in managed care and the pharmaceutical industry. Managed care is a unique niche for pharmacists to play a role in developing and implementing medication therapy management (MTM) programs, design pharmacy benefits, and manage quality and cost effectiveness by working for health plans, pharmacy benefit management companies, and several other organizations. Throughout the years, our UGA chapter has had internship workshops, guest speakers, topic discussion on pharmacogenetic testing and insurance coverage during Pharmtoberfest, and had five teams of pharmacy students participate in the local Pharmacy and Therapeutics competition. Lastly, AMCP has collaborated with IPhO to host a joint Roundtable Networking Dinner with representatives from companies, such as Express Scripts, Otsuka, and Boehringer Ingelheim. We hope to continue providing these learning and networking opportunities to our new members!
summary: AMCP is a student organization that encourages pharmacy students to explore non-traditional pharmacy careers, such as those in managed care and the pharmaceutical industry.

name: Georgia Association for Women Lawyers – University of Georgia Chapter
description:
The Georgia Association for Women Lawyers (GAWL) at the University of Georgia exists to serve the women within the law school by providing mentoring, networking, and other resources to its members and the law school community. Our members enjoy a great variety of activities and events that are especially created and tailored with their interests in mind. These include:

  • Panels and Speakers
  • Networking Opportunities
  • Community Service Projects
  • WLSA's Mentorship Program
  • The Edith House Lecture Series
summary: The Georgia Association for Women Lawyers (GAWL) at the University of Georgia exists to serve the women within the law school by providing mentoring, networking, and other resources to its members and the law school community.

name: Shop with a Bulldog
description: Through one, large event during the holiday season, this organization works with the local school district and businesses to create an event to celebrate children of families in the Athens-Clarke County community. University students sign up to be mentors for a child on the event day. The student, along with a partner, raise a joint-fund of $120 for the child. On the event day, children, families and students gather for a fun themed event that emphasizes community and education. The students and their child are bussed to Wal-Mart to spend the $120 on items for the child. Items include needs and wants ranging from clothes to books to toys.
summary: SWAB is an organization that strives to celebrate the value of community, the excitement of the holidays, and the children of Athens! We aim to grow every year in our relations within the community and across campus to further our mission.

name: African-American Choral Ensemble
description: The African American Choral Ensemble, also known as AACE, is a performance organization that was established as the Pamoja Singers 54 years ago. It has been under the direction of Dr. Gregory Broughton for the last 35 years. AACE holds concerts in the Fall and Spring semesters as well as performances in surrounding communities upon invitation. Membership is open to all University of Georgia students, faculty, and staff as well as members of the Athens-Clarke County community.
summary: Performance of traditional and contemporary black gospel, spiritual, and popular music.

name: Fashion Design Student Association
description: FDSA was founded for UGA students with an interest in fashion to help tailor portfolios, resumes, & skills as designers as well as provide a runway opportunity for designers, models, makeup artists, photographers, and journalists to showcase their talent
summary: FDSA was founded for UGA students with an interest in fashion to help tailor portfolios, resumes, & skills as designers as well as provide a runway opportunity for designers, models, makeup artists, photographers, and journalists to showcase their talent

name: Pre-Veterinary Medicine Club
description: We are a student organization on the UGA campus whose members are interested in veterinary medicine (and gaining admission into Vet School). Our organization meets every other week and all UGA students, faculty, and staff are welcome to join. Our club activities aim to enhance knowledge and experience within the veterinary medicine field. Club activities include guest speakers, volunteer activities, philanthropic events, and field trips. We also help students prepare for the application process into Vet School. The Pre-Vet Club at the University of Georgia is a great way to meet and work with fellow pre-veterinary students.
summary: The Pre-Vet Club is a student run organization focused on learning about veterinary medicine. We have speakers at each biweekly meetings that talk about different fields in veterinary medicine as well as many opportunities related to veterinary jobs.

name: WUOG 90.5FM
description:
WUOG 90.5 FM is the University of Georgia’s alternative college radio station. It is an all-student,
all-volunteer, non-commercial radio station run by a board of seventeen student executive directors assisted by a student organization advisor. The seventeen executive directors manage ten staffs: Music, Local Music, Digital Media, Operations, News, Sports, Publications, Events, Archiving, and Writing.

WUOG is an alternative media outlet, meaning that it is our duty to air quality programming that is otherwise neglected on commercially operated channels. Our station’s music philosophy is designed to help new and independent artists gain exposure on campus, in Athens, and throughout the nation. WUOG is much more than just a stepping-stone on the road to an artist’s commercial success— many artists who choose to avoid the world of “big media” can find a home on our cozy airwaves.
summary: WUOG provides an alternative to other media outlets in the community through programming that is diverse and unique, serving the public with music, news, public affairs, sports of local and national interest, and other events both on and off-campus.

name: University Union Student Programming Board
description:
University Union is a great way to get out of the classroom and get more involved on campus! Union strives to create entertaining and diverse experiences by providing affordable, inclusive, quality activities for the student body of The University of Georgia. Composed of three committees, University Union provides unique entertainment for students and the campus community. Union organizes a range of activities including film screenings, Dawgs After Dark, and comedy shows. Programs are created for students by students, resulting in a wide variety of shows suited to the diverse tastes of the campus community.

How to Join Union

Attend our weekly Unionwide meeting every Tuesday at 7:00pm in Tate 137 to help plan some of the biggest events on campus! Speak with the Vice-President of your choice or stop by the Union Office in room 102 of the Tate Student Center for more information.

Board of Directors

The ruling body of University Union is made up of 17 students, known as the Executive (Exec) Board. The Exec Board consists of the President, Chief of Staff, Vice President of Marketing and Communications, Vice President of Film, 2 Vice Presidents of Events, and eleven chairs. The Executive Board works with the Union advisors to oversee the operation of all divisions, coordinate programming, and implement Union-wide policies. Visit Tate room 102 to speak with one of the members of the Executive Board to learn more about University Union!
summary: University Union hosts a wide variety of events ranging from comedy shows, festivals, classic film experiences, and late night programming. Most importantly, all of the Union programs are selected, promoted, and produced for students by students.

name: EcoReach
description:
Purpose
The purpose of EcoReach is to connect graduate, undergraduate, and professional ecologists at the University of Georgia with school-age children in order to heighten awareness of the science of ecology and environmental issues.  By interacting with young people, we hope to raise awareness and enthusiasm for science, while supplementing the schools' curriculum to meet objectives in the state curriculum guidelines. 

EcoReach is here to interact with teachers to provide flexible programs designed to meet the needs of their students.  We also serve our community through informal educational opportunities, such as presenting programs to local scout groups, providing judges for science fairs, and presenting programs at local science days.

Our Volunteers
Our organization is made up primarily of graduate students from the Odum School of Ecology. We also have some undergraduate and faculty participants, as well as graduate students from other departments.
summary: EcoReach is an environmental education outreach group based out of the Odum School of Ecology. We develop and present environmental- and science-based lessons to local K-12 students and community members during Fall and Spring semesters.

name: Foundation for the International Medical Relief of Children
description: As the University of Georgia Chapter of FIMRC, our goal is to support the efforts of the national FIMRC organization through fundraising that will help provide medical care in under-served areas around the world. We also work to promote health awareness in Athens through volunteer activities and a health education program at local elementary schools, designed to improve local health education from a young age.
summary: We are a chapter of the national organization, FIMRC, which works to provide medical care in under-served areas around the world. We are involved in fundraising, community service, and volunteering activities to help support these efforts.

name: Whitewater Club of the University of Georgia
description:
The Whitewater club is set up in a way to invite both new and experienced paddlers in and give them a community in which they can build their skills and help teach other paddlers. With our regular pool sessions, members have a chance to gather and practice rescue techniques such as how to roll a kayak and how to perform a t-rescue, as well as work on more advanced skills such as free style play-boating. Many of these skills are vital to learn in order to navigate rivers and whitewater safely. More experienced members help the less experienced members by showing them down the rivers and teaching them these skills.

We charge dues in order to help buy gear. Once dues are paid, we allow any member to use our gear, providing all the necessary equipment for new whitewater paddlers to boat on the rivers. Many of our members eventually buy their own gear, but for those who can't afford gear or are not yet sure they want to invest their money in a boat, this gives them the opportunity to be a part of the club and practice skills on or off the rivers. Plus, with the wide variety of boats we provide, our paddlers become experienced in using several types of boats on different classes of rivers. 

For those who want to compete, we provide gear and gas money to individuals that want to race and represent our club. Many of our members regularly compete during the school year. It is not necessary for any of our members to compete, but those who have the skills to navigate the rivers that races are held on are encouraged to consider competing or going out to watch the races. 

In summary, our club helps both new and experienced paddlers by passing down information on rivers, gear, and techniques to safely navigate and enjoy whitewater within the south east and teach other paddlers so they can improve their skills.
summary: The Whitewater Club is designed to provide paddlers of all levels of proficiency a community in which to gather, paddle, and learn additional skills. With club trips almost every weekend, it's the ideal club for all interested in whitewater of any sort.

name: Science Olympiad at UGA
description:
Did you participate in Science Olympiad in high school? Sad because there's no Science Olympiad for the college level?

Be sad no more!

Science Olympiad at UGA works to engage Science Olympiad alumni and other interested students in promoting STEAM outreach in the Athens-Clarke county area and neighboring school systems. We also plan on organizing at least one Science Olympiad tournament per academic year for students all over Georgia!

Who should join?

  1. Students passionate about STEAM outreach
  2. Science Olympiad alumni
  3. Students who want to mentor other students
  4. Students interested in organizing state-wide events
  5. Anyone looking for fun!

So what are you waiting for? For more information, contact scienceolympiad@uga.edu. Our website is sciolyuga.weebly.com.
summary: Science Olympiad at UGA works to promote the STEAM fields in the local community while also organizing a Science Olympiad tournament each year.

name: Food Science Club
description: Professional development and social club for those interested in the field of Food Science. Typical activities include listening to industry guest speakers, making food products, industry tours, product development competitions, and community service.
summary: A place for those interested in general Food Science.

name: Plant Biology Graduate Students Association
description:
The purpose of the PBGSA is as follows:

  1. To provide an opportunity for the graduate students of the Department of Plant Biology of the University of Georgia to have true and meaningful representation in the administration of the Department,
  2. To relay important information to the graduate students regarding University affairs, scientific opportunities, and education resources,
  3. To promote communication among the diverse groups in the Department through social functions and other activities,
  4. To support graduate students in the Department in outreach and teaching endeavors, and
  5. To provide for any other such matters as the Association considers appropriate.
summary: The Plant Biology Graduate Students Association (PBGSA) is an association to coordinate and manage the affairs of graduate students in the UGA Department of Plant Biology.

name: Special Olympics at UGA
description:
Special Olympics at UGA was founded in 2003 and has progressively become more involved in programs that support the Special Olympics student-athletes of Athens-Clarke County. We work with children and adults with intellectual and developmental disabilities in every way that we can; this includes fundraising, planning fun outings, providing assistance in their P.E. classes, and helping out during their Special Olympic games. More importantly, we develop personal relationships with our athletes and give them thanks for the rewarding experiences and lessons they are providing for us. It is our hope that our organization continues to grow this year so that we make an even larger impact in the lives of our Athens-Clarke County athletes and their families. Some of the events we are currently planning for the 2024-2025 year are:

- Volunteering in Adapted PE classes in Clarke County Schools

- Monthly Social Group events for our adult athletes

- Weekly sports practices

- November Prom

- April Hometown Rivals baseball game

- Fundraising nights

.... and SO much more! 

You're invited to my new group 'SOatUGA GENERAL BODY' on GroupMe. Click here to join: https://groupme.com/join_group/88953924/t17sk8QU
summary: Special Olympics at UGA is a student organization on campus that assists the local Special Olympics program in Athens. Opportunities range from assisting weekly adapted PE classes to attending fun events to spending time with our wonderful athletes.

name: Rho Tau: Pre-Physical Therapy Club
description:
Rho Tau holds meetings to guide and inform prospective physical therapy students about graduate schools and various professional opportunities. In the fall, representatives from in-state and out-of-state physical therapy graduate programs come to speak about what their school has to offer and their admissions process. In the spring, local physical therapists come to talk about their different clinical settings and personal experiences. At the end of the year, we provide guidance on the PTCAS application process and GRE. We strive to provide a community here at UGA for Pre-Physical Therapy students by offering a mentorship program, headshot opportunities, and various means of communication between club members and professionals within the field.    

 

Please feel free to reach out about any questions you may have, and follow us on Instagram to stay up to date with all things Rho Tau!
summary: We hold meetings to guide and inform prospective physical therapy students about graduate schools and various professional opportunities. Open to all majors.

name: Linguistics Society at UGA
description: Linguistics Society at UGA is an organization that consists of UGA students in the Linguistics Program. We plan and support linguistics-related, academic, and social events.
summary: Linguistics Society at UGA is an organization that consists of graduate students in the UGA Department of Linguistics. We plan and support linguistics-related, academic, and social events.

name: Student Association of Family and Consumer Sciences
description:
We are a professional organization for students from all majors within the College of Family and Consumer Sciences. SAFCS membership includes membership on the local, state - Georgia Association of Family and Consumer Sciences and the national - American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences level.

As members of SAFCS, students can develop professional skills through organizational meetings, activities, and interact with students from other departments/majors within the College of Family and Consumer Sciences.

At the state level, students can attend meetings of the Georgia Association of Family and Consumer Sciences and at the national level the American Association of Family and Consumer Sciences.

SAFCS helps students broaden their horizons through leadership opportunities, community service and interaction with some of the top professionals in their areas of study. Students can enjoy camaraderie, refreshments and door prizes at each meeting.
summary: State and nationally affiliated professional organization for all majors within the College of Family and Consumer Sciences.

name: Residence Hall Association
description:
The Residence Hall Association (RHA) is the second-largest student organization at the University of Georgia, proudly representing and advocating for over 9,000 on-campus residents. Through a wide range of signature events, such as Taste of Athens and Beats & Eats, RHA brings residents together to celebrate campus life and create lasting memories. These events are free for all residents, and some programs are open to all students and the entire Athens community.  

In partnership with University Housing, we support initiatives that encourage personal growth, academic success, cultural awareness, and sustainability. Beyond programming, RHA actively works to enhance the residential experience through legislation and advocacy, ensuring that students’ voices are heard and their needs are met.
summary: The Residence Hall Association (RHA) focuses on creating communities within residence halls for students living on campus and on advocating on behalf of all residents through various initiatives and programs throughout campus.

name: Relay For Life at UGA
description:
Relay For Life:

Communities across the globe come together at Relay for Life events to celebrate cancer survivors, remember loved ones lost, and fight back against a disease that has already taken too much. The funds you raise truly make a difference in the fight against cancer – just ask one of the nearly 14 million cancer survivors who will celebrate another birthday this year!

Relay For Life is more than just a fundraiser. It’s a life-changing experience. At Relay, every person in the community has a chance to celebrate, remember, and fight back. And every person who participates joins others around the globe as part of this worldwide movement to end cancer through research, advocacy, and support programs. 

Everyone's reason to Relay is as unique as their own personal story. At Relay, you can find healing, comfort, and support from others who have faced cancer or who have lost a loved one to the disease. You have a chance to meet people in the community who are equally as passionate about finding an end to cancer in our lifetime. You can thank all the people who have done so much to support you through your personal cancer experience. And you can gather together with friends, family, and colleagues to laugh, cry, and create lasting memories.

No matter why you take part in Relay, however, one thing is clear: with every step you take, you are helping the American Cancer Society save lives. With your help, we aren’t just fighting one type of cancer – we’re fighting for every birthday threatened by every cancer in every community. Each person who shares the Relay experience can take pride in knowing that they are working to create a world where this disease will no longer threaten the lives of our loved ones or claim another year of anyone’s life.

Relay For Life at The University of Georgia

As the first collegiate Relay, UGA Relay For Life strives to be the best at innovating new ways to engage students and the Athens community in the fight against cancer. Through the Event Leadership Team, Team Captains, Committees, special events, and fundraising efforts UGA Relay fosters a network of friendships, support systems, and hope. We work to honor the strength and courage of our survivors, fondly reflect on the lives of our lost ones, and take action to end cancer’s grip on society and create a world where no one has to hear the words "you have cancer". The members of UGA Relay strive to serve as a beacon of light and hope to the Athens community by spreading our mission, resilience, and passion everywhere we go. 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=SfEgob3to9g
summary: Relay for Life is a program that benefits the American Cancer Society. We celebrate, remember, and fight back to honor and support cancer survivors & caregivers. Through community and campus events, we hope to fundraise, educate, and advocate for a cure.

name: Future Health Promoters at UGA
description: We are dedicated to promoting healthy lifestyles and educating the community about issues of health while providing students at UGA and community members of Athens-Clarke county an opportunity to learn about and practice the responsibilities of professional health promoters and educators. We encourage people to be active in their personal health and the health of their communities, and to educate themselves with the most current research available.
summary: Open to all majors, we are committed to raising health awareness and supporting causes that enhance the health and well-being of UGA and Athens communities. We do this through tabling, community service, fitness classes, and fundraising events

name: Indian Cultural Exchange
description:
The purpose of the Indian Cultural Exchange (ICX) at UGA is to unite students with an South Asian background and those who have interest in the South Asian culture through activities that invoke cultural, social, and community-service involvement. We wish to exchange culture and educate the greater university community through the use of service, social, and cultural events.

Our signature event is called UGA India Night, and it is an intercollegiate Bollywood-fusion dance competition that honors Indian culture and Athens, GA on a national scale with 2000 attendees. We have dance teams come in from across the nation to compete on the Classic Center stage in downtown Athens.
summary: The Indian Cultural Exchange (ICX) at UGA is a home away from home for anyone interested in South Asian culture. ICX works to bring the South Asian community, as well as others, together through various cultural, social, and community service events.

name: Students for Life
description:
Join our email list and GroupMe by visiting our website, prolifeuga.com. You can also follow our social media accounts on Facebook, Instagram and Twitter @prolifeuga to stay up-to-date.
 
Students for Life of UGA is a student-founded and student-run Pro-Life group at the University of Georgia in Athens, GA. Group activities are centered around three main goals:

1) To support pregnant women and unborn children in the Athens area and at UGA.
 
2) To educate the campus community about the dignity and humanity of life in the womb.
 
3) To break down the barrier that prevents people from discussing, with integrity and respect, the crucial human rights issue of abortion.
 
We are an official chapter of Students for Life of America, and we work closely with other groups that share our mission, including Georgia Right to Life, Secular Pro-Life, and more.
 
The best place to reach us is direst message on Instagram
summary: Students for Life works to educate fellow students about the loss of life due to abortion and to promote awareness of resources for pregnant and parenting students.

name: Ballroom Dance Club @ UGA
description:
The purpose of the Ballroom Dance Club, UGA shall be to provide social opportunities and instruction for ballroom dance on and off the UGA campus , including lessons, workshops, dance parties, and notice of ballroom related events on campus and in the Athens area.

There are no requirements for joining our club! No dues, meeting requirements, or dance experience. We regularly meet on Mondays and Wednesdays from 5:30-6:30pm, in the dance building completely free of charge.

Follow us on Facebook and/or Instagram to hear more about all of our events @ballroomdanceclubuga
summary: An open-involvement club that anyone can join and expresses interest in learning the different styles and techniques in Ballroom Dancing! No partner or experience necessary!

name: Prelude Dance Ensemble
description: Prelude Dance Ensemble welcomes dancers from a wide range of dance experience, though we prefer dancers who have had previous experience. We hold auditions at the beginning of each semester, allow choreographers to present their pieces to the ensemble, and assign dances to dancers based on how each dancer ranks the available pieces. Prelude exhibits our completed dances in a free, end-of-the-semester show open to the Athens-Clarke County and University of Georgia community in April for spring semester.
summary: Prelude Dance Ensemble provides University of Georgia students with a student-led, student-choreographed creative outlet that encourages artistic expression through all types of dance in a supportive and friendly environment.

name: Classic City Powerlifting
description: Club Powerlifting brings people from all levels of lifting together, from expert level record holders, to total first timer novices. We push for progress from all of our members, because we're all about getting strong. With many people sharing the same passion for iron, its impossible to not make friends and become close training partners with fellow members. Overall we want all our members to always get better, be competitive, and have a good time with their powerlifting friends.
summary: Club Powerlifting at UGA is a competitive powerlifting club sport that aims to help current or prospective powerlifters become the best they can be in local, national and international level competition.

name: American Society of Heating, Refrigeration, and Air-Conditioning Engineers
description:
ASHRAE, founded in 1894, is a global society advancing human well-being through sustainable technology for the built environment. The Society and its members focus on building systems, energy efficiency, indoor air quality, refrigeration and sustainability within the industry. Through research, standards writing, publishing and continuing education, ASHRAE shapes tomorrow’s built environment today. ASHRAE was formed as the American Society of Heating, Refrigerating and Air-Conditioning Engineers by the merger in 1959 of American Society of Heating and Air-Conditioning Engineers (ASHAE) founded in 1894 and The American Society of Refrigerating Engineers (ASRE) founded in 1904.

Mission:
To advance the arts and sciences of heating, ventilating, air conditioning and refrigerating to serve humanity and promote a sustainable world.

Vision
ASHRAE will be the global leader, the foremost source of technical and educational information, and the primary provider of opportunity for professional growth in the arts and sciences of heating, ventilating, air conditioning and refrigerating.
summary: ASHRAE, founded in 1894, is a global society advancing human well-being through sustainable technology for the built environment. This is the UGA student chapter of the Society.

name: Human Development and Family Sciences Graduate Student Organization
description:
HDFS GSO (or GSO for short) is a collection of student scholars who are focused on promoting the professional development of students who are interested in human development and family science.

The purpose of this organization is to create community, promote the professional development of its members, and to advance the professions related to Child and Family Development and to provide services to the College of Family and Consumer Sciences.
summary: HDFS GSO (or GSO for short) is a collection of student scholars who are focused on engendering the professional development of students who are interested in families.

name: Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club
description: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club raises funds and awareness for blood cancer research and treatments for patients. The organization holds various educational events and fundraisers in order to achieve this mission. Our events promote fostering the campus community by bringing the university together for a common cause: blood cancer.
summary: The Leukemia and Lymphoma Society Club raises funds and awareness for blood cancer research and treatments for patients.

name: American Pharmacists Association - Academy of Student Pharmacists
description:
UGA's chapter of the American Pharmacists Association Academy of Student Pharmacists (APhA-ASP) is proud to be the largest student organization at the College of Pharmacy. In the College of Pharmacy, APhA-ASP plays a key role in helping students navigate pharmacy school, advocate for policy that advances pharmacy practice, explore careers in pharmacy, and connect with student pharmacists and pharmacists nationwide. Our members experience a variety of benefits including professional development resources, career guidance, networking opportunities, leadership development, and study resources for national board exams. Our APhA-ASP chapter organizes several health fairs and patient care events throughout the year and sends members to local, state, and national conferences. Our mission is to be the “collective voice of student pharmacists, to provide opportunities for professional growth, and to envision and actively promote the future of pharmacy.”

APhA-ASP chapters from pharmacy schools nationwide in total represent over 36,000 student pharmacists. On an even larger scale, APhA-ASP is one of the three academies of the American Pharmacists Association, which is the largest pharmacy association in the country, representing over 60,000 members across all areas of pharmacy practice. By joining APhA-ASP, members are taking the first step in becoming actively involved with an organization that will represent them throughout their entire pharmacy career, whether it is retail, hospital, industry, or any other specialty in pharmacy. We look forward to a superior year in bettering ourselves as student pharmacists, serving our future patients through providing the highest quality of care, and laying a strong foundation for our future in pharmacy.
summary: The mission of the APhA-Academy of Student Pharmacists (APhA-ASP) is to be the collective voice of student pharmacists, to provide opportunities for professional growth, to improve patient care, and to envision and advance the future of pharmacy.

name: Integrative Conservation Student Organization
description: The mission of ICSO is to develop and promote learning, research, dialogue, and active engagement in the area of integrative conservation. This organization is based at University of Georgia, but seeks to increase communication and discussion with the greater academic and professional sectors involved in the research, application, or communication of conservation and sustainability science.
summary: The Integrative Conservation Student Organization serves the students of the Integrative Conservation (ICON) program by creating spaces for the exchange of ideas among students and conservation thinkers at UGA and beyond.

name: Clarinet Association at the University of Georgia
description: The purpose of this organization is to support the activities of the clarinet studio at the University of Georgia’s Hugh Hodgson School of Music,  to support and enhance student’s musicianship through unique experiences, and to increase awareness of campus clarinet events.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to support the activities of the clarinet studio at the University of Georgia, enhance musicianship through unique experiences, and to increase awareness of musical events both on and off campus.

name: Episcopal Campus Ministry at UGA
description:
As a mission of the Episcopal Diocese of Atlanta, we challenge ourselves and the world to love like Jesus as we worship joyfully, serve compassionately, and grow spiritually.

Each week, we hold worship, community, and service events that all members are invited to participate in. We strive to serve our community alongside one another while also fostering personal growth in faith. We provide Athens area students and young adults with opportunities to worship, build new relationships, study the Bible, and serve God on campus and in the local community, no matter who you are, what you believe, and where you are in your faith journey, all are welcome, NO EXCEPTIONS! Stop by anytime and see what life at ECM is all about. We can't wait to meet you!
summary: The Episcopal Campus Ministry at UGA strives to cultivate a community of belonging that fosters spiritual growth as we share God’s love with the world.

name: Phi Sigma Pi Beta Zeta
description:
Phi Sigma Pi is a gender-inclusive, diverse National Honors Fraternity with the purpose to uphold three ideals. The first is the acquisition and dissemination of information and knowledge through scholarship. The second is the application of professional skills and the fostering of leadership qualities by promoting and advancing the welfare of humanity. The third is the fostering of a non-discriminatory, fraternal fellowship within Phi Sigma Pi. These three ideals support the ultimate goal of social service.

There are only a few requirements making one eligible to join. You must currently be enrolled as a UGA undergraduate student and available to attend necessary on-campus meetings/events. Additionally, prospective members must meet one of the three following requirements: have completed at least 12 hours of college courses; have at least one semester left at UGA (not including summer semesters); have a minimum 3.0 GPA.

For recruitment information, visit our Instagram @psp.bz or our website https://phisigmapiuga.weebly.com/
summary: Phi Sigma Pi is a co-ed National Honor Fraternity founded upon the basis of an attitude of learning with the avowed purpose of advancing academic, professional, and social ideals.

name: Terry Student Consulting
description:
Founded in 2012 by Sanjay Menon and Ben Hooper, Terry Student Consulting (TSC) sought to bridge the gap between students' interests in business problem-solving and businesses who desired to grow but faced obstacles. What resulted was a group of the University's top students coming together to work on fast-paced teams with Athens and Atlanta based clients.

Each semester our members work in teams of 3-5 people to help actual clients solve business problems. In addition to working with actual clients in a professional environment, Terry Student Consulting develops its members knowledge of consulting through presentations, workshops, and guest speakers

Today, TSC is the top management consulting group on campus, and one of only handful in the nation that both emphasizes interview preparation as well as hands-on consulting with real businesses. We are proud to have members and alumni receive offers to work at some of the top firms in the world.

Our goal is to place our members with consulting careers post graduation.
summary: The purpose of Terry Student Consulting is to equip students with analytical and business skills through exposure to business problems, speakers, and developmental programs and to place those members into competitive consulting careers after graduation.

name: Plant Breeding Genetics and Genomics Graduate Student Association
description:
The mission of the Plant Breeding, Genetics and Genomics Graduate Student Association is to:

  • Sponsor projects within the Institute of Plant Breeding, Genetics and Genomics that will enhance the graduate program and the graduate experience (such as guest speakers, social events, research forums, and service events).
  • Support the Institute of Plant Breeding, Genetics and Genomics activities contributing to the graduate program.
  • Have a voice on issues that may have an impact on graduate students at the departmental, college, and university levels.
  • Provide opportunities for collaboration and interfacing with all students within the Institute of Plant Breeding, Genetics and Genomics to promote a welcoming and inclusive environment.
  • Allow members to become familiar with the research projects of each graduate student and to better understand the scope of the research program within the Institute.
  • Maintain an organizational web site to provide information to current and prospective students, as well as to support graduate students and the departmental graduate program.
summary: The PBGG GSA exists to provide a framework for developing professional, academic, and social networks and to foster an inclusive environment for graduate students, in recognition of the importance of positive peer relationships.

name: Asian American Student Association
description: The Asian American Student Association (AASA) is a student-run organization with the purpose of developing unity within the APIDA community of the UGA campus and its surroundings. AASA strives to promote the diversity of Asian culture, connecting students with their heritage and one another. AASA also aims to raise awareness and educate others about Asian American identities.
summary: The Asian American Student Association (AASA) is a student-run organization with the purpose of developing unity within the APIDA community and the community at large through activities that promote Asian culture.

name: Christian Students at UGA
description: Christian Students at UGA is a student group composed of believers in Christ from various backgrounds, and we stand absolutely for the faith common to all Christians. We love the Lord Jesus Christ, endeavor to give Him “the first place in all things” (Col. 1:18), and have the utmost respect for His Word. We meet simply as Christians without any denominational affiliation because we treasure the testimony of the oneness of the Body of Christ, and our desire is to cooperate with Him for the accomplishment of God’s eternal purpose (Eph. 3:11) by ministering the unsearchable riches of Christ (Eph. 3:18) for the building up of the Body of Christ (Eph. 4:12).
summary: We are students at the University of Georgia who are Christians. We love the Lord Jesus and His Word!

name: Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. - Alpha Chapter
description:
We are the first recognized Asian-Interest Sorority at the University of Georgia. We are dedicated to our mission of spreading Asian awareness though our Everlasting Sisterhood, contributing to the community, and fostering dedicated female leaders. We look for undergraduate women who display our seven virtues of Loyalty, Honesty, Respect, Dedication, Integrity, Discipline, and Academic Excellence. 

The University of Georgia Alpha Chapter is a co-founding member of the Multicultural Greek Council (MGC) established on October 7, 2004 alongside Sigma Beta Rho Fraternity, Inc., and Lambda Phi Epsilon Fraternity, Inc. We are also proud to be the first Asian-Interest sorority recognized not only by Greek Life at the University of Georgia but also in the entire state of Georgia!

Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. has 21 strong established chapters on campuses across the nation, 4 alumnae chapters, and 2 inactive chapters, including: 

[Active Chapters]

Alpha – University of Georgia

Beta – University of Cincinnati

Gamma – Georgia State University

Epsilon – University of Central Florida

Zeta – Georgia Institute of Technology

Eta – DePaul University

Theta – Emory University

Iota – Grand Valley State University

Kappa – Loyola University Chicago

Mu – University at Albany

Nu – Iowa State University

Xi - University of Iowa

Omicron - University of West Florida

Pi - University of North Carolina – Charlotte

Rho - University of Connecticut

Sigma - Clemson University

Tau - University of Wisconsin - Madison

[Associate Chapters]

Ohio State University 

University of Nebraska-Lincoln

University of Tennessee - Knoxville 

 

[Inactive Chapters]

Illinois State University

University of Delaware

 

[Alumnae Chapters] 

University of Georgia 

Central Florida 

University of Cincinnati

The University of North Carolina at Charlotte 



Though we are a fairly young sorority, we have grown tremendously over the years. We continue to spread Asian-awareness through sisterhood to more locations across the county, so that other collegiate women may be embraced by the everlasting sisterhood that has given sisters priceless lifelong experiences as well as treasured memories.
summary: “Delta Phi Lambda Sorority, Inc. advocates Asian awareness and empowers women leaders through its values-based programs and everlasting sisterhood.”

name: Beta Alpha Psi
description: Beta Alpha Psi is a national scholastic and professional fraternity. The purpose of Beta Alpha Psi includes advancing the study and practice of accounting by providing opportunities for self development and by promoting association among members and professional accountants, as well as encouraging a sense of ethical, social, and public responsibility.
summary: The primary objective of Beta Alpha Psi is to encourage and give recognition to scholastic and professional excellence in the business information field.

name: Students Oncological Advocates in Pharmacy
description:
Student Oncological Advocates in Pharmacy (SOAP) was founded in 2012 by UGA College of Pharmacy alumna, Dr. Michelle Borg. SOAP is dedicated to promoting awareness for all cancers, supporting cancer patients and survivors, and providing opportunities for students interested in oncology. SOAP hopes to garner support for oncological research and unite those who are affected by cancer through advocacy, education, and community involvement. The organization is open to any currently enrolled pharmacy school students or pre-pharmacy students at the University of Georgia. In 2022, SOAP became a part of the national oncology advocacy organization, NCODA, as a chapter of their Professional Student Organization.

 

Throughout the year, SOAP holds various general body meetings that aim to educate members on oncology through presentations given by various speakers and professors. This fall, Dr. Amber Clemmons, the clinical pharmacy specialist for the adult bone marrow transplantation team at Augusta University Medical Center, provided information on bone marrow transplants and donations. Dr. Wendy Johnson, a pediatric pharmacist at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta, shared her experiences in the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center. SOAP educates members during journal clubs and through the bi-annual publication of the Oncology Bulletin newsletter. Journal club allows members to learn about new oncology medications, analyze various drug comparison studies, and discuss the applicability of these new treatments and studies on pharmacy practice. Members are able to write for the annual Oncology Bulletin about various oncology topics. Dr. Clemmons serves as the editor of this student publication. SOAP is annually featured in the Georgia Pharmacist magazine and publishes its own biannual newsletter, providing students the opportunity to contribute oncology related articles.

 

SOAP participates in the College of Pharmacy’s annual health fair, Pharmtoberfest in October; during this year's event, SOAP held a bone marrow donor registration drive through DKMS, as well as provided education about prevention and breast cancer awareness. SOAP co-hosted a second bone marrow donor registration drive at the Tate Student Center. Each fall, SOAP members volunteer at the Free to Breathe 5K which raises awareness and support for the Lung Cancer Foundation. This past November, SOAP members gave back to the community by volunteering to make dinner at the Hope Lodge, a place for cancer patients to stay while undergoing treatment at Emory. Each February, SOAP participates in a national “Stand Up to Cancer” movement by hosting a World Cancer Day event at the COP. That same month, SOAP members made Valentine’s Day cards for the patients at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. Every April, SOAP participates in UGA Relay for Life; fundraising for this event is done throughout the year. The money donated at Relay for Life benefits The American Cancer Society. During the past year, SOAP has also raised money for Winship Cancer Institute at Emory University to aid in oncology clinical trials and research and for Children’s Miracle Network through UGA Miracle. The annual “Pillympics” fundraiser event benefiting Winship Cancer Institute is always a success. Students and faculty members at the COP competed in three events to claim the title of Pillympics Champions. The events SOAP has hosted gives students the opportunity to give back to the community, as well as interact with cancer patients and healthcare professionals in the field of oncology pharmacy. 

 

Overall, SOAP serves as a way to unite all those who have been touched by cancer.
summary: SOAP is dedicated to promoting awareness for all cancers, supporting cancer patients and survivors, and providing opportunities for students interested in oncology.

name: Geology Club at UGA
description:
The Geology Club is a professional organization of persons dedicated to the pursuit and improvement of geology and geosciences at all levels.  The club shall promote the Geology Department within the university community, the Athens community, within the state of Georgia, and beyond.  The club will facilitate interdisciplinary activities between the Geology department and other UGA departments, as well as with other universities' geoscience programs.  Interdisciplinary activities will include the coordination of collaborative events and field trips, etc. 

The Geology Club will provide a way for non-Geology majors to get involved with the Geology Department and learn more about the field.  The club will help provide a resource list of scholarships, grants, internships, research opportunities, and other opportunities for undergraduate and graduate students, and encourage members to apply for prestigious honors and awards.  The Geology Club will encourage and support undergraduate research in the UGA Geology Department, by working to provide financial resources, publicity, and incentives. The club will also serve as an avenue for graduate students to mentor undergraduate students within the UGA Geology department. The club will provide continuing support of UGA Geology department alumni by informing the community of graduates' successful careers, and helping alumni to stay connected with the UGA Geology department.
summary: The Geology Club is a professional organization dedicated to furthering one's knowledge of geology and the world around us. The Geology Club focuses on fun enriching trips, outreach opportunities, and educational talks.

name: Anime Club at UGA
description:
Have you just finished watching an amazing anime series, but you have no one to discuss it with? Do you want to talk about the latest chapter of your favorite manga with fellow readers? Do you just want friends who share your interests? Look no further! Anime Club at UGA is the #1 destination for discussion about anime and related interests for University of Georgia students!

We are an organization that is dedicated to the appreciation of the diverse and wonderful world of Japanese animation. At each meeting, we watch and discuss anime series that are voted on by club members and play anime themed games & activities. Apart from our weekly meetings, we host several fun events, such as Anime Day Out and Game Day. We also attend various anime conventions, such as Anime Weekend Atlanta in the fall, and MomoCon in the spring.

Meetings every Wednesday @ Dawson Hall, Room 0202 from 7-9PM

Sound like a good time? Join the fun and learn more information by clicking the link to our Discord server!

https://discord.gg/sWFkEcE
summary: Anime Club at UGA is dedicated to promoting Japanese animation as an art form and as a source of cultural discussion for the students of the University of Georgia.

name: Ecology Graduate Student Organization
description: The Ecology Graduate Student Organization serves as the representative and decision-making body for the Ecology graduate student community. The GSO is the voice of the graduate students, and representatives communicate to the faculty and staff within the campus and to the greater campus community. Additionally, this organization is in charge of Graduate Student Symposium, a campus-recognized, 30-year long symposium that features student research in ecology.
summary: GSO is an advocacy organization which serves as a representative and decision-making body for the Ecology graduate student community and provides a unified voice to faculty and staff within the department and to the greater campus community.

name: Art History Society
description:
AHS is a student-led organization at UGA that sponsors student interest and research in art history. AHS values art from all cultures of origin and strives to promote an interdisciplinary approach to art history. All students (undergraduate and graduate), are encouraged to join, regardless of their major. Our events include museum/gallery visits, local theater viewings, guest lectures, and mini socials. By joining the Art History Society, students can discuss with each other the ARHI courses at UGA, internships, projects, and their own art historical interests. In addition, our discussion-based meetings grant members opportunities to practice their academic and oratorical skills in a comfortable, informal space.

If you would like to join, please register through the UGA Involvement Network! When you come to your first meeting, we will provide you with the AHS GroupMe link. Follow us on social media to stay updated, especially our Instagram! (@uga.ahs)
summary: AHS focuses on creating a strong network of students who are interested in and passionate about art history through events, socials, and resources. All students are welcome and encouraged to join regardless of major!

name: Ecotones @ UGA
description: The Ecotones are a co-ed group of ecologically minded students at the University of Georgia who also happen to enjoy singing. The idea to start the Ecotones arose at a party on Saturday, November 14th, 2009. Several UGA Ecology graduate students were standing in a living room singing songs and the thought came up to start an a cappella group. The result was one broken chair and a new vocal group called the Ecotones. Recruiting began and on Sunday, January 17th, 2010 the Ecotones held their first rehearsal. Since then, we have grown tremendously. We partner with The Upper Oconee Watershed Network to help take care of our river system. Every semester we perform at a multitude of events, as well as have our own big concert (Ecopella in the Spring and an additional concert in the Fall). We are continually building our repertoire and will perform at more events in the 2025-2026 school year. Keep up with our Facebook and Instagram, check out our Youtube videos, and catch a performance!
summary: The Ecotones are the only ecologically minded a cappella group on campus. We're a co-ed group that brings our love of music and the environment together. Check us out on Facebook and Instagram to see when our auditions and shows are!

name: Chi Alpha
description:
Chi Alpha Campus Ministry is an international organization spread across over 250 campuses worldwide. We exist to reconcile students to Christ and equip them to transform the campus, the marketplace, and the world through establishing a culture of healthy leaders, disciple-making groups, cross-cultural missions, and Holy Spirit empowerment. 

Our Mission for Chi Alpha at UGA is simple but revolutionary when we can strive to achieve it. We believe we’re all called to the Great Commission as Christ followers, so anything short of our eternal calling is selling short the Gospel.  

Matthew 28:18-20

We have different activities including weekly Worship Services, Small Groups, fun nights and more!

Visit our website or Instagram to learn more and get in touch!

Instagram: @chialphaatuga

Website: chialphaatuga.com
summary: Chi Alpha exist to reconcile students to Christ and equip them to transform the campus, the marketplace, and the world through establishing a culture of healthy leaders, disciple-making groups, cross-cultural missions, and Holy Spirit empowerment.

name: Dean William Tate Honor Society
description:
The purpose of this organization shall be to recognize the top 24 first-year students each year who have made a contribution to the University of Georgia community through their academic excellence and extracurricular involvement. Acceptance into the Tate Society is recognized as the highest honor a first year can receive at the University of Georgia. Applications open to first-year students each February.

Our 3 pillars are leadership, scholarship, and service, and our selection criteria are built around this. During the year, our internal programming consists of member development, retreats, and service activities.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to recognize the top 24 first-year students each year who have made a contribution to the University of Georgia community through their academic excellence and extracurricular involvement.

name: Humans vs. Zombies
description:
Here's how our game works:

Using Nerf blasters and balled up socks, the "Human" players are to defend themselves from the "Zombie" players, whose aim is to tag as many Humans as possible. If hit by a NERF dart of balled-up sock, a Zombie is stunned for a certain amount of time, preventing their interaction with the game and allowing Humans to escape. Once tagged by a Zombie, Human players typically become a Zombie themselves and join the hunt.

Unless otherwise specified, we ONLY play on campus, and we DO NOT play inside UGA buildings.
summary: We organize games of NERF-based tag on campus between "Humans" & "Zombies", including a few weekend games each semester with missions that follow a unique story-line. We aim to promote an active social lifestyle and strong group atmosphere for all.

name: Chess Dawgs
description:
Chess Dawgs is the official UGA Chess Club dedicated to promoting the matching of wits in relaxed social gatherings, as well as in competitive tournaments.

Meetings are usually held on Tuesdays, from 7-9pm on the 3rd floor of the Tate Student Center near the Market at Tate.

Come play chess!
summary: UGA Chess Club

name: Poultry Science Club
description: The poultry science club consists of students who are interested in the poultry industry or just birds in general. Some meeting activities include guest speakers who come to promote internships and jobs, philanthropy such as canned good drives and collecting toys for needy children, and fun activities such as bonfires and chicken finger taste tests. We strive to help students get to know other students and faculty in the Poultry Science Department.
summary: The poultry science club is a club consisting of students who are interested in the poultry industry or birds in general. Meeting consist of a variety of things such as guest speakers, philanthropy, or fun activities to help students, others, and faculty

name: Women's Club Volleyball
description:
The Women's Club Volleyball at UGA is a competitive club sport that plays all year round. We travel the southeast region and participate in highly competitive tournaments in both the fall and spring, including Nationals in April. Our team is run by three captains who also play on the team and we do not have a coach.

We have tryouts for the fall season in August, and the spring season in January. Our team usually consists of 10-12 girls, (including the captains). Most girls have a lot of volleyball experience; however, anyone is welcome to try out. If you are interested in trying out, please email us at ugaclubvball@gmail.com to be added to the mailing list to receive information about upcoming open gyms and tryouts. 

We practice Mondays and Wednesdays, from 6:30-8:30pm at the Ramsey Athletic Center. We compete in about 2-3 tournaments during the fall and about 4-5 tournaments during the spring, including Nationals in early April. Dues in the fall are $200, and in the spring $200; these amounts are subject to change slightly. Team dues cover all team apparel, tournament fees, our gym space, and all travel costs (except for flights to nationals).

Our mission is to create a team of young women who have a passion for the sport of volleyball and want to continue playing at a competitive level in college. Our team is filled with women who upon coming to college were unable to give up the sport because of the dedication and passion that they have for the game. We also strive to develop close friendships within our team on and off the court.
summary: Women's Club Volleyball is a competitive club sport that plays all year round. We travel the southeast region and participate in highly competitive tournaments.

name: Alumni and Associates of Family, Career, & Community Leaders of America
description:
Alumni and Associates (AAFCCLA) is part of a network of adults who believe in Family, Career, and Community Leaders of America, its mission and purposes, and is key for anyone who is committed to investing in youth dedicated to becoming strong leaders in their families, careers, and communities. 

Since 1945, FCCLA members have been making a difference in their families, careers, and communities by addressing important personal, work, and societal issues through family and consumer sciences education. Today more than 215,000 members are active in a network of associations in 50 states as well as in the District of Columbia, the Virgin Islands, and Puerto Rico. AAFCCLA stands to help promote and support chapters of FCCLA.
summary: A professional organization for all majors within the College of Family and Consumer Sciences that supports middle and high school chapters of Family, Career, and Community Leaders of America (FCCLA).

name: Student Dietetic Association
description: The goal of SDA is to serve the college, community, and each other by involving as many dietetics students as possible to promote a message of health, scientifically-backed nutrition, and exercise. The club is open to all students who have an interest in food and nutrition. SDA provides a great opportunity to meet students with common interests, build your resume, and learn more about nutrition and nutrition-related careers. SDA holds biweekly meetings in Dawson Hall featuring guest speakers, provides opportunities for professional development and community service, and holds social events each semester.
summary: The Student Dietetic Association at the University of Georgia is a student-led group that provides information and support to students surrounding dietetic internship programs and careers as well as community service and networking opportunities.

name: Club Tennis
description: Club Tennis is an organization that competes around the country in competitive tennis tournaments in the USTA Tennis on Campus League. For tryout or more Information follow our Instagram Page: @UGAclubtennis
summary: Club Tennis is a student run organization that practices as a team a few times a week at the Intramural Fields and competes in USTA Tennis on Campus tournaments throughout the school year. If you are looking for more info follow the instagram page.

name: Sigma Alpha
description:
We are a social and professional sorority for women who have a strong interest in agriculture. Our pillars are Leadership, Scholarship, Fellowship, and Service.

 

The Objective of Sigma Alpha... 
Is to promote its members in all facets of agriculture and to strengthen the bonds of friendship among them. Members strive for achievement in scholarship, leadership, and service, and to further the development of excellence in women pursuing careers in agriculture.

 

Who can Join?

Women with an interest in agriculture who are not involved in another social sorority.

 

How to join?

We host recruitment every semester. Reach out to our Recruitment Director or other officers to find out times! Information will also be posted on our Instagram @ugasigmaalpha

 

When do we meet?

We have chapter meetings on Monday nights at 7:00 p.m. Our other events will be listed on a calendar for you upon joining!
summary: Sigma Alpha is a professional agricultural sorority that promotes scholarship, leadership, service and fellowship among its members.

name: Campus Connection
description:
We desire to connect the world to the gospel of Jesus Christ through our local college ministry here at home. We make it our mission to transform lives through Bible study, family groups, and discipleship relationships. Ultimately, that transformation leads us to share our faith with other people both here and abroad.

If you are new to the Athens area and looking for a home church, or simply college student trying to find a place to plug-in, we want to hear about it! Please email us and let us show you how to plug-in as so many other college-aged individuals have.
summary: Welcome to the Prince Avenue College Ministry! Our Mission is to effectively share the gospel and develop fully devoted followers of Jesus Christ through worship, discipleship, evangelism and missions.

name: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
description: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Student Branch at the University of Georgia (UGA) was established in 2013 to uphold IEEE's mission of fostering technological excellence and furthering the professional development of students. UGA IEEE strives to accomplish this goal by providing students with opportunities to gain hands-on experiences that exceed the classroom, as well as opportunities to network with professional corporations and other IEEE members and student branches. Through opportunities to work on projects, attend conferences and competitions, develop skills at workshops, and interact with professionals in our desired fields, UGA IEEE aims to aid the development of outstanding engineers who will make positive impacts for the benefit of humanity.
summary: The Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers (IEEE) Student Branch at the University of Georgia (UGA) was established in 2013 to uphold IEEE's mission of fostering technological excellence and furthering student professional development.

name: Herpetological Society at University of Georgia
description: Herpetology is the study of reptiles and amphibians.  Research in this field examines the ecology, evolution, physiology, and behavior of these animals.  The University of Georgia Herpetological Society (UGHS) is a student led organization open to anyone within and outside the University of Georgia community.  The society was founded in 2005 with three main goals: to learn, to teach, and to conserve. Currently serving over 100 members, the Society invites speakers from around the US for a meeting on the last Tuesday of each month. In addition, the Society provides live animal outreach programs to groups in the community, sponsors field trips to nearby locations and regional Herp Society meetings, and provides an outlet for herpetological husbandry, research, and conservation among UGA students and staff.
summary: UGHS is a student-led scientific organization that promotes the conservation and study of reptiles and amphibians. We strive to educate ourselves and others through live animal outreach, guest speakers, and field trips.

name: Baha'i Student Association
description:
The Bahá'i Student Association is the student-led organization of the Baha'i Faith on campus. Our discussion focus on the teachings of the Báb and Bahá'u'lláh: the oneness of God, all religions, and humanity; the equality of men and women; the agreement of science and religion; the need for universal education and language; and the independent investigation of truth. We seek to build a community based on Bahá'i principles and welcome all people to our meetings.

Every Thursday, we meet on campus to connect with friends and discuss Bahá'i principles. We also regularly volunteer at various organizations in the Athens community, hold monthly movie nights, attend local Báha'i community feasts and Holy Day celebrations, and host other social events to connect with the group.
summary: The Bahá'i Student Association applies the teachings of the Baha'i Faith to build community through meaningful discussion, devotional gatherings, community service and social activities. Open to everybody!

name: Christian Grads Fellowship - UGA
