description: The Christian Grads Fellowship exists to help graduate students in their spiritual journey by providing opportunities to know Christ, grow in relationship with Him, and integrate their faith in their academic discipline.
summary: The Christian Grads Fellowship seeks to provide community for UGA grad students by helping them in their spiritual journey by providing opportunities to know Christ, grow in relationship with Him, and integrate their faith in their academic discipline.

name: Spike Squad
description: Spike Squad is a UGA spirit group dedicated to cheering on all UGA sports by wearing spiked football pads, red and black paint, and attending athletic events with the intent of being as loud and supportive as possible. We enthusiastically support all of UGA and the Athens community through participating in campus outreach and community events.
summary: As the ultimate student spirit organization, our primary goal is to impact Dawg Nation and the Athens community in a way that supports our fellow Dawgs and furthers the reputation of the University of Georgia.

name: Georgia Museum of Art Student Association, The
description: The Georgia Museum of Art Student Association shares the mission of the Georgia Museum of Art and the University of Georgia to support and to promote teaching, research, and service. The GMOA Student Association is committed to promoting the museum to students and encouraging them to be involved in the arts here at UGA and in Athens. We do this by hosting Student Nights at the museum and organizing community art events, enabling students and community members to come together all in the name of art!
summary: The Georgia Museum of Art Student Association is committed to promoting the museum to students and encouraging them to be involved in the arts at UGA and in Athens. We do this by hosting Student Nights, holding meetings, & organizing community art events

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
description: The Chinese Students and Scholars Association (CSSA) is a non-profit and non-political Chinese student organization at UGA, affiliated with the Center for Global Student Engagement (GSE) and recognized by the Chinese Embassy. Our primary focus is to serve Chinese students and scholars from various regions of China, but we warmly welcome everyone to join us. We aim to unite the Chinese student community, fostering friendships through our activities. CSSA organizes regular team-building events, entertainment activities, and celebrations of major festivals such as the UGA Mid-Autumn Festival and the Spring Festival Gala.
summary: CSSA mainly consists of Chinese students and scholars from different backgrounds. We are working on promoting Chinese culture and general awareness of China, fostering global friendships, and organizing cultural events. Everyone is welcome to join us!

name: Amnesty International at UGA
description: Amnesty International at UGA is a student-led branch of Amnesty International, a non-governmental organization of more than 7 million supporters, members and in more than 150 countries and territories who put forth efforts to rectify and end all human rights abuses.
summary: Amnesty International at UGA strives to create platforms on campus for individual and collective awareness, education, and activism on behalf of human rights and social justice issues occurring locally and internationally.

name: Pre-Student Osteopathic Medical Association
description: The purpose of Pre-SOMA is to advocate and educate the public about osteopathic medicine. This would increase the number of osteopathic medical school applications, and prepare and guide members for entrance into DO schools. We also participate in educational and volunteer events in Athens, and will host guest speakers from the osteopathic medical field.
summary: The purpose of Pre-SOMA is to advocate and educate the public about Osteopathic Medicine and principles.  Thus, increasing the number of Osteopathic Medical School applications and preparing and guiding members for entrance into medical colleges.

name: Xi Delta Sorority, Inc.
description:
Xi Delta was founded at the University of Georgia in January of 1991. We are a diverse group of women all focused on attaining bonds of sisterhood, academic achievement, leadership development, and commitment to serving others.

Our smaller size gives us the unique ability to establish and maintain intimate life-long relationships while our independence allows us the autonomy to create the organization that best fits the needs of the members without the need for a national governing body.

For many college women, sorority membership offers a lifetime of learning and involvement, providing valuable opportunities for sisterhood, leadership, scholarship, social, and philanthropic activities during college years and beyond.

Xi Delta sisters have wonderful, lasting friendships that can help make a big school like the University of Georgia seem a little smaller, especially when you see a sister’s smiling face just when you need it on campus.

Xi Delta members participate in all types of activities, and our campus involvement helps us to make the most of our overall college experiences and our futures.

Through sisterhood, philanthropy, social, and community acts, Xi Delta sisters create the most memorable college experiences possible.
summary: Xi Delta was founded at the University of Georgia in January of 1991. We are a diverse group of women focused on creating lifelong friends, supporting each other's endeavors, and fostering a sense of community through service.

name: Pre-Occupational Therapy Club
description: Omega Tau is a network for pre-occupational therapy students. We hold monthly meetings in which we host speakers including occupational therapists, graduate program admissions counselors, current students, and others who are knowledgeable about the field to provide insight and advice to members. We also provide opportunities to become more involved on campus and in the community through volunteering and shadowing. Our main goal is to prepare students with greater skills and knowledge useful for pursuing an occupational therapy career.
summary: Omega Tau is a network for pre-occupational therapy students. We provide opportunities and resources useful for pursuing an occupational therapy career.

name: Gamma Sigma Sigma
description:
Gamma Sigma Sigma is a nonselective co-ed service sorority. We host multiple service, social, and sisterhood events each semester, and we recruit new members every semester!

In addition to hosting our own service events and fundraisers, we also have volunteering partnerships with organizations in the Athens-Clarke County community, such as Project Safe, Boys and Girls Club Camp Kiwanis, and the Athens Area Humane Society. We also participate in UGA-wide philanthropy events, such as SWAB, Relay for Life, and Dawg Day of Service. Among our social events are a semi-formal, a weekend-long formal that takes place outside of Athens, sisterhood retreats, date nights, tailgates, and family days.

Feel free to contact any of our executive board members or our social media platforms with questions. We’d love to see you at rush!
summary: The Chi Chapter of Gamma Sigma Sigma national service sorority at the University of Georgia. We recruit non-selectively at the beginning of each academic year and host multiple on and off campus philanthropic events.

name: Orthodox Christian Fellowship
description: OCF aims to connect Orthodox college students—and those interested in the Orthodox Christian faith—to Christ and His Church. We meet every other week, often for dinner and discussion or other fellowship activities like movie nights and bonfires!  OCF also involves itself in various service projects, including feeding the homeless and participating in RealBreak trips to minister in various countries.  Our community works to provide an anchor for students navigating and growing their faith through the transition into college life and beyond.
summary: Orthodox Christian Fellowship (OCF) is the official collegiate campus ministry program under the Assembly of Canonical Orthodox Bishops of the United States of America.

name: Classic City Jazz
description:
We are the University of Georgia’s only vocal jazz group. CCJ specializes in jazz styles from swing, ballad, bebop, funk, cool, fusion Latin, and more. We also dabble in contemporary and mainstream music - usually with a nice jazzy twist. CCJ performs at a variety of venues and concerts both on and off campus throughout the year such as private events, public concerts, and jazz jams.

Typically, we meet twice a week for an hour and a half each rehearsal. The weekly rehearsal schedule is determined after auditions, as we try to find times in which all of our members are available.

Classic City Jazz has been at UGA for over ten years. We were initially started by a music staff member in UGA's Choral program, Mitos Andaya. She has since left the university, but CCJ continues on. We are now a student-run Jazz group. We are made up of students from majors all across campus, but we come together to perform Jazz. Many of our members had never sung Jazz prior to joining this group. But all of us have found a love for the music we sing. You are welcome to contact us with interest in joining the group at any time!

Our Spring 2026 auditions will be planned some time in early February, follow our Instagram for updates (@ugaccj) or email us with any questions (ugaccj@gmail.com)!
summary: A student run vocal jazz group known for our close-knit members, tight harmonies, casual atmosphere, and beautiful music. We sing a cappella as well as accompanied pieces when we perform at private events, public concerts, jazz jams, and more.

name: Whatever It Takes at UGA
description:
Whatever It Takes at UGA (WIT) is a student-led organization committed to combatting educational inequity in our own backyard. Through our Elementary and Middle School mentoring programs, we aim to improve four critical areas of children's lives in Athens-Clarke County — academics, health, safety, and family-life. Our partnerships with the community centers, schools, and parents of the neighborhoods we serve enable WIT to truly impact the lives of Athens' youth. 

WIT at UGA also provides an avenue for UGA students to give back to the Athens community by serving as mentors to Elementary, Middle, and High School students. Our mentoring programs offer a unique opportunity to build lasting relationships with children of all ages.

Whatever It Takes is about people: the people who we help, the people in our organization, and the people we partner with in order to achieve our goals. They are what matter to us; and like our name states, we will do whatever it takes for them. WIT at UGA is unique on the campus of the University of Georgia. We are about working directly in the neighboring community and stressing hands-on community involvement. We do not just fundraise to pay for a difference. We aim to be that difference.

For this reason, we care not only about the people we are helping, the Athens-Clarke County Community, but about the people who make us and are a part of us. WIT at UGA is a team. We are a team that comes together to fight for the children in our local Athens community.  We want everyone in our organization to know each other and see what projects we are involved in. We hope this encourages communication between our group to come up with new ideas, new projects, and reach new successes.

Our goal is to make a difference in the Athens-Clarke County Community. There are a lot of people who need that extra push or can use an extra hand in order to get up and get going. We aim to take on every aspect of life through improving saftey, health, education and family life within these communities. Join us in the fight!
summary: We are a student run initiative to build stronger communities in Athens. Through an after school elementary tutoring program, and special events within the community, we are striving to give back to Athens-Clarke county.

name: Wildlife Society, The
description: The student chapter of TWS at UGA holds biweekly meetings consisting of a professional speaker in the natural resources field in order to broaden students' ideas of natural resource careers as well as expand their professional network
summary: The student chapter of The Wildlife Society at UGA promotes conservation and responsible management of natural resources while supporting members in becoming certified wildlife biologists through education, outreach, fieldwork, and career development.

name: Undergraduate Neuroscience Organization
description:
The Undergraduate Neuroscience Organization is a group of undergraduate students at the University of Georgia who are focused on the field of neuroscience in undergraduate school, graduate school, and beyond. We aim to serve UGA, the Athens community, and people everywhere affected by neurological diseases by teaching the value of neuroscience and by showing the necessity of excellence in research and in medicine. We were founded in October of 2008 as an official University of Georgia organization, and we have been growing ever since.

We strive to create a sense of fellowship and community among students at UGA who share a passion for learning about the human brain. We hope to also create an association that strengthens the academic and professional needs of those pursuing medical school, graduate school, or other studies in the sciences. We provide opportunities for leadership and for networking among students and between students and faculty. We engage in philanthropic projects to aid basic neuroscience research and to spread awareness of the practical benefits of striving to unlock the secrets of the brain and push forward the frontiers of science.
summary: The Undergraduate Neuroscience Organization is a group of students from all majors interested in neuroscience. We bring in researchers & physicians to talk about their experiences. We serve the UGA & Athens communities by supporting Parkinson's Research.

name: WattyCollege
description: Confrontational approaches to evangelism usually backfire, especially today. At The Great Exchange, we use surveys to allow people to open up and talk about their spiritual background and their understanding of God, Jesus, and Christianity. After they have explained their views, we ask if we can offer some of our own. When students see that we genuinely care and are open to listen, they often, in turn, are eager to hear from us.
summary: Confrontational approaches to evangelism usually backfire. We use surveys to allow people to open up and talk about their spiritual background and their understanding of God, Jesus, and Christianity. After that, we ask if we can offer some of our own.

name: Arab Cultural Association
description: The Arab Cultural Association is an organization at the University of Georgia that intends to provide cultural education and awareness of Arab traditions, history, customs, culture, and language. The Arab Cultural Association is committed to showcasing and experiencing Arab culture, while providing a space for students to create a close community for both Arabs and non-Arabs alike.
summary: An organization that seeks to provide cultural education and awareness of Arabic traditions, history, customs, culture, and language.

name: Field
description:
The kingdom of heaven is like unto treasure hid in a field; the which when a man hath found, he hideth, and for joy thereof goeth and selleth all that he hath, and buyeth that field. Matthew 13:44 In this student-led organization, our mission is founded on the vision of Matthew 13:44. We want to help students, new and experienced Christians alike, with the resources, direction, and guidance to help them invest in finding their place in the FIELD. As a student organization, our goal is also to help students realize that as we are going through college, God is our ultimate help, and He not only wants us to feel the joy of his salvation, but He also wants us to share it with others. Come join us for prayer, Bible study, discussion of God and His word, and free food!

 

If you would like to learn more about our mission, we are connected with Campus Ministry International and you can visit their website here: https://campusministryonline.com/

 

More information: https://linktr.ee/thefielduga?utm_source=linktree_admin_share
summary: The Field is a student-led, Apostolic organization providing both new and experienced Christians alike with an opportunity to discover their purpose through faith in God. We welcome everyone for food and discussion about God’s word.

name: Kendo
description:
General Information:

Kendo (剣道) means the way of the sword (剣-sword 道-way/path). Kendo is a challenge that requires you to balance the cultivation of the mind, spirit, and body as one with continuous practice. Practice will teach/increase discipline and strengthen your body. Regular keiko (practice) includes footwork, sword (shinai and bokken) technique, sparring, and the occasional art forms. As for our team, we are a small group of individuals who have come together to enjoy kendo. We hope to provide an outlet for others to enjoy this practice as we have. We hope to create a place that people want to return to, while also carving out a place for ourselves and others. If you find the need for self-improvement, company, or if you desire to challenge yourself, this is a great place to start. We hope you will practice with us. Please note that we are not an official club recognised by SEUSKF, our goal is to give you the means to begin and continue practice.

お願いします

For more information please view this document https://docs.google.com/document/d/1-76OBMzuEQeDZJ9lt7EXUsZYnWroyyyD73RnTbLzeMY/edit?usp=sharing.

Beginners (and people who do not wish to spar): We will mostly focus on general conditioning. If you are looking for a fun way to keep in shape this is a good fit. Beginners will work on footwork, kiai (yelling), endurance training, general strength training, and basic shinai exercises. You will learn basic terminology, how tournaments work, and how to count to 10 in Japanese.

Bogu members should know what is expected of them.

A useful link:

Discord: https://discord.gg/cmuNMQspf7

From the All Japan Kendo Federation:
The purpose of practicing kendo is:
To mold the mind and body,
To cultivate a vigorous spirit,
And through correct and rigid training,
To strive for improvement in the art of kendo,
To hold in esteem human courtesy and honour,
To associate with others with sincerity,
And to forever pursue the cultivation of oneself.
This will make one be able:
To love his/her country and society,
To contribute to the development of culture,
And to promote peace and prosperity among all peoples.

https://www.kendo.or.jp/en/knowledge/kendo-concept/
summary: Our mission, at Kendo at UGA, is to cultivate the understanding, promote the tradition, and exercise our minds and bodies in The Way of the Sword.

name: Christus Victor Lutheran Church and Student Center
description: We live in Christ's victory here at Christus Victor and we hope you can join us in that celebration and share it with our community and world. We encourage you to look through our site at the various opportunities to plug in and invite you to join us as you are able. God's blessings to you!
summary: We are a close-knit group of Lutherans celebrating and sharing Christ's victory at UGA!

name: Quiz Bowl Club
description:
The Quiz Bowl Club is an academic trivia based club that competes primarily in intercollegiate tournaments throughout the Southeast! The questions are a variety of subject levels and tournaments vary at different levels of difficulty. Through Quiz Bowl, we hope to instill a team community while simultaneously promoting the academic interests of our members.

Fall 2025:

We meet Tuesday 6pm In Sanford Hall Room 212

We have a GroupMe for the club! Contact uga.quizbowl@gmail.com for more info (:
summary: To promote academic competition and interest at the college, high school, and middle school levels, and to compete against like-minded organizations from other schools.

name: Campus Kitchen at the University of Georgia, The
description:
The Campus Kitchen at UGA (CKUGA) is the student-powered hunger relief program of the UGA Office of Service-Learning. Each week, student volunteers take unused food collected from grocery stores, restaurants, and farmers markets to prepare meals and pack grocery bags for older adults and service agencies in Athens. CKUGA goes beyond meals by using food as a tool to combat senior isolation, foster community connections, and engage UGA students and faculty in developing sustainable solutions to food waste and hunger.

Please visit our website for more details and ways to get involved. 

Fill out our interest form if you want to get involved! 

 

Note: We do not accept memberships through the Involvement Network. See our website for information on joining our Leadership Team or to volunteer.
summary: The Campus Kitchen at the University of Georgia is a student-powered hunger relief program of the Office of Service-Learning, focusing on food waste and senior hunger in Athens, GA via weekly food recovery and redistribution operations.

name: Ice Hockey Club at UGA
description:
The Ice Hockey Team at UGA, dubbed the “Ice Dawgs”, was established in 1987. The Ice Dawgs now compete in the South Eastern Conference in the American Collegient Hockey Association, and play under NCAA rules. Georgia faces-off against ten SEC opponents including Florida, Tennessee, Kentucky, South Carolina & more. Other southern rival opponents include cross-state rival Georgia Tech, Clemson, Tampa, and many of the other 38 teams in the South region. The Georgia Ice Hockey Club has made great strides in the last decade. One of the newest members of CHF DI, UGA Hockey has participated in national post season tournament play in 13 of the last 15 seasons, including a recent semifinals appearance at the CHF National Tournament in Jacksonville, FL.  The Ice Dogs are nine-time winners of the coveted Thrasher Cup tournament held in Savannah, GA, won multiple season conference titles, conference championships and played in 10 conference championship games, including back to back SECHC champions in 2015-16 & 2016-17. The Ice Dawgs were the 2019 and 2022 SEC Conference Champions. 

The Ice Dawgs home ice is the Akins Ford Arena right in the heart of downtown Athens, GA. Players have the opportunity to play in front of 5000+ people including classmates, friends, family, students, and other people in the Athens Community.

Players on the Ice Dawgs team have received countless awards and accolades over the past thirty-one seasons, including 24 tournament MVP’s, 34 all-tournament team awards, 42 Academic All-Americans, 9 All-Regional team members, and 5 All-American team members .
summary: The UGA Hockey team is formed by a collection of former AA, AAA, High School and Junior players playing hockey while attending the University of Georgia.

name: Catholic Student Association
description: The Catholic Student Association is excited to welcome students into our community at the Catholic Center. Being a “Catholic Dawg” goes beyond weekly Mass; it’s Sunday night dinner, Tuesday praise and worship nights with our Wellspring band, finals week pancakes, naps on the Upper Room couches, laughs at the Mr. Catholic Center pageant, service opportunities, and countless other acts of ministry that build our home away from home. We pray that the Catholic Center becomes exactly that for you: a home. Our home is open to all, united with the universal church through the body of Christ. In Him, the CSA board welcomes all who come, and strives to serve the students in any way possible!
summary: The Catholic Student Association (CSA) offers a variety of events and ministries that engage students socially, spiritually, and intellectually within the supportive Catholic community.

name: Cellular Biology Graduate Student Association
description: The aim of CBGSA is to promote research and academic endeavors of our members, as well as provide opportunities for students to connect and build community.
summary: CBGSA builds a sense of community among the graduate students and faculty of the Cellular Biology Department.

name: First Page at UGA
description: First Page at UGA seeks to close the literacy gap in Athens-Clarke County by providing free books to low-income students. In middle income neighborhoods, the ratio of books to children is 1 to 13; in low-income neighborhoods, that ratio can get as high as 1 age-appropriate book for every 300 children. Our organization works hard to close that gap and provide children in Athens-Clarke County with new books. In the past five years, we have given away over 7,500 books to Title 1 elementary schools. We raise money through book sales and fundraising events, as well as hosting educational events on campus like our Banned Books Festival. We also volunteer to record audiobooks for the blind and dyslexic at Learning Ally, read with children at elementary schools, and distribute books to children in need. For many of them, the books we provide are the first books they can call their own.
summary: First Page at UGA seeks to close the literacy gap in Athens-Clarke County by providing free books to low-income students. We do so by volunteering in the local community and fundraising money for our grant program.

name: Swing Dance Club
description:
Join https://groupme.com/join_group/42707021/QvRfbJ for lesson and event times

Swing dancing was a group of street dances created along with the birth of Jazz music in the United States. It started out with Charleston from the Hot Jazz of the 1920s. In the 1930s and 40s Lindy Hop developed along with Big Band music. In the 1950s the dance evolved with early Rock 'N' Roll into what is now called East Coast Swing.

Each semester the club learns all these dances - East Coast Swing, Charleston and Lindy Hop. No previous experience or partners are required to join the club.  Dancing with others is a great way to make long-lasting friendships, test your memory, and get some cardio!

Lessons are free and offered to all UGA students and faculty. We meet every Tuesday from 6:00-7:00 pm in Tate, starting the second week of each semester.
summary: Each semester the club learns East Coast Swing, Charleston, West Coast Swing and Lindy Hop through group lessons.  These social dances foster friendship and dancing skill. No previous experience or partners are required to join the club.

name: Pride Alliance
description: Pride Alliance is the University of Georgia’s LGBTQ organization. Our goal is to empower LGBTQ students to become involved in local grassroots queer advocacy,  and nurture spaces for LGBTQ students to learn, lead, thrive, and heal.
summary: Pride Alliance is the University of Georgia’s LGBTQ organization where LGBTQ+ students and allies can come together to get involved in the Athens and UGA community through advocacy, leadership, education, and camaraderie.

name: Student Merchandising Association
description:
The University of Georgia's Student Merchandising Association is an organization designed for students of all majors especially those majoring or minoring in Fashion Merchandising in the College of Family and Consumer Sciences. 

SMA’s purpose is to provide students the opportunity to develop and enhance skills needed to obtain a career in the fashion industry. Career development is emphasized through merchandising, look-books,  fashion show production, public relations, building connections with businesses, styling and more when preparing for the annual fashion show as part of UGA Fashion Month. During fashion show preparations, students will be working with local boutiques and networking with other student organizations. 

SMA prides itself on member participation, and 100% involvement is needed for events like the annual fashion show and video look-books. Leadership roles are highly encouraged as well, since many committees are needed to successfully host events. These committees include: public relations, styling, events and business relations.

Members not only enjoy satisfaction from serving the community and the college, but they also have the opportunity to develop a network of friendships through the Student Merchandising Association.
summary: The Student Merchandising Association at UGA provides students with the opportunity to develop & enhance skills needed to obtain a career in the fashion industry.

name: Women's Studies Student Organization
description:
We are a student organization focused on feminist activism and bringing the discussion found in Women's Studies classes outside of the classroom.  You do not need to be a Women’s Studies student or identify as woman to participate in WSSO! All are welcome! Our events are a space for people of all gender identities and expressions, racial and ethnic backgrounds, religious affiliations, income levels, sexual orientations, ages, and abilities and disabilities.

Follow us on Instagram @wssouga and on Facebook @ugawsso :)
summary: We are a student organization focused on intersectional feminist dialogue and activism.

name: Biochemistry Graduate Student Association
description: The BGSA is an organization for all Biochemistry graduate students. We host the annual Biochemistry Holiday Party and Spring Party. We bring in renowned speakers from across the country to give talks twice a year. We are also dedicated to service of the community and work to make it easy for the Biochemistry graduate students to give back.
summary: We are an organization for the Biochemistry graduate students that encourages involvement in our department and community. We host seminars for internal and external speakers as well as social events.

name: Dean's Ambassadors
description: Through tours, receptions, open houses, and other events, Dean's Ambassadors promote the University of Georgia School of Law to a wide variety of stakeholders.
summary: Dean's Ambassadors work to promote the University of Georgia School of Law in the public arena to prospective students, alumni, attorneys, and other special guests.

name: Graduate and Professional Scholars
description:
The purpose of this organization is to utilize the collective resources available to the organization, including members, alumni and friends, for the creation and maintenance of an academic environment that is conducive to the successful matriculation of historically underrepresented graduate and professional students through the University of Georgia. We aim to do this by:

  • Articulating and addressing the academic, financial, political, social and professional needs of members
  • Supporting the recruitment and retention of historically underrepresented graduate and professional students
  • Providing an effective voice and advocacy for historically underrepresented graduate and professional students at the University of Georgia by investigating issues, actively participating in the Graduate Student Association and making recommendations to the University administration
  • Establishing and improving interdepartmental and interorganizational communication and scholarship  between graduate and professional students
summary: GAPS is a student-run organization dedicated to serving the needs of historically underrepresented graduate and professional students at the University of Georgia.

name: RxPups-Student Society of Pediatric Advocates
description: The Mission of the RxPups-Student Society of Pediatric Advocates is to bring awareness to the proper use of medication therapy in pediatric populations through various service and education-based initiatives. Service activities center around lending our medication-based knowledge to pediatric patients and their parents in our community. Educational activities are directed toward student members in an effort to safely and effectively extend pharmacy practice to pediatric populations by building relationships with mentors and professionals in the health care community, as well as supplementing didactic coursework with lectures by specialists and our peers. Overall, RxPups-SSPA advocates for the safety and happiness of young patients while learning and having fun along the way.
summary: RxPups-SSPA is a professional student organization apart of the UGA College of Pharmacy. We aim to bring awareness to the proper use of medication therapy in pediatric populations through various service and education-based initiatives.

name: Muslim Student Association
description: The Muslim Student Association at the University of Georgia is a religious and social student organization that seeks to network Muslim students on campus and serve as their voice in the community. MSA seeks to reach out to the general student body in interfaith cooperation and work with other organizations to bring positive change within the UGA campus. By striving to adhere to the principles of the Quran and Sunnah, the MSA strives to uphold values of tolerance, sincerity, and community to build strong and lasting relationships with each other and outlying campus communities.
summary: The Muslim Student Association is a religious and social student organization that seeks to network the Muslim students on campus and serve as their voice in the community. MSA seeks to interact with the student body through interfaith cooperation.

name: Model United Nations
description:
Model UN is an interactive simulation of the United Nations that challenges students to learn about international topics and issues. Students act as delegates from various countries and debate topics ranging from economics to global terrorism to human trafficking. Model UN tests students’ rhetorical abilities, but also encourages them to compromise in order to build a consensus. The ultimate goal is to show that diplomacy involves complex, structured debate that will, at its best, produce effective resolutions. Model UN also emphasizes community involvement throughout our team and many of our members are active participants throughout many campus organizations across campus and our community.

The Model UN team at UGA was founded more than 80 years ago by the School of Public and International Affairs and has since been a distinguished organization in the SPIA department. Every year, the organization hosts one of the largest high school Model UN conferences in the southeast with over 600 high school students participating from all across the country. UGA Model UN also hosts ArchMUN, a ranked collegiate conference hosting college students from across North America. The club has over 60 active members, with undergraduate and graduate students eligible. Our travel team attends conferences across North America, competing in Chicago, Florida, Montreal, Washington D.C., Virginia, and many more places.
summary: Model UN is an interactive crisis simulation of the United Nations and other bodies. We challenge students to learn about international diplomacy through competitions across North America and the two conferences UGA Model UN hosts on-campus.

name: Thalian Blackfriars, The
description:
A student-run theatre company at the University of Georgia, The Thalian Blackfriars have been active on campus since 1893! Today TBF produces several shows a year, focusing on producing student-written work on a student schedule. Each show is entirely picked, produced, performed, and publicized by UGA students.

Our mission is to provide undergraduate students with experience in all areas of theatrical productions, including acting, directing, writing, stage management, light/costume/set design, and producing. TBF strives to create theatre that is relevant to our education, community, and world.
summary: The Thalian Blackfriars is a student run theatre company at the University of Georgia that has been active on campus since 1893! Today TBF produces several shows a year of both original and published works.

name: Mock Trial
description:
Mock trial is a high-energy, fast-paced blend of theatre, debate, and just a touch of science. It’s objections and responses, rules of evidence and rules of procedure – a battle of wits to the very end. The trials may be fake, but the experience is very, very real.

Every year, attorneys and witnesses act and argue their way through a set fact pattern, going head-to-head against other collegiate teams and being evaluated by real attorneys. Each round simulates a full trial – opening statements, closing arguments, and witness examination – and each team is responsible for developing its own case theory and strategy from a set of affidavits, expert reports and evidence provided by the American Mock Trial Association. Because every team’s theory is different, team members must adapt their responses and think on their feet in order to be successful – no two rounds are ever exactly the same. If you're interested, sign up to receive more information here.
summary: Mock Trial is an activity in which students prepare and perform a full court case against other schools' teams. It teaches skills such as critical thinking, public and extemporaneous speaking, teamwork, leadership, and many aspects of legal procedure.

name: American Society of Interior Designers at UGA
description:
The UGA Chapter of ASID is one of the 280 student chapters across America. The Society's membership also includes more than 6,500 students of interior design. ASID Industry Partners include more than 2,100 member firms with some 6,000 individual representatives, uniting the professional designer with manufacturers of design-related products and services.

Through ASID, we hope to foster relationships within cohorts, our program and in the interior design industry to provide a network in which all students will prosper.
summary: ASID is a community of people —designers, industry representatives, educators and students— committed to interior design. We emphasize education, knowledge sharing, advocacy, community building and outreach to advance the interior design profession.

name: Pre-Physician Assistant Association at UGA
description:
The Pre-PA Association at UGA strives to provide students with the opportunity to learn about the growing PA career, stay updated on current health trends, and meet students with similar interests. It is the organization's goal to provide each member with important information to be well prepared and successfully apply to PA schools of their choice.

The organization is determined to help members be completely prepared for applying to PA school. Our monthly meetings are based around giving information about the PA profession and all necessary application information (the GRE, CASPA, personal statements, etc). Throughout semester meetings, the members will hear from a panel of current PA students, current PA providers, and admission counselors from different PA schools. The organization also strives to inform members about current volunteer opportunities in the Athens area.

The organization would be excited to welcome any interested student to the club. Membership dues are $30 and include a t-shirt. Feel free to come to our meetings to find out what we are all about…. and eat free food!
summary: The Pre-PA Association at UGA is open to all students who are interested in the Physician Assistant profession. We meet monthly and provide information about PAs, advice on admission to PA school, and support one another by sharing experiences.

name: Warnell Graduate Student Association
description: Our purpose is to enhance educational and professional development opportunities for Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources students; to facilitate social and scholarly interaction among graduate students of the diverse disciplines within Warnell, as well as between graduate students and faculty; and to serve as an advocacy organization for graduate student concerns.
summary: WGSA is an advocacy and service organization composed of all graduate, post-baccalaureate, and professional students at the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources.

name: American Marketing Association at the University of Georgia
description:
The American Marketing Association at The University of Georgia is a student-led marketing organization created in 2007. AMA at UGA is comprised of University of Georgia students in the Terry College of Business, all of whom possess a passion for marketing. The AMA at UGA chapter offers students the opportunity to become strong and skilled marketers. From hands on experience to exclusive company visits, AMA at UGA provides students with an environment to enhance their skills and network.

Our organization provides marketing solutions to local, regional, and national companies. Our members are highly motivated individuals who apply their knowledge and skill set to achieve our clients’ marketing and promotional objectives. Our members’ dedication, innovation, and hard work have consistently led, and will continue to lead, to outstanding results for our clients.

Please apply to be a member by visiting our Instagram page @amaatuga and clicking on the LinkTree in our bio to access the applications! Thank you!
summary: Enhances students' marketing and networking skills through hands on experience, exclusive company visits, professional speakers, professional development workshops and connections to various marketing related events and opportunities.

name: Phi Kappa Literary Society
description:
Founded in 1820, The Phi Kappa Literary Society is a debate organization that meets every academic Thursday at 7:00 PM in the historic Phi Kappa Hall, located on North Campus. The goal of the society is to improve our members’ oratory skills, argument formation, and logical and critical thinking. We also pride ourselves on our strong sense of siblinghood as well as our adherence to Robert’s Rules of Order.

A typical Phi Kappa meeting begins at 7:00 PM with a call to order and any society business, followed by the evening’s main debate. We then take a 45 minute recess and head to Little Italy downtown, and afterwards we return to the Hall for creative writings and orations, during which any member or guest can share a speech, poem, or other prepared creative writing. All guests are encouraged (but not required) to speak or ask questions at any point throughout the evening.

For more information about our history and what we do, check out www.phikappauga.org and follow us on Facebook and Instagram. We hope to see you in the Hall. To Order!
summary: The Phi Kappa Literary Society is a debate organization founded in 1820 at University of Georgia. We meet once a week in Phi Kappa Hall during the academic year to practice oratory, debate, and creative writing.

name: Women in Business
description:
Women in Business is a student-run organization affiliated with the Terry College of Business, and is open to all students! Through bi-monthly meetings with intriguing guest speakers, our mission is to inform aspiring businesswomen (and men) of career opportunities, current business trends, and networking and interviewing tips.  

WIB hopes to connect members with the right tools and resources they need to succeed at Terry and in the business world. We also aim to develop member’s leadership, time management, and organizational skills. Our professional environment, friendly guest speakers, and fun social opportunities will help each member gain insight into the business world.

Our bi-monthly meetings feature experienced guest speakers from a wide range of companies. The topic of each meeting will vary depending on the company in attendance. Typically, alumni and company reps talk about their experiences and give advice and useful tips. Many also attend these meetings to actively recruit students from our organization as they know that WIB members are the top-tier of Terry students. Previous speakers include Amazon, PwC, Deloitte, Protiviti, Aldi and more.

In addition to our meetings, there are social and philanthropy events hosted throughout the year designed to provide members with opportunities to socialize and get to know other women in business.

This year we plan to create more opportunities for members to get involved with philanthropic events!  We are also expanding into general professionalism meetings. Need someone to take a look at your resume or discuss the best way to find internships? We can't wait to see you at WiB!
summary: Women in Business is open to all students and aims to provide valuable resources, tools, and networking opportunities with professionals while also building relationships with fellow students. Come to WiB's socials, corporate meetings, and other events

name: Cru
description: We’re a network of students and staff on thousands of college campuses in the US and around the world. Facilitating spiritual awareness on campus by introducing students to a personal relationship with Jesus Christ, providing students opportunities to grow in that relationship, and training them to help reach others is what we major in. Although we each have different experiences and interests, we all share two things in common: we love to have a good time, and we are convinced that our personal relationship with God is the most fulfilling thing in the world. It’s what we were made for.
summary: A campus ministry that strives to help students know God, grow in their faith, and go out into the world.

name: Pre-Pharmacy Society of UGA
description: Pre-Pharmacy Society of UGA is an organization for students who plan on pursuing a career in pharmacy or have interest in pharmacy and wish to learn more. Our monthly meetings are announced via the listserv, Instagram, Groupme and posters ahead of time. During our meetings, the Pre-Pharmacy Society of UGA hosts representatives from a variety of pharmacy schools as well as developmental meetings that prepare students to be strong candidates for pharmacy school. The officers of PPS are also valuable assets to the club, providing information about the application process on PharmCAS, the required classes given at UGA, as well as extras such as the application process for pharmacy technician licenses and access to multiple pharmacy contacts in the area. If you have an interest in pharmacy or would like to learn more, this club is for you!
summary: Pre-Pharmacy Society is an organization dedicated to providing future pharmacy students with valuable information regarding pharmacy school admissions, PCAT, and interviews. It also encourages networking among pre-pharmacy students and representatives.

name: Men's Club Lacrosse at UGA
description: A top lacrosse team in the SELC that competes nationally. 2013 SELC Champions.
summary: Men's lacrosse team that competes in the Men's Collegiate Lacrosse Association (MCLA) and the Southeastern Lacrosse Conference (SELC).

name: Association of School Psychologists
description: The Association of School Psychologists (ASP) is an organization for students interested in pursuing a graduate degree in school psychology. School psychologists supports students, teachers, and families in and outside of schools. ASP activities focus on providing resources and training experiences related to assessment and intervention in the areas of mental health, effective instruction, and behavioral supports.
summary: To advise the scholarly goals and professional interests of future school psychologists.

name: University Chorus, The
description:
The University Chorus is one of the largest choral organizations on campus and is open to university students as well as faculty, staff, and local community members. Consisting of approximately one hundred voices, its large membership includes upper-level music major/minors and experienced choral singers from throughout the university in all years and areas of study. The University Chorus performs choral literature for mixed voices from all periods of music history, but specializes in classical choral and choral/orchestral repertoire and often collaborates with the Hodgson Singers and the University Symphonic Orchestra. The chorus performs throughout the year including the Fall & Spring Concerts, Holiday Concert, and other special events.

Due to its once-a-week rehearsal schedule and the challenge of its repertoire, University Chorus is geared for experienced singers with strong music-reading skills. The ensemble accepts new members at the beginning of each fall and spring semester, and members are asked to schedule an informal placement hearing when joining.

The University Chorus is supported by the University of Georgia American Choral Directors Association (UGACDA), a student-led chapter of the National American Choral Directors Association. UGACDA's mission is to promote excellence in the field of choral music, support the activities and concerts of the University of Georgia Choirs, and advance the professional aspirations of its individual members.

The seven member choirs of UGACDA include the UGA Hodgson Singers, University Chorus, Glee Club, UGA Treble Choir, Repertory Singers, and the African American Choral Ensemble.

UGACDA seeks to promote musicianship and artistic performance of the highest caliber. We encourage any students or community members interested in joining UGACDA to contact Dr. Bara, Dr. Mann, or any of its current members.
summary: The University Chorus is one of the largest choral organizations on campus, with a membership that includes music majors, music enthusiasts, and music lovers from the local community.

name: Minority Student Science Association
description: The Minority Student Science Association is a UGA student-run organization that strives to "bring excellence and diversity to the sciences by providing resources and fostering relationships between minorities at the university and professionals in the community," as per our mission statement. We serve minority students with intent in pre-health pathways or in the science-based disciplines. This organization serves as a liaison to ensure our members have reliable access to proper resources and events that will enhance their academic and professional careers. MSSA is dedicated to ensuring that we fulfill the needs of our student members and exceed their expectations, especially since we were founded on the principles of educating and preparing members to be leaders for tomorrow. Membership in MSSA is extended to all students.
summary: MSSA serves as a resource to support the academic and professional careers of all students interested in pursuing pre-health and science-based career paths. We provide several opportunities that better prepare our members to succeed.

name: Miracle at UGA
description:
Welcome UGA Miracle, one of UGA's largest student-run organizations that raises money for Children's Healthcare of Atlanta. This year is our 31st anniversary, and last year we raised $1.201 million dollars! This was our 10th year in a row raising over $1 million for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta, and we are excited to keep that streak going. We help fund Rehabilitation Services and the Aflac Cancer and Blood Disorders Center at Children’s. Children’s works with patients with diverse conditions, from traumatic brain injuries and spinal cord injuries to neurological diseases and other childhood impairments resulting from accidents, illnesses, injuries, congenital defects, syndromes, and other conditions. 

 

This organization brings together people from all parts of campus to offer support to Children’s. Without the support and help from team members, committee members, friends, and families, we wouldn't be able to provide the funding Children’s so desperately needs. Children's devotion to their patients means that life-saving treatments are offered to patients, no matter the family’s ability to pay for their child’s healthcare. So with that, we invite you to join our mission to bring miracles to the patients and families of Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta! 

 

Together, we can Change Kids' Health and Change The Future! Go Dawgs!
summary: UGA Miracle is a student-run philanthropy that raises funds for Rehabilitation Services and Aflac Center at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. It is our mission to support our hospital, champion our families, and engage our campus and local community.

name: Cycling Club at UGA
description:
UGA Club Cycling is a group of people stoked on bikes! We host and compete in competitive events, as well as doing recreational group rides and skill-building sessions and volunteer projects! All are welcome

We welcome anyone with a passion for cycling- whether that means commuting to class, ripping up trails, or speeding around the Athens Twilight Criterium course.
summary: We are a welcoming club for anyone looking to have fun on bikes! We have both recreational and competitive riders, competing in SECCC as well as national competitions in Mountain, Cyclocross, Track, and Road.

name: American Institute of Chemical Engineers
description:
The American Institute of Chemical Engineers (AIChE) at UGA builds a supportive community for students studying or interested in chemical engineering. We ease the challenges of the major by fostering peer connections and providing professional resources. Our events include resume workshops, internship guidance, alumni and industry panels, and lab and facility tours. Through networking, academic support, and hands-on exposure, we help students explore opportunities and succeed both in college and beyond.

We are diverse group of students made up of students majoring in biochemical engineering, biomedical engineering, biochemistry, chemistry, and much more. 

In addition, we attend the Regional AIChE Student Conference in the spring, where we compete in the Chem-E Car and Jeopardy competitions. 

If you are a biochemical engineering major or interested in this industry, we highly encourage you to attend one of our meetings. Feel free to reach out to any of our officers through email, GroupMe, the AIChE Instagram, or any other way. We would love to have you!
summary: AIChE at UGA supports students exploring chemical engineering by building community, fostering connections, and providing opportunities for academic growth, career exploration, and professional development.

name: Nutritional Sciences Graduate Student Organization
description: The Nutritional Sciences Graduate Student Organization (NUTR GSO) is a graduate student-led organization for students pursuing an advanced degree in the Department of Nutritional Sciences at the University of Georgia.
summary: The Nutritional Sciences Graduate Student Organization (NUTR GSO) is a graduate student-led organization for students pursuing an advanced degree in the Department of Nutritional Sciences at the University of Georgia.

name: Georgia Debate Union
description: The purpose of the Georgia Debate Union is to provide students of all experience levels with the opportunities to engage in intercollegiate debating, to attend tournaments throughout the United States, and to carry on the research associated with a competitive debate program.
summary: The purpose of the Georgia Debate Union is to provide students of all levels with the opportunity to engage in intercollegiate debate, to attend tournaments throughout the U.S.A., and to carry on the research associated with a competitive debate program.

name: Christian Legal Society
description: The University of Georgia chapter of Christian Legal Society is a group of Believers in Jesus who seek the wisdom of Heaven for the practice of law. CLS provides weekly opportunities for fellowship, charity, and thought-provoking discussion on what it means to follow Jesus as a lawyer. All law students are welcome at CLS.
summary: We provide an opportunity to belong to a community of Jesus-following law students who study and serve together.

name: Russian Language Club
description:
The Russian Language Club is dedicated to sharing the Russian language and cultures of the Russian speaking world with everyone who is interested. We believe that with increased knowledge about the Russian speaking world, we can promote cross-cultural and cross-national respect and understanding. 

 

Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/uga.russianlanguageclub?utm_source=ig_web_button_share_sheet&igsh=ZDNlZDc0MzIxNw==

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/45794065/nZTObO
summary: Club dedicated to teaching non-Russians and Russians alike about culture, customs, and way of life of the Russian speaking world.

name: Student Government Association
description: The Student Government Association (SGA) of the University of Georgia is the organized voice for each student. SGA protects students' rights and advocates for the students' welfare in the University community. The organization also serves as a liaison between students and the administrative bodies on campus.
summary: SGA shall be the organized voice of the student body's academic, social, and cultural welfare at UGA, serving as a liaison between students, administrators, and impactful, external entities.

name: Young Democrats
description: The Young Democrats of UGA and Athens-Clarke County are dedicated to stimulating interest in government and politics, educating students on political issues and candidates, and advocating for a more progressive community, state, nation, and world. Members of this club have the opportunity to meet and work with rising political stars from our state in order to help push for a more equitable Georgia for all. Find us on Instagram @youngdemsuga for updated information about our meetings & community involvement!
summary: The Young Democrats are dedicated to stimulating interest in government and politics, educating students on political issues and candidates, while advocating for a more progressive future! Follow us on Instagram @youngdemsuga for updated info!

name: Red Cross at UGA
description:
Become a member of Red Cross at UGA and play an active role in making an impact in our community! As an official chapter affiliated with the esteemed American Red Cross, our club is dedicated to furthering their mission.

By becoming a member of Red Cross, you'll have the opportunity to engage in meaningful leadership and community service initiatives. Get involved in organizing and volunteering at blood drives, where you can directly contribute to saving lives. Additionally, join us as we collaborate on public health and disaster projects, such as our Home Fire Campaigns where we install free fire alarms in underprivileged neighborhoods. One of our exciting upcoming programs is the Pillow Case Project, where we'll visit elementary schools in Athens to educate young students about disaster safety. As a member, you'll play a vital role in empowering the next generation to be prepared and resilient in the face of adversity.

Apart from the intrinsic satisfaction of serving others, Red Cross at UGA offers great benefits for your personal and academic development. We provide opportunities for leadership growth, and our service hour tracking program allows you to conveniently document your volunteer hours for future references, ideal for those planning to pursue graduate school or other endeavors.

Join our dedicated community of compassionate individuals and be part of a network that makes a tangible difference in people's lives. Together, let's embody the spirit of service and create a brighter, safer future for our community. Join Red Cross at UGA today! We're excited to meet you!

Join our GroupMe for up to date information: https://groupme.com/join_group/88353916/DUDdQZ2Y

Follow us on Instagram: blooddrivesatuga
summary: Red Cross at UGA is a student-lead volunteer organization that supports the American Red Cross and its mission. We work directly with the Northeast GA Chapter of the American Red Cross in Athens to help better the lives of citizens in our community.

name: Student Veterans Association
description:
Veterans are a special non-traditional student demographic. We come from many different backgrounds and walks of life, and we welcome any and all students. We need to be a support and advocacy group to ensure an appropriate transition from military to college.

  In order to do this we must maintain the resolve of our organization as long as there is a UGA and veterans in attendance.  In return we offer leadership, social maturity, and service to the university. With a service background and many diverse skills we offer a loaded community that can work well together to serve the community beyond military service.
summary: The Student Veterans Association aims to help build a community of students who are meaningfully engaged with our fellow students, faculty, and staff through activities and engagement.

name: Business Law Society
description: The Business Law Society was formed, and continues to exist, to help bring together law students interested in corporate and business law for educational and professional development, including the sponsoring of events involving corporate and business law firms and organizations.
summary: The Business Law Society was formed, and continues to exist, to help bring together law students interested in corporate and business law for educational and professional development.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
description: ASME at UGA is a student run academic club intended to provide our engineering students with an opportunity to connect with fellow engineering students and learn about various fields within mechanical engineering through corporate, guest speaker, and social events. Hands on experience is offered through competition teams (NASA HERC rover, Fountain Wars) and interest groups (Biomechanics/Prosthetics, Blacksmithing/Welding, Manufacturing).
summary: ASME’s mission is to serve diverse global communities by advancing, disseminating and applying engineering knowledge for improving the quality of life; and communicating the excitement of engineering.

name: Stethoscope Magazine
description:
Stethoscope Magazine is a student-run publication at UGA that is dedicated to promoting science and providing relevant healthcare news. We seek to publish healthcare-related articles to keep readers up-to-date on news and innovations in the field. Our staff includes writers, copy editors, photographers, and designers. Applications to join our staff are available during the fall and spring semesters, and UGA students of all majors and years are welcome to apply! 

Here is the link to apply! Apply by September 20th, 2025: https://forms.gle/mZvMaGPDswFqcd9bA
summary: We are a student-run magazine at the University of Georgia dedicated to promoting science and providing relevant healthcare news.

name: Intellectual Property Law Society
description: IPLS works to inform law students on intellectual property law, including: patents, trademarks, copyrights, trade secrets, and right of publicity. IPLS also attempts to demonstrate to law students why intellectual property is an important area of law and the impact it has in their every day lives. IPLS encourages all law students to look into the area intellectual property law and discover whether the field is one they are interested in. In this dynamic area of law, IPLS strives to enable its law student-members to stay informed of current issues and advances in this area of law as well as general information about transitioning into the practice of law by organizing: perspective panel series, perspective lecture series, social events, and informational meetings with law firms.
summary: Comprised of students from the University of Georgia School of Law, IPLS serves the needs of law students that are interested in the following: patent law, trademark law, copyright law, trade secret protection, and publicity rights.

name: Industrial-Organizational Psychology Student Association
description: Students in the Industrial-Organizational Psychology Program at UGA are invited to join IOPSA (Industrial-Organizational Psychology Student Association), a unique feature of our program that aims to ensure a high quality of student life. In conjunction with the faculty, IOPSA plays a major role in helping our program function smoothly. As members of IOPSA, we serve our program by helping recruit talented graduate students to our program, organizing networking socials with our broad alumni base, planning frequent social events for students and faculty at Athens’ many lively locales, and regularly arranging research and career talks with individuals in various lines of work related to Industrial-Organizational Psychology. It is our belief that IOPSA plays an integral role in our career development as well as our social development as graduate students at the University of Georgia.
summary: Association for graduate students in the I-O Psychology program at UGA

name: Latinos Invested in the Students of Tomorrow
description: LISTo is dedicated to providing academic, social, and emotional support for incoming Latinx first-year and transfer students through mentorship. The mission is to help provide students with academic success, time management skills, networking, opportunities for internships, and to build camaraderie, and to help them transition to college.
summary:
LISTo is a student mentorship and outreach program designed to help familiarize first-year 
 and transfer students with the campus and opportunities at UGA.

name: Language and Literacy Education Graduate Organization
description: LLEGO is the graduate organization of the Language and Literacy Education Department in the College of Education.  LLEGO strives to provide both professional development and social opportunities to LLED faculty, staff, and graduate students to build a sense of community within the department.
summary: LLEGO provides professional development and social opportunities to faculty, staff, and graduate students in the Language and Literacy Education department.

name: African Student Union at UGA
description: The African Student Union is an organization committed to educating the community, and the University of Georgia about Africa through various efforts. The mission of ASU is to bridge the gap between Africans and non-Africans alike to better help diversify the campus and enrich the lives of those who come into contact with members of the organization. Through community service projects, educational programs, social activities, outreach efforts, and the influences of music, dance, and fashion, ASU goes above and beyond to create an atmosphere where all can thrive. Through annual events such as African Night, Outreach Event, and Africa Week we help to raise awareness of issues stemming not only within the community but also abroad in the continent of Africa. Africa is a vast continent filled with many countries and multiple countries, and it is ASU's joy to share that with everyone.
summary: The African Student Union is an organization committed to educating the community about Africa and African Cultures through community service projects, educational programs, several social activities and our Annual 'Africa Night'.

name: Pi Sigma Epsilon
description: PSE is a co-ed fraternity of students interested in the advancement of business skills. Our purpose is to develop the business skills of our members through experiential learning and professional opportunities. Our application link will be in the bio of our Instagram (@pseuga)
summary: PSE is a co-ed fraternity of students interested in the advancement of business skills. Our purpose is to develop the business skills of our members through experiential learning and professional opportunities.

name: Student Financial Planning Association
description: SFPA aims to provide its members with the professional opportunities and skills to thrive in the Financial Planning industry. Our goal is to bridge the gap between students interested in/studying Financial Planning and professionals in the Financial Planning field.
summary: SFPA exists to prepare financial planning students, both undergraduate and graduate, for their careers. We provide students with hiring opportunities, networking visits from employers, scholarship/conference opportunities, group social events, and more!

name: Ratio Christi
description:
Ratio Christi is organized to encourage and strengthen the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions around the world, while sharing Christ’s message and love with those who have not yet accepted Him. By situating apologetic clubs at universities nationally and internationally, Ratio Christi hopes to encourage dialogue and stimulating discussion directed towards answering life’s pressing questions.

 

We currently meet on Monday nights in MLC Rm 251 from 7pm-8:30pm.  Feel free to contact us by emailing jrh32740@uga.edu
summary: Ratio Christi is a Christian apologetics group organized to encourage the faith of Christian students at public and private institutions around the world, while sharing Christ’s message and love with those who have not yet accepted Him.

name: Caribbean Student Association
description: The purpose of the Caribbean Student Association shall be to promote awareness of the Caribbean, to provide an outlet to experience its culture, economy, and history within the University and Athens community, to provide a support structure for Caribbean students, and to provide educational and social opportunities for its members.
summary: The purpose of the Caribbean Student Association is to promote awareness of the Caribbean culture on the University of Georgia campus.

name: Society for Management Information Systems
description:
The Society for Management Information Systems (SMIS), was founded thirty years ago by Dr. Hugh Watson to develop MIS students into future professional business leaders. Today, SMIS has over hundreds of active members who regularly participate in SMIS meetings. During the 2024-2025 academic year, we hosted 52 company presentations for a total of 3000+ student attendance records. We also initiated and sustained substantial relationships with other student organizations which created numerous new leadership development opportunities for MIS students.

Join our GroupMe to stay updated on all of our events!

https://groupme.com/join_group/102511910/ZtE2XPRK
summary: Our goal is to build a relationship among MIS students, UGA / MIS alumni, company recruiters and MIS faculty. To accomplish this goal, we frequently host company presentations, resume and interview workshops, a formal recruiting dinner, alumni function.

name: Thomas Lay After-School Program
shortName: Thomas Lay, TLay, Thomas Lay ASP
description: The Thomas Lay After School Program was established in the Spring of 2006, and since that time, the program has served elementary and middle school students in the Athens area. Every weekday during the academic year, UGA students provide individual tutoring and mentoring for local students. By providing homework help and extended learning activities, Thomas Lay creates an atmosphere that encourages enthusiastic learning and play. Housed at the Thomas N. Lay Park Community Center, the program serves about 50 elementary school students, and is supported by approximately 125 UGA volunteers every semester. Our mission statement is as follows: The Thomas Lay After-School Program is dedicated to providing educational assistance while developing positive relationships with children ages five and above in a safe and fun environment.
summary: The Thomas Lay After School Program serves elementary and middle school students of the Athens area. Every weekday, UGA students come to volunteer as tutors and mentors. This program creates an atmosphere that encourages enthusiastic learning and play.

name: Black Affairs Council
description: The Black Affairs Council (BAC) of the University of Georgia serves to assist the University in meeting the needs of the Black student body through programming, social, academic and political events, and community service initiatives.
summary: An organization created to Preserve, Enhance, Examine, and Celebrate African-American and other minority culture on UGA's campus and in the Athens-Clarke County community.

name: Student Composers Association
description:
The Student Composers Association is an organization of students, faculty, and community members who are interested in the creation of original music. SCA supports and encourages creating, researching, and performing new works.

In our weekly meeting, we provide opportunities for members and guests to engage with topics related to music creation and the field of music composition to inspire creativity and prepare students for the professional world of writing and composing music, and working in music technology. Our seminar often hosts artists from around the world and opportunities to workshop, present, and perform new music. We present multiple recitals each semester to showcase the work of our students. The weekly seminar is generally held in the Dancz Center for New Music (School of Music Room 264) on Fridays at 11:35am.

Please contact us if you are interested!
summary: The Student Composers Association is an organization of students and faculty members who are interested in composing music. SCA supports and encourages creating, researching, and performing new music.

name: Kesem at the University of Georgia
description:
Kesem is a nationwide community, driven by passionate college student leaders, that supports children through and beyond their parent’s cancer. Camp Kesem operates over 100 free summer camps in 44 states for children ages 6 to 18 who have been touched by a parent’s cancer. This camping experience has a lasting impact on children by providing them a peer support network that understands their unique needs, builds confidence and strengthens their communication skills. Each camp is funded, planned and run entirely by student volunteers.

Kesem at the University of Georgia was founded in 2011 and supports children in the Greater Athens/Atlanta communities by providing two weeklong sessions of summer camp and yearlong peer support to families affected by cancer. Our mission of supporting children through and beyond a parent’s cancer has transformed the lives of all involved, and we hope to continue making magic for an often-overlooked population of children.

From a small campus organization created by a grant from the Livestrong foundation, Kesem at the University of Georgia has grown exponentially in the number of children served each year and the number of volunteers engaged in our work. During Summer 2025, UGA's chapter will serve over 200 campers!

For email updates on Kesem at the University of Georgia, please contact us at uga@kesem.org.
summary: Kesem is a national nonprofit organization that supports children through and beyond their parents' cancer, a mission rooted in free summer camp and supplemented by year-round programming. Both are funded, planned and run entirely by students.

name: Asura
description: Asura was created in 2008 in order to represent the University of Georgia in the Bollywood-Fusion dance circuit throughout the United States and Canada. We incorporate a variety of dance styles, including Bollywood, Hip Hop, Contemporary, Bhangra, Bharatanatyam and Kathak, Latin, and any other forms of dance that we can learn. Each year, we push ourselves to new heights, placing at different competitions and striving to see more complexity in our choreography. Both on campus and during our travels, we're not only a dance team, but a close-knit family that would do anything for each other. Please contact us at ugaasura@gmail.com with any questions!
summary: The purpose and mission of Asura shall be to represent the University of Georgia in various shows, events, and competitions throughout the nation through the art of Bollywood fusion dance and to promote cultural diversity.

name: Genetics Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of the GGSA shall be to (1) provide intra-departmental and university-wide representation for genetics graduate students, (2) foster scientific communication between current genetics graduate students and prominent scientists in the field of genetics by sponsoring seminar speakers and invited lecturers, (3) promote interactions within the department by organizing social events, and (4) provide a means for discussion and implementation of policies pertaining to the genetics graduate student experience.
summary: The purpose of the GGSA shall be to provide intra-departmental and university-wide representation for genetics graduate students and foster scientific communication between current genetics graduate students and prominent scientists in the field.

name: Society of Women Engineers
description:
Our mission is to encourage all students—while honoring our legacy of supporting women—to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, to expand the image of the engineering profession as a positive force in improving the quality of life, and to demonstrate the value of diversity.

Listed below are our core values. 

 

Integrity
We aspire to the highest level of ethical behavior as evidenced by honesty and dignity in our personal and professional relationships and responsibilities. 

 

Inclusive Environment
We embrace diversity in its broadest interpretation and commit to creating an inclusive environment for all our members and stakeholders.  We value the contributions of a diverse membership, which enables SWE to achieve its full potential.

 

Mutual Support 
We provide an organization that fosters mentoring, and the development of professional and personal networks.

 

Professional Excellence 
We conduct our activities in a professional manner, demonstrating and demanding the highest standards of business practices.

 

Trust 
We share a common definition of success with open, transparent access to common information, building mutual respect and confidence in the competence of those with whom we lead, serve and partner with.
summary: Our mission is to encourage all students—while honoring our legacy of supporting women—to achieve their full potential in careers as engineers and leaders, expanding the image of the engineering profession as a positive force.

name: Criminal Justice Society
description: Criminal Justice Society is a student organization that exposes students from all majors to the field of Criminal Justice. It is an excellent opportunity to network with students and professionals in the Criminal Justice system. We hold meetings on Tuesdays at 6 pm that feature speakers from various areas in the Criminal Justice system (local, state, and federal law enforcement, corrections, law, academia, etc). CJ Society holds field trips each semester that provide students with exposure and experience in various fields of Criminal Justice. We have visited UGA's Law School, local jails, the local courthouse, the Federal Building in Atlanta, the Atlanta Federal Penitentiary, and UGA Police Department. Service is also a key part of CJ Society. CJ Society loves to give back to the Athens community by holding several service projects each semester including food drives, letter drives, road cleanups, and a give-back cookout for the officers of Athens.
