Along with speakers, field trips, and service, Criminal Justice Society sponsors several events throughout the year, including an Alumni Panel and the annual Talarico Lecture.
summary: Criminal Justice Society is a student lead organization that welcomes all majors. Meetings feature speakers from various areas of the Criminal Justice system, along with field trips, service opportunities, and other events throughout the year!

name: Phi Lambda Sigma
description:
In 1974, the Delta Chapter of Phi Lambda Sigma was organized at the University of Georgia. Membership consists of four categories: student, faculty, honorary, and alumni. To be eligible for membership, a student shall be in their second or third year of study, of high moral and ethical character, and shall meet scholastic and curriculum standards. Nomination for membership is made on the basis of the nominee’s demonstration of dedication, service, and leadership in the advancement of pharmacy.

The purpose of Phi Lambda Sigma is to recognize those individuals who have attained a high degree of leadership through their time and efforts focused on the betterment of the profession. The objective is to encourage these people to continue their worthy endeavor and to stimulate others to attain these high ideals. To be elected into this society is rightfully considered one of the high points of one’s career. As an organization, we hope to encourage leadership and service throughout the College of Pharmacy.
summary: Phi Lambda Sigma, the Pharmacy Leadership Society, is a national honor society whose mission is to recognize student pharmacist leaders and to promote the development of leadership within the pharmacy profession.

name: Persian Student Union
description: Our Mission Statement: The purpose of the Persian Students Union shall be to broaden Persian horizons via networking, interaction, and socializing amongst the community of UGA. The Society shall enlighten our fellow students as to our culture, diversity, and heritage, by becoming established as an influential student organization.
summary: We are an organization that strives to promote cultural awareness through our cultural-related events that include Persian delicacies and activities. We work to include more than just Persians at UGA and welcome people of all cultures.

name: Black Male Leadership Society
description:
The purpose of BMLS is to establish and foster a sense of unity, strength, and love among Black males and to direct it toward uplifting the community at large. While BMLS centers the experiences of Black male undergraduate and graduate students and alumni, membership is open to all UGA students who align with the organization’s mission.

BMLS supports the academic, social, intellectual, and spiritual growth of its members by maintaining a community of men who continuously support and encourage one another. It aims to foster positive relationships for Black men with others, highlight their leadership in communities, and engage lives beyond the UGA campus. Through community service and mentorship, BMLS ensures the talents of UGA Black men are reinvested into the broader community. Lastly, BMLS promotes the professional, academic, and personal success of Black men at UGA while intellectually engaging them in a broad range of issues facing Black men and boys.
summary: BMLS fosters unity and growth among Black males while uplifting the broader community. Though centered on Black men’s experiences, membership is open to all UGA students who support the mission and values of the organization.

name: Chapel Bell, The: Positive Press Publication
description:
Founded by the Alpha Upsilon Circle of Omicron Delta Kappa (ODK) National Leadership Honor Society in 2011, The Chapel Bell is an organization which utilizes the creativity of its members in order to create positive, impactful content.

Named after the Chapel Bell at the University of Georgia which represents gratitude and celebration, the organization strives to cultivate an atmosphere of happiness on campus. We have several unofficial mottos: "Read and be inspired," "Warriors of good and awesome," and "Smile, it's Monday!" TCB looks forward to distributing positive news and personal stories every other Monday with the hope that our publication will inspire action and excitement throughout the following week.

Our members encompass a wonderful, eclectic group of UGA students with diverse artistic interests and personalities; as an organization, we strive to give each member a home on campus and a fulfilling outlet for creative energy. We embrace what each member has to bring to the table, but we all have one important thing in common: our passion for connection and our love for spreading joy at UGA.

To find out more information and look for the online version of the publication, visit TCB’s website at www.thechapelbell.com.
summary: By creating and distributing a unique, campus-wide publication, we aim to inspire students toward action and positivity on campus and beyond.

name: American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers
description: The goal of this society is to promote directly, and indirectly, the interest of the students in Agricultural and Biological Engineering, namely those related to professional development and the parent society, the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers.
summary: The goal of this society is to promote directly, and indirectly, the interest of the students involved with Agricultural and Biological engineering, namely those related to professional development and the parent society of ASABE.

name: College Student Affairs Administration Graduate Association
description: The College Student Affairs Administration Student Association (CSASAGA) is a student-led organization that serves as a social and educational group for students interested in student affairs, faculty, and staff.  This group also helps facilitate the College Student Affairs Administration (CSAA) master’s degree program orientation that is designed to assist the adjustment process for new students entering the program. Throughout the year, additional social and educational events are promoted, sponsored, and coordinated by CSAAGA.
summary: The College Student Affairs Administration Student Association (CSAAGA) is a student-led organization that serves as a social and educational group for students interested in student affairs, faculty, and staff, and represents the program across campus.

name: Taiwanese Student Association
description:
The Taiwanese Student Association (TSA) serves as an international organization at UGA. Our missions are to promote the Taiwanese culture, establish a sense of family between our diversity members, and increase cultural exchanges among different associations in US southeast regions. We are open to anyone interested in Taiwanese culture!!! 

Events organized by TSA includes

  1. Lunar new year celebration,
  2. Street festival,
  3. Coffee hour,
  4. Mid-autumn festival BBQ
  5. and other cultural sharing events
summary: TSA is devoted to promoting Taiwanese culture at UGA, establishing a sense of family among our diverse members, and increasing cultural exchanges among different associations in the US Southeast region.

name: Real Estate and Property Organization
description: The University of Georgia School of Law's Real Estate and Property Organization was founded in the summer of 2013 as the Land Use and Planning Organization and was rebranded in 2018. Our goal is to create a forum for the discussion and analysis of information related to real estate, land use, urban planning, and other topics in property law in Athens, Atlanta, and the Southeast. We are actively engaged in the community by providing thoughtful analysis and resources pertaining to pressing local issues.
summary: The University of Georgia School of Law Real Estate & Property Organization is committed to creating a forum for the discussion and analysis of information related to real estate, land use, urban planning, governance, and other topics in property law.

name: Alzheimer's Association at UGA
description:
This organization will provide students with an opportunity to join the team fighting against Alzheimer's disease. We host several events throughout the semester to raise money and awareness for the Walk to End Alzheimer's, which occurs annually (November 8th this year). Our past fundraisers for this event include Don't Forget to Laugh, bake sales, restaurant percentage nights, and many more. 

In addition to the Walk, we will present opportunities for students to learn more about the disease and help support an effort towards Brain Health Promotion on campus. We have volunteer opportunities at local nursing homes working with Alzheimer's patients on a bi-weekly basis allowing us to have an impact on the Athens community.
summary: Our organization represents the Alzheimer's Association here at UGA. We participate in the annual Walk to End Alzheimer's and organize many fundraising and service opportunities in our community. We look toward a future without Alzheimer's disease.

name: Women's Club Soccer
description: While our team is focused on our goals, equally important to us is our mission as a student organization. We exist for students to make new friends and keep playing the game they love for four more years in a structured, meaningful environment. We encourage anyone with interest in the team to tryout. We practice two nights per week and travel around the Southeast during fall and spring weekends to play against other colleges. Our tryouts are typically held during the first full week of school in August, at the start of each school year.  Please email us, check out our Instagram or website for more details about the club and/or tryout info!
summary: Competitive soccer without the time commitment or pressure of varsity soccer. We compete at the regional and national level.

name: Vietnamese Student Association at UGA
description:
The University of Georgia's Vietnamese Student Association (VSA) was established in 2002 as a campus student organization in Athens, Georgia. Ever since, VSA has committed to the preservation of the Vietnamese culture and heritage through cultural awareness and educating those who are interested in learning our customs, practices, and interests.

Our mission is to create and stimulate awareness of the transforming Vietnamese-American cultural identity, while also fostering friendship, good will, and community among our members of all racial and ethnic backgrounds at the University of Georgia.

Our organization also strives to create a safe, fun environment with our active network of members through weekly sports gatherings, a media team, our Anh Chi Em (ACE) program, and much more. It is our goal to create a sense of family, an extremely important aspect in the Vietnamese culture. 

In all events, VSA actively strives to help the Vietnamese community both overseas and within our national borders. In support of the Union of North American Vietnamese Student Associations (UNAVSA), VSA hosts our annual culture show, Night In Saigon, and other events in order to raise money for the Collective Philanthropy Project (CPP) each year.
summary: Our organization creates and stimulates awareness of the transforming Vietnamese-American cultural identity, while also fostering friendship, good will, and community among our members of all racial and ethnic backgrounds at the University of Georgia.

name: HEROs at UGA
description:
UGA HEROs is one of the largest student-run philanthropy at the University of Georgia. Over 1,000 students each year take part in a year-long fundraiser to support H.E.R.O. for Children, Inc, an organization focused on improving the quality of life for kids in Georgia infected with and affected by HIV/AIDS. UGA is engrained in the founding of HEROs—we were founded by two UGA students who saw a population of kids not being helped. As the only organization in the state working to improve quality of life for the kids we serve, we have seen the power of bulldogs of all stripes coming together to raise over $3M since 2005!

Fill out our interest to find out how to get involved this fall: Fall 2025 UGA HEROs Interest Form  

Join our Groupme to stay up-to-date about all the exciting things we have planned: 2025 UGA HEROs Groupme

Check out our instagram @ugaheros or ugaheros.org/join for more information!

Video
summary: UGA HEROs strives to improve the quality of life for kids in Georgia affected by HIV/AIDS through enriching programs, unforgettable experiences, and connections to the community.

name: Cattlemen's Association
description:
The mission of the UGA Cattlemen's Association is to unite cattle producers to advance the economic,
political, and social interests of Georgia's cattle industry while being an affiliate of the Georgia Cattlemen's Association.
summary:
The mission of the UGA Cattlemen's Association is to unite cattle producers to advance the economic,
political, and social interests of Georgia's cattle industry while being an affiliate of the Georgia Cattlemen's Association.

name: Empower In & Out
description: The purpose of Empower In & Out shall be to serve as a liaison between Pinewoods and the University of Georgia students, staff, and faculty. Donation drives, percentage nights, and engagement events will be hosted to benefit the elementary school students of this community directly, enriching their childhood and encouraging bright futures.
summary: Empower In & Out seeks to meet needs of the Pinewoods community, an underserved mobile home community just outside of Athens.

name: Bethel Campus Fellowship
description: The mission of Bethel Campus Fellowship is to lead students to Jesus Christ and prepare them to become reliable men and women who can positively impact their world and communities when they complete their college experience. We are well aware of the many issues that college students face on a daily basis (depression, stress, financial issues, family problems, academic difficulty) and our goal is to help students work through these issues positively and achieve their greatest potential through sharing the Gospel and meeting together frequently to encourage each other.
summary: Leading students to Christ and preparing them to become reliable men and women whom God can entrust with His word for the next generation. Evangelism. Discipleship. Leadership.

name: Student College of Clinical Pharmacy
description: The Student College of Clinical Pharmacy (SCCP) is the student branch of the national organization, American College of Clinical Pharmacy (ACCP). Membership is open to all students at the UGA College of Pharmacy interested in learning more and/or pursuing a career in clinical pharmacy. SCCP aims to educate all members about opportunities in the ever-changing career of pharmacy and provide them with the knowledge and resources to pursue a role in clinical pharmacy. Some resources include residency education and workshops, CV reviews, journal club and scientific writing opportunities, publication and volunteer opportunities, and interviews with clinical experienced clinical pharmacists.
summary: The Student College of Clinical Pharmacy focuses on educating student pharmacists about clinical pharmacy and opportunities to further their career both during and after pharmacy school.

name: Workforce Education and Instructional Technology Graduate Association
description: The Workforce Education and Instructional Technology Graduate Association (WEITGA) shall foster relationships and communication between students and faculty in the Department of Workforce Education and Instructional Technology to establish a community of shared knowledge and collaboration. Through the promotion of cross-departmental partnerships and community affiliations, this organization shall increase students’ engagement in professional practices that surpass the boundaries of the university.
summary: Workforce Education and Instructional Technology Graduate Association (WEITGA), a graduate association committed to academic engagement, professional development, and collaborative partnerships among students, faculty, and community members.

name: Wesley/Freshley
description:
The UGA Wesley Foundation exists to enable students to encounter God & become disciples of Jesus Christ.

We Believe In...

  • Being Biblically Based
  • Living Empowered by the Holy Spirit
  • God's Love For Everyone
  • The Power of Prayer
  • Personal Transformation through Discipleship

We Give Ourselves To...

  • Corporate Worship
  • Intentional Relationships
  • A Prayer Lifestyle
  • Abundant Living

Our Core Values Are...

  • Intimacy
  • Community
  • Mission
  • Fun
summary: United Methodist campus ministry.

name: Microbiology Graduate Student Association
description: Led by graduate students in the microbiology department, MGSA enhances the graduate student experience and facilitates communication with faculty and the department as a whole. This is achieved through student-invited seminar speakers, professional development workshops, peer mentorship, social gatherings, and recruiting new graduate students to the department.
summary: A group dedicated to improving the lives of microbiology graduate students through on-campus events, seminar series, and social happenings.

name: Club Golf Team at UGA
description:
The Club Golf Team at UGA is a highly competitive club sport with a roster of around 50 members. We participate in 2-3 events per semester, organized by the National Collegiate Club Golf Association (NCCGA), in addition to numerous intrasquad matches. While we encourage our members to practice and play as frequently as their schedules allow, we do not hold formal practices. However, the club convenes once a month for fun intrasquad events such as team Ryder Cups, match play tournaments, and scramble/best ball rounds.

As two-time national champions, we set high standards to bring out the best in each of our players. However, our focus extends beyond just posting scores; we aim to foster individual growth through the game of golf, emphasizing integrity and character, all while enjoying the game we love to play and share together. We welcome golfers of all skill levels, particularly those seeking to develop their game in a supportive, like-minded community. For those aspiring to compete in national tournaments, we prefer players who consistently score 80 or below.

Please join our GroupMe for updates about what's going on this semester.

https://groupme.com/join_group/108715429/dSFC1v9q
summary: Our organization is committed to playing competitively in the National Collegiate Club Golf Association on both a regional and national scale, as well as bettering the overall individual through the game of golf.

name: Reformed University Fellowship
description:
RUF is here to meet every UGA student with the grace of Jesus right where they are without leaving them stuck there.

 

Regardless of your beliefs or doubts, Reformed University Fellowship is a safe place to explore and experience genuine Christianity. Over the past 20 years, thousands of students have seen their understanding transformed about who Jesus is and what it means to follow Him in all of life for the rest of their lives. 

 

Come find out for yourself why so many at UGA find RUF to be their home away from home.

 

Click here for our welcome gift to you! 

_____________________________________________________________________________________

 

Wednesday night Large Group: Large Group is a room full of genuine, welcoming, engaging people; worship that catches you off guard with its honesty and depth; and teaching from the Bible that is compelling and transforming. So why don't you grab a friend and come see why hundreds of other students at UGA right now have found a home at RUF?!

 

Freshmen Fellowship Trailer

 

Tuesday night Freshmen Fellowship: Freshmen Fellowship is here to be a place that's easy to slip into whether it's your first or tenth visit, connect with other freshmen, build deeper friendships, hear a thought-provoking message from Scripture, and share the ups and downs of the year with people who care. 

 
Freshman Taco Party 2019
 
Regular Community Groups: A community group is any 5-15 students who gather together weekly to better understand and apply the Bible together in community. It's a place where we apply specific pieces of Scripture to specific, honest pieces of our lives. RUF Community Groups are geared to be places where both believers and skeptics can feel at home. We don’t assume anything about people joining a group other than that they want to become better connected to others in RUF and that they are open to seriously considering the claims of Scripture in community with others.
 
Fall, Winter and Summer Conferences: RUF is committed to getting out of town and gathering with other RUF students from around Georgia, the region, and the country for conferences. These conferences and retreats are times of unparalleled spiritual, relational, and personal growth--and they're unforgettably fun, too! 
 
Winter Retreat
 
Spring Break mission trips: Our Spring Break Mission Trip is a great opportunity for UGA students to have our eyes opened to the needs, struggles, and growth of Christ's church in our own neighborhoods and around the world. Over the past twenty years, UGA RUF has established long-term relationships and made lasting investments in churches in Corozal, Belize, Puerto Rico and Athens, Greece.
 
Puerto Rico Mission Trip
summary: RUF is here to meet every student with the grace of Jesus right where they are without leaving them stuck there. Regardless of your beliefs or doubts, RUF is a place for you to explore Christianity and grow in your understanding of who Jesus is.

name: National Kitchen and Bath Association Student Chapter
description:
The National Kitchen and Bath Association Student Chapter at UGA is an organization that strives to serve as a resource for members interested in the residential interior design industry. We hold meetings every other week; meetings may include guest speakers, historic site tours, showroom visits, service events, and more. UGA NKBA is committed to promoting professionalism and providing leadership and direction for students, as well as opportunities for members to network with industry professionals. We put a focus on philanthropy, offering community engagement through volunteer days, service drives, and various service events throughout the year. Featured events include the NKBA Gala, Atlanta Holiday Home Volunteer Day, spring Career Fair, and Senior Portfolio Showcase.

We welcome any students with an interest in the interior design field!
summary: NKBA Student Chapter strives to enhance member success in the design field, promote professionalism and ethical business practices, and provide leadership and direction for the kitchen and bath/interior design industry.

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association at UGA
description: The University of Georgia NSSLHA Chapter is a student led club for individuals with interests in the Communication Sciences and Disorders major or interest in the fields of Speech-Language Pathology/Audiology.  We hold monthly meetings as well as orchestrate several fundraisers each year in order to raise money and bring awareness to the UGA Speech and Hearing Clinic.  There are many ways to get involved in NSSLHA, and we welcome your participation!
summary: The National Student Speech Language Hearing Association at UGA is the only official national student association recognized by our field’s national organization, ASHA (American Speech-Language-Hearing Association).

name: Biomedical Engineering Society
description: Biomedical Engineering Society is the University of Georgia student chapter for the internationally recognized professional society, the Biomedical Engineering Society (BMES). Our mission is to cultivate the professional development and personal growth of our members through active learning experiences in the biomedical and biological engineering field. We aim to deliver success to our members in their biomedical goals whether it be in undergraduate pursuits, graduate school, medical school, industry, research, etc. We work to provide knowledge of the biomedical engineering field and bioengineering major, guest speakers, design projects, networking opportunities, social events, and community involvement while fostering a sense of community among our members.
summary: BMES serves to promote professional development and personal growth for students interested in exploring and pursuing the biomedical engineering field.

name: Circle K International
description:
Circle K International (CKI) is the premier collegiate and university community service, leadership development, and friendship organization in the world. With more than 13,800 members in 17 nations, CKI is making a positive impact on the world every day. 

Circle K at UGA is an organization that serves the community of Athens and surrounding areas through service and fundraising. CKI at UGA incorporates various service projects and fundraisers throughout the year. Not only will members be serving at a local level, but also statewide through the Georgia District of Circle K International and the international level through Circle K International. CKI at UGA abides by the principles set by Circle K International and Kiwanis International.

CKI at UGA is organized and sponsored by the Athens Kiwanis Club. This is a self-governing organization electing its own officers, conducting its own meetings, and determining its own service activities.

Join our givepulse page for additional information! https://givepul.se/6yurzl
summary: Circle K International (CKI) is a collegiate, international, and non-profit organization. This organization is a student-led, community service and leadership oriented group, reaching a global level with the help of Kiwanis International.

name: German Student Organization
description: The German Student Organization (GSO) is open to everyone interested in German language and culture! The club fosters an appreciation of German culture and a love for language regardless of German-speaking experience. Events organized by GSO include game nights, field trips, themed crafts, karaoke, presentations, pretzel tasting, and opportunities for practicing the German language. Stay updated with our LinkTree!
summary: GSO is devoted to promoting German language and German-speaking culture at UGA. It is a meeting point for German speakers and students and provides a platform for German-related events and social activities.

name: Grady Graduate Caucus
description: The purpose of Grady Grad Caucus is to provide all current graduate students attending Grady College of Journalism and Mass Communication here at UGA a community and space for social and academic achievement. In doing so, Grady Grad Caucus provides a means for students at UGA to meet and interact, and to provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of our general interest.
summary: The social and academic organization of all current Master's and Ph.D students attending the Grady College of Journalism and Mass Communication.

name: OUTLaw/Stonewall Law Student Association
description: Promotes education and discussion of legal and societal issues relating to sexual orientation and gender identity.
summary: OUTLaw is an organization dedicated to fostering activism, equality, and community for LGBTQ+ individuals and the allies that love and support us. We promote education, discussion, an of legal and societal issues relating to sexual orientation and gender

name: Multiracial Student Union
description: Originally known as the Multiracial Student Organization, the mission of the Multiracial Student Union is "to provide a safe space for multiracial students at the University of Georgia and to educate members and the public about the multiracial experience as well as multicultural issues."  MSU provides a place and an opportunity to build community for a group that, as we believe, has been underrepresented on campus. MSU allows multiracial students and students of other multicultural backgrounds to explore their own personal identity and embrace everything that makes them unique. A secondary goal of the union is to serve our community and show we are here.
summary: The MSU focuses on community, education, and personal identification for multiracial students and students of multicultural backgrounds while giving our time through service.

name: Student Alumni Council
description:
The Student Alumni Council (SAC) is an organization that serves as a liaison between students and the alumni of the University of Georgia. SAC serves as part of the UGA Development and Alumni Relations, aiming to be the voice of current students in the large network of alumni. As the student representatives of the UGA Alumni Association, SAC advocates for a united and committed university community, strengthening relationships between students and alumni by celebrating university traditions, expanding the culture of student donorship, and connecting students to alumni through a series of programs and events. Some of these events include:

  • Freshman Welcome
  • Senior Signature
  • Homecoming activities on behalf of SAC
  • Beat Week
  • Founder's Day
  • Ring Ceremony
  • Dialogue with a Dawg
  • Dawg Day of Giving
summary: The mission of SAC is to enhance the collegiate experience of current UGA students by providing programs and events focused on promoting university traditions, fostering a culture of donorship amongst students, and connecting students to UGA alumni.

name: Pakistani Student Association
description: The Pakistani Student Association is an ISL organization that aims to promote and celebrate the rich and vibrant culture of Pakistan through educational and cultural events. PSA is going to be holding many events during the Fall and Spring semester. We will host monthly meetings as well as hosting a Coffee Hour with ISL. Our major event this year will be a mock traditional Pakistani wedding event called Mehndi Night. Mehndi Night is an event where all are welcome to join us in a fun-filled evening with singing, dance performances, food, henna, and a sense of community.
summary: The purpose and mission of the Pakistani Student Association shall be to represent, preserve, and promote the Pakistani Culture while creating a family atmosphere in which Pakistani students can network and interact with one another.

name: Noteworthy
description:
Founded in 1988 in Athens, GA, Noteworthy is still UGA's only all-treble a cappella ensemble. Each member is passionate about achieving vocal excellence and producing riveting performances, and dedicated towards ensuring the group remains unified and student-led.

Throughout the years, Noteworthy has jumped to the top of collegiate charts with multiple selections in BOCA (Best of Collegiate a Cappella) and nominations for the CARA awards.

​In 2013, Noteworthy received two awards in the regional quarterfinals of Varsity Vocals' International Championship of Collegiate a Cappella: Best Vocal Percussionist and Third Place Ensemble.

In 2017, Noteworthy received 2nd place in the regional quarterfinals Varsity Vocals' International Championship of Collegiate a Cappella and moved on to semifinals.

Currently, Noteworthy continues to perform at various events around campus and the greater Athens community, spreading their love for music to all they can!
summary: Noteworthy is UGA's student-led treble a cappella group. We are a competitive group that produces music on Spotify and puts on several concerts throughout the year! Our goal is to show the possibilities of music by using only the voice.

name: Order of Omega
description: Order of Omega's purpose is to recognize fraternity men and women who have attained a high standard of leadership, service, involvement, and academic achievement. We represent the top 5% of Greek Life in these aspects of chapter membership.
summary: Greek honor society represented by members from top 5% of the Greek community on campus.

name: Georgia Society of Health System Pharmacists - UGA
description:
The University of Georgia Student Chapter of the Georgia Society of Health-System Pharmacists serves as a forum to provide students with information on careers in hospital pharmacy and other organized health care delivery systems. Speakers from area hospitals discuss subjects related to institutional pharmaceutical care and offer insight into specific areas of hospital pharmacy practice. Often, speakers on health-system pharmacy address the Academy of Students of Pharmacy meeting, enabling the entire student body to attend.

Membership can start at any time during the school year. Members receive the complete range of membership services and publications available through the Georgia Society of Health-System Pharmacists and can attend meetings of the GSHP at reduced registration costs. Most members have prior experience in health system pharmacy, but membership is open to all students of the College of Pharmacy.
summary: The Georgia Society of Health System Pharmacists - UGA Chapter is open to any College of Pharmacy student interested in hospital, clinical, or health-administration pharmacy.

name: Kappa Psi Pharmaceutical Fraternity
description: Kappa Psi at the University of Georgia is a pharmaceutical fraternity that is committed to the development and success of its brothers. Through strong brotherhood, involvement, leadership development and networking, we strive to develop more competent and prepared brothers.
summary: Kappa Psi at the University of Georgia is a pharmaceutical fraternity committed to the development and success of its brothers. It is a brotherhood for like-minded and aspiring student pharmacists wanting to succeed in their future pharmacy endeavors.

name: Dawgs for Diabetes Awareness at UGA
description:
Mission: The mission of DAWGS FOR DIABETES is to provide information to the community about diabetes and provide support for those living with and affected by diabetes. Dawgs for Diabetes will establish a climate of enthusiasm, openness, and fellowship of support.

Dawgs for Diabetes Awareness (D4D) is a student run organization at the University of Georgia and a chapter of the College Diabetes Network.

Our goals:

  1. Educate and Raise Awareness in the Athens, Georgia community and beyond about Diabetes
  2. Participate in the Athens community and the Diabetes community
  3. Raise money for CDN, Breakthrough T1D, Camp Kudzu, GA’s Camp for kids with Diabetes, and other awareness raising groups

We are also a BreakthroughT1D Support Group-

  • For those living with Diabetes or impacted by Diabetes
  • Various events, meet after general body meeting
  • Provide support and community throughout the year.

You do not have to have Diabetes or know anything about Diabetes to be involved in our organization. UGA students and faculty along with members of the community can become involved in the organization.

We participate in events throughout the year, but are not limited to our annual Campus Tour: A Diabetes Perspective, the BreakthroughT1D One Walk, Camp Kudzu, World Diabetes Day and Diabetes Awareness Month, Fundraising, Awareness Raising, Community Service, Social Events, and more...

If you have any questions or would like to know more information, please feel free to send us an email at dawgs4diabetesuga@gmail.com

Follow our Instagram @dawgs4diabetes to get information on meetings, as well!
summary:
Dawgs for Diabetes Awareness at UGA is a chapter of The Diabetes Link.

Our Goals:
-Educate and Raise Awareness
-Community Involvement
-Fundraising (JDRF, CDN, etc.)

You don't have to have diabetes or know anything to be involved.

name: Baptist Collegiate Ministries
description:
BCM is a Christ-centered, student-led, campus minister-directed ministry that seeks to provide opportunities for spiritual growth and Christian community throughout college. We provide several different ministries to get involved in:

Discipleship - Our ministry at BCM is small-group based. Gathering (our large weekly meeting) features a large group worship and teaching time, and our small groups meet throughout the week. Small groups are where relationships will be built with other students and where students will be challenged to live their lives for the Gospel of Christ. 

Worship - A critical part of any discipleship program and college ministry is learning to worship God privately and corporately. As you are experiencing the joys of college life, we will help you incorporate worship into your weekly routine. While we are not a church, we can provide you with worship experiences that fit your life as a busy collegian.

Missions/Ministry - Just as worship is an essential element of discipleship, learning to serve others is also vital. Through the Dawg Packs you will have an opportunity to participate in local missions and ministry projects. BCM also has a long tradition of sending missionaries through spring-break trips and summer trips, both domestic and international (www.sendmenow.net)!

Fellowship - Fun and play are also important aspects of what we do at BCM. We love dances, socials, fun trips, retreats, tailgates, and any reason to get together and get to know each other better!  We would love to get to know you better and have you be a part of the Lord's work here in Athens!
summary: BCM is a Christian ministry that meets on the campus of the University of Georgia. We exist to connect students to Christ, Community, the Church, and their Calling. We meet in on campus on Monday nights at 8pm. Come join us!

name: Social and Personality Psychology at UGA
description: Social and Personality Psychology at UGA (SPPUGA) is a graduate student organization geared toward advancing the research of social and personality psychology, broadly defined to include topics like relationships (e.g., parent-child, friendships, intimate relationships), health and wellbeing, workplace psychology, biological foundations of psychology, motivation and self-regulation, cultural psychology, and individual differences. Our organization is open to any and all graduate students who are interested in Social and Personality Psychology.
summary: Social and Personality Psychology at UGA (SPPUGA) is an organization of graduate students interested in learning and discussing past and current issues and topics in the field of Social/Personality Psychology.

name: Society of Environmental Engineers
description: The purpose of this organization is to create a group of friends within the UGA engineering community. SEE provides a combination of social, professional development, and service events to engage our members, prepare them for their careers, and enact positive change in our community and environment.
summary: This organization fosters a network of members and alumni eager to enact positive change in our community, the environment, and the world.

name: Agency, The
description: Founded in 2011, The Agency is a non-profit university talent agency run by students, for students, with a focus on providing our members with portfolio building opportunities from industry professionals with experience in the modeling and fashion world. We host model castings during the second week of each semester that are open to all UGA students, including anyone who just wants to try it out! We book talent for stylistic photoshoots, host fashion runway shows, collaborate with local businesses, and expand networks in order to connect talent with skilled local photographers, artistic directors, and photography studios. We also accept aspiring student creatives who are interested in the behind-the-scenes work of the fashion industry and who wish to hone their skills with the help of our organization. For more information, please visit our website at www.theugagency.com, or send us an email at theagency.uga@gmail.com. So take some agency, and start your journey into the fashion industry today!
summary: A non-profit dedicated to affecting change in the modeling world via an active community and accomplished individuals, both experienced and non-experienced. We book talent, host runway shows, work with local stores, and expand professional networks.

name: Classic City Bhangra
description: Classic City Bhangra is a nationally competitive Indian folk dance team. Our dance form, Bhangra, originates from Punjab, India. It signifies the success of the harvest season. We strive to spread our culture and our love for dance across campus. We encourage everyone to join our family.
summary: Classic City Bhangra is a nationally competitive Indian folk dance team. Our dance form, Bhangra, originates from Punjab, India. It signifies the success of the harvest season. We strive to spread our culture and our love for dance across campus.

name: Arnold Air Society
description: Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power. It is comprised of cadets throughout the USAF officer candidate training programs, including ROTC throughout university-level institutions, and the Air Force Academy. It is a professional organization because of the volunteer work we do throughout our communities and the opportunities we get to improve our leadership skills. Arnold Air Society is an honorary service organization due to the rigorous nature of the initiation program required for entry.
summary: Arnold Air Society is a professional, honorary service organization advocating the support of aerospace power.

name: Improv Athens
description: The mission of Improv Athens is not only to perform improvisational comedy but to teach it, emphasizing partner and group cooperation. We aim to provide a friendly environment for all to come and learn how to think and respond quickly while creating characters and scenes on the fly. Members, who audition to join the group, perform on campus and compete with other improv groups across the United States. Improv Athens won the 2011, 2012, 2014, 2017, and 2020 South Regionals and placed 3rd in the nation in 2015, 8th in 2016, and 3rd in 2020 in the College Improv Tournament (CIT).
summary: Improv Athens is an award-winning improvisational comedy group that performs on campus and in regional and national competitions. The group provides a friendly environment for members and non-members to think quickly while creating scenes on the fly.

name: Black Theatrical Ensemble, The
description: The Black Theatrical Ensemble creates an opportunity for students interested in drama to showcase their talents while exposing members and audiences to the works of African American playwrights. Membership is open to all (both undergraduate and graduate) interested in dramatic and performing arts from all cultural backgrounds and does not require a theatrical background. BTE’s drama troupe is available for individuals interested in performing in other venues. BTE is a diverse student organization under Multicultural Services and Programs. Email us for more information about membership and how you can get involved!
summary: BTE creates an opportunity for students interested in drama to showcase their talents while exposing members and audiences to the works of African American playwrights. Membership is open to all and does not require a theatrical background!

name: Real Estate Society
description:
To promote interest in the field of real estate.

To encourage fraternal fellowship among those men and women who have major interest in real estate (between students, between professionals, between students and professionals).

To promote professional and academic study and research in the field of real estate.

To promote better understanding of the field of real estate among all students of the University of Georgia.
summary: To promote interest in the field of real estate; to promote academic study in the field of real estate; to encourage internal fellowship among students and professionals; to assist Terry real estate students in finding real estate jobs.

name: IHSA Equestrian Team at UGA
description:
Our team competes in the Intercollegiate Horse Show Association at a varsity level against teams from schools like Berry College, University of Alabama, Mississippi State, and Georgia Tech. We compete in hunt seat equitation. Members are placed into a division from walk/trot to open over fences based on your experience. At shows, horses are assigned to riders by random draw and riders then compete on a horse they have most likely never ridden before without warmup. This attempts to judge riders solely on their equitation and ability to ride horses rather than the horse.
 
We ride with Kelly Boltz at her facility, KB Sport Horses, which is located about 30 minutes from campus. Weekly lessons are required. We have team meetings every other Tuesday night. Members are also allowed and opportunity to "free ride" or hack once a week in addition to their weekly lesson. Riders are required to attend a barn workday each semester. In addition the team attends 6-8 shows each year. No riding experience necessary. IHSA has divisions from walk/trot to 3' fences. You're placed into divisions based on your experience.
 
We have tryouts at the beginning of the fall semester. We look for people that are going to be really committed to our team. We want our members to take the time to clean tack and wait for the sweat marks to dry on our horses after lessons. That kind of thing is more important to us than riding ability.
summary: Equestrian Club Team at UGA competes at Huntseat Equitation through the IHSA, competing all the way through the national level. All levels and disciplines of riding are accepted and no experience is necessary.

name: Students for Latinx Empowerment
description: Students for Latinx Empowerment (SLE) is an activist, advocacy, and community outreach group comprised of students from the University of Georgia. SLE gives UGA students the opportunity to volunteer and serve as mentors to Georgia high school students who come to SLE's annual empowerment and leadership conference called "Sigueme". SLE strives to promote higher education to Latinx students in Georgia.
summary: We strive to promote higher education to Latinx high school students in Georgia. Our endgame is that more Latinx students attend and succeed at the University of Georgia.

name: Economics Society
description:
The Economics Society is a student organization dedicated to enriching the lives of all students, regardless of major, through academic lectures, professional development, and community-building events centered around the field of economics. Whether you are a budding economist or a curious amateur, the Economics Society has programming for you. Register for membership on our website, ugaeconomics.com

Follow us on Twitter @EconomicsUGA, Facebook @UGAEconomicsSociety, Instagram @UGAEconSociety
summary: We exist to add value to the lives of students at the University of Georgia through 3 primary avenues: academic lectures (human capital), professional development (economic capital), and cultivating a community around economics (social/cultural capital).

name: Historical European Martial Arts at UGA
description: Our interests are as varied as the people in the eras we study. Historical fencing, Armor making, Leather-working, Sewing. You name it and we do it. We partner with the local chapter of the Society for Creative Anachronism (SCA), Other Historical European Martial Arts (HEMA) clubs and have access to many skilled fighters and artisans to help us teach workshops and host events. Truly, if you have ever been interested in anything historical, this is the club for you. Good people and fun times abound.
summary: For those who have ever enjoyed the medieval and renaissance eras and wanted to explore them more than a textbook allows. We do historical fencing and armored combat, not to mention a myriad of Historical Arts and Sciences.

name: ASL Dawgs
description: ASL Dawgs is an interest- and academic-based organization which provides educational opportunities for all students interested in learning about American Sign Language and Deaf culture. We host meetings once a month on Wednesdays.
summary: ASL Dawgs strives to provide educational opportunities for all students interested in American Sign Language and Deaf culture by providing social programming for students centered around American Sign Language as well as exposure to Deaf culture.

name: Psi Chi the National Honor Society in Psychology
description: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology. The mission of Psi Chi is to produce a well-educated, ethical, and socially responsible member committed to contributing to the science and profession of psychology and to society in general. For more information on how to join our organization, please visit our website here.
summary: Psi Chi is an international honor society whose purpose shall be to encourage, stimulate, and maintain excellence in scholarship of the individual members in all fields, particularly in psychology, and to advance the science of psychology.

name: Student Historic Preservation Organization
description: Student Historic Preservation Organization is a student organization for students interested in historic preservation, and provides them with an opportunity to engage more deeply with the field. SHPO puts on lectures, social events, film screenings, site visits, and volunteers for local events.
summary: SHPO is a student organization for students interested in historic preservation. SHPO holds philanthropy, educational, social and networking events throughout the year.

name: With Someone Else's Money A Cappella
description:
With Someone Else’s Money, or WSEM, is the oldest mixed-voice a cappella group on UGA’s campus. We pride ourselves on inclusivity, fun, and, of course, our music. If you want an easy-going creative outlet, then this is the group for you. We perform mostly pop songs and occasionally musical theatre, and all of our decisions are voted on as a group. There is an easy audition process, and we accept people of all musical experience levels and voice parts. Click the linktree below for our social media, audition sign-ups, and the audition interest form. 
https://linktr.ee/ugawsem?utm_source=linktree_profile_share&ltsid=67fa4a5c-d061-4592-8ade-b835f4a7da70
summary: WSEM is the oldest mixed-voice a cappella group on UGA’s campus. We pride ourselves on inclusivity, fun, and, of course, our music. Check us out on Instagram to access the linktree on our bio or and feel free to message with any questions! @ugawsem

name: Graduate Researchers in Educational Psychology
description:
The organization shall have the primary responsibilities in the department for the following:
organizing professional development session of general interest to the students; supporting students for conference travel; maintaining the Graduate Student Lounge; providing student representation within the department and on department committees; communicating the needs of students in department to the GSA and communicating relevant GSA notices to its members; and supporting and promoting concentration area student associations within EPSY.
summary: GREP is designed to foster contacts and communication between students and faculty in the Department of Educational Psychology and to encourage active participation in projects relating to the various fields represented in the department.

name: Adventist Christian Fellowship
description: Adventist Christian Fellowship is a vibrant group on fire for God and is here to serve whatever needs our small community has. This year, it is our goal to reach out to our campus and share the love of God in practical ways. You do not have to be a Seventh-Day Adventist or go to UGA to join this group! Our sole mission is to help others prepare for eternity.
summary: A diverse and multicultural group that believes in the Second Coming of Jesus Christ and in affiliation with the Seventh-Day Adventist Church. We are established to meet the spiritual, emotional, and social needs of the students and faculty of UGA.

name: Rowing Team at The University of Georgia®, Inc.
description:
Accept every challenge. Push every boundary. United in excellence.

We are UGA Rowing. We strive to achieve consistent success on a national level. We strive to make a meaningful impact on our members’ lives and the communities in which they reside. We embrace a competitive atmosphere and provide the tools that allow hard workers to become champions.
summary: We are a nationally ranked rowing club that strives to make a meaningful impact on our members’ lives and our community. We embrace a competitive atmosphere and provide the skills and tools that allow hard workers to become champions.

name: Operation Smile
description: As an international organization, Operation Smile promotes the health and well-being of society starting by offering affordable and safe comprehensive care for children, specifically with cleft lip and cleft palates. Our charity efforts are not only for providing surgeries, but also ensuring that each child has specialized care so they can live healthy and productive lives.
summary: Operation Smile is an international children's medical charity aiming to provide comprehensive care, specifically cleft lip/palate surgery to children in impoverished countries. We strive to spread awareness and raise money to change one life at a time.

name: Insurance Society
description:
The purpose of the Insurance Society is five-fold:

A. To promote interest in the field of insurance.

B. To promote a better understanding of the field of insurance among all students of The University of Georgia.

C. To further insurance knowledge; to coordinate the study of insurance with professional leaders in the field.

D. To cultivate a fraternal spirit between its members.

E. To cultivate personal acquaintance of its members for business and social betterment, so that the highest degree of mutual benefit and cooperation may be obtained.

All Risk Management students are strongly encouraged to join the Insurance Society as soon as they become departmental majors. Membership in the society not only makes the student eligible for scholarship awards, but also entitles the student to free attendance at the Careers Day program and the Spring Awards Banquet. In addition, only members of the Insurance Society are entitled to submit resumes for inclusion in the departmental resume book.

To join the Insurance Society, complete an application available at the link below. You will be given the option to pay via debit/credit card when you complete your online application, or a check for dues made out to the Insurance Society may be brought to the Departmental Office (A405 Moore-Rooker Hall). We do not accept cash payments. Because the dues payments are a one-time only fee, it is advantageous to join both Gamma Iota Sigma and the Insurance Society as early in a student’s college career as possible. Questions about the Insurance Society should be directed to the organization's faculty advisor, Jenny Atkinson (A416 Moore-Rooker).

To become a member of the Insurance Society please complete the application found at https://www.terry.uga.edu/risk-management-insurance/insurance-society.php
summary: Insurance Society is an organization committed to promoting interest and a better understanding of the field of insurance as well as coordinating the study of insurance with professional leaders in the field.

name: Culture Club: Critical Media Studies Interest Group
description: Culture Club: Critical Media Studies Interest Group is a forum for exploring critical approaches to media study. Membership includes faculty, staff and graduate students. Housed in Grady College and advised by Dr. James Hamilton, hamilton@uga.edu.
summary: Forum for exploring critical approaches to media study.

name: Wishmakers On Campus
description: Wishmakers on Campus grants wishes to children with life-threatening medical conditions. A wish experience can be a game-changer for a child with a life-threatening medical condition and costs on average $10,000 for just one wish. Wishes are more than just a nice thing. This one belief guides us in everything we. It inspires us to grant wishes that change the lives of the kids we serve. It compels us to be creative in exceeding the expectations of every wish kid. It drives us to make our donated resources go as far as possible. Most of all, it's the founding principle of our vision to grant the wish of every eligible child. Wishes are far more than gifts, or singular events in time. Wishes impact everyone involved - wish kids, volunteers, donors, sponsors, medical professionals and communities. The impact varies. For wish kids, just the act of making their wish come true can give them the courage to comply with their medical treatments. Parents might finally feel like they can be optimistic. And still others might realize all they have to offer the world through volunteer work or philanthropy. Whatever the odds, whatever the obstacles ... wishes find a way to make the world better.
summary: The mission of Wishmakers on Campus is to raise money for Make-A-Wish Georgia® who allows us to grant wishes for children with life-threatening medical conditions to enrich the human experience with hope, strength and joy.

name: Bag the Bag
description: Bag the Bag UGA is a group of concerned individuals advocating for the reduction of plastic waste while promoting sustainable and reusable alternatives. We strive to raise awareness about the ecological and economic dangers of single-use plastic while promoting the use of reusable plastics, such as tote bags, as a sustainable alternative. We encourage the Athens community to stop the plastic bag habit through education both on campus and at local schools and community events, outreach with local organizations and businesses, and grassroots advocacy. We are committed to working together with all members of the Athens-Clarke County community to make our home a more sustainable place.
summary: Bag the Bag works on campus and in the ACC community to reduce plastic waste through education, political advocacy, and recycling. We advocate for a more responsible and sustainable community that works together to change consumer habits inour society.

name: Women's Rugby Football Club at UGA
description:
The mission of UGA Womens Rugby club is to promote and participate in playing rugby at a safe and competitive level so that team members can grow stronger both physically and mentally, increase their confidence, and become part of the amazing rugby community! 

We practice Mondays and Wednesdays 9:00pm-11:00pm at the Recreational Sports complex on IM Field All are welcome and no experience is required!
summary: We are a SARC Division II club sport that competes throughout the Southeast. We work to foster a love of rugby through tough competition, team bonding, and community outreach.

name: Silver Wings
description:
Silver Wings’ predecessor organization, Angel Flight, had a specific objective of supporting the mission of Arnold Air Society. While this is no longer explicitly stated, Silver Wings remains committed to developing strong civilian leaders with an understanding of aerospace power–and obviously, the continuing partnership between the sister organization, Arnold Air Society, and Silver Wings is critical to building this understanding.

Some of the missions of SW include the following interrelated objectives:

Personal Development of Individual Members:
Local chapter activities and projects provide character growth through service to the community, which enables the individual member to develop a sense of purpose within and a responsibility to the local community.

Professional Development of Individual Members:
Local chapter activities and the national organizational structure provide members with leadership opportunities and the chance to develop professional skills. These include, but are not limited to oral/written communication, public speaking, and project planning and execution.

Civic Awareness:
Exposure to Air Force issues and personnel enables members to develop a personal awareness of aerospace power and the role that it plays in the national defense. The leadership and skills development objectives are enhanced by opportunities for members to actively promote aerospace issues in the community through educational programs, seminars, public relations activities, and voter awareness projects.


While Arnold Air Society and Silver Wings are separate organizations, our strong partnership is based on highly complementary missions. The focus of the Arnold Air Society mission is to build strong officers for the United States Air Force. Silver Wings also focuses on leadership development, and both organizations work together to further each others’ goals.
summary: Silver Wings is a national, co-ed, professional organization dedicated to creating proactive, knowledgeable, and effective civic leaders through community service and education about national defense.

name: UNICEF at UGA
description:
The United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) has saved more children’s lives than any other humanitarian organization in the world. We believe that every child has the right to a safe and healthy childhood that allows them to fulfill their potential, and we are working toward the day when zero children die of preventable causes.

 

Whether you are passionate about health, education, international development, or human rights, we want each one of our UNICEF at UGA members to have every opportunity to make an impact. For members in recent years, this has meant meeting with elected officials on Capitol Hill, planning a speaker panel highlighted by Forbes, and even serving as a UNICEF USA Youth Representative. To learn more and get involved, please email unicefatuga@gmail.com, check out our website, find us on Facebook and Instagram, or check out one of our upcoming meetings. 

UNICEF at UGA website
summary: We work to save children’s lives and defend their rights by fundraising, educating, and advocating on behalf of the United Nations Children's Fund.

name: Alpha Omega Campus Ministry
description: As Alpha Omega, we are a group of young student disciples belonging to a much bigger church family (Athens Church of Christ). We believe in God and his Son, Jesus Christ! We believe that Jesus died on the cross as an atoning sacrifice for our sins, that God raised him from the dead on the third day, and that God offers forgiveness of sins, the gift of his Holy Spirit, and eternal life to all who believe, repent, are baptized and follow Jesus as Lord! (Acts 2:36-38; Romans 10:9). We embrace the Bible as God’s inspired Word and the standard by which we strive to live. (2 Timothy 3:16). We believe that to truly be Christians we must be devoted disciples of Jesus living our lives daily for Him as our Lord. (Acts 11:25-26). Alpha Omega meets each Wednesday night on campus for a midweek service consisting of worship, teaching from the inspired Word of God, and rich fellowship. We also meet frequently throughout the week for opportunities to serve our community but also to have fun and build family. Come and see what life to the full is all about!
summary: Alpha Omega is a campus ministry devoted to living for Christ with the Bible as our standard. We are a ministry that focuses on sharing the good news around the campus of UGA, building deep relationships, and serving minority groups in the Athens area.

name: Sports Business Club
description: To better serve all members of the University of Georgia’s student body interested in pursuing careers involving sports. SBC hopes to further the exchange of information and co-operation among members, to promote knowledge, and to learn from professionals in the industry. Overall, SBC is resource to learn more about the various sectors involving sports business in order for members to narrow down their own interests.
summary: Our mission is to provide students at the University of Georgia with the resources, connections, and skills needed to excel in the sport management industry through monthly meetings with guest speakers, monthly socials, and facility tours.

name: Musicology/Ethnomusicology Student Association
description: The Musicology/Ethnomusicology Student Association (MESA) is a student organization devoted to music research. Over the past several years, MESA has aimed to tear down barriers of exclusivity by questioning previously accepted ideologies that favored only Western Art Music ("Classical" music). The University of Georgia's MESA organization holds an Annual music research symposium hosted at the Hugh Hodgson School of Music on campus. UGA's MESA also takes part in an annual rotating music research symposium in collaboration with other Universities in the Southeastern United States.
summary: This organization aims to provide both academic and social opportunities for students interested in musical scholarship. We host speakers, an annual symposium, and other various social events throughout the year.

name: Marine Science Graduate Student Association
description: The Marine Sciences Graduate Student Association organizes social, outreach, and professional development activities, and articulates students' needs and concerns within the Department.
summary: The Marine Sciences Graduate Student Association organizes social and outreach activities, and articulates students' needs and concerns within the Department.

name: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International Pre-Law Chapter
description: The purpose of this Pre-Law Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law; to stimulate excellence in scholarship; to inspire the virtues of compassion and courage; to foster integrity and professional competence; to promote the welfare of its members; and to encourage their moral, intellectual, and cultural advancement; so that each member may enjoy a lifetime of honorable professional and public service.
summary: Phi Alpha Delta Law Fraternity, International is a professional law fraternity advancing integrity, compassion and courage through service to the student, the school, the profession and the community.

name: Sociology Graduate Student Society
description: The Sociology Graduate Student Society aims to confer distinction for high achievement in scholarship in conjunction with the department of Sociology, to promote support for and enhance community among Sociology graduate students, to provide a forum for the interchange of ideas among those students and scholars who are seriously pursuing knowledge in these areas and thereby, to promote the study of society, both locally and globally.
summary: The SGSS aims to promote support for and enhance community among Sociology graduate students, to provide a forum for the interchange of ideas among those students and scholars in these areas and thereby, to promote the study of society.

name: Pandora Yearbook
description:
“The mission of the yearbook is to represent all aspects of student life at the University of Georgia and provide a pictorial record of the past, present, and future.  In addition, the Pandora allows students to volunteer and gain invaluable leadership, design, photography, and marketing experience through teamwork.”

   According to Greek mythology, Pandora, which means "she who has all gifts," opened the box in which all the evils of the world had been suppressed. The evils of the world flew out to plague mankind, leaving only Hope inside the box.

  In 1886, representatives of the eight existing fraternities on campus worked together to create the first yearbook at the University of Georgia. The yearbook was a collection of the Greeks' roles and of their activities on campus. It was a paperback book, much smaller in size than ours today, giving the histories of each class, of the school, and of the organizations at the University of Georgia. In recent years, a combination of photographs and written copy has prevailed.
summary: Pandora Yearbook allows students to volunteer and gain invaluable leadership, design, photography, writing, and marketing experience through teamwork all while putting together a yearbook for the student body to enjoy.

name: Club Softball
description: The UGA Club Softball team was founded in the fall of 2002 and has progressed into one of the top ranked club softball organizations in the state. Our team chemistry is what we most pride ourselves on and we are always looking for new girls to join. UGA Club Softball is a competitive, fast pitch softball team that is a part of the National Club Softball Association. We practice on Tuesday and Thursday evenings and play conference games and tournaments on the weekends. During each semester, we play approximately 3-5 weekends.
summary: The UGA Club Softball team was founded in 2002 and has progressed into becoming one of the highest ranked team in the state of Georgia. We practice twice a week, play on weekends, and are always looking for new girls to join our team and compete with us.

name: Habitat for Humanity at UGA
description: Habitat for Humanity at UGA works in the Athens community to both raise awareness for a lack of affordable housing and also to help combat it with hands-on volunteering opportunities for UGA students.
summary: Habitat for Humanity at UGA is a campus affiliate to the non-profit organization Athens Area Habitat for Humanity. Our mission is to provide affordable housing for families in need. We do this through fundraising and volunteering to build in our area.

name: Flying Bulldog Student Association
description: The Flying Bulldog Student Association is an organization aimed at building the leaders of tomorrow. Students work to promote networking, personal and professional development, and engagement in unique leadership opportunities with our fellow students through activities and events. Utilizing soft skills, life-long learning, and critical thinking students are able to foster an environment of growth and excellence preparing them to join the workforce.
summary: The Flying Bulldog Student Association is an organization aimed at building the leaders of tomorrow. Students work to promote networking, personal and professional development, and engagement in unique leadership opportunities with fellow students.

name: Collegiate 4-H
description:
The Collegiate 4-H Program at the University of Georgia promotes the Georgia 4-H Program, as a part of the National 4-H Organization, by service, leadership, and mentorship. Our Collegiate 4-H Club has given thousands of hours to surrounding 4-H clubs in the Athens area by volunteering at camp, serving as judges, and also serving alongside our extension professionals.

Collegiate 4-H is a nation-wide program that allows are members to network both across the state and country. 

Our mission: Fostering the next generation of Georgia 4-H’ers by volunteering at the county, state, and national level.

Our National Collegiate 4-H Slogan: Continuing to Serve. Serving to Continue.
summary: Collegiate 4-H at the University of Georgia strives to serve UGA and surrounding communities through our service, leadership, and dedication to 4-H and its members by extending 4-H involvement at local, district, state, and national levels.

name: Red Hotz Competitive Dance Team @ UGA
description: The Red Hotz has been the competitive dance team at the University of Georgia since August 29th, 2001. It was founded as a club sport by Devon Bollman to represent UGA dance on a National level. Entering its twenty-fourth season this coming August, the Red Hotz has become a large part of the UGA tradition by performing at events on campus such as Homecoming, Greek Grind, Relay for Life, etc. The team thrives in a competitive environment and competes several times a season, including NDA College Nationals. Red Hotz provides the much-needed outlet for dancers to continue developing their craft throughout college. Please email us or DM us if you have any questions or are interested in trying out!
summary: The UGA Red Hotz are the only competition dance team at the University of Georgia. Red Hotz compete regionally and nationally, representing UGA at Collegiate Nationals annually. The UGA Red Hotz also perform at various campus and regional events.

name: Georgia Club Dance Team
description:
The Georgia Club Dance Team is one of the University of Georgia's student-run dance groups! Established in 2012, the team welcomes enthusiastic dancers seeking a creative outlet for performance and choreography. Choreography for the routines is provided by team members as well as members of the UGA and Athens community. Our mission is to provide UGA students with a safe and creative setting to continue their dance journey. The Georgia Dance Team hosts auditions at the beginning of every Fall semester. For questions regarding auditions, please email us at gadanceteam@gmail.com or DM our Instagram @ugaclubdanceteam. To watch videos of past performances, check out our Youtube https://www.youtube.com/@ugaclubdanceteam. 

Each spring, the Georgia Dance Team hosts an exhibition featuring dancers performing three group pieces, which include lyrical, jazz, and hip-hop styles, along with small group pieces choreographed by team members. In the past, small groups have explored various styles such as musical theater, heels, ballet, contemporary, and more!
summary: The Georgia Club Dance Team is one of the University of Georgia's student-run dance groups! The team seeks passionate dancers looking for a creative performance and choreography outlet.

name: Club Swimming at UGA
description: This organization was formed for students of all experience levels who enjoy the sport of swimming. We emphasize the importance of good sportsmanship and team unity in a welcoming, fun, and competitive environment.
summary: This organization was formed for students of all experience levels who enjoy the sport of swimming. We emphasize the importance of good sportsmanship and team unity in a welcoming, fun, and competitive environment.

name: Wrestling Club at UGA
description: The Wrestling Club at UGA exists to provide students an opportunity to wrestle at the college level. As a team registered with the National Collegiate Wrestling Association, the Wrestling Club at UGA competes with small scholarship schools and other club level programs around the country. Our members consist of many former highschool wrestlers who had state placing level careers as well as other members who simply love the uniqueness of the sport.
summary: The Wrestling Club at UGA exists to provide students an opportunity to wrestle at the college level. As a team registered with the NCWA, the Wrestling Club at UGA competes with small scholarship schools and other club level programs around the country.

name: International Business Society
description:
The International Business Society is a student-led organization in the Terry College of Business that aims to unite all students interested in global business. The IB Society offers many opportunities to network with international companies, professors in Terry with international experience, and students with a passion for international travel.

 

Why Join??  Boost your resume and get involved!

  • Develop leadership skills by serving on the IB Society Executive Board
  • Participate in discussions revolving around international trade, finance, and social action
  • Network with other students that share similar interests in traveling, interning, and studying abroad
  • Get to know Terry professors and faculty in a setting outside of the classroom
  • Enjoy the exclusive perks of membership - free food at meetings, field trips to international company headquarters, exclusive Terry gear, and the opportunity to participate in the Collaborative Online international Learning (C.O.I.L.) Program with Liverpool University

Applications will reopen at the start of the Fall Semester! Stay tuned for our open house meetings!
summary: Join the International Business Society for access to invaluable professional development opportunities related to careers in international business and connect with like minded members who have a passion for travel!

name: Student Chapter of the American Veterinary Medical Association
description:
The University of Georgia Chapter of the Student American Veterinary Medical Association (SAVMAC) is the governing body for student issues within the College of Veterinary Medicine at the University of Georgia and funds all clubs/organization within the College.

SAVMA's goals are to:
• Promote a spirit of friendly relations among those enrolled in the College of Veterinary Medicine;
• Promote friendly relations and open communications between the students and the faculty of the College of Veterinary Medicine;
• Provide opportunities for members to gain knowledge and an understanding for professional ethics and conduct;
• To make available to the students the opportunities offered by the American Veterinary Medical Association and its student chapters;
• Develop character and leadership;
• Promote the College of Veterinary Medicine and the veterinary profession
summary: The University of Georgia Student Chapter of the American Veterinary Medical Association is the governing body for student issues within the College of Veterinary Medicine and funds all club organizations within the College.

name: Rotaract of UGA
description:
Rotaract is a service organization for young men and women that focuses on the development of young adults as servant leaders in their community and the workplace. Our club has volunteered over 100 hours in the past year through various projects across the Athens area. Some of the organizations we frequently work with include the Athens Humane Society, Habitat for Humanity, Athens-Clarke County Animal Shelter, and more! We are always looking for more organizations to offer our members a wide range of opportunities.

Additionally, Rotaract offers many benefits for a wide range of majors as we host speakers from various career fields. This provides networking opportunities with prominent business leaders and professionals who are involved with our sponsoring club, the Athens Rotary Club.

We meet monthly and host speakers from the local area and the occasional in-meeting service project.
summary: Rotaract is a leadership and service organization for young men and women that focuses on the development of young adults as leaders in their communities and the workplace.

name: Student Affiliates of the American Chemical Society
description:
We are the Chemistry Club on UGA's main campus in Athens, GA. We typically meet about once every month in the STEM building on campus to discuss chemistry, outreach, research, and more! We hope that you will consider joining our expanding student organization and get involved with our great group of students!

ALL majors are welcome!
