summary: Chem club hosts monthly to monthly meetings for chemistry students and students of all other majors and provides many opportunities for involvement in academia. Join us as we learn and have a good time together as we stay positive (like protons)!

name: Society of Xi Sigma Pi
description:
The Society’s objectives are to promote a high standard of scholarship, to work for the improvement of forest resource professions, and to foster a fraternal spirit among those engaged in forest related activities.
Membership is by invitation; requirements include a 3.0 GPA in professional coursework and an honorable personal character. Xi Sigma Pi is an active service organization. Activities planned for the year include the food drive, Relay for Life, and Custodial Staff Appreciation Day.
New member induction takes place at the beginning of each semester and meetings are scheduled as needed.
summary: The objectives of this Society is to secure and maintain a high standard of scholarship in forest resources management education and to promote a fraternal spirit among forest resources.

name: Scuba Club
description: Scuba Club will provide a welcoming and fun environment for anyone interested in diving, no matter the experience level! In partnership with Athens Scuba, we will host trial drives in the pool, short trips for all types of certifications, and dive trips for those who are certified. These places will include Lake Jocassee, Florida Springs, the Georgia Aquarium, and others!
summary: Scuba Club will provide a welcoming and fun environment for anyone interested in diving! In partnership with Athens Scuba, we will host trial drives in the pool, short trips for all types of certifications, and dive trips for those who are certified.

name: Sky Dogs Skydiving Club at UGA
description:
Whatever form of skydiving you wish to participate in, Sky Dogs at UGA offers the cheapest rates for students. Our home dropzone has been a great partner in making skydiving a more affordable sport for college students.

 

Who can recieve these rates?

  • Any student, faculty, or staff member of the University of Georgia. All you have to show is your UGA ID Card. 

 

Types of Skydiving available:

  • Tandem — most common form of skydiving where you are strapped onto an instructor.

  • AFF certification — Accelerated Freefall is a 7 jump certification course to become a solo skydiver

Group Me Link:

https://groupme.com/join_group/92820220/TguzAYa2
summary: We are the Sky Dogs at UGA, a student-run club with the goal of promoting the sport of skydiving on campus. We come in all skill levels! Whether you want a one-time thrill or want to become a certified skydiver, YOU could be a SKY DOG!

name: American Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing - UGA Student Chapter
description: Founded in 1934, the American Society for Photogrammetry and Remote Sensing (ASPRS) is a scientific association serving over 7,000 professionals members around the world. Its' mission is to advance knowledge and improve understanding of mapping sciences and to promote the responsible applications of photogrammetry, remote sensing, geographic information systems (GIS), and supporting technologies. The University of Georgia (UGA) Chapter of the ASPRS Mid-South Region was formed in April 2006. This chapter conducts events and activities at UGA and beyond to promote the mission and vision of the ASPRS.
summary: The UGA-ASPRS Student Chapter strives to advance knowledge and improve understanding of mapping sciences and the responsible applications of geospatial technology through events and activities at UGA.

name: Society of American Foresters - Forestry Club
description:
The Forestry Club is designed to help students learn more about their future careers while having fun and socializing with people of similar interests. Pre-professional, professional, and graduate students attend bimonthly meetings to listen to speakers from a variety of forestry professions. Food, refreshments and chances to meet other students are provided. The club is also involved in environmental outreach and education, FFA Field Days and participation in an annual intercollegiate conclave event.

The Forestry Club is an informative, yet relaxed, way to find out about issues and opportunities in forest resources. The club is the oldest at the Warnell School of Forest Resources, being founded in 1914. The school is open to all majors, not just forestry.
 
Join the Groupme - https://groupme.com/join_group/100658514/PBdiCDsb or click the globe below!
summary: The University of Georgia student chapter of the Society of American Foresters.

name: Women in Technology
description: The purpose of Women In Technology is to provide opportunities for UGA students. Our focus is on the success of women in the technology industry. WIT seeks to foster strong personal and professional development among our members, and to strengthen leadership qualities and technical skills to encourage career success. We seek to improve the educational, social, and cultural standards surrounding the under-representation of women in the field of technology. Membership is open to everyone and is not exclusive to women or woman-identifying individuals.
summary: UGA Women in Technology addresses the under-representation of women in STEM fields. WIT provides career development opportunities for these students.

name: Online News Association at UGA
description: We, the members of the Online News Association at UGA, and subscribing to the regulations and policies of the University of Georgia, establish this constitution to govern the matters within our organization. Acknowledging that an organization is needed to cultivate online skills within the Grady College and act as a bridge between the Grady College journalism program and technical programs in other colleges, we, passionate students dedicated to such a cause, do hereby establish the following constitution:
summary: ONA at UGA is part of a proud chapter of a national organization which celebrates journalism, supports freedom of the press, and welcomes new technologies to share our stories.

name: Rugby Football Club (Men's)
description:
UGA Rugby is the oldest club sport at UGA. We compete against other teams in the Southeast, including South Carolina, Tennessee, Kentucky, Clemson, and Alabama, as well as in invitational, regional, and national tournaments. 

We have several different teams that can accommodate both seasoned and rookie players. For those that do not know how to play, we will teach you. Most of us had never played before we came out to practice for the first time. If you are curious, the best thing to do is just come out to practice one day and see what it is like.

SIZE DOES NOT MATTER! We have players that range from 5'5" 125lbs to 6'10" 270lbs. There is a position for every sized player.

Fans are always welcome and are sure to have a good time watching our matches.

Practice is held twice a week. All practices and matches are held at field one of the Intramural Fields (9330 River Rd, Athens, GA 30602). For current practice times, contact our email or Instagram.

If you are interested in playing, please contact us by our email: ugarugbymens@gmail.com, or reach out to our Instagram @uga_rugby
summary: The UGA Rugby Football Club is a competitive club sport at UGA. We play other club rugby teams in 15s in the fall, and 7s in the spring. New players are welcome to come out, regardless of experience. All home matches are free for fans to spectate!

name: Atlas Business Society
description:
The mission of the Atlas Business Society is to give Freshmen and Sophomores a competitive edge in business before they enter the Terry College. The society will help members create stellar resumes, build LinkedIn profiles, and interview successfully. The society hosts accomplished speakers such as venture capitalists, corporate leaders, and recruiters who speak to members about leadership, the importance of networking, and their journeys to success. In addition to regular meetings, the organization completes a variety of community service events which give members a chance to give back to the Athens-Clarke County community. Additionally, there are Fall and Spring end of the semester socials to encourage bonding amongst members.

 

Fall 2020 marked the inaugural recruiting cycle for the Magellan Program. The Magellan Program (Housed within Atlas), provides Freshmen interested in business the unique opportunity to continue the mission of Atlas through building resumes, LinkedIn pages, prepping for interviews, etc. In contrast to Atlas, this program only takes place for one semester where Atlas is a two-semester commitment. While both will provide the fundamentals of business acumen, Atlas will consist of primarily corporate speakers and Magellan will be focused primarily on hard/soft skill development and peer networking. 

For more information on the Atlas Business Society, the Magellan Program and how to apply, visit our website at www.atlasuga.com. Please note that the application is only open at the beginning of the fall semester to freshmen, sophomores and those who have not yet been accepted to a Terry Major.
summary:
The Terry College-sponsored Atlas Business Society prepares UGA Freshmen and Sophomores of all majors for a successful career in business. Applications are due in Early September every year.
Please visit atlasuga.com for more information and to apply.

name: Mathematics Education Student Association
description: The Mathematics Education Student Association is an organization of persons individually and collectively dedicated to the improvement of mathematics education at all levels. Through its services and programs, the association seeks to engage and support UGA mathematics education students in community, professional improvement, and academic excellence.
summary: The Mathematics Education Student Association is a professional organization of persons individually and collectively dedicated to the improvement of mathematics education at all levels.

name: Serve UGA
description:
Mission

Serve UGA builds sustainable relationships of support among students and Athens citizens through critical learning, reflection, and service that creates positive change from the individual to the community level.  

 

Engage Deeper with Athens-Clarke County

Ongoing volunteering opportunities are available through Serve UGA's Serve Athens initiative. Through Serve Athens, Serve UGA partners with a small number of local non-profit organizations for reoccurring volunteering opportunities. Transportation is provided for students who participate.
summary: Create change in Athens-Clarke County by volunteering with Serve UGA! We work together to bridge the gaps between UGA students and the greater Athens community we reside in.

name: International Justice Mission at UGA
description:
As one of the largest anti-slavery organizations in the world, IJM is committed to ending slavery in this generation. Our UGA campus club helps by fundraising, raising awareness, and volunteering at local anti-slavery agencies.

 

IJM works in four ways: 

-Rescuing victims 

-Bringing criminals to justice 

-Restoring survivors 

-Strengthening justice systems 

 

IJM has 17 field offices all over the globe, all fighting for change and freedom. Not only do they physically free slaves, but also they prosecute the criminals behind it, and help the victims recover afterward. 

For more information about how you can get involved, check out https://www.ijm.org/ or email us at ijmuga1@gmail.com.
summary: As one of the largest anti-slavery organizations in the world, IJM is committed to ending slavery in this generation. Our UGA campus club helps by fundraising, raising awareness, and volunteering at local anti-slavery agencies. We're in it to end it!

name: Lambda Kappa Sigma Alpha Kappa Chapter
description: LKS is a pharmacy fraternity for women here at UGA. The purpose of LKS is to support women in pharmacy and provide opportunities for both personal and professional growth by promoting leadership, service, and integrity. LKS allows its members to get to know women with similar interests and passions as themselves. We participate in events, such as bake sales, networking night, and recently sponsored a pageant at the College of Pharmacy "Dr. PharmDawg" to raise money for our philanthropy Project HOPE. Project HOPE provides long-term, sustainable healthcare for people around the world. We actively engage in the Athens community by volunteering at the local food bank and several other entities in Athens. We love to meet new members who have a passion for the pharmacy profession. If interested, please feel free to reach out and contact us! Can't wait to meet you.
summary: LKS is a professional pharmaceutical fraternity for women. The organization was founded in 1913, and the Alpha Kappa Chapter was founded at UGA in 1954. The mission of the organization is to promote the interests of women in the profession of pharmacy.

name: Lunchbox Garden Project
description:
The Lunchbox Garden Project strives to engage children in conversations about nutrition, agriculture, and sustainability. Once a week, we go to Barnett Shoals, Winterville, and Johnnie Lay Burks Elementary Schools where we work with children as part of an after school program, helping them tend to a garden. We also teach lessons related to food and/or the environment. 

Involvement with the Lunchbox Garden Project allows our members to build meaningful and fulfilling relationships with the children of Athens-Clarke County. If you're looking for a great community of friends on campus and want to be more involved off-campus, we are always looking for new volunteers and would love to have you!
summary: The Lunchbox Garden Project cares about the nutritional and overall well-being of elementary school students in Athens. We help kids learn about food, nature, and healthy living through fun, hands-on activities that inspire sustainable habits.

name: Higher Learning Society
description:
We are a group of Christian students who meet in the MLC most Thursdays at 7:30 to discuss the Bible in an open and honest manner, emphasizing how it applies to the every-day life of students at the University of Georgia. Our purpose is to declare the wonderful news of the Gospel—the love of Jesus Christ—to every student at the University of Georgia. Every member of this Bible study is a living testimony of the power of Jesus Christ at work in their lives.

 

In addition to hosting Bible studies on Thursdays, we minister through live music outside of Bolton Dining Commons, MLC, or Ramsey every Tuesday night. So if you want to experience the life changing power of Jesus Christ at work in your life, listen to live music, enjoy fellowships in a safe and clean environment with other students that encourage you in your walk with Jesus Christ, and much more, contact us through the link provided on the UGA involvement website and we will be sure to get back to you!
summary: We are a group of Christian students who meet in the Miller Learning Center (MLC) every Thursday to discuss the Bible in an open and honest manner, emphasizing how it applies to the every-day life of students at the University of Georgia.

name: Delta Sigma Pi
description:
As the foremost professional fraternity for men and women, Delta Sigma Pi develops principled business leaders for the future by providing a lifetime of opportunity for our members. We are Pi chapter of the international fraternity of Delta Sigma Pi.
From undergraduates to young professionals, mid-career managers to retirees, our business fraternity impacts members throughout their lives. Delta Sigma Pi trains members in professional skills, leadership, and service. Our members turn to Delta Sigma Pi for business development throughout their careers, and also to form lifelong friendships within the Fraternity. For business professionals at all career stages, the social and professional events Delta Sigma Pi sponsors around the world are valuable resources and rewarding investments.
Pi chapter is one of the most widely recognized chapters in the nation. Our members are distinguished by their willingness to learn and their openness to different points of view, making them leaders in problem solving and organizational management.
summary: This co-ed professional Fraternity prepares ambitious students for the business world. We achieve this through  professional, fundraising, service, and social events as well as offering leadership opportunities and an extensive professional network.

name: Math Club
description: We are a student organization at UGA for those interested in mathematics. We host regular talks by undergrads, grad students, faculty, and visiting professors on interesting topics in mathematics. Although geared toward curious students involved in math, many of our lectures are designed to be accessible to anyone with a high school math background. All are welcome!
summary: We are a student organization at UGA for people of ALL majors interested in mathematics. We host regular talks by undergrads, grad students, faculty, and visiting professors on interesting topics and hold social events to meet other math enthusiasts.

name: American Society of Civil Engineers
description:
UGA ASCE is one of the leading STEM-focused student organizations on campus with an active mission of providing unique opportunities and experiences to our members. ASCE UGA is all-inclusive, welcoming all students of all majors who have an interest in civil development, whether it is engineering design, architecture, urban planning, or construction management.

In previous years, we have been able to provide many opportunities for our members. We have brought guest lecturers from various engineering industries to allow our members to better understand the responsibilities and duties of practicing engineers and construction professionals working in the engineering and construction industries. We have provided community service opportunities for our members such as working with Athens Habitat for Humanity to provide housing for those in need and tutoring kids about the wonders of engineering. We have also organized site visits to various exciting construction projects around Athens such as the Terry College of Business, the STEM Building, Sanford Stadium West End Zone Improvements, and many more.

UGA ASCE is also recognized as a national ASCE Student Chapter and we have continually participated in leadership conferences as well as our annual collegiate conference in which we compete against other universities in thrilling competitions such as the concrete canoe.

At UGA ASCE, we strive to develop a strong community that is united by our passion for civil engineering. We are continuously improving and we provide opportunities to allow our members to be the best engineers and collaborators that they can.
summary: UGA ASCE is devoted to developing industry-ready professional civil engineers through exposure to real-life engineering applications, networking opportunities, community service, engineering competitions, and internal relationship-building events.

name: Nichiren Buddhism Academy
description:
★ The fundamental purpose of the Nichiren Buddhism Academy is to help students unlock their hidden potential and achieve creative harmony with their environment through the philosophy and practice of Nichiren Buddhism in the SGI-USA.

★ This is the ultimate expression of individual empowerment – that each student can transform the inevitable challenges of daily life into sources of growth and fulfillment and become a positive influence in his or her school, workplace, family and community.

★ By promoting an understanding of how Nichiren Buddhism can be applied to the challenges of modern living, our activities contribute to the development of peace, culture, and education within society, inspire respect for cultural diversity and human rights, and encourage the protection of nature and the environment.

★ Our meetings consist of:

- An Introduction to Nichiren Buddhism

- A study on tackling life problems from the Buddhist perspective (different topics each month!) 

- Empowering Dialogue & a fun activity 

 

Interested? Join GroupMe and fill out our interest form!

https://linktr.ee/NichirenBuddhismAcademyUGA?utm_source=linktree_profile_share&ltsid=45b833e2-93fb-4eb3-8029-89a724e8abdc
summary: The fundamental purpose of the Nichiren Buddhist Association is to help students unlock their hidden potential and achieve creative harmony with their environment through the philosophy and practice of Nichiren Buddhism in the SGI-USA.

name: College Republicans
description: UGA’s chapter of College Republicans is the nation’s largest chapter. The College Republicans educate members of the UGA community about the principles of the Republican Party, aids in the election of Republican candidates at the local, state and national levels and seeks to develop the political skills and leadership abilities of its members in order to prepare them for future service to the Republican Party and their community. Sign-up on our website at https://www.ugarepublicans.com/join-us/ !
summary: The College Republicans at UGA, the nation's largest chapter, educates members of the UGA community about the Republican Party, aids in the election of Republican candidates, and seeks to develop the professional skills and abilities of its membership.

name: American Meteorological Society and National Weather Association at UGA
description: The American Meteorological Society (AMS) is the premier scientific and professional organization in the United States promoting and disseminating information about the atmospheric, oceanic, and hydrologic sciences. The National Weather Association (NWA) fosters the growth of operational meteorology through research, forecasting, broadcasting, and education. As a chapter of both the AMS and the NWA, our purpose is to give students and faculty alike the opportunity to discuss and enjoy the weather and atmospheric sciences outside of a classroom setting by sponsoring speakers, field trips, and social events.
summary: Our purpose is to give students and faculty alike the opportunity to discuss and enjoy the weather and atmospheric sciences outside of a classroom setting by sponsoring speakers, field trips, and social events.

name: Pamoja Dance Company
description: Pamoja Dance Company was founded to create an artistic outlet for minority students at UGA regardless of dance experience. Members are able to explore various dance styles through performance and choreography across campus. Pamoja hosts a themed end of semester showcase bi-annually, where members showcase several performances from the semester. Outside of the semesterly showcase, Pamoja hosts bi-monthly workshops for various dance styles led by different members of the company. Pamoja also promotes collaboration with other student organizations by performing at events hosted by organizations with similar vision as the company across campus as a dance troupe. Throughout the semester, each Pamoja event is catered for minority students to find a community at the University of Georgia.
summary: Pamoja Dance Company was founded for the purpose of providing University of Georgia students with an opportunity to become a part of a performing dance company without majoring in dance.

name: Campus Girl Scouts at UGA
description: Campus Scouts was created under the belief that socioeconomic status should not be a barrier to allowing girls to achieve their full potential as vibrant, smart, and confident leaders. By teaming with Girl Scouts of Historic Georgia via Girl Scouts of America, UGA students oversee Girl Scout troops in the Athens area. Additionally, Campus Scouts engages in volunteer work both on our campus and in our community and plans events that provide opportunities for local Girl Scouts learn and grow. All UGA students are welcome to join this growing organization!
summary: An outreach initiative offering underserved girls at locations throughout Athens the opportunity to experience Girl Scouting; here's to Samoas and self-confidence.

name: Phi Upsilon Omicron
description: Every Fall semester, a new group of qualified students are offered admission into the society. We hold monthly meetings throughout the year, as well as one Initiation ceremony every October. Our elections for new officers are typically held during the April meeting. During our meetings we brainstorm various service project ideas, discuss outside service opportunities, and participate in various activities to enhance our understanding of leadership and service. This past year, we began using it as a way for the members to network with fellow members and broadcast other organizations they are a part of. SNACS for FACS is our bi-annual club service project, for which we make home-made goodies and treats to hand out to students during finals week. We also complete mini-service projects like making Valentine's Day cards for nursing home residents, organizing trips to volunteer at the local animal shelter, and bringing in canned goods for local homeless shelters, just to name a few. We always encourage new ideas, as we are a rapidly growing society! Please feel free to email facsugaphiu@gmail.com if you have any questions!
summary: Phi Upsilon Omicron is the Honors Society of the College of Family and Consumer Sciences that offers membership to the top 10% of students in the college. Its purpose is to recognize academic excellence while enhancing leadership through service.

name: National Association for the Advancement of Colored People
description:
The principal objectives of the Association shall be:

  • To ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of all citizens
  • To achieve equality of rights and eliminate race prejudice among the citizens of the United States
  • To remove all barriers of racial discrimination through democratic processes
  • To seek enactment and enforcement of federal, state, and local laws securing civil rights
  • To inform the public of the adverse effects of racial discrimination and to seek its elimination
  • To educate persons as to their constitutional rights and to take all lawful action to secure the exercise thereof, and to take any other lawful action in furtherance of these objectives.
summary: The mission of the National Association for the Advancement of Colored People is to ensure the political, educational, social, and economic equality of rights of all persons and to eliminate race-based discrimination.

name: Association of Graduate Art Students
description: AGAS invites and hosts prominent scholars in the field of Art History. Graduate students are given the opportunity to meet and converse with successful academics from their fields of interest.
summary: Each year the Association of Graduate Art Students (A.G.A.S.) sponsors a lecture series featuring prominent scholars in the field of Art History.  It is governed by the graduate students in Art History at the Lamar Dodd School of Art.

name: Human Development and Family Science Association
description: HDFSA is an undergraduate student organization put in place to provide and to promote the professional development of the College of Family and Consumer Sciences students who are majoring in Human Development and Family Science. HDFSA works to foster the growth and development of children, adolescents, and families through education, interdisciplinary planning, stimulation and support of research, and interaction with other individuals, organizations, and agencies that have the same purpose.
summary: HDFSA is an undergraduate student organization for students majoring in or interested in Human Development and Family Science. We focus on professional development, community service, and personal growth.

name: Student National Pharmaceutical Association
description:
The Student National Pharmaceutical Association (SNPhA) is an educational service association that focuses on pharmacy and health-related issues as well as increasing minority representation in health care professions. The national objectives are as follows: to offer student members the opportunity to develop leadership and professional skills; to educate students and promote active participation in national health care issues; to develop the role of minority health professionals as being members of the health care team; to develop within communities a positive image of minority health professionals; and to educate communities on better health practices and to increase their awareness and understanding of disease states.

The University of Georgia chapter of SNPhA was charted on January 25, 1994. The chapter started out with 10 members and is now a large-sized chapter with more than 100 active members. Our chapter prides itself in giving back to the community by educating students and promoting active participation in national health care issues. Along with our commitment to health care, we also encourage our members to take on leadership roles and develop professional skills in the field of pharmacy.
summary: The Student National Pharmaceutical Association (SNPhA) is an educational service association that focuses on pharmacy and health related issues as well as increasing minority representation in health care professions.

name: Competitive Club Cheerleading
description:
A collegiate level cheerleading team that combines stunting, tumbling, and school spirit. Our team competes at NCA Nationals in Daytona, while also showcasing at competitions, holding clinics, and fundraising throughout the year. 

Recent Accomplishments:

  • 2013 Cheersport National Champions
  • 2013 Top 3 in NCA College Nationals  
  • 2014 Cheersport National Champions 
  • 2014 College Nationals Finalists
  • 2015 Cheersport Runers-up
  • 2016 Challenge Cup Runners-up
  • 2017 Challenge Cup Runners-up
  • 2018 Challenge Cup Runners-up
  • 2019 Limestone College Competition Champions
  • 2019 Atlanta Big Bid Champions
  • 2019 Top 5 in NCA College Nationals 
  • 2022 Top 10 in NCA College Nationals 
  • 2023 Top 10 in NCA College Nationals
  • 2024 Top 10 in NCA College Nationals
summary: A collegiate level cheerleading team that combines stunting, tumbling, and school spirit. Our team competes at NCA Nationals in Daytona, while also showcasing at competitions, holding clinics, and fundraising throughout the year.

name: Next Act
description: Next Act is UGA's only student-run musical theatre group. Our goal is to provide an outlet for all students interested in any aspect of musical theatre, regardless of experience. We strive to provide a positive, creative environment, and to produce professional theatrical works. We produce a season of cabarets and one full musical every year, and host other community events like movie nights, karaoke, and dance workshops.
summary: Next Act strives to produce pivotal, personal, and professional theatre that challenges and showcases student artists. Through our musicals, cabarets, and events, we aim to develop a community for UGA students interested in musical theatre.

name: Eta Sigma Phi- Zeta Iota
description: The purposes of the fraternity, as stated by the Constitution, are as follows: "to develop and promote interest in classical study among the students of colleges and universities; to promote closer fraternal relationship among students who are interested in classical study, including inter-campus relationship; to engage generally in an effort to stimulate interest in classical study, and in the history, art, and literature of ancient Greece and Rome." While membership is limited only to students enrolled in ancient language courses, we are open to everyone who is interested in the classical world! If you are interested in Eta Sigma Phi, but are not a student of the ancient languages, we encourage you to check out our sister organization, the UGA Classics Club.
summary: The Eta Sigma Phi honor society at the University of Georgia is an organization for those students studying the languages of Greek and Latin. Speakers of all levels, as well as students interested in classical culture are encouraged to check us out!

name: Hispanic Student Association
description:
The Hispanic Student Association (HSA) of the University of Georgia aims to support the needs of the Hispanic and Latinx student body, as well as preserve and promote the cultural diversity of the Hispanic/Latinx culture across the university. By creating cultural, educational, service, and social events, HSA aims to build a family of Latinx and non-Latinx students who are interested in celebrating culture, serving the Latinx community, and cultivating relations among the UGA and Athens-Clarke County communities. HSA is a student led organization, along with being an all inclusive organizaition where anyone is welcomed!

Our signature programs and events include:

  • Noche Latina
  • Voces Unidas
  • Orgullo Hispano
  • Spring Gala
  • ALAS Graduation Celebration
summary: The Hispanic Student Association (HSA) of the UGA aims to support the needs of the Latinx student body, and to promote the cultural diversity of the Hispanic/Latinx culture. HSA is an all inclusive organization where all students are welcome!

name: Korean Conversation Club
description: Conversational speaking is one of the hardest parts about learning a language, but it can be hard to find opportunities to practice speaking with other language learners.  The Korean Conversation Club provides an encouraging, relaxed space for UGA students interested in Korean language and culture to speak Korean with their peers.  Through conversation tables, games, and other activities, we aim to help our members practice and improve their Korean conversational proficiency. We welcome Korean learners at every skill level and there are no dues or special requirements to join our club--all we ask is that you speak entirely in Korean as much as you can!
summary: Welcome to KCC, a place for Korean learners and speakers at UGA to practice and improve their Korean conversational skills in a fun, relaxed setting!

name: Arch Society
description:
The Arch Society is a unique student organization for students in their third year or higher. The Arch Society provides service to the University of Georgia as official hosts and goodwill ambassadors. The People of the Arch seek to serve the University of Georgia at all functions where it is desired to have students welcome guests and visitors to the University of Georgia. The Arch Society seeks to advance the University of Georgia through service to the offices of the President, the Provost, the Vice President for Student Affairs, the Senior Vice President for External Affairs, Alumni Relations, the Athletic Association and other University offices or officials at appropriate functions.

The Arch Society is named after the most prominent symbol of the University, the Arch. In 1857, an iron fence was erected along the northern borders of the campus. The Arch, located on Broad Street at College Avenue, became the main entrance and was modeled after the Great Seal of the State of Georgia with three columns representing wisdom, justice, and moderation.
summary: The Arch Society provides service to the University of Georgia as the official hosts and goodwill ambassadors on campus.

name: Graduate Student Association
description:
GSA is an advocacy organization composed of full-time, fee-paying graduate students at the University of Georgia. Representatives from all graduate departments and programs, as well as University-recognized graduate-student organizations, serve as a formal representative body, the Graduate Student Council that is assisted and guided by the GSA Executive Board. The executive-board members and the Council representatives serve to provide a voice for the University of Georgia’s graduate and professional student body.

All are welcome to contact us at gsa@uga.edu.
summary: The Graduate Student Association (GSA) is the primary advocacy and social body for graduate students. Its Graduate Student Council consists of representatives elected from departments across campus who voice the needs of their fellow graduate students.

name: Hugh Hodgson Singers
description:
The 40-48 member Hodgson Singers was formed by E. Pierce Arant Jr. in the late 1970's and serves as the ambassadorial choral ensemble of the Hugh Hodgson School of Music at the University of Georgia. Comprising UGA's most gifted and talented vocalists, membership in the Hodgson Singers is contingent upon absolute excellence in musicianship, vocal artistry, and dedication. This ensemble provides vibrant, pre-professional ensemble training for the next generation of qualified singers, choral music educators, and talented choral enthusiasts and performs noteworthy choral repertoire from throughout the Western Music canon, with emphasis on unaccompanied works, recently composed works, and baroque and classical masterworks.

The Hodgson Singers have performed in some of the most famous cathedrals of Prague, Salzburg, Baden, and Vienna. They recently competed at the 2023 International Chamber Choir Competition Marktoberdorf as the only American choir to compete. They also competed in the 2014 International Choral Competition Ave Verum in Baden, Austria. After their incredible journey, the Hodgson Singers proudly returned to the University of Georgia as the 2014 ICC AV Grand Prix Champions. Additionally, they were the first choir in the history of the competition to score in the Golden Superior category. Other honors included winning Best Interpretations of a 20th Century Piece, a Classical Piece, and a Romantic Piece. A heartfelt record of this tour, entirely written and produced by the singers, can be found on our blog: hodgsonsingers.wordpress.com

Students are eligible for membership in the Hodgson Singers only after participating in one of the other UGA choral ensembles for at least one semester and after completing a thorough audition. The University of Georgia's Hodgson Singers is open to all UGA students by audition regardless of major! Auditions take place in the fall of each year and members are expected to make a yearlong commitment. The ensemble is conducted by Dr. Daniel Bara.
summary: The UGA Hodgson Singers serves as the flagship choral ensemble of the Hugh Hodgson School of Music at the University of Georgia, representing undergraduate through doctoral students of all disciplines.

name: Designated Dawgs
description: Designated Dawgs is a 501(c)3 non-profit organization staffed by student volunteers that provides free, safe, nonjudgmental rides home for all UGA students. The mission of Designated Dawgs is to make the University of Georgia and Athens community a safer place by being an aid to UGA and Athens-Clarke County Police Departments by preventing walking home, driving under the influence, and preventing crime overall. Over the past twenty years, we've provided over 90,000 rides home!
summary: Designated Dawgs is a student-run, 501(c)3 non-profit that provides free, safe, non-judgmental rides home for University of Georgia students.

name: Arts Collaborative
description:
The Arts Collaborative student organization is a network of undergraduate and graduate students interested in interdisciplinary arts collaboration. Members plan events and creative projects. The Arts Collaborative is affiliated with the UGA Arts Collaborative, whose mission is as follows:

The UGA Arts Collaborative is a catalyst for innovative, interdisciplinary creative projects, advanced research and critical discourse in the arts, and for creative applications of technologies, concepts, and practices found across disciplines. It is a collaborative network of faculty, students, and community members from all disciplines of the visual and performing arts in addition to other disciplines in the humanities and sciences. The Arts Collaborative enables all stages of creative activity, from concept and team formation through production, documentation, and dissemination of research.

All Arts Collaborative student organization members are full-time, fee-paying students at the University of Georgia.

Newsletter
summary: The Arts Collaborative student organization is a network of undergraduate and graduate students interested in interdisciplinary arts collaboration.

name: Kappa Sigma Fraternity
description: Kappa Sigma is a national fraternity founded on four pillars: Fellowship, Leadership, Scholarship, and Service. We plan to bring these ideals to The University of Georgia and set an example on campus with our community engagement, academic excellence, philanthropy, and community service.
summary: National fraternity that takes part in community service, social events, and academics.

name: American Medical Student Association
description:
Registration will open back up in the fall!If you are interested in joining this organization, please fill out this interest form so we can send you info on our first meeting and on how to join AMSA! Interest Form

Learn more about our organization and find the registration form at www.ugapremed.com

        AMSA works to empower all students, regardless of their career goals, with medical and volunteer opportunities, as well as cultivate a positive peer network for our members based on their specific academic and extracurricular interests. Our meetings feature physician speakers, interview workshops, preparation for the medical school admissions process, and so much more! We also provide members with many opportunities to participate in volunteer activities, attend member events, and raise awareness and funding for our philanthropy Relay for Life.

      The American Medical Student Association (AMSA), with a half-century history of medical student activism, is the oldest and largest independent association of physicians-in-training in the United States. "The American Medical Student Association is committed to improving health care and healthcare delivery to all people; promoting active improvement in medical education; involving its members in the social, moral and ethical obligations of the profession of medicine; assisting in the improvement and understanding of world health problems; contributing to the welfare of medical students, interns, residents and post-MD/DO trainees; and advancing the profession of medicine."

 "Mission and Aspirations." American Medical Student Association. 2010. Web.
summary: A premedical organization dedicated to educating, empowering, and equipping future physicians at UGA by providing pre-professional development opportunities, informational meetings, and service-learning events. Check out our website at ugapremed.com!

name: Music Therapy Student Association
description:
We exist to spread advocacy throughout our campus about the field of music therapy. Our efforts will include conducting fundraisers, engaging our fellow colleagues in music therapy activities, performing in benefit concerts and serving our community by leading music sessions at various centers in Athens.

We will also work to raise funds to send our students to our regional music therapy conference in the spring. Furthermore, we will submit research proposals each year to present at these conferences. 

We will be hosting events such as benefit concerts and our annual Sensory Friendly Concert. All events are to benefit fundraising for music therapy charities, money for conference attendance, or to simply share music therapy with the Athens community.
summary: Our mission is to promote music therapy throughout our campus and share our journey as we learn more about this incredible profession.

name: Semper Fi Society
description:
The process to becoming a Marine Corps officer can be a long and difficult process. It requires dedication, determination, and training. The Semper Fi Society is designed to assist Marine Corps officer candidates in this process. While the Semper Fi Society is run by UGA students who are fellow candidates, the official process is run through the Marine Corps Officer Selection Office.  

Some of the things the Semper Fi Society does on campus include physical training, holding classes meant to prepare candidates for Officer Candidates School, and help with promotional events for the office on campus.
summary: Our club is designed to train, educate, and mentor United States Marine Corps officer candidates in the process to becoming a Marine Corps officer.

name: Environmental Health Science Club
description:
To foster mutual interest and enthusiasm amongst the students, faculty, and community of the University of Georgia in regard to the environment. We are dedicated to environmental health activism as it relates to public health and the community. This club has been established to also serve as a network for students interested in Environmental Health to meet and exchange knowledge.
 
Join our groupme! - https://groupme.com/join_group/47482306/1ZBQBKyy
summary: EHS Club focuses on environmental issues related to human health and occupations within the field. Our mission is to raise environmental awareness and improve the quality of life in our community.

name: Badminton Club
description: The purpose of the Badminton Club is to provide weekly opportunities for individuals interested in the sport of badminton to be able to play together and learn from each other. We hope to foster an encouraging collegiate environment, and hopefully introduce the sport to new players as well as further the talents of preexisting players. We also provide other opportunities within the club for upper-level competition and community. The past few semesters, we've made it to collegiate nationals and are becoming more and more competitive with each passing year. Badminton is a good way to find, not only a fast-paced and exciting pastime, but also new friends and great team members! Come see us in the East gym @ Ramsey!
summary: Our organization is responsible for promoting badminton as a recreational and competitive sport on UGA's campus and engaging in opportunities for intercollegiate badminton tournaments.

name: Graphic Design Club
description: Our mission is to foster a strong sense of community between the University of Georgia's students and the local professional design community. The Graphic Design Club's purpose is to educate, expose, and provide resources to students interested in anything and everything relevant, historical or current, in the world of graphic design.
summary: The Graphic Design Club is made up of student designers that work to build a strong design community and creative professional connections in the Athens and Atlanta, Georgia.

name: Karate Club at UGA, The
description:
Founded in 1962, the Karate Club is the oldest and largest active martial arts group on campus.  All levels of experience are welcome to join us and we accept new members year round. 

 

We meet at Ramsey in the Mind Body Studio on Tuesdays and Thursdays from 7:00 pm - 9:00 pm, and on Sundays from 2:00 pm - 4:00 pm.

 

We promote belts once at the end of the semester. Our goal is to advance members through a mixture of Tae Kwon Do and Karate martial art forms, self-defense routines and demonstrations of skill mastery. Our practices regularly include cardiovascular warm-up, striking and kicking drills, supplemented by combat and self-defense drill practices. 

 

If you are interested in attending practices, please fill out the 2024-2025 Karate Competitive Sports Waiver. After receiving an approval email from Competitive Sports, forward that email to karate@uga.edu through your UGA email. Please include your first and last name and phone number so that you can be added to the roster.
summary:
We seek to teach our members martial arts in a safe and fun manner.  All experience levels are welcome.

Class times are Tuesday/Thursday 7 PM-9 PM and Sunday 2 PM-4PM in the Mind-Body Studio of Ramsey (Studio F). 

Website: karateuga.weebly.com

name: Women's Ultimate Frisbee
description:
Athena (our A team) and Medusa (our B team) are the two teams that make up the UGA Women's Club Ultimate program. We strive to be competitive, build positive relationships with fellow athletes, and have fun! No experience is required to join the team, and we have a learn-to-play period in the fall for people who want to learn. 

We practice two days a night throughout the whole year. Our main season is in the spring, where we compete in tournaments around the southeast. Athena usually makes it to nationals!
summary: We are the Women's Club Ultimate Frisbee team

name: Instructional Technology Student Association
description: The Instructional Technology Students Association at the Univeristy of Georgia’s mission is to learn about, develop skills in, practice, and promote the field of instructional technology with the goal of improving educational practices in both academic and business settings.
summary:
ITSA fosters communication and networking between students and faculty in the
Department of Career and Information Studies, specifically the graduate programs in Learning, Design, and Technology.

name: Young Life
description: Young Life thinks the world of kids. We believe that they deserve to know what life can hold for them. Young Life leaders are caring adults who come sharing that hope as well as fun and lasting friendships with kids. Young Life is also all about fun. From club and camp to Campaigners and other activities, teenagers will have blast laughing, trying new things and being with their friends. Leaders are committed to being involved with kids' lives--from supporting them at their sporting events to listening to them talk about what's important to them.  Our Young Life and WyldLife leaders help kids consider the direction of their lives, and they offer hope for their future.
summary: Young Life is a mission community of Christ-centered people committed to reaching adolescent youth with the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

name: Communication Studies Graduate Forum
description: The purpose of the CSGF is to promote communication, educational opportunities and scholarly activity among graduate students in the Department of Communication Studies. We provide social networking opportunities for graduate students and student representation within the Department of Communication Studies.
summary: The CSGF promotes communication, educational opportunities, and scholarly activity among graduate students in the Department of Communication Studies.

name: Pi Sigma Alpha
description: Pi Sigma Alpha is the National Political Science Honor Society focused on connecting all SPIA students to research opportunities and local community service events in Athens-Clarke County. The chapter will focus on enhancing SPIA students research skills by helping students throughout the research process and directing them to research events. Additionally, the organization focuses on fostering a sense of community by hosting monthly community service events and fun activities.
summary: Pi Sigma Alpha seeks to engage SPIA students in research opportunities both on the local and national level. The local chapter focuses on strengthening members researching abilities and also focuses on civic engagement within the local community.

name: Lifelong Education, Administration, and Policy Graduate Student Network
description: The mission of LEAP GSN is to provide a communication network and support system for students interested in adult education and lifelong learning. We facilitate social and professional interaction among LLOD (Learning, Leadership and Organization Development), EDAP (Educational Administration and Policy), and QUAL (Qualitative Research) learners in the LEAP (Lifelong Education, Administration, and Policy) Department within the UGA’s College of Education (COE).
summary: LEAP GSN facilitates social and professional interaction among graduate students in the Department of Lifelong Education, Administration, and Policy within UGA's College of Education. We provide learners with a communication network and a support system.

name: Young Americans for Liberty
description: Young Americans for Liberty at UGA was created to offer students a new political voice on campus. Our main goal is to educate, train, and mobilize our members with libertarian philosophy and to work toward a campus that gives a voice to all students.
summary: Young Americans for Liberty at UGA was created to offer students a new political voice on campus. Our main goal is to educate, train, and mobilize our members with libertarian philosophy and to work toward a campus that gives a voice to all students.

name: Family Law Society
description: Family Law Society is a student led organization which seeks to familiarize students interested in family law with the emerging issues in the field and to connect interested students with a professional network of alumni and practicing attorneys through career panels, small group gatherings, and fundraising events benefiting local organizations.
summary: A student organization focusing on the various careers and opportunities in the area of family and domestic law.

name: Finance Society
description: Finance Society is an active business organization that provides its members with valuable resources, information and contacts. Its main objectives are to maintain communication between the business community and the academic world, to introduce and broaden network opportunities for members, and help bridge the gap between academics and career. Finance Society meetings have a variety of content including: professional speakers, employer presentations, career advice and strategies, and networking events.
summary: Finance Society is a business organization that provides its members with valuable information and contacts. Its goals are to support communications between the academic and business worlds while fostering network opportunities for students.

name: American Fisheries Society at UGA
description:
The Society's objectives are to:

  1. Promote the conservation, development, and wise use of fisheries resources;
  2. Promote and evaluate the development and advancement of all branches of fisheries science and practice;
  3. Gather and disseminate to Society members and the general public scientific, technical, and other information about fisheries science and practice through publications, meetings, and other forms of communication;
  4. Encourage the teaching of fisheries science and practice in colleges and universities; and
  5. Develop technical and professional skills related to fisheries biology and management.
  6. Specifically, the UGA Fisheries Society seeks to provide educational enrichment, encourage community service, and promote professional development among University of Georgia students, faculty, and staff interested in fisheries and aquatic sciences.
summary: Our goal is to understand and conserve Georgia's fishery resources and aquatic ecosystems through education, professional development, service, and society membership.

name: School of Public and International Affairs Student Union
description: As the School of Public and International Affairs Student Union, it is our goal to build and maintain a stronger sense of community throughout SPIA. We will hold various events throughout the year that will bring students closer to one another and their professors. Check out our website (spiastudentunion.wordpress.com) or follow us on Instagram (@spia_su) for updates on news and events! Questions and ideas for activities may be directed to spiastudentunion@gmail.com
summary: We aim to build a sense of community and provide opportunities for networking and career development to SPIA students at UGA.

name: Georgia Daze Minority Recruitment Program
description:
Georgia Daze is a two-tiered recruitment program.

Tier One caters to prospective high school students

  • by enabling admitted high school seniors from historically underrepresented groups to experience the University of Georgia and its campus life through an overnight visitation program.
    • During the weekend, these students are able to attend class, tour campus and its facilities, stay with a current on-campus student in one of the residence halls, eat in the dining halls, and participate in social events.
  • and by giving high school students from historically underrepresented groups the opportunity to experience the University of Georgia by hosting various events in those high schools and cities.

Tier Two caters to current UGA students

  • by providing programs and opportunities for current UGA students to become connected to faculty and staff, students like themselves, and alumni.
    • We help students build their personal and professional resumes through these various connections and provide academic and social support.
summary: Georgia Daze aims to encourage talented, ambitious and innovative students to enroll at UGA, while working to provide programs and opportunities for current UGA students to bridge the University-at-large population and our community!

name: Sigma Alpha Omega
description:
Follow us! Instagram: @saoatuga 

Sigma Alpha Omega is a National Christian social sorority, and we represent the Mu Chapter here at UGA. We are a group of college-aged girls who love the Lord and are interested in pursuing a relationship with Jesus Christ and other fellow believers through social events and fellowship. Our nationally recognized philanthropic cause is Ovarian Cancer Awareness, and we participate in events throughout the year to raise funds and awareness for Ovarian Cancer Research. We also engage in a variety of local and campus oriented service projects and social events.

Our recruitment process takes place during the Fall semester only.

Purpose: The purpose of this organization shall be to assemble Christian women in a Christ-centered sisterhood for the purpose of enjoying Christian fellowship, service, and social activities in accordance with the purpose of the sorority.

Mission: The Mission Statement for Sigma Alpha Omega is: To glorify God by reaching women across the world through the expansion of the sisterhood, by encouraging spiritual development through Christ-inspired accountability and unity, by participating in evangelistic and philanthropic endeavors, and by focusing on good scholarship and humble Leadership.

Vision: The Vision of Sigma Alpha Omega is: To become an international Christ-centered sisterhood that equips women to grow in their relationships with Christ through unity and service to others at institutions of higher learning.
summary: The Mu Chapter of Sigma Alpha Omega Christian Sorority, Inc. founded in 2004 at the University of Georgia. One in Christ through Unity in Sisterhood.

name: Christian Campus Fellowship
description:
Christian Campus Fellowship is a registered student organization at the University of Georgia that strives to follow Jesus Christ as Lord. We are a diverse group that ministers to the UGA community through Bible Studies, worship times, free meals, retreats, socials, intramurals, service projects, and small groups.

Meeting times:

Thursday night - Dinner and A Message - free dinner at 6:45 and worship service at 7:30

Small groups - various times throughout the week (see our website for updates!)

 

CCF exists for students - and the things we do reflect what YOU want to do. CCF is a non-denominational campus ministry, and students of all different backgrounds make up our group - and we welcome diversity! Are you looking for a place to grow spiritually? One where you will be welcomed and feel at home? If so, we invite you to try Christian Campus Fellowship.​

 

Want to learn more about God? Do you know nothing about God but want more out of life? Has your faith become stagnant? Are you a growing, excited Christian who wants to stay encouraged? Whoever you are, we want to help you get closer to God. We know life is hard, but we also know God is good. Questions are respected and welcomed at CCF, as are all people- regardless of Biblical knowledge, background, or lifestyle. Come to a place where you can be yourself and seek God in your own time, with some helpful guidance from our student leaders, ministry assistants, and staff. Come try CCF!
summary: A non-denominational Christian organization that exists to serve and love the way Christ loves us. We meet weekly on Thursday nights for free food and worship. We want to be your Athens home away from home!

name: COMPASS
description: COMPASS seeks to serve as a platform for a collegiate exchange of ideas, and facilitate avenues for support towards building a generative and inclusive community of scholars for cultural, professional, social and intellectual enrichment.
summary: The Comparative Literature and Intercultural Studies Graduate Student Organization at UGA

name: Phi Delta Chi
description:
Phi Delta Chi is a professional pharmacy fraternity orginally founded at the University of Michigan on November 2, 1883. The Alpha Iota chapter at the University of Georgia was founded on May 29th, 1937. Notable alumni of PDC include Eli Lilly (drug manufacturing company founder) and Robert C. Wilson (UGA Pharmacy Building).

The chapter actively participates and promotes professional, service, and leadership events throughout the semester. Events include - St. Jude 5K, Adopt-A-Hwy, Brown Bag Events, Faculty Appreciation Breakfasts, CV/Resume Workshops, National Conventions such as Grand Council, Leadership Development Seminar, Southeast Regional Conference and many more. 

Besides helping Brothers grow professionally, Alpha Iota also focuses on social aspects and promotes a fraternal spirit among its Brothers. Events such as football tailgates, Halloween Party, Carnivore Carnival, Thanksgiving/Holiday party, Spring Formal, Summer and Winter Retreats are just a few of the events we conduct to foster Brotherhood among members.
summary: To advance the science of pharmacy and its allied interests, and to foster and promote a fraternal spirit among its Brothers.

name: Stillpoint Literary Magazine
description: Stillpoint Literary Magazine was founded by University of Georgia undergraduates circa 1967 as a medium for student writers and artists to express themselves. Today, we curate and feature poetry, prose, creative non-fiction, and artwork in our annual print publication, on our website, and at readings held throughout the year. The print magazine is published at the end of every spring semester; our online content is published on a rolling basis. We strive to always improve the quality of Stillpoint's content and design.
summary: Stillpoint Literary Magazine is a student-run literary magazine that annually publishes poetry, prose, and visual arts in print and online. The organization also hosts literary events and fundraisers throughout the year.

name: Public Interest Law Council
description:
The Public Interest Law Council (PILC) is a student-run organization dedicated to expanding the University of Georgia School of Law’s commitment to serving the public good. PILC works to connect students with opportunities to engage in meaningful, community-focused, and justice-oriented legal work.

Our mission is to promote awareness, education, and engagement in the public interest sector by hosting events, panels, and service opportunities that highlight diverse paths within public service law.
summary: PILC is dedicated to the promotion of service of the public interest, civilly and criminally, at the University of Georgia School of Law. We hope to foster involvement and interest in every area of public interest law.

name: UGA Club Cross Country and Track Team
description:
The University of Georgia Club Cross Country and Track and Field Team is made up of undergraduate students, graduate students, and employees at the University of Georgia who run and compete together at the club level. The club races against other collegiate club teams in association with NIRCA (National Intercollegiate Running Club Association). Throughout the year the team trains for and travels to various meets across the country. The club includes runners of all skill levels from beginner to expert. Anyone can join the club, run workouts, and compete in NIRCA qualifier meets. This club is a great way to connect with other student runners at the University of Georgia and at other universities. 
Interest Form

Signup Process

Club Records
summary: The UGA Club Cross Country and Track and Field Team is made up of students who share the love for running and a desire to compete. The Club team competes in NIRCA (National Intercollegiate Running Club Association) against other collegiate club teams.

name: Anthropology Graduate Student Organization
description: AGSO is an academic and social organization that includes all registered Anthropology graduate students at the University of Georgia. AGSO facilitates personal and professional development by organizing events for students, faculty and staff, mentoring undergraduate Anthropology students, hosting guest lecturers, and supplementing costs for students to attend professional meetings. AGSO also organizes a number of events for the promotion of Anthropology at the University of Georgia.
summary: AGSO supports the personal growth and professional development of Anthropology graduate students through fostering collegiality among graduate students, organizing networking events, inviting speakers and supporting grads attending academic conferences.

name: Men's Club Ultimate Frisbee
description:
We are a competitive Ultimate Frisbee team that travels across the country to play against other collegiate teams! We have multiple teams depending on your skills and time commitment.

For more information, contact the club at our email - ugaultimate@gmail.com
summary:
We are a competitive Ultimate Frisbee team that travels across the country to play against other collegiate teams! We have multiple teams depending on your skills and time commitment.

For more information, contact the club at ugaultimate@gmail.com

name: Sailing Club at UGA
description:
The Sailing Club at UGA has been actively promoting the sport of sailing to university students for over 40 years. No experience is necessary to get involved.

Our team is composed of two groups that are categorized by level of commitment: the race team and the cruising team.

The race teams compete within SAISA (South Atlantic Intercollegiate Sailing Association) against other "southern" schools like Georgia Tech, Vanderbilt, Duke, University of Florida, Clemson, etc. We attend 6-8 regattas every semester (weekend trips) across the southeast and practice weekly. Traveling fees for these trips are included in your dues. (See below) Seeing as you will be representing UGA at these competitions, we prefer for members who are racing to come once a week as much as possible to get some practice in.

Our cruising sailing team program allows members the opportunity to learn to sail without the pressures of racing. You can come to practices whenever it's convenient and can take it at your own pace. Highly recommended if you are just getting into the sport, or have lots of time conflicts and can't make it to practice as much.

Both teams practice every week at the same times, allowing inexperienced members to learn from more senior members on multiple different sizes and types of sailboats. 

Both parts of the club want to instill a lifelong love for sailing and share it with as many people as possible, whether you feel called to race or cruise.

Send us a message on here or to our email linked below for more information! We'd Love to have you! (As of Fall 2024)

For a look inside our team, feel free to check out our Youtube channel full of fun and educational sailing videos!

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rHsGpw9EU_E
summary: UGA Sailing is a competitive intercollegiate racing team. Our mission is to provide college students the means to compete and entry into the sport, as well as to foster a lifelong love for sailing with a strong foundation of knowledge.

name: American Water Resources Association UGA Student Chapter
description: We are American Water Resources Association (AWRA) UGA student chapter. Nationally, AWRA leads a multidisciplinary effort to encourage exploration, collaboration, and education in all things related to water resources management. Here at UGA, we are led by graduate and undergraduate students who have expressed interest and commitment to extending these national efforts to the campus community and the greater Athens-Clarke County area. UGA AWRA holds monthly meetings where we watch water-related films, host guest speakers, facilitate hands-on outreach and educational activities, and participate in community service events. You can find out more on our social media (Instagram and Facebook) and website. Join us in our mission to educate our local community on water resources management!
summary:
AWRA (American Water Resources Association) UGA Student Chapter 
Our mission is to advance multidisciplinary water resources education, management, and research on the UGA campus. We invite anyone interested in water to join.

name: Her Campus UGA
description: Her Campus UGA is UGA's very own chapter of the national Her Campus website. Her Campus is the #1 online community written for college students by college students. Our chapter publishes content that focuses on the interests of our staff and readers, ranging from personal stories to news and profiles of prominent campus individuals. In addition, HC UGA plans events with other organizations and plans fundraisers in order to fulfill its mission of serving college women on every platform. If you have an interest in journalism, fashion and beauty, editorial writing, social media, public relations, and/or event planning, check out Her Campus UGA. All majors are welcome, and no previous writing experience is required!
summary: Her Campus is the #1 online community written by college women for college women with content focused on style, beauty, life, love, career, health, and school.

name: Society of Entrepreneurs
description:
SOE is a student organization that provides an opportunity for like-minded change-makers to connect, share ideas, and pursue innovation.

Essentially, SOE is an intuitive group of students connected to a network of startups, entrepreneurs, and communities focused on innovation.
summary: Society of Entrepreneurs is made of a community of like-minded change-makers with an entrepreneurial spirit. SOE connects students with opportunities to drive and inspire entrepreneurial change through our three pillars: to connect, inspire, and create.

name: Alpha Lambda Delta Honors Society
description: Alpha Lambda Delta is a national honor society that recognizes academically outstanding students during their first year on campus. ALD was founded in 1924 at the University of Illinois. Since then, it has grown to include 665,000 students and 225 chapters across the United States. Upon joining ALD, members work to improve the community by participating in service events/activities and build leadership skills through ALD information sessions. Leadership opportunities on the executive board are made available once a year to interested individuals. ALD also strives to be a community where new members can come and make long lasting friendships.
summary: Alpha Lambda Delta is a national honor society for recognizing academically outstanding freshman. ALD was founded in 1924 at the University of Illinois. Since then, it has grown to include 665,000 students and 225 chapters across the United States.

name: Asha For Education @ UGA
description: Asha Athens (also known as Asha-UGA) is a non-profit organization and also an active Student Group at the University of Georgia. It is a completely volunteer driven organization, comprising of dedicated individuals from the University of Georgia and Athens area, striving to make a difference to the society. We believe that education is one of the primary factors in shaping a child’s future and by extension, future of the entire country. Asha UGA works towards promoting the education of underprivileged children in India by providing financial assistance and support to the NGOs focused on education projects. Volunteers of Asha work closely with the project partners in India, while locally organizing fundraisers and community awareness events.
summary: Asha for Education is an international non-profit organization that raises money through donations for the funding of education opportunities for underprivileged children in in the U.S. and Southeast Asia. Our focus is shaping tomorrow's leaders, kids.

name: Budokai
description:
The Budokai, a Recreational Sports club, has offered classes in traditional Japanese karate at the University of Georgia since 1982. Classes are open to UGA students, faculty, and staff. No fees are charged for instruction.

 

What is Shorinji Ryu Karatedo?

Karatedo ("empty hand way") is a traditional Japanese martial art that teaches unarmed methods for self defense and the protection of others. Originally practiced in Okinawa, karatedo was introduced to Japan in the early 20th century and has since spread across the world. Shorinji Ryu is one of many schools or "ryu" of karatedo. The name "Shorinji" pays homage to the Shaolin temple, from which most forms of karatedo claim descent.

The rankings of UGA Budokai instructors and members are certified by the Zen Tohoku Karatedo Kempo Kumiai (All Northern Japan Karatedo and Kempo Union), one of the 47 prefectural organizations that comprise the Japan Karatedo Federation.

Why Practice Karate?

All over the world, people practice karate for many reasons: for personal security; to improve health and fitness; as a sport; and as a means of personal development. Karate differs from other martial arts and combat sports in a number of ways:

• Karate can be learned and practiced effectively regardless of size, weight, or sex.

• Participants can begin training at almost any level of fitness and make sustainable progress.

• Karate can be practiced and refined across the lifespan, from childhood to old age.

• Karate training is often supplemented by training with traditional kobudo weapons such as the bo, sai, and tonfa.

• Karate is grounded in a philosophy of non-violence, service to others, and self-improvement.

 

HOW TO JOIN:

As with all Club Sports at UGA, participants are required to fulfill two basic requirements BEFORE BEING ABLE TO PRACTICE in the club:

I. HAVE AN APPROVED WAIVER.

II. EMAIL THE CLUB (uga.budokai@gmail.com) TO BE ADDED TO THE ROSTER. 

Both steps are simple.

To Sign the Waiver, follow the steps below:

  1. Log into the UGA Involvement Network
  2. Go to the drop-down menu in the top left hand corner. 
  3. Click on “Forms”.
  4. Scroll down to Form “5. Competitive Sports Waiver - Budokai, Classic City Bhangra, Cheer, Georgia Dance Team, Gymnastics, Karate, Red Hotz, and Shikaari: 2024-2025”.
  5. Complete the steps and submit the form.
  6. You will receive an email confirming your submission.
  7. A few days later, you will receive an email saying your waiver has been approved.

          → Forward the approval email to uga.budokai@gmail.com to get added to the Roster.

 

Prospective members only need to fulfill these two basic requirements in order to practice with the Budokai. No additional steps are needed for the rest of the year once these steps have been taken successfully (aside from the general roster check-in before every club practice--a quick verification of your STUDENT ID). 

 

Any questions concerning finding the waiver, membership, or club practice can be emailed to uga.budokai@gmail.com. 

Although attending practice requires the completion of these initial processes, simply observing practice has no prerequisites and anyone interested is welcome to come do so.
summary: We offer classes in traditional Japanese karate at UGA. We emphasize traditional martial training in Shorinji Ryu Karatedo, self defense application, and personal development. We meet M&W 8-9:30/F 8-9 in Studio F (Ramsey). Email uga.budokai@gmail.com

name: National Society of Collegiate Scholars
description:
NSCS provides its members with useful networking, leadership and service opportunities and gives out a million dollars in scholarships annually. NSCS members at UGA are deeply committed to impacting their campus and the Athens-Clarke county daily. 

The UGA chapter is involved with Athens-Clarke county through various on-going community service projects. Our chapter meets the last Wednesday of every month for general body meetings at 7pm!*
summary: The National Society of Collegiate Scholars is an honors organization that recognizes and elevates high achievers. NSCS stands on three pillars: scholarship, leadership, and service to promote success in its members and their surrounding communities.

name: Minorities in Agriculture, Natural Resources, and Related Sciences
description:
Minorities in Agriculture, Natural Resources and Related Sciences (MANRRS) began in 1986 as the shared vision of a group of agriculture students and faculty at Michigan State University and Pennsylvania State University. Their goal was to develop a network between minority agriculture and natural resources students and professionals from academic institutions, government, and industry.

Today, the organization has grown into a vibrant national society of students and professionals from all racial, cultural, and ethnic backgrounds. Members have the opportunity to participate in diverse activities that support their and others' academic and professional development and achievement. 

Some of the fun meeting themes, volunteer, and fundraising opportunities that members get to experience are Resume Writing/Critique Workshops, Dinner Etiquette Workshops, Professional/Business Casual Fashion Shows, can food drives, soup kitchen volunteering, Rivers Alive: Cleaning Oconnee River via KAYAKS, Annual Battle of the Dances Competition, Regional Conference, National Conference and so much more!

Every Fall Semester our Chapter attends a Regional Cluster Conference in Atlanta. Every Spring our Chapter attends the National MANRRS Conference.

Membership is open to all students!
summary: The mission of MANRRS is to promulgate diversity, promote academic proficiency and professional development by empowering minorities in agriculture, natural resources, and related sciences. Membership is open to all students!

name: National Association Of Black Accountants
description:
The Association shall unite through membership accountants, finance and business related professionals and students who have similar interests and ideals, are committed to professional and academic excellence, possess a sense of professional and civic responsibility, and are concerned with enhancing opportunities for minorities in the accounting, finance and business related professions. The goals of the Association include, but are not limited to, the following: 
  • To promote and develop the professional skills of our members
  • To encourage and assist minority students entering the accounting profession
  • To provide opportunities for members to fulfill their civic responsibilities
  • To promote public confidence in our members and the services they provide
  • To encourage cordial relations among members and cooperative relationships with other professionals
