  • To represent the interests of current and prospective minority accounting professionals
  • To ensure long-term financial stability and provide adequate resources to implement chapter, regional, and national programs.
summary: The mission of NABA, Inc. is to address the professional needs of its members and to build leaders that shape the future of the accounting and finance profession with an unfaltering commitment to inspire the same in their successors.

name: Creative Writing Club
description:
Our meetings consist of the following: 

Workshops are meetings in which members submit their work to be reviewed by other members. The purpose of these is to give you a variety of perspectives and feedback to your work.

Roundtables involve discussions about selected topics pertaining to writing. 

Yap Night gives you ten minutes to discuss your project freely and answer questions.

Write-ins are a time for members to put some work into their current projects. 

It is recommended that anyone participating in club bring a WiFi-capable electronic device. 

Announcements and meeting information can be found on our Facebook page and Instagram. If you have any questions about joining, send us a message through these platforms or an email at ugacreativewritingclub@gmail.com!
summary: A casual group of writers dedicated to discussing creative writing topics, sharing projects, and reviewing student-written works in a workshop setting. Creative Writing Club meets every Wednesday at 6:30 in Park Hall 144!

name: Public Health Association
description: The Public Health Association at UGA endeavors to encourage the exploration of public health issues through discussions, service activities, and meaningful interactions between students within the college. Our goals are: to foster a close relationship between students and our community,  help students explore their career goal and interests, and create links within the Athens community through service projects.
summary: The Public Health Association is a club that unites students from varying fields and backgrounds in the name of communal health. This organization is open to all students in related fields, or to those with a passion for the health of their community.

name: AGHON
description: AGHON was founded in 1920 and seeks to recognize students on campus with outstanding leadership and character and a passion for agriculture. AGHON also seeks to award honorary membership to leaders with these same characteristics who have made an impact in some field of agriculture.  Students in the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences, the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources, the College of Veterinary Medicine and the College of Engineering are nominated and selected by their peers to participate in a semester-long commitment to enhance their understanding and appreciation of the University of Georgia and agriculture history.
summary: To honor students in the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences, the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources, the College of Veterinary Medicine and the College of Engineering.

name: Alpha Epsilon Delta, Georgia Alpha Chapter
description: The purpose of AED is to encourage excellence in premedical, predental, preoptometry, and prepodiarty scholarship, to stimulate an appreciation of the importance of premedical education in the study of medicine, to promote cooperation and contacts between medical and premedical students and educators in developing an adequate program of premedical education, and to promote cooperation and camaraderie among similar interested students.
summary: Alpha Epsilon Delta is a pre-health honors society. Our meetings include speakers from different healthcare fields and community service events around Athens. We strive to build connections between all pre-health students on campus.

name: Geography Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of the GGSA is to organize forums for the dissemination of geographic ideas and research within the department, UGA, and universities in the southeastern USA. This is a graduate student organization of current geography graduate students at the University of Georgia.
summary: The purpose of the GGSA is to organize forums for the dissemination of geographic ideas and research within the department, UGA, and universities in the southeastern USA.

name: Georgia Students of Landscape Architecture
description: Georgia Students of Landscape Architecture strives to unite graduate and undergraduate students of Landscape Architecture, Historic Preservation, and Urban Planning with professionals in an effort to foster interest toward fulfilling educational and social needs as well as broadening the understanding of our profession from lectures, workshops, and campus and community service events.
summary: To unite interested graduate and undergraduate landscape architecture, historic preservation, and urban planning students at the University of Georgia.

name: Turkish Student Association
description: The Turkish Student Association at the University of Georgia is a cultural and social organization dedicated to supporting students and promoting cross-cultural understanding on campus. We work to introduce Turkish history, traditions, and values to the UGA community through cultural celebrations, educational events, outreach activities, and social gatherings. Our organization provides a welcoming environment where students can connect, share experiences, and build friendships. In addition to cultural engagement, we aim to support our members' academic and personal success through community, collaboration, and mentorship. Membership in the Turkish Student Association is open to all UGA students regardless of cultural or ethnic background, and we welcome anyone interested in learning about or engaging with Turkish culture.
summary: The Turkish Student Association at UGA celebrates Turkish culture and fosters community through cultural, educational, and social events. We welcome all UGA students interested in learning and connecting.

name: Bangladeshi Students Association
description:
The Bangladeshi Students Association (BASA) at UGA was established to foster a supportive community among Bangladeshi students, promoting academic collaboration, cultural exchange, and mutual assistance.

Our primary objective is to provide a platform for Bangladeshi students across all academic disciplines at UGA to connect, share experiences, and support one another in navigating student life. We believe that collective knowledge and peer support significantly enhance the student experience.

BASA also serves as a resource for prospective students, offering guidance on housing, academics, and life in Athens based on firsthand experiences. For inquiries, please reach out to basa.uga.chapter@gmail.com. We welcome contributions from current members who wish to share insights and personal experiences to benefit the broader community.

In addition to academic and peer support, BASA actively celebrates cultural events and organizes social programs that bring students together and foster a sense of belonging. These events provide opportunities to reconnect with familiar faces or build new friendships within a welcoming environment.

Membership is free to all currently enrolled UGA students. To access our resources and participate in upcoming activities, we invite you to join BASA and become part of our growing community.
summary: BaSA is an organization of Bangladeshi students of the UGA. This organization is a platform for Bangladeshi UGA students to make Athens their home outside their home country. It's also a way of sharing our culture and heritage to the world.

name: Association of Latino Professionals for America
description: ALPFA is the largest association of Latino/Hispanic professionals! We have chapters located all around the country and thousands of students and alumni nationwide! ALPFA is dedicated to enhancing opportunities for Latino/Hispanic individuals and building leadership and professionalism skills. ALPFA is a non-profit entity registered with the Internal Revenue Service. Membership is open to anyone who shares our values, vision, and mission. Join the ALPFAmilia Today!
summary: ALPFA is the premier professionalism organization for expanding opportunities for Latino Leadership in the global market.

name: College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences Student Ambassadors
description: The CAES Ambassadors are a select group of students dedicated to enhancing the image of and interest in the College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences at the University of Georgia.  Ambassadors assist at College or University sponsored events and are available to speak on topics related to agriculture, the environment or youth opportunities before groups, schools, clubs, assemblies, banquets or similar events.  Ambassadors also work in conjunction with the CAES Office of Academic Affairs to recruit prospective students.
summary: The CAES Ambassadors are 28-32 students enrolled in CAES who assist with recruitment and college-related activities to enhance the public and instructional image of the college, its students and its programs.

name: HOUSE
description: HOUSE at UGA is for students interested in residential property management and real estate. We provide our members with the opportunity to network with current industry professionals and learn about different facets of the industry. We encourage and support our members to take advantage of every opportunity to grow, connect and set yourself up for success.
summary: HOUSE is a student organization aimed at the professional development of students through career coaching, leadership-development, and strategic networking with peers, faculty, and industry professionals.

name: Athens Church Student Organization
description: Our Organization exists to connect students to Athens Church and its different ministry environments.
summary: We exist to be a Home Away From Home for students through small groups, community service opportunities, and large-scale events called College Nights.

name: Christian Pharmacists Fellowship International
description: The CPFI student chapter at the University of Georgia College of Pharmacy is designed to achieve the mission and goals of the national organization while encouraging and challenging our fellow pharmacy students in their relationships with God. Organization activities include Bible studies, social gatherings, and community service events.
summary: Christian Pharmacists Fellowship International (CPFI) is a worldwide ministry of individuals working in all areas of pharmaceutical service and practice. Our mission is to serve Christ and the world through pharmacy.

name: Tax Law Society
description: The Purpose of the UGA Tax Law Society shall be to: (1) promote the study of tax law and interest in the tax law profession through professional speakers, informational meetings, and networking events; (2) help students gain practical experience in tax law through participation in national and regional tax competitions; and (3) promote community involvement through participation in the Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program.
summary: The Tax Law Society promotes the study of tax law and interest in the tax law profession through professional speakers, informational meetings, networking events, tax competitions, and community service with VITA.

name: Agricultural and Applied Economics Graduate Student Association
description: We are the Graduate Student Association for Agricultural & Applied Economics at UGA. We cultivate a student network out of Conner Hall by organizing social and academic events for the benefit of AAEC grad students.
summary: Student-run social and academic club for Agricultural & Applied Economics graduate students at UGA.

name: Hispanic & Latino Law Students Association
description:
Hello, Hola, Olá, and Bonjou!

The Hispanic & Latino Law Students Association (HLLSA) is a student-run organization that aims to promote Hispanic and Latino students' academic and professional success.

Hispanic and Latino mean different things to many people. Thus, we encourage ALL that identify with our community to join…regardless of race, ethnicity, skin complexion, or fluency in languages other than English.

HLLSA is a diverse organization available to members of all backgrounds. If you are interested in connecting with other Hispanic and Latino law students or are interested in any area of law that strongly affects the Hispanic and Latino communities, please join!

Feel free to contact one of the student officers via: ugalawhlsa@gmail.com
summary: Although we aim to raise cultural awareness about issues affecting the Hispanic & Latino communities, HLLSA is a diverse organization available to members of all backgrounds. If you are interested in connecting with other Hispanic & Latino law students

name: CommunicAID+Nation
description: CommunicAID+Nation was founded as a non-profit organization in Atlanta, GA in 2012 as a means to provide resources and support for individuals with communication needs. By offering access to direct and affordable services, providing financial assistance, and by supplying assistive technology devices, CommunicAID Nation enables individuals with communication disorders to access the world around them.
summary: CommunicAID+Nation raises money and awareness for individuals with communication disorders. We work with local Speech-Language Pathologists to provide communication devices to those in need. We are open to members of all majors and interests. Go Dawgs!

name: Environmental Law Association
description: The Environmental Law Association (ELA) is an organization within the University of Georgia School of Law for JD, LLM, and MSL students who seek to learn about, develop, and advance efforts made in environmental law. Our signature event is the annual Red Clay Conference.
summary: For those interested in how the power of the legal system is used to protect the environment.

name: Sigma Alpha Iota, Iota Zeta Chapter
description:
Sigma Alpha Iota is an organization which promotes interaction among those who share a commitment to music. Members of SAI are active in all areas of campus music and campus life, working closely with faculty, administration, campus and community groups, music professionals and patrons. In addition to personal encouragement and support, members may receive scholarships, loans and awards in many areas and at all levels of music-related study. Sigma Alpha Iota has long been recognized as a leader in the field of music and provides a lifetime of fraternity contact.

    The Iota Zeta chapter consists of sisters with majors ranging from Music Education to Avian Biology. We hold recruitment events in the fall for anyone interested in seeing what SAI is all about. Learn more by checking out our website!
summary: SAI is an International Music Fraternity for Women. Members are active in all areas of campus music and campus life. We work closely with people both on and off campus to further our musical endeavors, career goals, and our community.

name: International Association of Reading Graduate Students
description: The purpose of IARGS is to support study and initiatives related to the teaching and learning of reading. The organization is comprised of graduate students within the Department of Language and Literacy Education.
summary: IARGS supports study and initiatives related to the teaching and learning of reading. The organization is comprised of graduate students within LLED (COE), specifically those in the Literacies and Children's Literature focus area.

name: Tau Sigma National Honor Society
description:
Tau Sigma National Honor Society at the University of Georgia recognizes the academic achievement of the most outstanding transfers. Tau Sigma members (among the most accomplished transfer students on campus) are perhaps the greatest resource available to serve the current and future transfers.

Objectives:

  • address the current lack of recognition of outstanding academic achievement of transfer students
  • provide motivation for the academic excellence of all incoming transfer students
  • enhance the reputation of all transfer students
  • provide a common bond among transfer students
  • promote the involvement of transfer students
  • form a group of students who can help the university address and meet the needs of incoming transfer students

Membership Criteria:

  • You must transfer to a four year institution of higher learning from another academic institution with at least one full years’ academic credits satisfied at the prior institution(s).
  • You must be registered in a course of study leading to a bachelor's degree for an amount of work equal to an average full load.
  • Membership in the Organization shall be based upon superior academic achievement by students during their first term at the University of Georgia (only credit hours that count toward a bachelor's degree can be used to determine Tau Sigma eligibility).
  • The minimum academic average required for membership is either a 3.5 on a 4.0 scale or an academic average that places the prospective candidate in the top 20% of all incoming transfer students during the term in question. 
  • Academic eligibility shall be obtained on the grades of one full semester. Eligibility is limited to the first term of enrollment at the institution to which the student transfers. Summer school sessions equivalent to a full curricular period may be used to meet the academic requirement.
summary: Tau Sigma National Honor Society at the University of Georgia is an academic honor society designed specifically to recognize and promote the academic excellence and involvement of transfer students.

name: Life on Life Campus Ministries
description:
We are an organization that is focused on making disciples here at UGA and training others to become Laborers and disciple makers in the Kingdom as well. Our vision is to Know Christ and to make him known to others.  We seek to grow and maintain a community of like minded but diverse individuals around this focus and vision.

Join us for Bible study on Thursday nights at 6:00 in MLC room 277, or come talk to us on Wednesday at our table outside of the MLC from 10:00 to 3:00!
summary: We are an organization that is focused on making disciples here at UGA and training others to become Laborers and disciple makers in the Kingdom as well. Our vision is to Know Christ and to make him known to others.

name: Horticulture Club @ UGA, The
description:
Like plants? So do we! We are the Horticulture Club at UGA! We learn about all things horticulture to grow and sell plants each semester while connecting our members with the ins and outs of the green industry. Meetings are held biweekly on Tuesdays in Miller Plant Sciences room 1205 at 7PM. Come and hang out!

Please contact our secretary Allison at allison.waldrop@uga.edu for more information on how to join our club. We do not charge dues and we welcome plant lovers from all majors!
summary: We are the Horticulture Club at UGA! We learn about all things horticulture to grow and sell plants each semester while connecting our members with the ins and outs of the green industry! Meetings are on Tuesdays at 7pm.

name: VAW Global Health Alliances
description:
VAW Global Health Alliances (VAW Global) is a global health and development organization committed to cultivating sustainable and equitable partnerships with local communities and professionals through support and capacity-building for essential and accessible health services. Our focus is to work hand-in-hand with local initiatives and leaders to provide communities with improved access to medical, dental, and veterinary treatment. We also work to provide medication, nutritional security, clean water, and health education to these communities. We have a Medical, Dental, Veterinary, and Global Health Team that all travel to various countries each year. 

VAW Website
summary: VAW Global Health Alliances (VAW Global) is a global health and development organization committed to cultivating sustainable and equitable partnerships with local communities and professionals.

name: MathCounts Outreach at UGA
description:
MathCounts is a student-run organization sponsored by the UGA Honors Program that provides a unique opportunity for UGA students to give back to the Athens community by helping elementary, middle, and high school students strengthen their math skills through math games and challenges.

As a part of the organization, volunteers devote approximately one hour per week teaching students new math concepts, practicing competition-style problems, and strengthening students' problem areas.

Additionally, each Spring semester, MathCounts volunteers host a friendly tournament for our MathCounts students. To volunteer with MathCounts, only basic knowledge of middle school geometry, algebra, and other mathematical concepts is required.
summary: MathCounts provides the opportunity for UGA students to give back to the Athens community by helping middle school students strengthen their math skills and serving as role models. Volunteers spend approximately 1hr/week inspiring Athens-area students!

name: Blue Key Honor Society
description:
Blue Key National Honor Society was founded in October of 1924 at the University of Florida.  The second chapter in the national organization was established at The University of Georgia in 1926, and for nearly nine decades, membership in this organization has been considered one of the highest honors on campus.

Blue Key recognizes second, third, and fourth-year undergraduate students, as well as graduate and professional students of outstanding character and ability who have achieved distinction for scholarship and recognition for service and leadership.

An important tradition of The University of Georgia Chapter is the Blue Key Alumni Banquet which was designed to bring together business and government leaders from across the state to participate in a dinner program hosted by the student Blue Key members. The Banquet also has served as an occasion to present an annual Blue Key Award from the student chapter to distinguished individuals who have made significant contributions to The University of Georgia, the state and the nation. Over one hundred such awards have been given since the Chapter was founded.

The concept for the first Blue Key Banquet, held in 1963, is credited to John Tucker Dorsey II.  An accomplished student leader at the University, Tucker Dorsey died in an automobile accident in December of 1965, returning home for the holidays.  In June of 1966, he was awarded the A.B. degree from The University of Georgia in absentia.

In Tucker’s memory, the UGA Blue Key Chapter named the Awards Banquet for him and his friends initiated the Tucker Dorsey Memorial Scholarships. 

Tucker Dorsey’s father, the late Jasper N. Dorsey, was a devoted University of Georgia alumnus who maintained a special interest in Blue Key throughout his long association with campus activities.  Jasper Dorsey served as both national president of the Alumni Society (1967-1969) and as chairman of the UGA Foundation Board of Trustees (1977-1979).  He helped found the Young Alumni Council, and in 1970, Dorsey received the Alumni Merit Award. In 1967, he received the Blue Key Award.
summary: Blue Key recognizes junior and senior men and women of outstanding character and ability who have achieved distinction for scholarship and recognition for service and leadership.

name: Robotics Club
description: Robotics Club is a group of motivated college students aimed at robotic development, research and competition. As a group, we strive to educate and motivate college students, from any academic background, with fun and exciting projects. Our group is not limited to engineering students and accepts anyone looking to get involved in the wide world of robotics. Participation in this group is a great avenue to gain experience in embedded development, electrical engineering, algorithmic development, robotics research, business development in the robotics industry and much more. By creating such an environment we hope to also improve interdisciplinary relationships, teamwork and spread awareness of the University of Georgia’s ability to educate students in the field of robotics.
summary: We are a group of motivated college students aimed at robotic development, research and competition. Our organization expands classroom learning through hands-on application and offers resources for STEM projects and interests.

name: UGArden Club
description:
The mission of UGArden Club is to build a community of students centered on a sustainable food system. UGArden Club meets weekly to help support the goals of UGArden, which are: 1) to teach students to grow food using organic practices through experiential learning; 2) to share healthy, sustainably grown food with members of the local community who are facing food insecurity; 3) to provide an opportunity for engagement, service and experimentation; and 4) to foster an entrepreneurial spirit.

We meet at 4:30pm - 6pm every Wednesday at 2500 S Milledge Ave.

Club members assist the farm through weeding, harvesting, planting, and other hands-on activities. In addition to working in the fields, UGArden Club members deliver weekly produce deliveries to the UGA Food Pantry to combat student food insecurity. Each spring we host an annual Spring Festival in which we bring together students and community members to celebrate sustainable agriculture and clean eating through live music, local food, and fun activities. 

Visit our Instagram @ugardenclub and join our GroupMe here

Check out our video for some cool shots of the farm!: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Et4wKWgixnZ-SyO0JIP7bl10qP9vc5yS/view
summary: The mission of UGArden Club is to build a community of students centered on a sustainable food system. The club introduces students to UGArden, a student-run, organic farm, and provides hands-on instruction about gardening and sustainable farming.

name: International Law Society
description:
The International Law Society is our students’ chapter of the International Law Students Association, a Washington, D.C.-based organization that for decades has provided students with opportunities to study, research, and network in the international law arena, through conferences, publications, and administration of the Philip C. Jessup International Law Moot Court Competition. At the University of Georgia School of Law, ILS hosts events and projects designed to stimulate and advance understanding of international, comparative, foreign, and transnational law and institutions. The International Law Society often partners with the law school’s Dean Rusk International Law Center.

*Open only to current UGA School Of Law students*
summary: ILSA is a student organization at The University of Georgia School of Law. It is comprised of law students with a desire to learn and work in the international legal community.

name: Ballroom Performance Group at UGA
description:
The Ballroom Performance Group (BPG) is a pre-professional student dance company at the University of Georgia, promoting the appreciation of ballroom dance through performances on campus and around the community. The company was founded in 1991 by Mark Wheeler and is comprised of both undergraduate and graduate students from a wide range of majors and departments. 

BPG hosts its annual performance, "Ballroom Magic," the second semester of each school year. The show consists of about 20 to 25 choreographed pieces in smooth and Latin dancing, along with guest performances.

Dancing with BPG develops a serious ballroom dancer's technique and style, which enables them to perform or even compete professionally.

BPG has auditions twice yearly at the end of the spring and the fall semesters. We also have Friday Night Dance Parties twice a month that start with a free beginner ballroom lesson. For more information, like us on Facebook and Instagram (UGA Ballroom Performance Group) or check our website. You can even watch some of our student-choreographed dances on our YouTube channel

Come dance with us!
summary: The Ballroom Performance Group (BPG) is a pre-professional student dance company at the University of Georgia, promoting the appreciation of ballroom dance through performances and dance lessons on campus and around the community.

name: DiGamma Kappa
description: The DiGamma Kappa Professional Broadcast Society was established at the University of Georgia in 1939. The purpose of DGK is to provide members with networking opportunities, activities, and social functions within the broadcasting industry; to provide an atmosphere for students to learn about jobs and internships in their future industry; and to offer members hands-on experience with writing, directing, producing, shooting, and editing while maintaining a professional competence.
summary: DGK provides members with networking opportunities, activities, and social functions within the broadcasting industry as well as an atmosphere to learn about future jobs and internships in the broadcasting realm, all while gaining hands-on experience.

name: Students Supporting Israel at UGA
description: Students Supporting Israel (SSI) provides UGA with a community for Zionists, or anyone looking to be informed on the State of Israel. In this chapter, we will be discussing tough questions, looking at facts, and taking part in fun activities to show our support for Israel. SSI used to exist here at UGA a few years ago bust died out. It is important now more than ever to re-establish this Zionist chapter. AM YISRAEL CHAI
summary: SSI at UGA is a community of well informed, Israel-supporting students. We will provide educational and engaging activities while spreading awareness and exploring tough questions.

name: Men's Club Volleyball
description:
Every Fall and Spring semester, we hold tryouts to form 2 student-run competitive teams. These teams practice every Tuesday and Thursday from 7-9pm. Each semester, we charge dues in order to cover tournament, travel, equipment, and other necessary costs. These dues vary based on our schedule. We typically travel to 1-3 tournaments in the Fall and 4-6 in the Spring, all in preparation for Nationals. 

If you have any further questions, please see the contact information section.
summary: Men's Club Volleyball is a competitive, traveling volleyball team that competes in several tournaments during the Fall and Spring semesters, all in preparation for the NCVF Championships held every spring.

name: Federalist Society
description:
The Federalist Society is a group of conservatives and libertarians interested in the current state of the legal order.  It is founded on the principles that the state exists to preserve freedom, that the separation of governmental powers is central to our Constitution, and that it is emphatically the province and duty of the judiciary to say what the law is, not what it should be. The Society seeks both to promote an awareness of these principles and to further their application through its activities.

Membership for the Georgia Student Chapter of the Federalist Society is open to all UGA Law JD, LLM, and MSL students.
summary: National organization for collaboration of conservatives and libertarians to address issues relevant to the rule of law in America and abroad.

name: Gamma Iota Sigma
description:
Please refer to http://www.terry.uga.edu/academics/offices/risk-management-insurance/gis for full organizational information and application into GIS. 

The involvement network does not serve as official membership application and you should go through the above link. 

The purpose of this fraternity is to promote, encourage, and sustain student interest in insurance, risk management, and actuarial science. The fraternity strives to encourage high moral and scholastic attainments of its members; and to facilitate interaction of educational institutions and industry through networking and by fostering community service and scholarship.

Students applying to GIS must be Risk Management and Insurance majors. We have opportunities for extensive contact and interaction with industry representatives, through meetings and special conferences sponsored by the fraternity, and through the services offered through the Grand Chapter.
summary: Gamma Iota Sigma is the international Risk Management, Insurance, and Actuarial Science fraternity.

name: Club Gymnastics at UGA
description: We are UGA's only co-ed gymnastics team! All are welcome, and prior experience isn't necessary! We compete with the National Association of Intercollegiate Gymnastics Clubs (NAIGC), typically 5 meets per year, including one national championship, although these are all optional. We encourage anyone interested in gymnastics to come to try it out, and we'll do our best to help you get what you want out of the experience! We practice at Leading Edge Gymnastics (868 Commerce Ct, Winder, GA 30680) on Mondays and Thursdays, starting in the fall. Feel free to text the current president (Natalie Hayden) anytime with any questions you may have!
summary: We are UGA's only competitive co-ed gymnastics team! We are open to all levels (including first-timers!) and compete at a regional and national level.

name: Grace Athens Students
description:
Grace Athens Students exists to reach out to the students looking for a church home. Modeled after the early church, Grace Athens Students seeks to partner with the Kingdom and empower the next generation through discipleship. We seek to be a center and sender of leaders. Additionally, we gather every Sunday at North Oconee High School.

Interested in joining the student group? Please email us at Athens@gfc.tv
summary: As an extension of Grace Athens Church, Grace Athens Students exists as a medium for UGA students to get involved with the family of believers. We seek to disciple and advance the Kingdom in Athens and the neighborhoods, nations, and next generation.

name: National Society of Leadership and Success
description:
The NSLS is an organization that helps people discover and achieve their goals. They offer life-changing advice from the nation's leading presenters and a community where like-minded, success-oriented individuals come together and help one another succeed. The NSLS also serves as a powerful force of good in the greater community by encouraging and organizing action to better the world.

The guiding question we established to shape all the important decisions the organization makes is: "Are we helping the greatest number of people in the greatest way?" When asked how he might sum up the key thing that the Society does, founder Gary Tuerack shared, "We are dream supporters – we build leaders, support people in achieving their dreams, and better the world in the process. We get people to ask the all-important question, 'What would you do if you knew you couldn't fail?' and then help them to achieve those goals.
summary: We Build Leaders Who Make a Better World

name: Labor and Employment Law Association
description: Provides a forum to discuss issues related to the area of labor and employment law and network among students and practitioners interested in this practice area. Law students from UGA law will hear alumni speakers currently working in labor and employment law. The group will allow questions and discussion about different career paths within this sector of the law.
summary: Provides a forum to discuss issues related to the area of labor and employment law and network among students and practitioners interested in this practice area

name: Repertory Singers
description: The Repertory Singers is a mixed chamber choir designed to offer laboratory rehearsal and performance experience for graduate-student conductors and upper-level music education students. The ensemble is designed to appeal to students (music-majors and non-music-majors) who wish to be part of an ongoing conducting masterclass environment as well as those wanting to build knowledge and skill in the areas of sight-reading, conducting gesture, rehearsal techniques, and choral repertoire. This class is a recommended small-ensemble option for choral music education majors and others wishing to learn more about choral conducting. While concert performance is not the primary objective of Repertory Singers, 3 class conducting recitals will take place each semester. Permission of instructor is required for enrollment.
summary: The Repertory Singers is a mixed chamber choir designed for students who wish to be part of an ongoing conducting masterclass environment, as well as those wanting to build knowledge and skill in other areas of choral performance.

name: Law Democrats
description: The purpose of this organization shall be to allow Democratic Law Students to organize and meet one another, and get involved in the Democratic Party in the community, in Georgia, and nationally. We also hope to stimulate a more active interest in governmental affairs, to recruit new members for the Democratic Party, and to help acquaint voters with Democratic issues and the candidates.
summary: The purpose of this organization shall be to allow Democratic Law Students to organize and meet one another, and get involved in the Democratic Party in the community, in Georgia, and nationally.

name: Health Law Society
description: The Health Law Society (HLS) is a student-run organization with the mission of connecting interested students with each other and with professionals in the health law field; advising interested students about all health law-related opportunities available to them; and opening a dialogue on campus about current issues in health law.
summary: HLS offers members the opportunity to stay up to date on current issues in health law, to engage with other students interested in a career in health law, and to connect with practicing health law professionals.

name: Black Law Students Association
description:
The purpose of The Davenport-Benham Chapter of the Black Law Students Association at the University of Georgia School of Law is to utilize the collective resources available to the chapter, including members, alumni and friends for the creation and maintenance of a law school environment that is conducive to Black students' effective study of the law by:

  • Articulating and addressing the academic, financial, political, social and professional needs of its membership;
  • Fostering and encouraging professional competence;
  • Encouraging the increase in the number of Black students and addressing problems of attrition (should they exist);
  • Instilling in members a greater awareness and commitment to the needs of the Black community;
  • Encouraging the increase in the number of Black faculty in the law school;
  • Promoting the interaction between the membership and other organizations within the law school and the University specifically, and the legal community and Black community generally; and
  • Doing all things necessary and appropriate to accomplish these purposes.

The content and opinions expressed on this Web page do not necessarily reflect the views of, nor are they endorsed by, the University of Georgia or the University System of Georgia.
summary: The purpose of BLSA is to utilize the collective resources available to the chapter, including members, alumni and friends for the creation and maintenance of a law school environment that is conducive to Black students' effective study of the law.

name: Student Bar Association
description: The Student Bar Association (“SBA”) is the student government body at the University of Georgia School of Law.  The officers are here to ensure that your law school experience is as memorable and enjoyable as possible!  In addition to being the voice of the students, we plan events throughout the year so that we can all take time away from reading cases to build a community and professional relationships that will last a lifetime. Events range from an annual golf tournament and Barristers Ball to community service days and food drives. Student Bar Association members receive discounted prices and advanced registration to our numerous events.
summary: The Student Bar Association (“SBA”) is the student government body at the University of Georgia School of Law.

name: Jewish Law Students Association
description: The Jewish Law Student Association (JLSA) at the University of Georgia School of Law is an organization designed for Jewish students, but open to all students regardless of their religion, nationality, or race. JLSA provides many opportunities to its members for community building, education and networking. During the school year, JLSA hosts numerous social events including happy hours, and student mixers with other Jewish graduate students from around the state of Georgia. JLSA also hosts Jewish holiday celebrations, lectures and seminars, and opportunities to meet and mingle with Jewish faculty.
summary: The Jewish Law Students Association (JLSA) is a cultural, social, educational, and religious organization that reflects the varied interests of the Jewish student community of the University of Georgia School of Law.

name: Equal Justice Foundation
description: Equal Justice Foundation is a student-run organization devoted to the promotion of public interest law. EJF works to provide stipends for UGA law students who have chosen to work in unpaid, public interest positions over the summer.
summary: Equal Justice Foundation is a student-run organization devoted to the promotion of public interest law. EJF works to provide stipends for University of Georgia School of Law students who have chosen to work in unpaid, public interest summer positions.

name: Asian Law Students Association
description:
1.     Promote the solidarity and friendship among Asian law students and other student groups at UGA Law

2.     Promote legal advancement opportunities via professional networking and notification of job opportunities

3.     Promote cultural awareness of Asian students and facilitate the cultural exchange between students of different cultural backgrounds within the law school and beyond, encouraging all who are interested to join.

4.     Strengthen the Asian legal community within UGA Law and the greater Georgia community
summary: This organization is designed to create a strong Asian community at the law school and to increase awareness of issues Asians often face.

name: Beta Upsilon Chi - Brothers Under Christ | Pi Chapter
description: Beta Upsilon Chi at the University of Georgia was formed in the spring of 2005. It was the vision of the founding fathers that BYX would provide an alternative to the regular Greek life, where BYX could mirror the social aspects of a fraternity, all the while encouraging growth in Jesus Christ. It is the heart of the founding fathers that BYX would serve to forcefully advance the kingdom of God on the campus of the University of Georgia, and raise up a generation of men made in God’s very own image.
summary: Beta Upsilon Chi, a National Christian Fraternity, exists for the purpose of establishing brotherhood and unity among college men based on the common bond of Jesus Christ.

name: Hillel UGA
description:
The purpose of Hillel at UGA is to provide a Jewish community for students on campus. We provide educational opportunities for all students to learn about Jewish life and Jewish culture, facilitating constructive dialogue about a variety of topics. 

Hillel at UGA is for helping students to discover what it means to be Jewish at UGA and after their time here. It is for encouraging them to pursue tzedek (social justice) and tikkun olam (repairing the world), and to support Israel and the global Jewish community. Lastly, it is for encouraging Jewish learning through whatever means is meaningful for them.
summary: The purpose of Hillel at UGA is to foster enthusiasm for and educational opportunities about Jewish life and culture on campus.

name: Orthodox Christian Campus Ministries
description:
Our mission statement stems from four pillars of the Orthodox Christian lifestyle: orthodoxia, orthopraxia, orthokoinonia, and orthokrisia. These are the correct belief (faith), correct acts/practice, correct community, and correct judgment and values.

 

The vision of OCCM is to:

  1. Inspire and instill in students the commitment to live an Orthodox Christian way of life daily.

  2. Be an integral part of students’ spiritual formation by nurturing them spiritually, scholastically, socially, and even emotionally.

  3. Assist students who desire to start an Orthodox Christian campus ministry organization with the proper steps and support to achieve that interest successfully.

  4. Support campus ministry organizations to ensure the organic growth and sustainability of the group in the Orthodox Faith.

  5. Provide existing campus organizations with a forum to share ideas, programs, and events so as to better serve our brothers and sisters.
summary: OCCM anchors college students to the values and ancient faith of the Orthodox Christian Church through fellowship, prayer, service to others, and study of the faith.

name: Phi Alpha Theta, Epsilon Pi Chapter
description:
Phi Alpha Theta is an American honor society for undergraduate and graduate students and professors of history. The society has over 350,000 members, with about 9,500 new members joining each year through 860 local chapters

Phi Alpha Theta was established on March 17, 1921 at the University of Arkansas by Professor Nels Cleven. Cleven had become convinced in his time at the university that a fraternity of scholars (which would accept men or women) was important for the study of history. He invited students to a meeting to form the society (then called the “University Historical Society”) on March 14, and the society was officially recognized on the 17th. In April, the decision was made for the society to be known by the Greek letters Phi Alpha Theta. - See more at: http://phialphatheta.org/about#sthash.aWzeCNca.dpuf
summary: Phi Alpha Theta is a professional society whose mission is to promote the study of history and build a community for history lovers of all stripes.

name: Rouge Magazine
description:
Previously known as “The Little Red Book” for 10 years, Rouge Magazine is a fully student-run organization.

From backend production, social media management and event planning, to full-print publication, every element of our magazine represents a labor of love of the students at the University of Georgia. We boldly wear our hearts for fashion on our sleeves and we are so thankful to be a part of something rooted so deeply in our passions. We hope that this publication can be something that represents more than well-styled clothes on models, but rather a chance for people to be united under one roof, all collectively working towards the same goal: to show the world that fashion is more than clothes.

Fashion is our identity, our self-expression, and our loudest voice.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8ZKNLTMWwqU
summary: Rouge Magazine is the first fashion magazine on the University of Georgia's campus.

name: Outdoor Adventure Club
description: Please join our groupme for all information and updates!
summary: This is a student organization dedicated to planning and participating in affordable outdoor activities. This includes camping, rock climbing, canoeing, hiking, caving, scuba diving, skiing/snowboarding and just about any other outdoor activity.

name: National Association of Black Journalists
description: We are committed to informing the campus, the local community and the world about the importance of journalism. Here you can find out more about our organization, stay up-to-date on upcoming events, and communicate with our staff. The National Association of Black Journalists (NABJ) is an organization of journalists, students and media-related professionals that provide quality programs and services for and and on behalf of black journalists worldwide.
summary: NABJ strives to bring awareness to issues concerning minority populations in the media industry. Our mission is to nurture relationships and interests in journalism and other media related fields while strengthening ties among black journalists on campus

name: Table, The
description: The Table is an inclusive Christian campus ministry at UGA which strives to be community of unqualified belonging, spiritual growth, and compassionate service
summary: The Table is an inclusive Christian campus ministry at UGA which strives to be community of unqualified belonging, spiritual growth, and compassionate service

name: H.O. Lund Entomology Club
description: The H.O. Lund Club is a service- and social-oriented student organization in the Department of Entomology, composed of anyone interested in the science of entomology. The Lund Club is dedicated to informing members of the latest advancements in the fields of entomology, providing a venue for professional and social interaction, and representing student members in departmental, collegiate, and University councils and committees.  However, the primary service provided by the Lund Club is educational outreach programs to local schools and UGA events in Athens-Clarke and the surrounding counties.  In addition to their outreach efforts, members of the Lund Club are involved in selecting and organizing the H.O. Lund Seminar (in conjunction with Lund Week), the Brady Seminar, the Schmidt seminar, and social functions.
summary: The H.O. Lund Club is a service- and social-oriented student organization  in the Department of Entomology, composed of anyone interested in the science of entomology.

name: Fencing Club
description:
The Fencing Club at UGA is comprised of experienced fencers and beginners alike. Some took up the sport many years ago, while others discovered fencing upon arriving at UGA. We strive to train new fencers and increase the skill of experienced fencers. Practices are structured with stretching and warming up, exercise, drilling and teaching, and bouting. Members who choose to be competitive regularly travel together to local and out of state tournaments. New fencers are encouraged to try competition whenever they feel comfortable doing so. Practice for experienced fencers will begin when classes start- those who are new to the sport will be invited to an introductory event later.

A request to join via the involvement network is not the same thing as joining the team - Competitive Sports has a separate process. Please fill out an interest survey if you want to hear more about the club.
summary: The Fencing Club at UGA provides a welcoming environment for anyone who wants to fence. We strive for a club that fosters growth and opportunity for members, both competitively and recreationally. No experience is required!

name: Theta Tau
description: Theta Tau is open to all Engineering and Computer Science majors. We focus on Professional Development, Brotherhood, and Service.
summary: A Professional Co-Ed Engineering Fraternity

name: Association for Computing Machinery
description:
ACM, the world’s largest educational and scientific computing society, delivers resources that advance computing as a science and a profession. ACM provides students interested in computer science the opportunity to network with potential employers, participate in technical workshops, and be involved outside of class. 

Our mission is to help advance computer science as a profession, major, and community through local events and meetings.
summary: Our mission is to help advance computer science as a profession, major, and community through local events and meetings.

name: Elections Committee
description:
The Elections Committee shall plan, monitor, manage, and define the annual elections procedures of the Student Government Association (SGA) in order to maintain an effective and meaningful process as prescribed by the Constitution.  The Elections Code shall be the rule upon which the Elections Committee operates.  In addition, the Elections Committee helps support the Graduate Student Association's elections process.

Committee Composition

  1. Shall be chaired by the Attorney General who shall vote only in the case of a tie.
  2. Shall be comprised of two non-active members of SGA appointed by the President and approved by a majority vote of the Senate, two active members of SGA appointed by the President and approved by a majority vote of the Senate, and the President of the Senate.
  3. The SGA Graduate Advisor and the SGA Faculty Advisor shall serve as non-voting members. 
  4. The majority of students on this committee must be graduating seniors.
  5. Should the President of the Senate not be able to impartially serve on the elections committee, he/she must appoint a Senator to serve on the committee.
  6. Quorum shall consist of four of the five voting members and the Attorney General.
summary: The Elections Committee has oversight of the Student Government Association's elections process.

name: Society of Aspiring Plant Pathologists
description:
• The purpose of the Society of Aspiring Pathologists shall be to:
  • (I) foster relations among undergraduate and graduate students, post-doctoral associates, faculty, and staff in the Department of Plant Pathology at the University of Georgia;
  • (II) improve the quality of programs within our department and the campus at large;
  • (III) advance the quality of student teaching and professional development within the Department of Plant Pathology; 
  • (IV) inform the public of the purpose and importance of the field of plant pathology in modern society.
summary: We endeavor to foster relations and engagement within the Department of Plant Pathology to improve program quality, enhance member satisfaction and career preparedness, and increase public awareness of the importance of plant pathology in modern society.

name: Mind Blowers
description:
Mindblowers Program is an initiative under the Terry College of Business that strives to leave a lasting impact on the youth of the community. Through our partnerships with the Boys & Girls Clubs of Athens, we aim to help middle school students develop financial literacy and quantitative skills, as well as career goals, showing them what it is like to be college students and illustrate how attainable it can be for them.

The curriculum and academic nature of the program is supplemented with the following:

  • Hands-on Activities
  • Close Interactions
  • Mentorship
  • Stimulating Environment 

Instructional Learning Coaches work directly with the kids at the Boys and Girls Club to guide them through the learning sessions.

Instructional Learning Coaches are not merely volunteers, but they are the primary agents that provide one on one instruction and facilitate the learning process.
summary: Mind Blowers Program is an initiative partnered with the Boys & Girls Clubs of Athens, that aims to help middle school students develop financial literacy and quantitative skills, as well as career goals and professional development.

name: Brazilian Student Association
description: The Brazilian Student Association promotes Brazilian culture and the Portuguese language at the University of Georgia, offering extracurricular opportunities for all students interested in learning more about Brazil.
summary: The Brazilian Student Association promotes Brazilian culture and the Portuguese language at the University of Georgia, offering extracurricular opportunities for all students interested in learning more about Brazil.

name: Bachelor of Social Work Club
description:
The Bachelor of Social Work Club at the University of Georgia brings like-minded individuals together to educate and provide mentorships for all those interested in Social Work. The goal of the organization is to bring unity to Social Work education at UGA, emphasize the importance of Social Work in society, and promote the advancement of the next generation of helping professionals.

 

The organization is founded on the Council on Social Work Education (CSWE) accreditation principles and the National Association of Social Workers Code of Ethics. The execution of these principals shall be done through the four tenants of our organization:

  1. Personal and Professional Development
  2. Advocacy and Community Service
  3. Anti-Racism, Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion
  4. Interdisciplinary Education
summary: The Bachelor of Social Work Club at the UGA brings like-minded individuals together to educate, provide mentorships for, and promote the advancement of the next generation of helping professionals. We are open to all that are interested in social work.

name: Red & Black Publishing, The
description:
The Red & Black is an independent, nonprofit student news organization. Since 1893, The Red & Black has served the University of Georgia and Athens with a twofold mission: providing hands-on training for the next generation of news professionals and serving our audience with accurate and thoughtful news and information.

We receive no funding from the university and our operations are supported through advertising sales and donations. Our weekly print editions and daily online coverage are free to all. 

As the largest college newspaper in Georgia, we aim to cover all facets of the UGA and Athens communities as completely as possible. Our award-winning newsroom reports on news, sports, arts, culture and more. Outside of journalism, students interested in anything from graphic design to social media management can find a place in the organization. 

To join, please fill out this form.
summary: Since 1893, The Red & Black has served UGA and Athens with a twofold mission: training the next generation of news professionals and providing our audience with accurate and thoughtful news and information.

name: National Society of Black Engineers
description:
Our mission is, "to increase the number of culturally responsible Black engineers who excel academically, succeed professionally, and positively impact the community." We welcome you to help us fulfill this mission which reaches to diverse cultures, all majors related to STEM (science, technology, engineering, and math) fields, and undergraduates, graduates, and professionals in the UGA and Athens community. Membership in this organization is extended to all students. This includes our graduate student members.

 

Specifically, to promote inclusivity and encourage active engagement among our graduate student members, we have established a dedicated subchapter that is managed and led by graduate students themselves, under the leadership of Taiwo Feyijimi, our Graduate President.

 

Graduate Subchapter

NSBE (STEM) UGA: National Society of Black Engineers (STEM) at the University of Georgia (UGA)

 

GPE: Graduate and Professional Executives

 
NOTE: NSBE (STEM) GPE and NSBE GPE will be used interchangeably throughout all of our publications. They both refer to the same idea.
 
The NSBE (STEM) UGA GPE subchapter is dedicated to advancing the academic and professional growth of Black graduate students and professionals in STEM. Membership provides access to specialized resources tailored to members' unique needs, including participation in academic and industry conferences, research publication support, professional development funding and supporting our undergraduate student members in their research skills development via workshops and tailored sessions. NSBE UGA GPE fosters leadership and innovation by connecting members with industry professionals and academic experts, helping them achieve academic and career success. Members gain exclusive access to workshops on emerging technologies, such as Generative AI, and opportunities to apply their skills through consulting projects, which can lead to internships or full-time roles. Additionally, members can present their research at prestigious conferences, enhancing their visibility and expanding their professional networks. Joining NSBE UGA GPE offers a clear path to career advancement through hands-on experience, networking, and community engagement in STEM.
summary: The National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE) at the University of Georgia strives to encourage STEM exploration in the community, increase retention in these fields, and equip members to excel professionally. NSBE membership is extended to all students

name: Parks, Recreation, and Tourism Management Society
description:
The PRTM Society is a student club within the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources, dedicated to the understanding and implementation of sustainable recreation and tourism in natural resources through education, outreach, and fellowship.

The PRTM Society holds official meetings once monthly. We typically invite speakers from relevant organizations within the community to attend and speak with members on issues relevant to work in natural resources, such as employment and volunteer opportunities, diversity in outdoor recreation, professionalism within our field, and other topics of interest. If speakers are unable to visit Warnell, some meetings may become field trips (carpooling available.)

We plan one trip for the Fall semester, and one for the Spring semester. These are often camping trips, but may also consist of hiking, tubing, swimming, caving, visiting and exploring parks, and other suggestions. 

We host many on-campus fundraisers such as bake sales and chili sales in order to raise funds for the PRTM Society. These funds help provide financial support for our students to experience group trips, attend events, and other club functions. 

This year we hope to diversify our events and membership, as well as increase our frequency of volunteer and outreach opportunities. We also hope to increase our fundraising in order to expand our ability to experience the things our field has to offer such as trips, conferences, and more. We are open to students of all backgrounds and fields of study, and hope to see you soon!
summary: The PRTM Society is a student club within the Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources, dedicated to the understanding and implementation of sustainable recreation and tourism in natural resources through education, outreach, and fellowship

name: Bass Anglers Team
description: The purpose of Bass Anglers at UGA is to create activities and events that help current and future members grow their knowledge and skill in the sport of bass fishing. We strive to work together to keep waters clean, protect anglers’ rights, and support land and water conservation nationwide through a respectful and sustainable approach to our activities. Finally, we are committed to providing our members with opportunities to compete in prestigious tournaments around the world, supported by financial aid, school backing, and the strength of team companionship.
summary: The mission of the UGA Bass Anglers is to provide a way for its members to fulfill their passion of fishing competitively at the collegiate level in a way that enhances their knowledge and understanding of the sport of bass fishing.

name: Agricultural Communicators of Tomorrow
description:
What can ACT do for you?

Through ACT, you will meet students with similar career interests and help you develop a broad network of professional contacts who are helpful in guiding you in your career. You'll also have the opportunity to serve in leadership roles and participate in professional, social and service activities. As an affiliate of the national ACT, all members receive a copy of the National ACTion Newsletter.

The goals of the ACT chapter at UGA are

  • To stimulate interest in the profession of agricultural communications;
  • To promote the interchange of ideas among students, faculty members and professional agricultural communicators for their mutual benefit;
  • To provide an opportunity for the development of personality and leadership qualities which will enable members to effectively participate in community organizations;
  • To broaden knowledge about agricultural communications; and
  • To provide a means of contact between students and members of professional agricultural communications organizations.

Membership

Active membership in ACT at the University of Georgia is open to all regularly enrolled undergraduate students who express an interest in agricultural communication. Membership in the organization is not limited to majors in agricultural journalism or communication. Any student who is professionally interested in some form of communication with an interest in agriculture can join. Activities ACT members have the opportunity to participate in numerous activities during the year. These activities include:

  • Guest speakers
  • Alumni, industry leaders, professional mentors
  • Service projects
  • Social events
  • Fund-raisers
  • Professional development
summary: Agricultural Communicators of Tomorrow is the only student organization of its kind within the agricultural communications field. It is designed for college students who have a professional interest in the field of communications.

name: Unite for Reproductive & Gender Equity (URGE)
description:
URGE is driven by young leaders. We build infrastructure through campus chapters and Community Activist Networks, where we invite individuals to discover their own power and transform it into action. Together, URGE members educate their communities and advocate for local, state, and national policies in solidarity with other justice focused groups.

Together, URGE members educate their communities and advocate for local, state, and national policies in solidarity with other justice focused groups. Hone your leadership and communication skills while creating real change in your community!
summary: Welcome to the URGE chapter at UGA! We believe that reproductive justice will only be achieved once each of us recognizes the power of our own experience and joins up with others in solidarity to create a different future. We are here to support you.

name: National Community Pharmacists Association
description: Our mission is to encourage, foster and recognize an interest in community pharmacy ownership and entrepreneurship among the future leaders of their profession.
summary: NCPA's student chapter of UGA is the representative group for National Community Pharmacists Association. NCPA is "the voice of the community pharmacist."

name: Student Pharmacist Diabetes Organization
description:
The Student Pharmacist Diabetes Organization (SPDO) is dedicated to empowering pharmacy students to become confident, compassionate, and forward-thinking leaders in diabetes care. We create a collaborative environment where students can connect with peers, faculty, and healthcare professionals who share a passion for improving the lives of patients living with diabetes. Through interactive events and engagement opportunities, members gain experience that goes beyond the classroom.

Whether students are newly exploring their interest in diabetes care or looking to deepen their knowledge, SPDO provides the tools, mentorship, and support to help them grow professionally and personally. SPDO's goal is to inspire students to think creatively, work collaboratively, and make a meaningful impact in patient care from the very beginning of their pharmacy journey.
summary: SPDO empowers pharmacy students to become confident leaders in diabetes care through collaboration, hands-on experience, and clinical insights that would help them make an impact on patients' health.

name: Georgia Political Review
description: Georgia Political Review publishes original content from University of Georgia undergraduates in the fields of political science, international affairs, history, and economics. Published each semester since 2011 in print and online, the GPR is the only publication of its kind in the state university system, and is a nationwide model for articulate, timely commentary on politics and foreign affairs.
summary: Georgia Political Review is a student-run political magazine.

name: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Incorporated
description:
Lambda Theta Alpha was founded at Kean University in Union, New Jersey in December 1975. It was the first sorority to ever cater to the needs of the Latina population in the United States. Our mission is to create a support group for Latinas in higher education and unite the Latino community on campus.

LTA at UGA: On April 10, 2005 nine young women founded the Delta Alpha chapter here at the University of Georgia. Since then, nine subsequent lines have crossed, continuing the enchanting legacy of this academic sorority.

Membership: We are not an exclusionary organizaton. Our sisters are women of all ethnicities.

"Latin by tradition, not by definition."

Goals: Scholarly excellence, empowerment of Latinas and the universal woman, cultural and political awareness, and community activism

Academic Support: Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority Inc. was incorporated as an Academic Sorority in 1979. Scholarly excellence is our strongest mission. Each chapter has an Academic Chair to oversee the members' academic progress. The chapters host study skills workshops, time management workshops, study sessions and many other educational programs not only for the members but for the campus community as a whole.

Traditions: Lambda Theta Alpha members have a hand sign and traditional call. LTA's do not step, but they do salute and stroll.

Saluting: Saluting is a tradition unique to Lambda Theta Alpha Latin Sorority, Inc. Saluting is a means by which members of an organization can praise a particular member within their organization or to honor the organization's past and current accomplishments. Members write a salute, and tend to include words of modern-day songs. Once they have the text, they proceed to add motions to the salute. Typically, if there is more than one person performing a salute, the members will synchronize their movements. Saluting symbolizes unity and respect.

Strolling or Party Walking: Strolling is a tradition that traces back to the inception of historically African American Greek organizations and is practiced by many organizations to this day. Members of a Greek organization line up one after another. Similar to saluting, they move forward in a line performing the same dance. Strolling exhibits unity and pride.
summary: The purpose of Lambda Theta Alpha shall be to establish a sisterhood based on Unity, Love, and Respect; to provide social and cultural activities, and to carry on charitable and educational programs.

name: Tango Club at UGA
description: This club is for anyone interesting in learning to dance Argentine Tango. The Tango club is a group dedicated to the pursuit of increasing the awareness and appreciation of Argentine Tango within the university and the surrounding area. This includes hosting both lessons and events with separate classes for beginner and intermediate dancers. Lessons are held weekly with one or two larger events per semester.
summary: The Tango club is a group dedicated to the pursuit of increasing the awareness and appreciation of Argentine Tango within the university and the surrounding area. We have weekly meetings to learn and explore various aspects of the dance.

name: United States Institute of Theatre Technologies, Student Chapter at the University of Georgia
description: This group aims to foster a free exchange of ideas and information regarding theatre technology and design; to engage in research and other projects; and to promote the stated purposes of United States Institute for Theatre Technologies.
summary: This group aims to foster a free exchange of ideas and information regarding theatre technology and design; to engage in research and other projects; and to promote the stated purposes of United States Institute for Theatre Technologies.

name: Chemistry Graduate Student Organization
description: The CGSO is an organization devoted to improving the lives of the students in the Department of Chemistry at the University of Georgia, through social functions, community outreach, and professional development opportunities.
summary: The mission of CGSO will be to provide for the professional development needs of graduate students in chemistry and science at UGA.

name: Cultural Student Engagement
description:
It’s our mission to support all students. No matter your held identity, perspective, and worldview, there’s a place for everyone. Attend an upcoming event and learn more about how you can get involved.

Physical Spaces: Looking to use Adinkra Hall in Memorial Hall or The Tate Intersection for your next meeting or event? We’re here to help! meeting or event? We’re here to help!

Heritage Months: We acknowledge and celebrate all federally recognized heritage months with fun events in partnership with our student orgs.

Student Orgs:  Learn more and get connected with our student-led organizations.

Lunch and Learn: One-hour event at Tate Intersection where you can explore exciting topics presented by fellow peers and enjoy lively, engaging discussions.

Celebrations: Take part in long held UGA traditions with celebrations such as Rite of Sankofa or ALAS honoring all of your big accomplishments.

Workshops: Our engaging presentations and workshops delve into the heart of belonging

Dinner with Strangers: Nothing brings people together quite like a meal. Enjoy dinner in a welcoming atmosphere and engage in authentic conversation. Enter as strangers, but walk away with friends.

Newsletter: Never miss out and stay up to date with our latest events.
summary: It’s our mission to support all students. No matter your held identity, perspective, and worldview, there’s a place for everyone. Attend an upcoming event and learn more about how you can get involved.

name: Global Student Engagement
description:
It’s our mission to enhance your college experience through the internationalization of campus. No matter your held identity, perspective, and worldview, there’s a place for everyone. Attend an upcoming event and learn more about how you can get involved.

Whether you’re an international student looking for help or a global-minded student looking for ways to make new friends, we’re here for you. Check out our upcoming events, online resources, or schedule an in-person meeting with our team.

What We Offer:

  • International Student Orientation
  • International Coffee Hour
  • International Street Festival
  • Cultural Nights
  • Student Organizations
summary: It’s our mission to enhance your college experience through the internationalization of campus. No matter your held identity, perspective, and worldview, there’s a place for everyone. Attend an upcoming event and learn more about how you can get involved

name: Pride Center
description: The Pride Center's mission is to create an inclusive, sustainable space of self-discovery for the LGBT community within the University of Georgia. We support and affirm every student regardless of their sexual orientation, gender, gender identity and expression. The Pride Center serves as a space by which all members of the University of Georgia community can engage and explore the issues associated with sexual and gender identities. This mission is achieved by a commitment to an intersectional social justice framework, leadership development and equity.
summary: The Pride Center's mission is to create an inclusive, sustainable space of self-discovery for the LGBT community within the University of Georgia.

name: Men's Club Soccer
description:
The University of Georgia Men’s Club soccer team is comprised of 
some of the most talented players from around the state and country. Founded in 
1979, the team is dedicated to promoting the sport of soccer and the University 
of Georgia through fair play, good sportsmanship, and community outreach. Our 
goal is to use UGA’s name to build and endorse soccer throughout Athens and the 
country.

We are holding open tryouts for the Fall semester in late August at the Rec Sports Complex. Bring your student ID, cleats, and a ball. There will be a $10 tryout fee which can be paid with cash or check payable to UGA Men's Club Soccer. Venmo/Cashapp will not be accepted. Thank you and hope to see you there!
summary: Founded in 1979, the University of Georgia Men’s Club soccer team is comprised of some of the most talented players from around the state and country.

name: Georgia Students for Public Administration
description: The Georgia Students for Public Administration have three core values. We are inclusive, welcoming all students of public administration and policy at the University of Georgia. We are balanced, providing opportunities to engage in professional development, academic enhancement, and social recreation. We are connecting, fostering friendly and professional networks within our diverse student body and in the community. We hope to encourage students not just to learn their chosen profession but to form lasting bonds with their peers, faculty, staff, and community.
summary: The Georgia Students for Public Administration strive to help all students of public administration and policy at the University of Georgia build social and academic networks during and after their tenure at the University.

name: Thai Student Association
description:
The Thai Student Association (ThSA) is a student-run organization at the University of Georgia (UGA) that was established over 30 years ago. The main goal of this association is to spread Thai culture to the University of Georgia, surrounding community, and give them a better understanding of our people and traditions. In addition, we aim to provide home away from home for Thai students, but we also welcome anyone who is interested. 

Join us on GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/92965780/23p5L3yF

Check the links below for additional socials :)
summary: The Thai Student Organization is an ethnic-cultural community club for the Thai community at UGA and those interested in Thai culture.

name: Counterpoint Dance Company
description: Counterpoint Dance Company creates a space in which the members of the company can express their ideas and feel accepted in a dance community. This company will focus on the importance of technically trained dancers in order to create a challenging environment where all members reach their utmost potential. As a completely student-run dance company, Counterpoint allows members to be involved in all aspects of dance, including performing, choreographing, teaching, and learning a variety of dance styles. We primarily focus on contemporary, hip-hop, and jazz genres. We host auditions once a semester. Please feel free to reach out for more information!
summary: Counterpoint Dance Company is a community of experienced dancers who wish to continue their passion for dance and performing in college. Members will develop their dance and choreography skills through local Athens shows and our annual student-run show.

name: Homecoming Committee
description:
UGA Homecoming Committee is the perfect opportunity to show school spirit and participate in the Homecoming tradition. You can work alongside a group of people passionate about bringing spirit and tradition to Athens as we cheer on the Dawgs!
