Homecoming is an annual celebration planned by the UGA Homecoming Committee that welcomes back alumni and encourages students, University community members, and the Athens community to participate in a week of activities. The Committee collaborates with liaisons from multiple areas such as the Alumni Association, Athletics, Greek Life, and University Union Student Programming Board to organize and facilitate a campus-wide and city-wide Homecoming experience. The Homecoming Committee is responsible for coordinating and promoting entertainment for the campus and community during Homecoming Week each fall, including a kickoff event and parade and a series of cooperative and competitive events such as the Spirit of Homecoming Competition and the King/Queen competition.
summary: The Homecoming Committee is a unifying force dedicated to sharing the Bulldog pride through programmatic efforts, celebration of tradition, and University values. Our programs look to serve the whole student body of UGA with a variety of events.

name: Arch Policy Institute
description: Arch Policy Institute, API, is a student-run think tank and civic engagement organization. We seek to spark policy-related discussions, increase voter participation, and provide resources for students interested in developing their own policy projects. API provides opportunities for its members to engage in active learning about current policy issues, advocate for policy change in their local communities, and publish original policy memos in our annual Arch Policy Journal. API is sponsored by the Morehead Honors College, but it serves all students and fosters partnerships across our campus and community.
summary: Arch Policy Institute is a student-driven, non-partisan think tank that empowers students to discuss, develop, and advocate for policy change in their own communities. We seek to promote civic engagement and foster partnerships across UGA and Athens.

name: Club Baseball
description: The Club Baseball Team at the University of Georgia is a group of athletes that compete at a high level against other universities around the country. The team is made up of athletes that respect one another as they strive toward the common goal of winning every game that we play. We are led by a dedicated Executive team that acts for the greater good of the group. If you're interested in working hard, and playing even harder, then UGA Club Baseball is for you.
summary: No coach. No problem. This team is built on fun, competitiveness, and Bulldawg spirit. Built for students looking to continue playing the great game of baseball. Doesn't matter the background, all are welcome. Road to Alton starts NOW!

name: ICMA Student Chapter- UGA
description: The purpose of this organization is to introduce and integrate students into the local government management profession, familiarize students with ICMA, the premier local government management association, its members, resources, and policies, establish connections between local government management theories, practices, and applications through tangible and actual examples, cultivate a learning community wherein members actively share experiences and learn from one another, and provide resources and opportunities for students to increase their professional knowledge with local government practitioners.
summary: ICMA Student Chapter at UGA introduces and integrates students into the local government management profession, familiarizes students with ICMA, and provides resources and opportunities for students to connect with local government practitioners.

name: Indian Student Association
description: ISA hosts several events which include Ice breakers for new students, India Night (the annual cultural extravaganza), India's Independence and Republic days, Indian festivals. We act as a liaison between Indian students at the University of Georgia and the Indian, international cultures including students, community and faculty members.
summary:
The ISA is a non-profit, non-political, and non-religious organization whose primary objectives are 
a. to safeguard and promote the cultural heritage of India
b. to represent and assist Indian students associated with the University of Georgia

name: Bioinformatics Graduate Student Association
description:
In the pursuant of academic excellence, there is always the need to communicate and learn from both the leaders in the field as well as fellow students. We, the graduate students of the Institute of Bioinformatics at the University of Georgia, Athens (UGA) therefore resolve to establish the Bioinformatics Graduate Students Association (BIGSA)

BIGSA IS ESTABLISHED FOR THE EXPRESSED PURPOSE OF

  1. CREATING AND MAINTAINING A COHESIVE BIOINFORMATICS COMMUNITY AT UGA

  2. PROVIDING THE STRUCTURE FOR MUTUAL SUPPORT, CO-OPERATION AND ACADEMIC DEVELOPMENT.

  3. PROVIDING THE MEANS FOR ENGAGING EACH OTHER SOCIALLY AND INTELLECTUALLY.

  4. STRIVE TO SHARE THE UNDERSTANDING OF BIOINFORMATICS WITH A BROADER COMMUNITY BY ORGANIZING VARIOUS FORUMS TO SHARE INFORMATION ABOUT WHEN AND HOW BIOINFORMATICS INTERLOCKS WITH VARIOUS ACADEMIC DISCIPLINES.
summary: BIGSA is a graduate student organization that promotes community and  collaboration in bioinformatics research at UGA.  Annually, we hold many social and educational events to support these goals.

name: Aces for Athens
description: Aces for Athens is a University of Georgia based volunteer student-run organization dedicated to mentoring and educating local Elementary school students through the sport of tennis. As an organization, we understand the importance of role models in a child’s development and aim to support and engage these children outside of their classroom environments. We want our members to acknowledge that being a student at UGA extends beyond the campus, and by volunteering we can positively impact the community around us!
summary: The mission of Aces for Athens is to provide tennis lessons in our local community, absolutely free of cost, to children of all abilities and disabilities that would otherwise not be able to enjoy the sport.

name: Demosthenian Literary Society
description: Have opinions? Want to learn to share them effectively? We invite you to Demosthenian Hall! We are the oldest student organization at UGA, and our history is as rich and colorful as the history of the university itself. We are an extemporaneous debate club dedicated to promoting a correct mode of speaking. Extemporaneous debate means the topics for debate are not known before the start of each meeting. This organization is a great way to build your confidence and to learn to develop ideas on the fly! Meetings are Thursdays at 7:00 PM in Demosthenian Hall, located by the Chapel.
summary: This organization works to improve discourse through extemporaneous speaking in the fields of politics, philosophy, and comedy. We also strive to teach parliamentary procedure through the application of Robert's Rules of Order.

name: Educational Theory & Practice Graduate Student Organization
description: The Educational Theory and Practice Graduate Student Organization (ETAP GSO) comprises doctoral and specialist students in the Department of Educational Theory and Practice within the Mary Frances Early College of Education. Our mission is to advance the study of educational theory and practice, offer networking and social opportunities, provide professional development, encourage cross-disciplinary collaboration, and foster a collegial environment for sharing ideas.
summary: The ETAP GSO represents graduate students and provides networking, professional development, and cross-disciplinary study opportunities.

name: Spoon University
description:
Spoon University is an online food and media publication that produces various forms of content like articles, videos, and photos. If you like creative writing or making videos similar to Buzzfeed Tasty and Bon Appetit, we're the club for you.

We also act as a food society on campus where you can geek out on all things foodie culture. We have a culinary aspect of the club where we cook as a group and visit local restaurants to talk to their chefs. 

Want to know more? Visit our page here: https://spoonuniversity.com/chapter/uga

Follow us on Instagram: @spoonuga
summary: Spoon University is an online food and media publication. We produce articles, videos, and photos to help young adults navigate the food world. We are UGA's only club focused on foodie culture. We also cook at our meetings and visit local restaurants.

name: Spike Dawgs® Roundnet Club
description: Club Sports team at UGA
summary: We are a co-rec, travel team representing UGA in the Spikeball Roundnet Association Collegiate Division. We are one of the top ranked college clubs in the nation. Find us on Instagram @spikedawgsroundnet. Email ugaspikeballclub@gmail.com for more info.

name: Agricultural Leadership, Education, and Communication Graduate Student Association
description:
The Agricultural Leadership, Education and Communication Graduate Student Association is a departmental branch of the UGA Graduate Student Association. We represent the graduate students in our department at Graduate Student Association meetings and serve as liaison to department faculty. We strive to conduct events for our graduate students that are both professional and social.

Activities include both professional and social events which allow members to strengthen their professional cohort and enrich their academic experience.
summary: The ALEC Graduate Student Association is a departmental branch of the UGA Graduate Student Association. We represent the graduate students in our department at Graduate Student Association meetings and serve as a liaison to department faculty.

name: Ronald McDonald House Charities Club @UGA
description: The purpose of the Ronald McDonald House Charities Club is to improve the health and well-being of children by volunteering at the Atlanta Ronald McDonald Houses, planning fundraisers for Ronald McDonald House Charities, and participating in fundraising events organized by Ronald McDonald House Charities.
summary: The Ronald McDonald House Charities Club provides volunteering and fundraising opportunities that support programs that directly improve the health and well-being of children and their families.

name: Education Abroad Ambassadors
description: EAA joins students of diverse backgrounds and allows one another to learn the several study away programs UGA offers. Those interested in studying abroad or wanting to share their own study away experiences are encouraged to join! We host several educational workshops for prospective study away students, where ambassadors are encouraged to share their personal experiences, the lessons they learned, and how to best prepare for the program. Education Abroad Ambassadors easily offers volunteer hours and aids potential students throughout the process for those seeking to obtain the Global Education and Leadership Certificate.
summary: The purpose/mission of the Education Abroad Ambassadors (EAA) serves to promote and support UGA students interested in education abroad experiences.

name: Breaking the Shackles: Athens
description: Breaking the Shackles: Athens is a UGA campus representation of Breaking the Shackles, Inc. 501(c)(3). The vision of Breaking the Shackles is "Leveraging music as a platform to illuminate slavery. Creating a community of advocates to advance freedom." Through our community events, we gather to strengthen our team and bond as a community of advocates. Through our cause events, we raise awareness and learn together from experts, partners, researchers, survivors, and more about the issue of modern slavery. Through our concerts, we raise money for our partner charities and spread awareness about human trafficking all while having a great time and engaging with the Athens music scene. Breaking the Shackles: Athens strives to engage and equip the Athens community and UGA students to join our fight to end human trafficking.
summary: Leveraging music as a platform to illuminate slavery. Creating a community of advocates to advance freedom.

name: Statistics Club
description:
Stat Club primarily serves as a host of informal social experiences designed to connect members of the statistical community, regardless of experience in the field. We hold monthly meetings and get-togethers in which both students and faculty may participate, creating a sense of purpose and belonging for newcomers, international students, and graduate professionals. Specific duties of Stat Club include sponsoring/hosting the annual Bradley Lecture & Picnic, in which students get an opportunity to interact with a visiting scholar and learn more about their area of research, both professionally and informally. Another primary event hosted by Stat Club is the International Potluck, held in November, where students share and celebrate dishes from their home culture, to further embrace the diversity among the statistics department/community.

Please join the GroupMe for updates!: GroupMe Link.

If you don't have a GroupMe, reach out to us using this interest form: Interest form link.

2023-2024 Meetings:

Regular meetings will be hosted once per month in Brooks Hall, Room 520, both in-person and over Zoom. We'll discuss upcoming events, ways to get involved, and connect with other students from inside and outside the Stat department. We also host other informal socials, like ice cream and pizza hangouts, once per month to build community among the students.
summary: We build communion among the diverse members of the statistical community, promote scholarship and training through colloquia and seminars, and sponsor the development of statistics classrooms in and out of UGA through financial/technological donations.

name: Chamber Music Society
description: Chamber Music Society is a student-run organization designed to help undergraduate students of all majors continue their passion for music by forming music ensembles and performing throughout the community. This is accomplished by providing students a platform to gain invaluable performance experience and also share live music to the Athens community, performing in such venues as retirement homes, hospitals, weddings, receptions, cocktail hours, and holiday festivities.
summary: Chamber Music Society is dedicated to providing ensembles and performance opportunities to musicians of all majors.

name: Black Pre-Law Student Association
description:
The purpose of the Black Pre-Law Student Association (BPLSA) is to cultivate and foster community

amongst black students at the University of Georgia through motivation, mentorship, support, and

networking from various professionals of the legal profession and law-based fields including University

of Georgia alumni. We strive to build community, promote networking opportunities, and prepare

future black law students for a successful law school experience while supporting their professional

career and academic trajectories throughout their undergraduate experience. Membership is open to full-

time and part-time University of Georgia students pursuing undergraduate degrees in any area of study.
summary:
The purpose of the Black Pre-Law Student Association (BPLSA) is to cultivate and foster community
amongst black students at the University of Georgia through motivation, mentorship, support, and
networking from various professionals. Membership is open.

name: National Council of Negro Women, University of Georgia Section
description:
The University of Georgia chapter of the National Council of Negro Women aims to lead, develop, and advocate for Black women as they pursue their higher education. We accomplish this through programs focused on empowerment, unity, and service in the UGA and Athens-Clarke County communities

Objectives:

  • Maintain a community of support dedicated to empowering the black woman
  • Provide a network of faculty, staff, alumni, and working professionals for the development of our members
  • Support and provide for residents of the Athens-Clarke County community
summary: NCNW at UGA aims to lead, develop, and advocate for Black women as they pursue their higher education. We accomplish this through programs focused on empowerment, unity, and service in the University of Georgia and Athens-Clarke County communities.

name: Society of Automotive Engineers
description:
Founded in 2014, UGA Motorsports is a student-led organization affiliated with the University of Georgia College of Engineering. We are dedicated to providing students with hands-on experience in engineering design, manufacturing, racing, and business operations. With a strong emphasis on curiosity, innovation, and collaboration, our team gives students of all majors the chance to explore their passions and develop professional skills in a high-energy, competitive environment.

We operate four divisions, each offering unique opportunities:

Our Formula SAE Internal Combustion (FSAE-IC) and Formula SAE Electric (FSAE-EV) teams spend the academic year designing, manufacturing, and testing open-wheel, formula-style race cars—one powered by a traditional internal combustion engine and the other by a fully electric powertrain. These vehicles are developed from the ground up by students across multiple disciplines, incorporating skills in mechanical design, electrical systems, aerodynamics, software, and more. The culmination of their efforts is the Formula SAE competition in Michigan, where we compete against over 100 collegiate teams from around the world, presenting our design, engineering, and performance on one of the biggest stages in student motorsports.

Meanwhile, our ChampCar Endurance Racing team takes on the unique challenge of real-world, wheel-to-wheel racing through the ChampCar Endurance Series—an amateur, long-distance racing circuit held at tracks across the United States. UGA Motorsports is one of only three collegiate teams nationwide to compete in this demanding series. Students on this team work year-round on our race-prepped BMW 330i ZHP, focusing on performance upgrades, maintenance, and race-day operations. These endurance events can last from 7 to 24 hours, requiring strategic thinking, technical adaptability, and precise teamwork to succeed.

Supporting all of our racing efforts is the Business Team, which ensures the organization operates smoothly and sustainably. This team handles everything from marketing and branding—including managing social media, merchandise, and our website—to internal logistics, such as event planning and team coordination. The Business Team also plays a crucial role in sponsorship acquisition and management, building relationships with corporate partners, securing funding, and maintaining the resources our engineering teams need to innovate and compete.

Together, these teams form the foundation of UGA Motorsports—a multidisciplinary, student-driven organization where passion meets performance, and where every member plays a vital role in driving us forward.
summary: We are a group of students passionate about automotive engineering. Together we work with a diverse group of students to design and build Formula internal combustion, Formula electric, and Endurance racing vehicles, along with our business team.

name: NMDP at UGA
description: NMDP at UGA will be an integral part of the national, nonprofit organization that saves thousands of lives every year. Our organization hosts drives to register students and staff for the National Bone Marrow Registry, raises awareness and knowledge about NMDP's mission, and fundraises money to support those fighting blood diseases NMDP at UGA serves to ensure that bulldogs can be the life saving chance that those with life-threatening desperately need.
summary: NMDP at UGA is dedicated to encouraging fellow bulldogs to sign up for the National Bone Marrow Registry to save a life of someone fighting blood diseases including blood cancers and sickle cell.

name: Competitive Sports
description:
The Club Sports Program at the University of Georgia, administered by Recreational Sports, is comprised of around 40 unique clubs. Each Club Sport team is designed to serve individual interests in different sports and recreational activities. Our clubs are instructional, recreational, and/or competitive in nature and are strictly voluntary. Club sports are student-initiated, student-led organizations officially registered with the University. Several clubs are competitive and compete against other schools and universities, and in some cases against varsity teams, at the state, regional and national levels.

Each club is formed, developed, governed, and administered by the student membership of that particular club, working in conjunction with the Competitive Sports Office. The key to the success of this program is student leadership, interest, involvement, and participation.

 

'25-'26 Club Sports Handbook
Style Guide and Processes for Using Logos and Trademarks
Coaches Waiver
Non Travel Spending Request
Student Travel Spending Request 

How to Create a Vendor Profile for Reimbursement

Practice Request Form
Home Competition Request Form
summary: The administration that oversees the Club Sports Program.

name: Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints Institute of Religion
description:
The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints Institute of Religion is here to help students preserve their spiritual strength in the gospel of Jesus Christ through weekday classes and regular social and service opportunities.

On Tuesday Nights at 7pm we host activities that include sports and food. These are held at 706 Whitehead Rd. On Thursday Nights at 6:30pm we have Gospel Study and eat a free meal together. This is held at 1450 S Lumpkin St.

On Sunday Mornings we meet at 10am for church at 706 Whitehead Rd. We often have food and activities after church. 

Ultimately, we welcome anyone from any faith. We strive to build an environment of community and support, and to strength and increase one another's faith in Jesus Christ and the beautiful gospel he so freely shared.
summary: The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints Institute of Religion is here to help students preserve their spiritual strength in the gospel of Jesus Christ through weekday classes and regular social and service opportunities.

name: Saving Smiles at UGA
description: The mission of Saving Smiles at UGA shall be to connect pre-dental students with volunteer opportunities in the dental community. In doing so, students gain hands-on experience that allows them to expand their dental knowledge and be better prepared prior to matriculating into dental school. This relationship betwee­­­n members of Saving Smiles and the dental community is intended to be mutualistic; underserved communities will benefit from the outreach of Saving Smiles members. Outreach to all levels of community is the primary concern of Saving Smiles. This overall objective is to be achieved by providing education on oral healthcare and promotion of better hygiene habits in local schools, volunteering with state dental organizations, attending national conventions to better the future of the dental community, and traveling abroad as dental volunteers.
summary: The mission of Saving Smiles UGA shall be to help pre-dental students fundraise in order to donate dental supplies to those in need.

name: Romance Languages Graduate Student Organization
description: ROML-GSO is a Graduate Student Organization within the Romance Languages Department. The main goal of the organization is to foment a space in which graduate students of ROML can exchange scholarly and creative ideas as well receive mentorship and training from the experienced faculty in the department. GSO serves all four major languages in the department and both Literature and Linguistics tracks, as well as supports indigenous languages, film and culture. For the 2025/2026 academic year, we intend to bring back the Crossroads Conference, a multidisciplinary conference for graduate students within and outside UGA. Among the activities GSO hosts are: weekly colloquia to allow both graduate students and faculty present their work to their colleagues and receive friendly feedback; professional development talks by faculty and invited speakers; fitness club and social gatherings.
summary: The main goal of ROML-GSO is to create a space in which graduate students in the Department of Romance Languages can exchange scholarly and creative ideas as well receive mentorship and training from the experienced faculty in the department.

name: Collegiate FFA Alumni & Supporters at UGA
description: Collegiate FFA helps to enhance the collegiate experience through service and engagement to create premier leaders, enable personal growth, and ensure career success. Collegiate FFA is an opportunity for students in the College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences to remain involved in The National FFA Organization. Through volunteering at area, state, and national career development events, members have the opportunity to network with other collegiate members as well as agriculture professionals. Through providing numerous professional development and volunteer opportunities, Collegiate FFA members will be more prepared for today's job market. Whether members are interested in a career in agriculture education or in the agriculture industry, Collegiate FFA has something to offer!
summary: Collegiate FFA Alumni & Supporters enhances the collegiate experience through service and engagement to create premier leaders, enable personal growth, and ensure career success.

name: Food2Kids
description:
Food2Kids raises awareness and funds to benefit the Northeast Georgia Food Bank Program Food2Kids. This program provides food-insecure children in Athens-Clarke County and surrounding areas with bags of non-perishable, easily prepared, nutritious food on Friday afternoons. Many of these children are on the free or reduced lunch program, and the only meals they receive are during school hours. Food2Kids helps to bridge this "weekend hunger gap." We believe that for children to be well-educated, it is essential that they are well-fed.

The members of Food2Kids are passionate about serving the local Athens community. We believe that by helping those less fortunate, we not only help those individuals, but the community as a whole. We define service as anything that helps someone in need, no matter how big or how small. Sustainable service is essential to all service organizations. By having constant support, organizations who rely on volunteers will have a tangible resource that will allow them to reach their goals. Additionally sustainable service will aid in fostering a culture of service throughout the University of Georgia resulting in the entire Athens community seeing enormous benefits!

In lieue of baggings during the pandemic, we have adapted our partnership with the Food Bank to include mobile pantries and bag deliveries to provide meals to over 150 families each week.
summary: The purpose of this organization is to raise awareness and funds to benefit the Northeast GA Food Bank Program Food2Kids. This program provides food-insecure children in Athens-Clarke County and surrounding areas with bags of non-perishable food.

name: Alpha Kappa Psi - Alpha Epsilon
description:
Alpha Kappa Psi is a national, co-ed professional business fraternity. We recruit all majors and welcome anyone with at least three remaining semesters to join AKPsi. Our goal is to provide a brotherhood bond while enhancing the professional development of each brother. During the semester, we sponsor academic and professional seminars, community service projects, membership gatherings, and social events.

Follow our Instagram (@akpsi_uga) to stay connected and hear all the details about recruitment!
summary: Alpha Kappa Psi is a nationally recognized co-ed professional fraternity. Members participate in professional, social, and philanthropic events.

name: Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers
description:
The Society of Hispanic Professional Engineers was founded to promote and encourage the study and understanding of engineering and other STEM fields among Hispanic students. This is achieved through opportunities for networking, professional development, and access to a national network of high achieving students pursuing degrees in STEM and other related fields. Membership is open to all students.

 

Some of the many benefits of joining SHPE
summary: SHPE changes lives by empowering the Hispanic community to realize its fullest potential and to impact the world through STEM awareness, access, support, and development. Membership is extended to all students.

name: Pre-Nursing Society at UGA
description:
Pre-Nursing Society at UGA has been created in order to enable pre-nursing students at UGA to learn more about the nursing profession, connect with other pre-nursing students, and receive guidance on classes to take as well as the application process for nursing school.

We host monthly meetings at which we discuss different nursing schools and programs, common prerequisites for these programs, details regarding the entry examinations, and volunteer opportunities. We also invite guest speakers, whose credentials vary from practicing nurses, nursing students, and faculty from nursing schools.
summary: PNS has been created in order to enable pre-nursing students at UGA to learn more about the nursing profession, connect with other pre-nursing students, and receive guidance on classes to take as well as the application process for nursing school.

name: Eventing Team
description: The Eventing Team has approximately 25 members that compete from the starter through intermediate level of eventing. The team holds multiple clinics with top level riders throughout the year and attends collegiate challenges against other colleges such as the University of Kentucky, Auburn, Clemson, University of Florida and the University of South Carolina.
summary: Eventing is the equestrian triathlon sport including dressage, cross country, and show jumping. The Eventing Team gives college students the opportunity to continue their love of the sport while adjusting to the academic demands of college.

name: Tap Dawgs
description:
Tap Dawgs is a student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to keeping the art of tap dancing alive. We choreograph our own dances and perform them at events around the community. If you are interested in joining, want us to perform at your event, or just want to know more about us, please contact us at UGATapDawgsClub@gmail.com or through our Google form. And Check out our Youtube channel to see videos of our dances!

https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCf6ouXFqjptMAtIwIcJq9eA/feed
summary: Tap Dawgs is a student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to keeping the art of tap dancing alive. We choreograph our own dances and perform them at events around the community.

name: Shikaari
description: Established in 2005, Shikaari is the premier all-female, fusion dance team at the University of Georgia. We incorporate various dance styles, such as Bollywood, Hip-Hop, Classical, Contemporary, Rass, and Bhangra, with today’s modern beats. We travel and compete across the country and give back to our community and culture while showcasing our passion for dance. Through all of this, we create lifelong friendships and become a family. We encourage you to join our team!
summary: Shikaari is the premier all-female, fusion dance team at the University of Georgia. We incorporate South Asian and American dance styles and compete across the country!

name: Delight Ministries at UGA
description:
How different would your life be if you had a group of women chasing after God’s heart that you could rely on? How different would your life be if you could call these women your friends: if you could confide in them, grow with them, be mentored by them, and serve with them by your side? At Delight, our goal is to make that difference a reality. As stated beautifully by Delight Ministries’ headquarters, the mission of Delight is “to invite college women into a Christ-centered community that fosters vulnerability and transforms stories.” We aim to create a welcoming environment for every college woman, no matter where they may be on their faith journey, so they may genuinely connect with God and each other in a place they feel comfortable and accepted. We desire to change the college experience for women at UGA through the transformation that happens when we live fully invested in pursuing Christ. If you’d like to learn more, you can watch our information video, browse the Delight Ministries’ website, or contact us! See you Sunday! Everyone is welcome!

Welcome to Delight
summary: We are a college women's community that grows together, serves together, learns together, and does life together while chasing the heart of God. All are welcome!

name: Honors Student Council
description:
The Honors Student Council is comprised of roughly 30 students dedicated to enhancing the Honors experience and engaging their peers in the Honors community. HSC hosts a wide variety of events aimed to provide social experiences and stimulate academic curiosity. HSC also serves as a liaison between the Honors administration and the student body. This organization provides a vehicle for open communication between the two groups.
Meetings occur biweekly on Mondays at 7:15 PM. Applications open each winter for first, second, and third-years interested in dedicating themselves to the Honors community.
summary: HSC is comprised of roughly 30 students dedicated to enhancing the Honors experience and engaging their peers in the Honors community. HSC hosts a wide variety events aimed to provide social experiences and stimulate academic curiosity.

name: Geography Undergraduate Student Organization
description: The purpose of GUSTO at UGA shall be to promote the awareness of and interest in Geography as a discipline, to foster the growth of geographic skills and knowledge among the undergraduate student body, to assist in the professional development and continuing education of members, to allow for opportunities for interaction and networking for students with faculty members, and with professional geographers in the local area, to take part in outreach activities aimed toward increasing members' exposure to both human and physical geography-related activities, and to increase the organization's interaction with the local community.
summary: Primary objectives of the organization are to to promote the awareness of and interest in Geography as a discipline, and to foster the growth of geographic skills and knowledge among the undergraduate student body.

name: Aviation Club at UGA
description:
The Aviation Club is a social organization designed to provide unique opportunities for students at the University of Georgia to become involved with aviation, experience new career options and learn the thrill that aviation provides.

 

Welcome – we are the Aviation Club at the University of Georgia, and if you’re interested in flying, aircraft or careers in aviation, then you’ve come to the right place! We are a group dedicated to having fun, meeting other people with similar interests, networking and communicating with aviation professionals while learning more about all fundamentals of flight.

Some of our regular events include fly-ins, trips to air shows and museums, simulator visits, presentations by pilots and other professionals in the industry and movie nights. We also offer resources for prospective pilots, including discounts with the local flight school and introductory flights at some meetings. 

Our meetings are typically held Wednesday afternoons at 6:30 at the Athens-Ben Epps Airport, and carpooling is provided if you lack a means of transportation. Send us an email or see our Facebook page for contact information, more detailed directions, and up-to-date info on meetings. 

 

Blue skies!

 

www.ugaaviationclub.com
summary: We are the Aviation Club - the only organization on campus dedicated to all aspects of flying and aviation! We're open to all students, with any amount of experience or interest in aviation.

name: Chinese Student Christian Fellowship
description: "Chinese Student Christian Fellowship" is a group of Chinese UGA students with Christian beliefs that assembles to strengthen students in spiritual growth through participation of bible study, gospel choir, praise dancing, and worship services.
summary: "Chinese Student Christian Fellowship" is a religious student organization to foster a deeper experience of Christ for those students seeking a higher knowledge of the truth of the Bible.

name: Omicron Delta Kappa
description:
Purpose

The University of Georgia Alpha Upsilon circle of Omicron Delta Kappa unites students and faculty who have demonstrated leadership and achievement in the five phases of campus life in the interest of the development of the University through commitment to service, integrity, excellence, and friendship.

Nationally, the purpose of the Omicron Delta Kappa Society is threefold:

First, to recognize those who have attained a high standard of efficiency in collegiate activities and to inspire others to strive for conspicuous attainments along similar lines;

Second, to bring together the most representative students in all phases of collegiate life and thus to create an organization which will help to mold the sentiment of the institution on questions of local and intercollegiate interest;

Third, to bring together members of the faculty and student body of the institution, as well as other Omicron Delta Kappa members, on a basis of mutual interest, understanding and helpfulness.
summary: Omicron Delta Kappa unites students and faculty who have demonstrated leadership and achievement in the five phases of campus life in the interest of the development of the University through commitment to service, integrity, excellence, and friendship.

name: DL_NO
description: 1. 我们的组织是一个学生舞蹈俱乐部。我们的目标是在UGA建立第一个中国学生舞蹈俱乐部，我们将招聘中国学生或会说中文的学生。我们舞蹈社的正式名称是DL_NO，是“Dance Like No Other”的缩写。
summary: DL-NO is a non-profit dancing organization in the university of Georgia. We are aiming to provide a stage for all students who are interested in dancing and performing in public. We welcome any organization or individual to communicate and join us!

name: Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association
description: The Developmental Biology Graduate Student Association (DBGSA)’s mission is to operate as a liaison between graduate students from various home departments, the Developmental Biology Alliance, and associated University of Georgia administration. The DBGSA will strive to promote student needs and interests in an effort to further the professional development of its members, while maintaining a high degree of academic integrity. Furthermore, the DBSGA aims to encourage collaboration and communication among faculty, researchers, and graduate students by facilitating social events such as the annual Developmental Biology Retreat and Symposium. These social events will also provide graduate students with opportunities to network and establish professional connections with researchers from a variety of biological disciplines. Overall, the DBGSA seeks to act as a voice and support system for graduate students looking to enhance or focus their studies in the field of Developmental Biology.
summary: The DBGSA seeks to act as a voice and support system for graduate students looking to enhance or focus their studies in the field of Developmental Biology.

name: Phi Alpha Honor Society
description:
Phi Alpha is a social work honour society.

https://phialpha.org/
summary: Phi Alpha is an honor society created to foster high standards of education for social work and to invite into membership those who have attained excellence of scholarship and distinction of achievement as students of social work.

name: Energy Concept @ UGA, The
description:
Energy Concept @ UGA is a student-run organization dedicated to the interdisciplinary exploration of energy across business, policy, and technology.

Our mission is to build a dynamic community where students can engage in learning, industry collaboration, and hands-on projects to shape our energy future.
summary: Energy Concept @ UGA is a student-run organization dedicated to the interdisciplinary exploration of energy across business, policy, and technology.

name: Horticulture Graduate Student Association
description: An organization for graduate students in the Horticulture department of UGA.
summary: The Horticulture Graduate Student Association promotes social events, academic discussions, professional development training, and outdoor activities for its members.

name: Model African Union
description: The Model African union is an organization that gives students the opportunity to learn about diplomacy, leadership, and governmental organization through a unique simulation experience. The Model African Union simulation experience is a two-fold educational experience. The first phase of learning begins in the classroom or study sessions where the participant gathers information, does research, and learns about issues relating to a specific country. The second phase takes place in the simulated meetings of the actual commissions of the African Union. During the simulation, students will have an opportunity to meet ambassadors from African Union member states, and discuss major issues and problems facing the African continent. Students will also serve as ambassadors of African countries and will be charged with debating African issues and passing draft resolutions.
summary: Members of MAU learn a plethora of social, political, and economic issues facing countries in Africa.  Simulating the African Union, MAU allows students to develop formal negotiation, debate, and writing skills. No previous experience is required.

name: Breast Cancer Awareness Club
description: The Breast Cancer Awareness Club (BCAC) is dedicated to raising awareness, promoting education, and supporting individuals impacted by breast cancer. Our mission is to engage students and the community through outreach events, informational campaigns, and partnerships with healthcare professionals and local organizations. We strive to foster a supportive environment where members can volunteer, advocate for breast health, and contribute to ongoing efforts in cancer prevention, early detection, and patient support initiatives. Together, we aim to inspire hope, educate minds, and make a lasting impact on the fight against breast cancer.
summary: BCAC promotes breast cancer awareness through education, outreach, and support. We empower students to engage in prevention efforts, advocate for early detection, and provide a compassionate space for those impacted, fostering lasting community impact.

name: Knit and Crochet at UGA
description:
This group is for UGA students to socialize while engaging in fiber arts. Come learn, share techniques, visit yarn stores with us, and use our craft in service projects. 

Visit our Linktree for more information!

linktr.ee/knitandcrochetatuga
summary: Join us on Myers Quad Mondays and Tuesdays at 4 PM! All are welcome, regardless of skill level or craft type.

name: Dairy Science Club
description:
The University of Georgia Dairy Science Club gathers people with different backgrounds but similar interests in an effort to build friendships and gain familiarity with the dairy industry.  We are dedicated to fostering student involvement in the dairy industry.  We are committed to offering opportunities that allow for both student exposure and immersion within the industry. This includes activities that are professional, philanthropic, and social in nature.  Members need not have any previous knowledge or background in the industry just a sincere interest in promoting the industry and those that work within it. 

Through these efforts, we hope to grow our members love and knowledge for dairy cows, and inform not only the student body but also members of the community about an industry that is sometimes easily overlooked.
summary: DSC gathers people with different backgrounds but similar interests in an effort to build friendships and gain familiarity with the dairy industry. We are dedicated to immersing our members to all aspects of the industry and bonding over a love of cows!

name: Graduate Pride
description:
We are a group for graduate and professional students at UGA who identify as LGBTQIA+ to build community. The mission of Graduate Pride is to build community for, advocate for, and provide support to the LGBTQIA graduate and professional students.

The student leaders of Graduate Pride coordinate events and meetings for their members including:

  • Social Events On Campus
  • Professional Development Events
  • Social Events Off Campus including Local Places in Athens
  • Book Club
  • Group Study Sessions
  • Informational Sessions
summary: We are a group for graduate and professional students at UGA who identify as LGBTQIA+ to build community.

name: TONIC
description: TONIC (Teaching Others Notes, Instruments and Creativity) aims to provide an area of resource and connection for future music pedagogues. TONIC welcomes students who hope to build a private music studio of their own in the future. It encourages a community of peers who aspire to do the same in any capacity and brings together members who enjoy sharing music.
summary: UGA Collegiate Chapter of MTNA (Music Teacher's National Association)

name: Esports at UGA
description:
Esports at UGA is THE gaming club at the University of Georgia. Our mission is to provide an infrastructure for competitive gaming teams at UGA to organize and compete against other colleges. We also want to build a healthy gaming community and welcome more casual players to join us in any video game they want!

We strive to create a safe, friendly, and fun gaming community for every type of gamer--whether PC or console, competitive or casual.

Follow us on social media or join our discord! All links under link below!

 

All Links / Linktree
summary: Esports at UGA is the official gaming club at UGA. We work to be the platform for competitive and casual gamers at UGA to create meaningful relationships and profound experiences. We aim to inspire and empower each individual to make a mark in the world.

name: Exercise is Medicine at UGA
description:
Exercise is Medicine® on Campus (EIM-OC) is a program calling upon universities and colleges to engage in the promotion of physical activity as a vital sign of health. EIM-OC encourages faculty, staff and students to work together toward improving the health and well-being of the campus community by:

A. Making movement a part of the daily campus culture

B. Assessing physical activity at every student health visit

C. Providing students the tools necessary to strengthen healthy physical activity habits that can last a lifetime

D. Connecting university health care providers with university health fitness specialists to provide a referral system for exercise prescription.
summary: Exercise is Medicine at UGA aims to educate and offer programs to empower members of the UGA campus community and surrounding communities to engage in behaviors that promote health and wellbeing.

name: Global Brigades - Medical Chapter
description:
Along with affiliate groups at other distinguished universities, our UGA team recruits student volunteers and professionals, gathers supplies, and then travels overseas to provide program-specific service in a variety of communities. Our organization will hold one brigade per year, taking place during academic breaks, and provide ongoing year-long educational and professional development opportunities that relate to international health and development to members.

 https://youtu.be/UVtE23mO-Lo
summary: Global Brigades at the University of Georgia is a secular, international student-operated volunteer organization whose mission is to provide sustainable development to underserved communities throughout the world.

name: Philosophy Graduate Student Association
description: A graduate student organization aimed at the promotion of philosophical discourse amongst graduate philosophers as well as the development of professional skills related to academic life.
summary: An organization for graduate students in philosophy that holds meetings, conferences, and colloquia.

name: Sharkwing Sketch Comedy
description: Sharkwing is a sketch comedy troupe at UGA that produces sketches and performs them in a variety of ways, whether that may be in live performances or with a video sketch.
summary: UGA's very own student sketch comedy troupe that focuses on writing, producing, and performing sketch comedy. We produce and put on live shows every semester in addition to creating video sketches throughout the year. See our Instagram for more info! :)

name: American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists
description:
AAPS UGA is the local chapter of the American Association of Pharmaceutical Scientists (AAPS). AAPS is a professional, scientific organization of approximately 10,000 members employed in academia, industry, government, and other research institutes worldwide. The Organization was founded in 1986 and focuses on advancing the capacity of pharmaceutical scientists to develop products and therapies that improve global health. AAPS UGA was founded to propagate the AAPS mission and vision locally with our goal being the professional development of members. 

We plan to create an opportunity to support both the academic and scientific pursuits of our members while providing opportunities to further learning. We hope to advance the understanding and appreciation of various scientific disciplines through lectures, seminars, poster session, and meetings. Additionally, we want to utilize AAPS at UGA to bring together members through various social activities. Interpersonal skills are essential to a person both in and out of the lab and we would like to use events such as fundraising, potluck dinners, bowling and tailgates to further communication and connection between members. We also wish to promote scientific communications with other science departments at UGA such as the chemistry department as well as another AAPS chapters in local Atlanta such as AAPS Mercer University through field trips. We believe expanding AAPS at UGA networks will help us in both scientific and professional experience.

The UGA AAPS chapter has also established a tradition of supporting our community as well as other philanthropic efforts and we plan to continue this through Relay for Life and other local charities
summary: AAPS UGA is an organization dedicated to the professional development of pharmaceutical science students as well as students interested in pharmaceutical research here at UGA.

name: American Association of Psychiatric Pharmacists
description: The American Association of Psychiatric Pharmacists is dedicated to shedding light on the issues facing psychiatric and neurologic pharmacy. AAPP’s main goal is to spread awareness to the students and community. AAPP members organize events such as guest speakers in the field, posting social media blasts for awareness, participating in philanthropy walks, and participating in NAMI’s mental illness awareness week. AAPP members also believe that fundraising for national nonprofit organizations that advocate for psychological and neurologic disorders can have a huge impact on awareness and improving the lives of those living with these conditions. Members have participated in fundraising for the National Alliance on Mental Illness, Down Syndrome Association, Autism Association, National Eating Disorder Association, and more. AAPP also advocates for a stigma free environment in our school and profession. Since the rate of mental disorders is increasing in the American population, AAPP feels it is important to create a support system for its members, the college of pharmacy, and the community. As a unique feature of the UGA chapter, we organize mental health breaks for our members and friends. These events include movie nights, attending sporting events, yoga in the park, painting, and more! These events allow us to take a break from our stressful lives and bond with each other. We have created an environment of encouragement which benefits our own mental health while advocating for mental health among our profession and extending unto others.
summary: AAPP is an organization dedicated to reaching out to those who are affected by psychiatric and/or neurologic illnesses. The organization intends to plan outreach events that advocate for mental health awareness and provide interpersonal support.

name: Pre-Dental Society
description: The purpose of Pre-Dental Society is to provide a forum for students interested in the field of dentistry. Opportunities such as guest speakers from dental schools and community service events are provided to strengthen us as future applicants to dental school. In addition, Pre-Dental Society offers presentations that focus on important topics including the Dental Admissions Test (DAT), extracurriculars, the dental school application process, and student panels. Our organization provides a large network for pre-dental students to lean on one another through mentor-mentee relationships among other camaraderie.
summary: The purpose of Pre-Dental Society is to provide a forum for students interested in the field of dentistry. Through guest speakers, community service, and camaraderie we will help each other ace the DAT and become the strongest applicants possible.

name: Sri Lankan Student Association
description: The Sri Lankan Student Association is an organization at the University of Georgia that provide an opportunity to UGA community to explore the Sri Lankan History, Tradition, and Culture through events and communications with Sri Lankan community at UGA. The SLSA will play a pivotal role in providing a homely atmosphere for current and new Sri Lankan students.
summary: The Sri Lankan Student Association is an organization at the University of Georgia that provide an opportunity to UGA community to explore the Sri Lankan History, Tradition, and Culture through events and communications with Sri Lankan community at UGA.

name: Block and Bridle
description: Block and Bridle, or B&B, is extremely active on campus and produces several events annually. These events of activities include: The Little International Livestock Show (est. 1921), The Great Southland Stampede Rodeo (est. 1974), a Winter and Spring Formal, South Campus Tailgate, Summer Lamb Show, Steer & Heifer Show, Ag Day at Tate, Bull and Heifer Sale, and Animal and Dairy Science (ADS) Spring Awards Banquet. The UGA chapter also sends members to the National Block and Bridle Convention, as well as having hosted this convention in 2000. Block and Bridle provides funds for participants to represent the University of Georgia in the Southeastern Animal Academic Quadrathalon in addition to sponsoring judging teams within the Animal and Dairy Science Department. Bi-weekly meetings and socials are held at “The Pit”, a clubhouse built by members during the 70s, on South Milledge Avenue.
summary: The University of Georgia Block and Bridle Club is an organization dedicated to the study and promotion of the livestock industry and animal science. The club strives to bring together students, faculty, and local livestock producers in variety of events

name: 5 Rivers
description:
5 Rivers is the official fly fishing club of the University of Georgia. This club is the perfect community for those who care about the conservation of local fisheries, being outside, and fly fishing with friends. Join our GroupMe for the most up to date information: https://groupme.com/join_group/52475410/FrvSAr6o

Linktree: https://linktr.ee/ugafiverivers
summary:
5 Rivers is the official fly fishing club of the University of Georgia. Join our GroupMe for the most up to date information.
 https://groupme.com/join_group/52475410/FrvSAr6o

name: Nepalese Student Association
description: The purpose/mission of NSA@UGA shall be to spread the word about Nepal and its culture. It will also serve as a connecting organization for all the Nepalese students, staff, faculty at UGA, and their families, and also for other people residing around Athens-Clarke County and showing interest in Nepal and Nepalese culture. It will organize programs to increase the knowledge of Nepal and Nepalese culture in the UGA campus and also to help the newly arriving students.
summary:
NSA is formed by the current graduate and undergraduate students at UGA, who are 
originally from Nepal. The primary objective of the organization is to unite the Nepalese community in Athens so that we can share our cultures and traditions.

name: Sigma Tau Delta
description: We are the English Honors Society at the University of Georgia. Sigma Tau Delta confers distinction for high achievement in English language and literature in undergraduate, graduate, and professional studies. We promote interest in English language and literature and foster all aspects of the discipline of English, including literature, language, and writing. We also promote exemplary character, good fellowship among our members, exhibit high standards of academic excellence, and serve society by fostering literacy. Sigma Tau Delta, the International English Honors Society, Inc. has over 875 active chapters in the United States and abroad.
summary: We are the English Honors Society at the University of Georgia. Sigma Tau Delta confers distinction for high achievement in English language and literature in undergraduate, graduate, and professional studies

name: Biochemistry Undergraduate Society
description: We are a student organization at the University of Georgia focused on working with biochemistry-major and other STEM undergraduates. Our mission is to serve as a resource in engaging with faculty, arranging research and internship opportunities for professional development, and offering social and philanthropic opportunities for community involvement. All STEM students and faculty are welcome to join or attend events!
summary: Join BUS for biochemistry major-related social events, activities, professional opportunities, and engagement with the biochemistry community! All STEM students and faculty are welcome to join BUS or attend events!

name: Pharmaceutical and Biomedical Student Organization
description: This is the Department of Pharmaceutical and Biomedical Sciences student organization! We are an organization that requires no fees to join and welcomes students from the bachelor's, master's, and PhD programs. We plan to host exciting events that allow everyone to get out of the lab, out of the library, and remove all your stress. We also participate in community outreach by hosting science demonstrations, donating clothing, food, and emergency items to local shelters and food banks, and actively participating in Relay for Life.
summary: The main purpose of the organization is to provide a supportive student body and professional development opportunities to the students of the pharmaceutical and biomedical sciences department.

name: Sikh Student Association
description: SSA was founded on service and leadership. We plan to bring these ideals to UGA and set an example on campus with community service hours, dollars raised, and GPA. We also serve the purpose of engaging non-Sikhs in an ongoing educational dialog. The mission of the Sikh Student Association is to introduce to the UGA campus a forum for students to spread awareness about the Sikh community, to study and address issues that effect the Sikh community in Panjab and abroad, and to connect with other cultural, faith-based, and activist organizations. We spread knowledge on Sikh traditions, heritage, and culture, and social, political, and spiritual issues concerning Sikhs all across the globe.
summary: The Sikh Student Association was formulated with the intention to provide Athens Sikhs a forum for the discussion and advancement of the Sikh faith as well as to educate the greater campus about Sikhi and its many followers.

name: Society of Business Intelligence
description: The Society of Business Intelligence is primarily focused on developing and building the "hard" skills of all business and non-business students, which include data analytics skills such as Tableau, data visualization, SAS, and many more. Having these skills on your resume will enable an individual to stand out to TOP employers in the business industry, such as the Big Four. We will also provide ample opportunities to network with company executives and will do our best to make sure that by the end of the year, we've created more confident, well-rounded individuals that will later represent SBI, UGA, and Terry in the workplace.
summary: SBI is an organization dedicated to fostering the concrete technological and analytical skills needed to succeed in today's business world, and provide opportunities to find the intersection between business, technology, and leadership.

name: CHAARG at The University of Georgia
description:
CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged individuals for health + fitness. Too many college students rely solely on the elliptical for their exercise. CHAARG liberates students from the elliptical + shows them that fitness can [+ SHOULD] be fun! Since every person has a different preference of what fun is, we create opportunities for our CHAARG members to “find their fit.” 

Once a student joins CHAARG, they are welcomed into a supportive + empowering community of 10,000+ students! When you workout, you feel good — but, when you surround yourself with people to support you, you feel even better. CHAARG combines good friends + a good workout to keep every student feeling their happiest + healthiest self.
summary: CHAARG aims to ignite a passion in college-aged students for health + fitness through weekly workouts, small groups, socials, + more! Our goal is to build confidence and strength through friendships, exercise, + fun!

name: Child Life Organization
description: We exist to create community among all undergraduate and graduate students interested in the field of child life, using that community environment to equip students in developing leadership skills and personal competencies to be successful in the field. We exist to empower students in professional development and identity through networking and learning opportunities to connect with those in the profession of child life and other related fields. We exist to educate our campus and community on the importance of the field through awareness, public relations and community outreach. Finally, we exist to encourage the pediatric community through service towards the patients, families and staff.
summary: Child Life Organization is a student organization at UGA, striving to assist students in developing professional and leadership skills while educating our campus and community and encouraging the pediatric community through outreach and service.

name: Dogs for the Paws
description:
Join our GroupMe to stay updated with events and meetings! https://groupme.com/join_group/53089099/nJPaNDzW

We want to provide love and support to animals in need. As a team we work together to spread awareness, as well as working with multiple animal shelters to give them an extra helping hand. This organization is built upon its members love for animals and their determination to make a difference.
summary: Dogs for the Paws is a student organization with the goal to help animals in Athens by volunteering at shelters, hospitals, farms, and zoos. Carpooling is always available! We also fundraise and raise awareness.

name: Club Cricket
description: Club Cricket focuses on the training and competitive play of cricket among members. The club engages in drills and practice sessions to help improve and develop our skills. Club Cricket hopes to engage in matches with other clubs/teams in order for to have competitive games. The club also focus on engaging with interested students who want to learn cricket and more about the sport.
summary: Club Cricket is a club dedicated to the coaching and competitive play of cricket. We provide some equipment and can help people enjoy the sport of cricket. We encourage everyone with an interest in learning about and playing cricket to join!

name: Health for Kids
description: H4K’s mission is to connect Athens youth and UGA undergraduates with health education and wellness resources so that they may actively pursue a healthy lifestyle.
summary: Health For Kids facilitates UGA volunteers to teach interactive lessons about being healthy to elementary students in the local Athens community! Topics include mental health, first aid, dental hygiene, nutrition and much more!

name: Club Archery at UGA
description: Club Archery at UGA is a developed student organization and an official club sport here at UGA! We held our inaugural practices during the spring of 2017 and have since achieved club sport status, grown in numbers, and sent multiple archers to tournaments all over Georgia. We aim to be open to archers of all experience levels, ranging from the novice who has never picked up a bow to the accomplished national archer. If you are interested in joining the club and want to be on our email list or you would like information, please contact us at redandblackarchery@gmail.com.
summary:
Club Archery at UGA  is devoted to teaching and spreading the love of archery in the UGA student community.

We are open to everyone regardless of experience, no equipment necessary. Get involved here -> linktr.ee/ugaarchery

name: Alpha Epsilon Pi
description: The goal of Alpha Epsilon Pi is to help each student develop character, responsibility and a proper set of values through living together in brotherhood. Alpha Epsilon Pi prepares young men for their role in life as responsible citizens. The AEPi Omicron chapter is composed of the most dedicated and successful students at UGA. We pride ourselves on our ability to balance our academic and social lives. We consistently have one of the best GPAs on campus while still being heavily involved in weekly social events, intramural sports, philanthropy and other organizations around campus. Throughout the year, AEPi fills it's calendar with a plethora of events. This includes date nights, socials, a mountain retreat, band parties, and tailgates as well as much more. Our brotherhood is filled with members from across the United States, from differing backgrounds, studying various academic disciplines. We come together as brothers to celebrate one another and our collective accomplishments as a fraternity. Alpha Epsilon Pi is a Jewish organization, whose purpose is not specifically religious, but rather social and cultural in nature. Brotherhood in Alpha Epsilon Pi is open to all who are willing to espouse its values and mission. Tomorrow’s leaders are in our chapter today.

Greek Life Office

name: Alpha Gamma Rho
description: AGR is an agriculturally-based fraternity and enjoys a strong professional relationship with the University's south campus. However, a south-campus major or related background is not required for membership. A wide variety of colleges and majors at UGA are represented within our brotherhood.
summary: Professional and social fraternity aimed to increase exposure and strengthen appreciation of agriculture.

name: Alpha Tau Omega
description: The Alpha Beta chapter of the Alpha Tau Omega fraternity at the University of Georgia.
