Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Beta Theta Pi
description: Founded on August 8th, 1839 at Miami University, Beta Theta Pi is one of the nation's oldest social fraternities. Tracing our routes back to the first and oldest secret society on campus, the brothers of the Epsilon Epsilon chapter of Beta Theta Pi puts strong emphasis on the cultivation of well-rounded fraternity men. We believe in maintaining excellence in all aspects of fraternity, campus, and social life. Boasting eight John Reily Knox awards, and 24 Sisson Awards, we direct all strength and effort toward the achievement of these goals. Epsilon Epsilon drives men from several southern states outside of Georgia including South Carolina, Texas, Tennessee, Kentucky, Florida, and Louisiana. Also, Beta brothers are involved in prestigious organizations on campus including Student Judiciary, SGA, GIS, Corsair Society, Order of Omega, Terry Student Consulting, UGA Miracle, IFC, and other prominent groups. Beta's annual philanthropy event Barbecue with Beta has raised over $30,000 for the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention since 2022. Beta men are also a part of leadership for UGA Miracle, raising over $7,000 dollars benefitting CHOA in 2024. Beta along with Alpha Chi Omega, also raised $41,000 for relay for life in 2011, the most of any Greek organization. A Beta prides itself on an active social calendar that includes our annual formal in New Orleans, Nashville, or Miami, Spring Semi Formal, Ski Lodge party, Braves/Hawks date night, and socials with numerous sororities. Our social calendar culminates in our weeklong Beta Lei celebration in the spring.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Chi Phi
description: The Chi Phi fraternity, founded in 1824, is the oldest social fraternity in the country. The Eta Chapter was founded at the University of Georgia in 1867, the second fraternity founded on campus. Members of Chi Phi are involved in clubs and organizations across the UGA campus, including the Student Government Association, Inter-Fraternity Council, Corsair Society, Institute for Leadership Advancement (ILA), Student-Managed Investment Fund (SMIF), and Order of Omega. Our philanthropic efforts include annual fundraising basketball tournaments, blood drives, UGA Miracle, Operation Smile, a Sunshine Fund, and collaborations with the Backpack Project of Athens.
summary: Fraternity at the University of Georgia.

name: Chi Psi
description:
At Chi Psi, we pride ourselves as a fraternity with a deep rich history that provides a young man the opportunity to grow both academically and socially.  Both our National Central Office and our local Alumni Foundations offer a variety of programs and scholarships to help you through your time as an undergraduate and beyond.  As a “Pledge” you will automatically be participating in our Pledge Scholarship program which depending on your semester GPA can earn you a scholarship between $250 and $1,000.  Also, the “Pledge” with the highest GPA for the semester is awarded a $1,000. Scholarship.  Other scholarship opportunities are available to our undergraduates.  Our local Foundation awards a $1,000 scholarship annually to a well-rounded Brother who has academic achievement, fraternity involvement and campus involvement.  In addition, our National Fraternity offers a variety of scholarships that you can apply for on an annual basis.  We are a speaker’s program where alumni are invited to come and speak to the Brotherhood on their experience as an undergraduate, work experiences and life experiences.  These are invaluable as they give you a real-life perspective.

As a social fraternity, we have full calendar of fun filled events.  These include “socials” with sororities, our Mountain weekend getaway, our Formal and many other themed parties.  But the best times are being at the Lodge playing basketball, watching tv or just sitting on our awesome front porch watching the sights on Milledge Avenue. At Chi Psi, our goal is for  you to succeed both academically and socially and leave  with not only life long memories but lifelong “brothers” who you will share many experiences as you go through life.
summary:
Chi Psi is a Fraternity of gentlemen devoted to the personal and collective needs of its members.
A selective association of diverse individuals, we are dedicated to the cultivation of a unique environment that instills a lifelong commitment to brothers.

name: Delta Sigma Phi
description: Delta Sigma Phi is a values-based membership organization that seeks to enrich the lives of our members and the greater community. Our mission is to empower and encourage our members to become Better Men – men of courage, action and excellence.
summary: Delta Sigma Phi is a values-based membership organization that seeks to enrich the lives of our members and the greater community. Our mission is to empower and encourage our members to become Better Men – men of courage, action and excellence.

name: Delta Tau Delta
description: The Beta Delta Chapter of Delta Tau Delta Fraternity at the University of Georgia was founded on June 11, 1882. With thousands of alumni spanning almost a century and a half, the men of Beta Delta uphold our mission of being Committed To Lives of Excellence. Our four fundamental principles are Truth, Courage, Faith, and Power.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Kappa Alpha Order
description:
A Moral Compass for the Modern Gentleman

You come to college with your values intact; you’ve learned from family and friends what’s right and wrong. While in college, KA helps you keep your compass straight, develop your values-set and experiences, and points you in a great direction for your future. Then, with our history and your experience, your journey in life seems a little clearer. Choose any of the three paths below to learn more about Kappa Alpha Order and our members.
summary: Kappa Alpha Order - Gamma Chapter is a social fraternity and a fraternal order founded in 1865 at Washington College in Lexington, Virginia. The Gamma chapter was founded at UGA on December 21st, 1868.

name: Lambda Chi Alpha
description:
The Nu Zeta of Lambda Chi Alpha (Lambda Chi) at the University of Georgia was founded in 1915 under the ideals of inspiring and equipping men to lead an ethical life of growth, service, and leadership. We carry these ideals with us to this day as we strive to achieve perfect brotherly love. Men of Lambda Chi are scholars, leaders, and gentlemen in everything they do, both on and off campus. 

Lambda Chi is an organization focused on the continual betterment of its members. Lambda Chi incorporates the Ideal Man and Stead Leadership College programs which develop members from freshman to senior year both personally and professionally. These programs have created men such as the CEO and Founder of Raising Cane's, Todd Graves, current Georgia Governor Brian Kemp, Former Governor Joe Frank Harris, Former US Congressman Jack Kingston, and NFL Hall of Famer, Charley Trippi.
summary: We inspire and equip men to lead an ethical life of growth, service and leadership. Lambda Chi Alpha envisions a world where the ideal man pursues a life of respect and inclusion for all. A world where we support and encourage one another.

name: Phi Delta Theta
description:
The Georgia Alpha chapter of Phi Delta Theta was chartered at the University of Georgia in 1871 with adherence to three fundamental pillars with broad application to all facets of life: friendship, sound learning, and moral rectitude. The organization today remains as committed to these key principles as ever, exercising them through the consistent enactment of our mission to enrich not only the brothers of our fraternity, but the surrounding community as well. Through close-knit brotherhood, impactful campus involvement, and active philanthropy, the brothers of Phi Delta Theta contribute to the development of each other, just as they contribute to the world outside of fraternity life. 

We pride ourselves on our strong, close-knit brotherhood and the caliber of our chapter. Since 1871, Georgia Alpha has consistently produced some of Georgia’s greatest governors, lawyers, and businessmen, including the late Governors Vandiver and Busbee, and Olympic Committee Chairman and Chairman of Augusta National Billy Payne. Several members of our chapter serve in the Armed forces, and we are proud to count a recipient of the Congressional Medal of Honor among our distinguished Alumni.

Our philanthropic work with the Live Like Lou Foundation to fight ALS includes fundraising efforts that place the Georgia Alpha chapter as the top fundraising Phi Delta Theta chapter nationally, as well as engaging with local affected families through our Connect And Serve program.
summary: We are the Georgia Alpha chapter of Phi Delta Theta, chartered here at UGA in 1871. We pride ourselves on strong philanthropy and academics while providing brothers a full social calendar and strong brotherhood.

name: Phi Gamma Delta
description: The Kappa Deuteron chapter of Phi Gamma Delta. We pride ourselves in academics, athletics, campus involvement, and philanthropy.
summary: Fraternity at the University of Georgia.

name: Phi Kappa Tau
description: The Beta Xi chapter of Phi Kappa Tau is a social fraternity at the University of Georgia founded on the principles of fellowship, knowledge, and truth. Our brothers follow the mission, "to champion a lifelong commitment to brotherhood, learning, ethical leadership and exemplary character," in the way we support one another through our fraternity proceedings. Phi Taus challenge one another to grow into men of distinction through our commitment to academics, strong social ties, and steadfast morals. Phi Kappa Tau appreciates the principles of brotherhood each week through our various social events, brotherhood events, athletic events, and community efforts. Phi Tau supports The SeriousFun Children's Network, a nonprofit organization that provides excellent medical care along with a camp experience to children in need.
summary: The Beta Xi chapter of Phi Kappa Tau is a social fraternity at the University of Georgia founded on the principles of fellowship, knowledge, and truth.

name: Phi Kappa Theta
description: The mission of Phi Kappa Theta is to foster a brotherhood of exceptional men and guide them to become individuals who routinely display passion and dedication in their endeavors, always maintain integrity and humility, and show the ability to lead through influence. The Delta Rho chapter of Phi Kappa Theta was originally founded at the University of Georgia in 1963.
summary: Phi Kappa Theta actively develops men to be effective leaders who passionately serve society, Fraternity and God.

name: Pi Kappa Alpha
description:
Pi Kappa Alpha was founded at the University of Virginia in 1868 and has grown to become one of the largest national fraternities with more than 200 active chapters and over 250,000 initiates. The Alpha Mu Chapter at the University of Georgia was founded in 1908, making Pike one of the oldest fraternities on campus. In 2009, our chapter moved from 360 South Lumpkin Street to a new house in Greek Park by Sanford Stadium. 

The Alpha Mu brotherhood strongly emphasizes excellence in academics, athletics, leadership and community service. These qualities are exemplified by Alpha Mu’s representation in club athletics, IFC, SGA, Order of Omega, Corsair Society, UGA Miracle, UGA HEROs, Terry Management Society, Apollo Society, The Kennedy Society, SMIF, The Backpack Project, National Society of Collegiate Scholars, Golden Key Honor Society, and the UGA Honors Program. Our biannual philanthropy event – VB4ESP – is a beach volleyball tournament benefitting Extra Special People Inc. in Watkinsville, GA.

While academics and integrity are the main focus of Alpha Mu, the brothers still find time to continue the tradition of a strong social calendar. Our brothers enjoy numerous date nights, band parties, and socials throughout the year including our Epicurean Ball Formal in New Orleans, Mountain Weekend in Tennessee, and our annual series of concerts called Pike’s Peak. The Alpha Mu Chapter of Pi Kappa Alpha has and will continue to develop young men into leaders and gentlemen in the classroom and around campus. 

If you are interested in recruitment events and opportunities, please contact one of our recruitment chairmen listed here: https://georgiapikes.com/executive-committee/. Additionally you can send an email to PikeAlphaMu@gmail.com.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Pi Kappa Phi
description:
For over a century, Pi Kappa Phi has distinguished itself through strong brotherhood, academics, philanthropy, and a full social calendar here at UGA. We are proud to announce the completion of our three-million-dollar house renovation and expansion, providing one of the best living experiences on campus. Now the newest and largest fraternity house on Milledge, we boast over thirteen thousand square feet of lounge rooms, study rooms, a full kitchen and chef, an artificial turf front lawn, a basketball court, and double the living capacity. In addition, we host date nights, socials, tailgates, beach weekend, mountain weekend, parents weekend, an annual philanthropic dodgeball event, and our annual Rose Ball Formal.

Pi Kappa Phi is the only fraternity with its own philanthropy. The Ability Experience works to build a more inclusive community for people living with disabilities. 

In addition to being members of Pi Kappa Phi, several individuals hold leadership positions across campus such as the UGA Miracle team, Student Ambassadors, SGA, IFC exec, Georgia Athletics, and Wishmakers. We also excel in academics, once again maintaining a higher average GPA than men not involved in Greek life at UGA.
summary: Social Fraternity that continues a long standing brotherhood dedicated to service, philanthropy, and friendship

name: Sigma Alpha Epsilon
description: The Georgia Beta chapter of Sigma Alpha Epsilon here at UGA was the first Greek-letter society founded on campus in 1865. We are also the oldest SAE chapter in continuous existence in the country. Notable alumni include 2 US senators, 3 governors of the state of Georgia, 2 UGA presidents, and the current head football coach Kirby Smart. The historic Ross Crane House, built in 1842, is located in the heart of downtown Athens, making it an easy walk to campus. We annually host “Showercap” in the spring, an SAE tradition since 1979. In 2024 alone, our chapter raised $113,727 for the Ham Ansley Memorial Fund, National Suicide Prevention, The Jack O'Kelley Foundation, The Gleaton Pace Jones Foundation, DGD Outdoors, Campus Outreach, and UGA Miracle. Chapter members have a strong representation in Terry College, IFC, SGA, The Corsair Society, UGA Miracle, Relay for Life, Student Managed Investment Fund, Order of Omega, and the honors program.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Sigma Chi
description: Sigma Chi International Fraternity is one of the largest North American fraternal literary societies. The fraternity has 244 active chapters and 152 alumni chapters across the United States and Canada and has initiated more than 350,000 members.
summary: The Delta chapter of Sigma Chi Fraternity celebrated its 152nd anniversary on March 12th, 2024.

name: Sigma Nu
description: Sigma Nu is one of UGA’s oldest fraternities with over 3000 members initiated in its 150-year history. Mu Chapter is currently the oldest continuous Sigma Nu chapter in the nation, winning the National Rock Chapter Award for Excellence over the past 18 years. The Mu Chapter of Sigma Nu was the UGA IFC chapter of the year in 2021 and 2022. Outstanding UGA Sigma Nu members include the former president of CNN, the founder of Aflac, and multiple Georgia governors and senators. Sigma Nu boasts a full social calendar with events and unique date nights such as Woodstock, Oktoberfest, and our White Rose Formal. As the closest fraternity house to Sanford Stadium, we pride ourselves on our tailgates being within walking distance to the game. Sigma Nu is active on campus with a Foundation Fellow, multiple IFC executive members, leaders in the Student Government Association, Order of Omega members, Leonard Leadership Scholars and Fellows, future Marines, Corsair Society members, SMIF members, UGA Honors students, leaders in MEDLIFE, and more. Sigma Nu is committed to philanthropic efforts, hosting Jam For Cam, the largest Greek Life philanthropy event, which has raised over $1,600,000 since its creation in 2018 benefitting the Melanoma Research Foundation, American Cancer Society, and Camp Carolina. Sigma Nu’s annual full-pad charity football game, The Classic City Bowl, is played against members of another fraternity to raise money for Jam for Cam. The new Classic City Open has also become a tradition with proceeds also going towards Jam for Cam. Members also volunteer weekly at Extra Special People and are involved with UGA service organizations such as UGA HEROs and UGA Wishmakers. Mu Chapter’s active members completed over 3000 hours of community service this past semester volunteering for local organizations. Brothers of Sigma Nu are leaders in Greek Life and across all aspects of student life at UGA.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Sigma Phi Epsilon
description:
Sigma Phi Epsilon (SigEp) was founded at Richmond College in 1901 by Carter Ashton Jenkens. He proclaimed “this fraternity will be different” and envisioned a place where young men, bound by love, could be surrounded by leaders and high achievers. Based on the principles of virtue, diligence, and brotherly love, the men within Sigma Phi Epsilon (GA Delta) strive and work, together, to be better versions of themselves, all while building life-long relationships. The brothers do this by being leaders, scholars, athletes, and gentleman on campus and in their community. While they understand that recognition does not determine the men they are or the men they will become, SigEp GA Delta members continuously bring home IFC and campus awards, highlighting that their model works.

Additionally, in order to further the development of their members, SigEp has incorporated “The Balanced Man Program” which could best be defined as an alternative to the traditional pledgeship. Every member goes through a four-year program, each with new challenges designed to sharpen the men they are and make a lasting impact on their lives, not just their time in college. The program facilitates becoming and staying involved all the way up until graduation, not only in the fraternity but around campus.

During one’s time in Sigma Phi Epsilon, they will be pushed to become a better version of themself, all while building relationships that will last a lifetime.
summary: Sigma Phi Epsilon Fraternity is a student group that bases its foundation around three cardinal principles, Virtue, Diligence, and Brotherly Love in an effort to ensure that all members achieve the most beneficial experience possible while at UGA.

name: Sigma Pi
description: Sigma Pi was founded in 1897 on the principles of chivalry, scholarship, service, and brotherhood. The Alpha Phi Chapter here at UGA has carried out these ideals in a long and distinguished tradition of excellence since its inception in 1948. With over 113 chapters and 90,000 initiates, Sigma Pi continues to be one of the top fraternities in the country. At Alpha Phi, we believe that the college man should have a diverse and profitable experience. Academically, Sigma Pi is well represented at UGA by brothers in the Delta Epsilon Iota, Golden Key, ODK and Phi Kappa Phi honor societies. On campus, our Brothers are involved with over 20 different organizations. Our chapter also proudly participates in many venues of community service. Notably, our chapter was one of the largest fundraising fraternities for UGA Miracle during their 2024-2025 fundraising cycle. We maintain an active social calendar that includes tailgates for football games, socials, date nights, formal, Founder's Day, Parents Weekend, and many band parties. Ultimately, Sigma Pi Fraternity offers the exceptional college man a balanced college career based on brotherhood, traditional values, and excellence.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Tau Epsilon Phi
description: Established in 1919, the Nu Chapter of Tau Epsilon Phi is a close-knit brotherhood bound by the ideals of Friendship, Chivalry, and Service. TEP excels in all aspects of campus life including academics, philanthropy, and social life.
summary: Established in 1919, the Nu Chapter of Tau Epsilon Phi is a close-knit brotherhood bound by the ideals of Friendship, Chivalry, and Service. TEP excels in all aspects of campus life including academics, philanthropy, and social life.

name: Tau Kappa Epsilon
description:
Tau Kappa Epsilon at the University of Georgia is dedicated to building better men for a better world through leadership, scholarship, service, and brotherhood. TKE provides a supportive environment where members grow personally and professionally while contributing to the UGA and Athens communities.

Our members strive for excellence in academics, leadership, and campus involvement, with a strong emphasis on philanthropy and community outreach. Through social events, service initiatives, and leadership development opportunities, the Xi-Lambda Chapter helps members develop lifelong friendships and the skills necessary for success beyond college.
summary: Tau Kappa Epsilon at UGA is a values-based fraternity dedicated to building better men through leadership, scholarship, service, and brotherhood. We empower members to excel academically, grow personally, and positively impact our community.

name: Theta Chi
description: Founded on the principle of friendship through the mutual assistance of every member, Theta Chi aims to provide its members with an enjoyable, fulfilling college experience that prepares them for rewarding and successful lives after graduation. Our maxim, "Alma Mater First and Theta Chi for Alma Mater" ensures that every man focuses first and foremost on the importance of learning and dedication to schoolwork followed by loyalty and support of fraternity. The Delta Beta Chapter of Theta Chi was installed at the University of Georgia in 1949 by three Georgia Tech transfers, Robert Brown, Truitt James, and Charles Shepherd. Over the last 65 consecutive years on campus, over 1,200 brothers have been initiated into our chapter. Many have gone on to have successful careers using the foundation and principles they have gained through their experiences here, both from the University's goals of wisdom, justice, and moderation, and as a part of our chapter.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Zeta Beta Tau
description:
Zeta Beta Tau

A Historic Return. A Bold New Beginning.

Zeta Beta Tau (ZBT), the nation’s first and largest Jewish fraternity, proudly returns to the University of Georgia with the re-establishment of the Mu Chapter. With over a century of history on campus, ZBT invites a new generation of Founding Fathers—leaders ready to shape the chapter’s culture, values, and legacy.

Founded on the principles of Intellectual Awareness, Social Responsibility, Integrity, and Brotherly Love, ZBT is an all-inclusive brotherhood that honors its proud Jewish heritage while welcoming members from all backgrounds. In 1989, ZBT became the first fraternity to eliminate pledging. All brothers are fully initiated within 72 hours, ensuring respect, equality, and true brotherhood from day one.

ZBT’s signature program, The Journey, replaces the traditional pledge process with a focus on education, personal growth, inclusion, and leadership development—promoting lifelong bonds and meaningful engagement.

Our brothers are involved across campus in Student Government Association, Boys and Girls Club Athens, Ars Gratia Artis, and Intramural Sports, demonstrating leadership in service, the arts, and student life. ZBT is engaged with philanthropy through partnerships with Gift of Life and Children’s Miracle Network.

From socials, parties, and formals to executive brotherhood retreats, ZBT offers memorable experiences alongside opportunities for leadership and service. Whether you're looking to lead, connect, or grow, ZBT provides a platform to elevate your college experience and build lasting friendships.
summary:
Greek Life Office

description: Alpha Chi Omega is a sorority on campus based on the ideas of sisterhood, philanthropy, and friendship. Our philanthropy is domestic violence awareness, and we work closely with Project Safe.

Alpha Chi Omega is a sorority on campus based on the ideas of sisterhood, philanthropy, and friendship.

name: Alpha Gamma Delta
description: Alpha Gamma Delta is a sorority comprised of over 300 women and our recruitment process occurs during the beginning of the fall and spring semesters. As women of Alpha Gam, we are committed to living out the ideals of sisterhood, authenticity, and love for our community. The cherished friendships within this chapter and the shared loyalty to one another can be seen in everything from our dedication to our philanthropy to our social events. Our official philanthropy is Fighting Hunger through the Alpha Gamma Delta Foundation and we have two main events throughout the school year to help us raise money! More information about our organization can be found on our external website.

name: Alpha Omicron Pi
description: Alpha Omicron Pi is a Panhellenic sorority at the University of Georgia. Alpha Omicron Pi was founded in 1897 on a promise to serve not only one another but the greater community as well. This commitment reflects a philosophy of friendship, concern, and usefulness in the world.
summary: Alpha Omicron Pi is a current University of Georgia Panhellenic sorority. Our organization was founded in 1897 on the ideals of character, dignity, scholarship, and college loyalty.

name: Chi Omega
description: Chi Omega is a National Sorority a part of the National Panhellenic Council (NPC) and the Mu Beta chapter here at UGA is part of the UGA Panhellenic Council. It is a women's organization that stands for friendship, personal integrity, service to others, academic success and intellectual pursuits, community and campus involvement, and personal and career development. Our National vision statement is "Sisters inspired by our values who serve the world while keeping Chi Omega ever at heart."

name: Delta Delta Delta
description: The Purpose of Delta Delta Delta shall be to establish a perpetual bond of friendship among its members, to develop a stronger and more womanly character, to broaden the moral and intellectual life, and to assist its members in every possible way.

name: Delta Gamma
description:
"The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to foster high ideals of friendship among college women, promote their educational and cultural interests, create in them a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them high qualities of character." Consistent with our Founders' thoughts in 1873, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our vows of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our Fraternity.

 

Delta Gamma is more than just a collegiate experience; it is a lifetime of commitment, support and opportunity awaiting you. It marks the beginning of a relationship that will last for a lifetime.

name: Delta Phi Epsilon Sorority
description: The sisters of Delta Phi Epsilon make up a group of women who are philanthropic, passionate and driven. We strive every day to uphold our three founding principles: justice, sisterhood, and love. We live by the motto "Esse Quam Videri," which means "to be rather than to seem to be."  We start our journey together as college students and create bonds that will last a lifetime.
summary: DPhiE is a Panhellenic sorority within the Greek Life Office at UGA. We strive every day to uphold our three founding principles: justice, sisterhood, and love, and we live by the motto "Esse Quam Videri," which means "to be rather than to seem to be."

name: Delta Zeta
description:
Delta Zeta was founded in 1902 at Miami University in Oxford, Ohio. Our six founders joined together to form a social sorority with a vision of lifetime sisterhood. Delta Zeta has now grown into 170 collegiate chapters across North America enriching the lives of over 260,000 initiated members.

The Zeta Pi chapter at the University of Georgia was founded in 1961. We currently have 340 members with an average GPA of 3.59. Delta Zeta women are empowered to lead and serve. Every member is encouraged to join organizations outside of the sorority and are provided many resources to promote success. For almost 60 years, our members have been happily serving the Athens community and will continue to do so in the coming years.
summary: Panhellenic Sorority

name: Gamma Phi Beta
description: Our chapter strives to inspire the highest type of womanhood through community service, academic achievement, and commitment to sisterhood.
summary: Welcome to the Delta Upsilon chapter of Gamma Phi Beta at the University of Georgia!

name: Kappa Alpha Theta
description: In establishing Kappa Alpha Theta in 1870, our founders created a women’s fraternity in which they could learn, grow, and be part of something larger than themselves. Kappa Alpha Theta nurtures each member throughout her lifetime, offering opportunities for intellectual and personal growth. Our vision is to lead every member to personal excellence through lifelong sisterhood, and along with many others, our core values are scholarship, service, leadership, personal excellence, and friendship/sisterhood.

name: Kappa Kappa Gamma
description:
Joining Kappa is unlike joining any other group or club. Our members dream boldly and live fully as they carve their own paths in the world around them. In Kappa, you’ll find a lifelong network of loyal women who can’t wait to meet you!

Throughout each year, some of our favorite activities include our Kappa Krawfish Boil, cheering on the dawgs, and 5K for Kappie. As always, please feel free to reach out to anyone on our Chapter Council with any questions you may have. We cannot wait to meet you! Kappa's it!
summary: Welcome to all things UGA Kappa! My name is Sydney Chandler, and I am ecstatic to be able to lead such a wonderful group of young women. I believe that our chapter is known for being genuine, outgoing, and motivated. We cannot wait to meet you!

name: Phi Mu
description: Established in 1921 as the oldest founding chapter on campus, Phi Mu empowers ambitious women with lifelong personal enrichment rooted in our values of love, honor, and truth. We offer vibrant member experiences that build character, cultivate confident leaders, encourage leadership aspirations, and provide endless opportunities for community-minded women to make a meaningful impact through service and philanthropy. At our core, Phi Mu fosters a lasting sisterhood, recognizing that our deep connections are essential to our mutual success.

name: Pi Beta Phi
description: An organization whose mission is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential, and enrich lives through community service. The vision is to be recognized as a premier organization for women by providing lifelong enrichment to our members and contributing to the betterment of society.
summary: An organization whose mission is to promote friendship, develop women of intellect and integrity, cultivate leadership potential, and enrich lives through community service.

name: Sigma Delta Tau
description: This Sigma Delta Tau Eta chapter was chartered at the University of Georgia on April 6, 1924 as the fourth sorority on UGA's campus. In response to mostly closed doors, and as a way to meet their own social and housing needs, our local founders established a sorority which would respect the individuality of its members. The personal growth and social development of each individual was the basis upon which the new organization would be built. Today, we carry the legacy of our chapter founders by providing a safe space for UGA women to be themselves and build strong foundational skills in order to have a positive effect on UGA's campus. We fundraise for PCAA along with a local Athens charity every year, and along the way, we participate in various sisterhood activities to help facilitate long lasting friendships for our chapter members.

name: Sigma Kappa
description:
Membership in Sigma Kappa is not just for four years, it's for life.

Consistent with our Founders’ thoughts in 1874, our primary purpose is to create an environment for our members in which lasting friendships and our values of sisterhood emanate all aspects of our organization. We encourage an atmosphere which will foster high ideals of friendship among college women, promote their educational and cultural interests, create in them a true sense of social responsibility and develop in them high qualities of character.

Sigma Kappa is more than just a collegiate experience; it is a lifetime of commitment, support and opportunity awaiting you. It marks the beginning of a relationship that will last for a lifetime.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Zeta Tau Alpha
description: The Gamma Pi chapter of Zeta Tau Alpha received its charter for the Gamma Pi chapter in 1949. At the University of Georgia, the Zetas strive to reflect their open motto “Seek the Noblest” in every aspect. The Gamma Pi chapter, chartered in 1949, is committed to excellence and continually receives national awards of the highest distinction.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity, Inc.
description: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc. is a service organization that values scholarship, manly deeds, and love for all mankind.

name: Omega Psi Phi
description:
Omega Psi Phi Fraternity, Inc. is the first international fraternal organization founded on the campus of a historically black college.

On the evening of November 17, 1911, Omega Psi Phi was founded inside the Science Building (later renamed Thirkield Hall) at Howard University located in Washington, D.C. The founders were three undergraduates — Edgar Amos Love, Oscar James Cooper and Frank Coleman. Joining them was their faculty adviser, Ernest Everett Just.

From the initials of the Greek phrase meaning, “friendship is essential to the soul“, the name Omega Psi Phi was derived. That phrase was selected as the motto.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Phi Beta Sigma
description: The Zeta Nu Chapter of Phi Beta Sigma Fraternity, Incorporated would like to welcome you to our website. Chartered in 1974, on the campus of the University of Georgia, Zeta Nu has worked diligently for the past 49 years to create a "Culture for Service" in both the university and Athens-Clarke County communities. We truly pride ourselves on the work we do to bring the light of Sigma to others as one of the premiere student organizations on the University of Georgia's campus. We hope you will take a look around our website and learn a little bit more about us. Should you have any other questions, please feel free to contact any of the brothers you see around.
summary:

description: The Gamma Chi Chapter of Zeta Phi Beta Sorority, Incorporated was chartered February 11, 2024 at the University of Georgia by 11 women.

name: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description: Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. is a non-profit, collegiate organization founded by seven young African American women on the predominantly white campus of Butler University in Indianapolis, Indiana. With all of our founders being educators, we strive to promote education, leadership, and empowerment, as well as service to our community. For more information about the organization, please visit www.sgrho1922.org. To find out more about the SGRhos here at UGA, visit us at www.ugasgrhos.wixsite.com/website.
summary:
Greek Life Office Roster for CRETS EMS:
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority, Inc. is an international service organization with a mission to enhance the quality of life for women and their families globally, while promoting leadership, sisterhood, and scholarship.

name: Alpha Sigma Rho
description:
We, the Alpha Sigma Rho Sorority, recognize the necessity of promoting sisterhood and Asian-Awareness within the university campus and the community while in the process of pursuing academic excellence as well as interpersonal growth, moral development, and strength in unity.

Mission: To advance involvement in intercultural relationships and its associated interests and to foster and promote diversity. 

Vision: Achievement of academic excellence as well as interpersonal growth, moral development, and strength in unity.

Goals: To promote Asian awareness and sisterhood. 

Motto: "Strength in Unity"
summary: Alpha Sigma Rho Sorority Inc. is the first Asian-interest (but not exclusive) sorority founded in GA and the South. Since 1998, our organization has strived to promote academic excellence, interpersonal growth, moral development, and strength in unity.

name: Delta Epsilon Psi
description: Delta Epsilon Psi is a South Asian Social and Service Fraternity. We are apart of the Multi Cultural Greek Council and take part in countless events with other fraternities and sororities who are in MGC as well. We focus a large part of our efforts to helping our philanthropy, JDRF. Their goal is to help children with type 1 diabetes.
summary: We are a South Asian Social and Service Fraternity who’s goals are to expand throughout the community and to help raise awareness and donations for our philanthropy, JDRF.

name: Delta Phi Omega Sorority, Inc.
description: Established in 1998, Delta Phi Omega has grown to over 50 chapters and colonies across the nation. We are one of the largest, strongest, fastest-growing sororities of its kind. Membership in our organization provides a lifetime of friendships, numerous opportunities for personal and professional growth, and an unforgettable collegiate experience that you can choose to continue after graduation, upon becoming an alumna.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Lambda Phi Epsilon
description:
Lambda Phi Epsilon was founded on February 25, 1981 by a group of nineteen dedicated men led by Principal Founder Master Craig Ishigo. The goal of the founders was to transcend the limitations faced by traditional Asian-interest organizations on campus. While these organizations were often split along lines of national origins, the founders sought an establishment that drew its membership equally from the different segments of the Asian American community. Their vision was that the members would eventually become the leaders of their respective ethnic communities, and thus, bridge the gaps fragmenting the Asian American community through their affiliation with a common organization.

Today, Lambda Phi Epsilon is widely renowned as the preeminent international Asian-interest fraternal organization, providing outstanding leadership, philanthropy, and advocacy in the community.
summary: The AX chapter of Lambda Phi Epsilon has been developing leadership, philanthropy, and advocacy skills in its members since the chapter's establishment on June 10th, 2001. Since then, the AX chapter has been a strong leader in UGA's multicultural scene.

name: Sigma Sigma Rho Sorority, Inc.
description: Nationally founded in 1998 and chartered as the first South-Asian based sorority (not South Asian exclusive) at the University of Georgia in 2007, Sigma Sigma Rho is a social and service based sorority. With becoming a member of our sorority, you’ll find a supportive community of women who uphold the principles of Sisterhood, Society, and Remembrance with unwavering dedication and passion. With Sisterhood, we believe that by uplifting one another, we can achieve greatness together. Society is whom we give to and our national philanthropy is Domestic Violence Awareness & Prevention. We are committed to making a positive impact on our communities, both locally and globally. Remembrance is what lies in our hearts and we honor those who paved the way for us to be here; not only our founding members, but also by embracing our cultural ancestry.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Xi Kappa Asian-Interest Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Xi Kappa Inc. (ΞΚ, also known as XK) is established as the first Asian-interest fraternity in the Southeast United States. Xi Kappa has grown to represent 3 collegiate schools in the U.S. and is still growing. Based at three institutions, Xi Kappa has become an example of how Asian Americans of different ethnicities and backgrounds can unite under the banner of brotherhood and become a positive force in today’s society.

Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc. is committed to fostering individual growth through ensuring personal well-being, promoting scholarship, developing leadership skills, advocating cultural awareness, and instilling fellowship through unwavering Brotherhood.
summary: Xi Kappa Fraternity, Inc. is an Asian-interest fraternity committed to unwavering Brotherhood through its core values of Honor, Loyalty, and Respect.

name: BOLD Modeling Agency
description:
BOLD Modeling Agency is a dynamic organization founded by POC students with a compelling vision to celebrate, mold, and empower unrepresented faces in the fashion model scene, both on and off campus. Our agency's core mission is to shine a spotlight on a diverse array of cultures, religions, genders, sexual orientations, sizes, and creeds through the transformative power of fashion and the arts.

At BOLD Modeling Agency, we create a supportive and inclusive space that welcomes individuals of all backgrounds and experiences who are interested in pursuing creative careers such as modeling, photography, cosmetics, hair styling, etc. Our goal is to nurture and develop raw talent, offering opportunities for aspiring models to build their portfolios and embark on exciting career journeys.

Through professional development workshops, creative collaborations, and meaningful partnerships, we aim to empower our student models to embrace their unique identities and showcase their authentic selves. We believe in celebrating the beauty of diversity, and we strive to challenge traditional beauty standards, paving the way for positive and meaningful change within the fashion industry.

As an organization driven by passion and purpose, we are committed to breaking barriers and amplifying the voices of underrepresented communities. BOLD Modeling Agency envisions a future where everyone can see themselves reflected on the runway, in fashion campaigns, and beyond.
summary: BOLD Modeling Agency is an organization started by POC students with the goal of celebrating, molding, and empowering unrepresented faces in the fashion model scene both on and off campus.

name: Dodd Ambassadors
description:
The Dodd Ambassadors are the Face of Lamar Dodd School of Art. They are a select group of students dedicated to enhancing the image of and interest in the Lamar Dodd School of Art at the University of Georgia (UGA).  Ambassadors assist at School or University sponsored events and are available to speak on topics related to the Art school before groups, schools, clubs, assemblies, banquets or similar events.

Our Ambassadors can be found at every art exhibit openning or closing reception. They also support the UGA Spotlight on the Arts evenings at the Dodd.

Dodd Ambassadors are active in supporting at Dodd Award Presentations, Visiting Artist Lectures, Dodd Welcome Back Party, Dodd Graduation Reception, Career Days and More.  Our ambassadors are the key to prospective student recruitment and provide tours for these students as well as guided tours for visiting High Schools from around the nation.

Our main event of the year is the annual "Dodd Market" each fall and spring, supported by UGA's Parent Leadership Council. This event provides a forum for students to sell their own art and gain professional experience without financial barriers. It is also an opportunity for the UGA and Athens community to experience and support the talent at Lamar Dodd School of Art.
summary: The Dodd Ambassadors are a select group of students dedicated to enhancing the image of and interest in the Lamar Dodd School of Art at the University of Georgia

name: Christian Medical and Dental Association
description:
The Christian Medical & Dental Associations (CMDA) is made up of the Christian Medical Association (CMA) and the Christian Dental Association (CDA). CMDA provides resources, networking opportunities, education and a public voice for Christian healthcare professionals and students.

CMDA at UGA equips our members for their professional career by providing shadowing and volunteer opportunities, encourages our members to do their best amongst the competition within the healthcare field, and we choose to do so in order to glorify God and actively engage in His plan for our lives through the passion He has placed on our hearts. 

By being the "hands of Jesus" to needy people, CMDA seeks to fulfill His Great Commandment (Matthew 22:39; 25:36) and His Great Commission (Matthew 28:19).
summary: * EQUIP * ENCOURAGE * ENGAGE *

name: Partners in Health: Engage
description: PIH Engage supports the incredible work done by our parent group, Partners in Health. In 10 countries around the world, PIH has built model healthcare systems that are constantly revolutionizing what is considered possible in the field of global health. Their first projects abroad, in Haiti and Perú, are still relevant and actively helping impoverished communities receive the aid they desperately need. As a PIH Engage team, our work in Athens would ultimately make PIH's work possible. Of course, fundraising is a critical component in our work, and Engage teams around the country raised over $400,000 last year. Equally important is the political advocacy work that each team strives for. We have our eyes on current bills in Congress that would dramatically improve PIH's ability to create real, sustainable systems abroad. It's our goal to reach out to local congressmen who can push them forward. PIH Engage is an opportunity for everyday people to make an impact in global health.
summary: PIH Engage is a nationwide network of teams that support the work of Partners in Health, a global health non-profit. Our University of Georgia chapter strives to build a strong local community, advocate politically, and fundraise.

name: Salsa Club at UGA
description: Salsa Club at UGA is a group dedicated to the pursuit of increasing the awareness and appreciation of Cuban-style Salsa within the university and the surrounding area. We have weekly meetings to learn and explore various aspects of the dance.We offer beginner and intermediate dance lessons and explore Cuban music and dance.
summary: Salsa Club at UGA is a group dedicated to the pursuit of increasing the awareness and appreciation of Cuban-style Salsa within the university and the surrounding area. We have weekly meetings to learn and explore various aspects of the dance.

name: Men's Club Basketball
description: The Men's Club Basketball Team at The University of Georgia is a passionate group of individuals who meet regularly to teach, promote, and play basketball. We participate in the National Club Basketball Association, competing against other collegiate club teams across the southern region and across the nation.
summary: Men's Club Basketball is a group of competitive individuals who love the sport of basketball and represent UGA in the National Club Basketball Association, competing against other collegiate club teams regionally/nationwide.

name: Astronomy Club
description:
The Astronomy Club is an organization open to any students and faculty interested in astronomy! We are committed to spreading interest and education in astronomy among students and the Athens community through public outreach and frequent observing nights. Feel free to contact us if you're interested or have questions, or want to join our GroupMe!

 

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/95707262/J2QO7IyF
summary: The Astronomy Club is an organization for any students interested in astronomy! We are committed to spreading interest and education in astronomy among students and the Athens community.

name: Physics Club
description:
The Physics Club welcomes all students who have an interest in Physics or Astrophysics and is open to all majors. We host events that are geared toward the exchange of ideas and discussion on topics in the physical sciences. We aim to inform members about research and career opportunities in Physics, promote connections among students and faculty members, help with developing problem-solving skills, and provide general mentorship to students navigating the Physics and Astrophysics majors.

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Click here to join our GroupMe: Physics Club GroupMe
summary: The Physics Club seeks to form a community of students with an interest in the Physical Sciences across all majors.

name: American Society of Law, Medicine, & Ethics
description:
The American Society of Law, Medicine, & Ethics (ASLME) is an academic organization aimed at fostering high-quality scholarship, debate, and critical thought at the intersection of law, medicine, and ethics. Concerned with questions of justice, wellbeing, and morality, the organization strives to mobilize dissatisfaction into purpose, tying our lives as students back into our duties as people. To this end, ASLME provides various writing, research, presentation, leadership, and community involvement opportunities for all members centered around their individual interests. With an emphasis on collective growth, connection, and creativity, ASLME is committed to cultivating an environment where your voice can thrive. 

Previous meeting topics: media ecology, attention economy, critical psychiatry, women’s health, emergency use authorizations, surveillance, and propaganda.

Meetings take place on a bi-weekly basis (first meeting is 2/10/26); consider joining us for engaging discussions and fun activities! Check out the official GroupMe for more information: https://groupme.com/join_group/112640437/s0uwqgZT

Spring 2026 Application: https://forms.gle/Jyhd8QfhQwVqoQEs9
summary: The mission of the American Society of Law, Medicine, & Ethics shall be to provide high-quality scholarship, debate, and critical thought for undergraduate students at the intersection of law, medicine, and ethics.

name: Soon Movement
description: Soon Movement is a Christian ministry organization. We meet every week to grow in faith, learn, and fulfill the Great Commission.
summary: Soon Movement is a Christian ministry organization. We aim to fulfill the Great Commission in the power of the Holy Spirit by winning people to faith in Jesus Christ, building them in their faith, and sending them to win and build disciples.

name: DECA
description:
DECA allows students to travel across the state and country in pursuit of business related competitions and leadership opportunities. These competitions can be a case study, business game, or prepared event, and will be practiced throughout the year before competition. DECA members have the opportunity to win awards at international competitions and apply for scholarships throughout the year. DECA also prepares emerging business leaders for the expectations of the business world through real world applications.

DECA Membership Promo
summary: DECA prepares emerging leaders and entrepreneurs for careers in marketing, finance, hospitality and management in colleges around the globe.

name: Rescue Paws USA @ UGA
description: As a student-run initiative, our mission as a campus club is to involve, educate, and unite people in order to help animals in the Athens, GA community. This includes organizing and connecting fosters and adoptions, raising awareness for animal care and protection, volunteering time and supplies, as well as fundraising to sponsor animal wellbeing and operations at affiliated rescues and shelters.
summary: Rescue Paws USA @ UGA works with local animal shelters to help homeless animals in the Athens area.

name: Kappa Kappa Psi - Kappa Mu Chapter
description:
The Kappa Mu Chapter is a brotherhood dedicated to the UGA Bands through service, support, leadership and appreciation for music; operating in a respectable and enjoyable manner, with integrity and pride. With a dedicated spirit of unity and cooperation, we are unequivocally committed to become the world standard of excellence for band fraternal organizations.

The Kappa Kappa Psi Creed reads as follows: We, the brothers of Kappa Kappa Psi, believe that service to the college or university band program fosters responsibility, loyalty, and leadership; that a spirit of brotherhood is enhanced by the participation in a band program; that music is a universal language and truly the greatest of the arts; and that through fraternal participation, each member will strive for the highest.
summary: The Kappa Mu Chapter is a brotherhood dedicated to the University of Georgia bands through service, support, leadership and appreciation for music.

name: Themed Entertainment Association
description:
The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) is a professional organization representing the world’s leading creators of theme parks, interactive experiences, museums, restaurants, and more. We are UGA's official chapter of the TEA NextGen program. If you're interested in theme park design, engineering, hospitality, or any of the other hundreds of fields themed entertainment can encompass, this club is the place for you!

 

Our goal is to build awareness of the themed entertainment industry, provide interested students a place to share ideas and passions, and connect members with the larger Themed Entertainment Association, a global organization that organizes professional events for the industry.

 

Activities include: monthly meetings to discuss a topic related to themed entertainment, scheduled speakers who work in the industry (think interior decorators, drone technicians, story writers, or even chefs), and occasional creative events, like casual design competitions or group outings. We will also send regular emails with reminders about the next meeting dates.

You can also join the official TEA NextGen Discord server here: https://discord.com/invite/6z4kxzjThF
summary: The Themed Entertainment Association (TEA) is an organization for creators of theme parks, interactive experiences, museums, restaurants, and more. It encompasses design, engineering, hospitality, and hundreds of other fields.

name: Table Tennis at UGA
description: We are officially a club sport. All skill levels are welcome! Practice is on Thursday 6:00pm to 8:00pm
summary: The University of Georgia Club Table Tennis fosters a positive environment for competition and friendship through table tennis. All skill levels are welcome.

name: Young Americans for Freedom
description:
Young Americans for Freedom serves as the Young America's Foundation’s chapter affiliate on college and high school campuses across the country. Founded by William F. Buckley, Jr. and a group of young conservatives in 1961 at his home in Sharon, Connecticut, Young Americans for Freedom today promotes to youth the principles of limited government, individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional values, as outlined in the Sharon Statement. The chapters accomplish this by participating in a wide range of campus initiatives, such as the 9/11: Never Forget Project, Freedom Week, meetings every other week, community service projects, among others; and by hosting prominent conservative speakers.

Join our GroupMe for updates: https://groupme.com/join_group/98456730/cc7Ckayf

Follow @uga.yaf on Instagram!
summary: Young Americans for Freedom promotes the principles of limited government, individual freedom, free enterprise, a strong national defense, and traditional values by participating in a wide range of campus initiatives.

name: Society for Human Resource Management
description:
The Society for Human Resource Management (SHRM) is the world’s largest HR professional society, representing 300,000 members in more than 165 countries. For nearly seven decades, the Society has been the leading provider of resources serving the needs of HR professionals and advancing the practice of human resource management.

The UGA Chapter of SHRM is currently housed in the Terry College of Business and is the first and only Human Resource Management student organization on campus. Our chapter holds meetings to help members learn more about the role Human Resources plays in the functioning of a business and the different career paths within HR. We aim to achieve these goals by organizing activities such as:

  • Resume, Interviewing and Negotiations Workshops

  • Employer Visits and Networking Opportunities

  • Case Study Preparation

  • Community Service Opportunities

  • Social Events with the Management Department

  • Experienced HR professionals Guest Speakers
summary: A society for aspiring Human Resource Professionals to develop necessary skill sets, network with professionals, learn more about different career paths in HR, and connect with like-minded peers with similar interests.

name: Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu Club
description: The Brazilian Jiu Jitsu Club is a place where students of any skill level can learn the art of Jiu-Jitsu. Come join us to learn technique, train hard, and get together with other students. We would like to provide a space for jiu-jitsu practitioners to train for competition as well.
summary: Come train Brazilian Jiu-Jitsu! We are providing a space to learn the sport of Jiu Jitsu, train hard, learn self defense, and have fun. No Experience is necessary.

name: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc.
description:
Since its inception in 1982, La Unidad Latina has remained on the vanguard of political and community empowerment by developing influential leaders that strive to exert knowledge and power into its peers in order to attain mutual success. We commit ourselves to academic excellence, leadership development and cultural enlightenment, enhanced by a diverse cognizant membership. La Hermandad strives to preserve and promote an inclusive intellectual environment for its members, in addition to the general community. In the execution of its conceived mission, La Unidad Latina will:

  • Provide Latino students with the academic, cultural and social support necessary to excel in institutions of higher learning
  • Increase opportunities for Latino children and adolescents to achieve in elementary and secondary schools
  • Develop leaders that shall provide, develop and implement the tools for community empowerment
  • Inspire ethnic pride and cultural awareness in the Latino community
  • Support the efforts to enhance the growth and well-being of the Latino community
  • Collaborate with individuals, organizations and institutions that will join our efforts to improve the conditions of the Latino community.
summary: La Unidad Latina, Lambda Upsilon Lambda Fraternity, Inc. seeks to take a leadership role in meeting the needs of the Latino community through academic achievement, cultural awareness, community service and promotion of the Latino culture and people.

name: Interfraternity Council
description: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the self-governing body of the 27 traditionally social fraternities at the University of Georgia. The IFC is located in the Greek Life Office on the first floor of the Tate Center along with the Multicultural Greek Council, the National Panhellenic Council, and the Panhellenic Council. A full time staff member advises and assists IFC in all matters. The executive board consists of six positions: President, VP of Philanthropy and Service, VP of Finance and Administration, VP of Recruitment, VP of Risk Management, and VP of Marketing and Communications. The IFC Clerk Program is conducted by the VP of Philanthropy and Service and provides second-year fraternity members the opportunity to work with the executive board. Two of these Clerks are also chosen every summer to serve as Recruitment Directors at New Student Orientation Sessions. The Interfraternity Council strives to represent all of its member chapters and the University of Georgia to the highest degree in all of its endeavors.
summary: The Interfraternity Council (IFC) is the self-governing body of the 26 traditionally social fraternities at the University of Georgia. The Interfraternity Council strives to represent the ideals of fraternity life in its endeavours.

name: Panhellenic Council
description:
The University of Georgia Panhellenic Council governs the female fraternities and sororities that are members of the National Panhellenic Conference (NPC). It promotes cooperation and cohesiveness within the Greek community, oversees formal recruitment for its member groups, and sponsors community service opportunities, social events, and educational programs throughout the year.
There are currently 20 Panhellenic chapters recognized at UGA, each excelling in areas of leadership, philanthropic involvement, campus involvement and academics. Sorority involvement continues to provide young women with the opportunity to be a part of the tradition of excellence UGA Greek Life strives to sustain.
summary: We promote cooperation and cohesiveness within the Greek community, oversee formal recruitment for its member groups, and sponsor community service opportunities, social events, and educational programs throughout the year.

name: Multicultural Greek Council
description: The Multicultural Greek Council is the governing umbrella council for the eleven cultural-based Greek organizations at the University of Georgia.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: National Pan-Hellenic Council
description: The National Pan-Hellenic Council is comprised of the Historically Black Greek-Lettered Organizations. The purpose of NPHC shall be to foster unanimity in thought of and action as far as possible in conduct of Greek letter in fraternities and sororities and to consider problems of mutual interests to its member organizations
summary: The National Pan-Hellenic Council is comprised of the active Black Greek-Lettered Organizations at the University of Georgia.

name: Delta Sigma Theta Sorority, Inc.
description: Our sorority is a public service organization committed to the constructive development of its members. The organization prides itself on sisterhood, scholarship, service, and social action. 112 years later, we continue to follow in the footsteps of our founders by living in their legacy, making a positive impact in the community, and being a voice for those in need.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: Backpack Project of Athens
description:
To learn more about The Backpack Project of Athens and our work, check our website!

To become a member, sign up here!

To volunteer with us, attend a Volunteer Training!

To sign up for service events, visit our GivePulse!

 

The Backpack Project of Athens (TBP/ATH) has four main objectives:

(1) to engage with the Athens homeless population,

(2) to host charitable volunteer programs based on service gaps identified in the community,

(3) raise money and help assemble and distribute homeless care backpacks, and

(4) to raise awareness about the causes of homelessness and how people can help on UGA's campus and in the Athens community. 

Founded in 2019, service, engagement, and awareness are all core tenets of The Backpack Project of Athens' mission. These foundations are brought to life through a team of University of Georgia students and faculty who seek to drive long-term improvement within the homeless community in Athens. TBP/ATH is a chapter of the 501©(3) nonprofit organization, The Backpack Project, Inc. The Backpack Project, Inc. was founded in April 2015 by UGA students.
summary: TBP/ATH is a student-run 501©(3) nonprofit organization that serves the homeless community in Athens, Georgia. We aim to engage with the Athens homeless population through volunteer programs, fundraising, and raising awareness.

name: HOSA - Future Health Professionals at UGA
description:
HOSA at UGA is a healthcare organization geared towards providing resources, information and guidance to UGA pre-health students which help students to become effective, compassionate health professionals and leaders in their chosen field of work.
 
Founded in 1976, HOSA now has over 4000 secondary and postsecondary/collegiate chapters in 48 state
associations, and over 185,000 members. All professional level schools of health professions prefer applicants, who demonstrate leadership skills and actual experience with health-related community service and academic
achievement.
 
HOSA membership provides students with information on these key opportunities and more.
summary:
HOSA is an international student-led organization run by and for
students interested in pursuing careers in the health professions through state and national conferences, competitive events, scholarships, and volunteer/community service opportunities.

name: Warnell Pre-Vet Club
description: The Warnell Pre-Vet Club is aimed at providing opportunities and resources for pre-veterinary students who would like to specialize in wildlife/exotics/zoological medicine. While the club originally formed as an outlet for Wildlife Pre-Veterinary majors, we are inclusive of all majors interested. We have speakers, volunteer opportunities, and help put on a few events every semester. To stay up-to date, join our GroupMe (link is provided) for club announcements, and email to request to join the list-serv for wildlife experience opportunities.
summary: We are a pre-vet club aimed at providing a resource for students interested in careers in wildlife/zoological/exotic medicine or other related wildlife fields.

name: Billiard Club of UGA
description: Student-led organization of billiard players. Competitive games offered are 8-ball, 9-ball, and more!
summary: Come join a group of students who all share the joy of playing pool in a friendly and competitive atmosphere! Players of all skill levels are welcome!

name: Georgia Law Mental Health Alliance
description: A student-driven organization dedicated to increasing knowledge and awareness of wellness and self-care for everyone at the University of Georgia School of Law.
summary: GLMHA aims to destigmatize mental health and provide a safe space to openly discuss mental health issues impacting the law school community and how we can work together to support one another during our time here.

name: John Quincy Adams Society
description:
We are centered on promoting educational activities related to U.S. foreign policy. In particular, we hope to elevate discussion of alternatives to the status quo, especially the virtues of a more restrained foreign policy. JQA has a centering vision: we aim to provoke greater conversation and avoid narrow dogmatism that wouldn’t befit an educational institution like our own. 

We take our name from America’s sixth president, who was an accomplished diplomat and helped lay the foundations of the early Republic’s approach to the world. He famously encapsulated that spirit so:
"Wherever the standard of freedom and independence has been or shall be unfurled, there will [America’s] heart, her benedictions and her prayers be. But she goes not abroad in search of monsters to destroy. She is the well-wisher to the freedom and independence of all. She is the champion and vindicator only of her own."

The United States is far bigger and far stronger now than it was in Adams’s day, and that means it is in better position than ever to serve as an exemplar of liberty, justice, and peace to the world. While remaining a paragon of liberal virtues at home, America’s relations with foreign nations should be characterized by commerce and cultural exchange rather than entangling military and political ties. These beliefs were, for much of America’s history, one of the main currents in its foreign-policy debate. Yet now, they are underrepresented in academia, in the policy process, and in the press. The result has been a string of unsuccessful, expensive, destabilizing interventions, and, more subtly, a decline in the ability of that debate to distinguish between truly vital interests and those that are secondary. A less rigorous, lower-quality debate hinders all viewpoints. The John Quincy Adams Society aims to revive that conversation.
summary: We are centered on promoting educational activities related to U.S. foreign policy. In particular, we hope to elevate discussion of alternatives to the status quo, especially the virtues of a more restrained foreign policy.

name: Climbing Club at UGA
description:
Climbing Club at UGA is a student-led group focused on providing more opportunities to climb outside. Whether your goal is to learn a little more about climbing in the Southeast, go outside for the first time, or send a 5.12 route, you have a place here! We choose trips, clinics, and activities that encompass a broad range of skills, so everyone can focus on their goals and interests.

We participate in bouldering, top-rope, and sport climbing at a number of outdoor and indoor locations throughout the southeast, including Alabama, Tennessee, the Carolinas, and Georgia. We also attend optional climbing competitions and stewardship events throughout the school year that we travel to and participate in. We accept people at all levels of experience!

After you request membership, join our GroupMe! https://groupme.com/join_group/95584474/PHRGKafP. We want to keep our GroupMe as an exclusive benefit for those who have paid dues, to get their money's worth; after joining the GroupMe, you will have a week to decide if you are interested in officially joining or not. If you have not paid dues by this time, you may be removed from the GroupMe.
summary: Climbing Club at UGA is a group of students focused on outdoor climbing skills and meeting people with similar goals and interests. We enjoy bouldering, top-rope, and sport climbing at a number of outdoor locations.

name: Serving Others Unconditional Love
description:
SOUL Video

SOUL is a faith-based service organization at UGA that allows students opportunities to get involved and volunteer throughout the Athens community. SOUL regularly volunteers with Athens PBJ, ESP, Arbor Terrace, and City of Refuge. We also host "Spread the Joy" events that are geared towards loving on people in the community by passing out notes and treats on UGA’s campus or in downtown Athens. The mission of SOUL is to brighten the lives of others through acts of kindness by building relationships with those that we serve.
summary: SOUL will brighten Athens with the opportunity for UGA students to see the impacts of hands-on service. With so much love to give, students will enhance lives through volunteer work at various local organizations. Building relationships through service.

name: National Alliance on Mental Illness on Campus UGA
description: The National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) is the nation’s largest grassroots mental health organization dedicated to building better lives for the millions of Americans affected by mental health conditions. NAMI on Campus UGA works to end the stigma that makes it hard for students to talk about mental health and get the help they need. We host mental health awareness events, have student-led mental health support groups, and offer mental health leadership training opportunities in NAMI programs through partnerships with NAMI National, NAMI Georgia, and NAMI Hall.
summary: The National Alliance on Mental Illness (NAMI) on Campus UGA is a mental health organization on campus, and as a part of the larger national organization, our mission is to provide advocacy, education, support, and public awareness about mental health.

name: Kpop Dance Club
description: K-pop Dance Club is the intersection of dancing, socializing, and a love of Korean music. Our goal is to give UGA students the opportunity to learn K-pop choreography and to express themselves all while bonding with a group of likeminded individuals. KDC is open to all students regardless of skill level and emphasizes comfort and fun to provide a safe space to learn your favorite K-pop dances. We frequently hold dance workshops throughout the semester and hope to see you there. KDC has an audition only performance group known as EnKore Dance Crew.
summary: Held together by common interests in dancing and Korean pop music, the UGA K-Pop Dance Club provides a fun environment for dancing. We dance to a variety of K-Pop Styles. Our workshops are taught by EnKore Dance Crew, an audition only subset of KDC.

name: MIS Advisory Board
description:
The MIS Advisory Board works with the MIS department to develop curriculum and courses, award scholarships to outstanding MIS students, and foster valuable interactions between companies, faculty and students. These companies provide significant insights and contributions to the MIS Department in exchange for access to our highly sought after students.

As student representatives on the board, we:

  • Work directly with Hugh Watson, our board’s director, assisting him in a variety of Board-related tasks. Student Board members will have one or more projects that they are responsible for.
  • Have a direct impact on the MIS program, interacting with senior Terry faculty and having the opportunity to share your unique perspective and voice peer concerns
  • Reap the benefits of developing both IT and business skills as you help advance the Board, generating new memberships, shaping strategy, and managing communications.
  • Receive a one-year personal membership to the board after you graduate. This will allow you to continue networking with industry professionals and gain access to industry thought leaders
summary: The MIS Advisory Board in the MIS Department's primary mechanism for interacting with the business community to ensure that Georgia's MIS graduates meet the needs of industry.

name: Mary Frances Early College of Education Student Ambassadors
shortName: MFECOE Student Ambassadors, College of Education Ambassadors
description: The MFECOE Student Ambassadors are diverse student leaders representing the Mary Frances Early College of Education who are committed to cultivating a sense of community through service. MFECOE Student Ambassadors help connect current and prospective MFECOE students to resources in the College and the University. We also serve as student representatives and hosts for alumni and donor events held by the College and University. Ambassadors also provide input to senior administration regarding student programs, course of study, and initiatives to improve student life within the College.
summary: The MFECOE Student Ambassadors represent the students of the Mary Frances Early College of Education. We help connect students to resources, help host official programming, and provide input to college administration in order to improve student life.

name: Law Student Chapter of the Federal Bar Association at UGA Law
description: The purposes of the Chapter shall be to further the objectives of the Federal Bar Association (hereinafter referred to as the "Association"), to represent the Association to law students, to stimulate the interest of law students in the activities of the Association, to conduct programs of interest and value to law students, and to assist in the establishment, improvement and coordination of active student organizations in each Chapter and Circuit of the Association.
summary: To represent the Federal Bar Association to law students, to stimulate the interest in activities of FBA, to conduct programs of interest and value, and to assist the establishment, improvement and coordination of active student organizations in FBA.

name: Mycology Graduate Student Organization
description: The purpose/mission of the Mycology Graduate Student Organization shall be to foster the exchange of ideas relating to all areas of Mycology. MGSO strives to create interdepartmental collaboration in addition to enriching the Fungal Biology community on campus and in the Southeast region of the United States.
summary: The purpose/mission of the Mycology Graduate Student Organization shall be to foster the exchange of ideas relating to all areas of Mycology

name: Women in Finance
description: UGA Women in Finance is a student-run organization affiliated with the Terry College of Business. Our mission is “to educate, empower, and unite a network of women endeavoring to create long lasting professional relationships and careers in the finance field.” We are dedicated to helping women succeed personally & professionally in all fields of finance through networking events, mentorship opportunities, business workshops, resume help, and other corporate events. Any major and all genders are welcome to apply! Membership is open to anyone and is not exclusive to women or female-identifying individuals.
summary: Women in Finance's mission is to educate, empower, and unite a network of students endeavoring to create long lasting professional relationships and careers in the finance field.

name: TEST_ORGANIZATION
description: More detailed description on student organization's Involvement Network page
summary: Short form summary of the organization to be displayed on the Involvement Network.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics
description: The primary goal of AIAA is to further our member's understanding of aerospace engineering through projects such as building high-powered rockets from scratch, assembling RC planes, and competing in international contests. We also aim to connect our members to aerospace industry professionals as well as local organizations that have the same passion for aerospace. AIAA is an organization that is open to all individuals, regardless of skill level, to gain a fundamental understanding of the aerospace industry.
summary: A national organization dedicated to providing its members with hands-on experience in the design, construction, and flight of rockets and aircraft through team-based projects and international competitions.

name: Athens Prison Tutorial
description: The mission of Athens Prison Tutorial is to serve the interests of incarcerated peoples in Athens-Clarke County through regular educational programming. One-on-one and group tutoring led by UGA students will cover GED materials, SAT/ACT prep, and other forms of education deemed desirable or necessary.
summary: Athens Prison Tutorial serves the interests of incarcerated peoples in Athens-Clarke County through regular educational programming. This includes GED tutoring, SAT/ACT prep, and other forms of education deemed desirable or necessary.

name: Society For Cyber Security
description:
Welcome to the Society for Cybersecurity (SCS) at the University of Georgia! Prepare to dive into a world of cutting-edge technology, where cybersecurity becomes your superpower.
