With a mission to inspire, educate, and empower, SCS is your gateway to an exciting future in the realm of cybersecurity.

During our General Body Meetings, we bring renowned cybersecurity and tech companies to campus. Immerse yourself in captivating company presentations, demos, testimonies, and job details. These companies are actively seeking to hire SCS members for full-time, part-time, or internship positions. Get ready to ignite your career!

But that's not all. At our Hack Hours, our experienced hackers lead workshops, providing you with invaluable skills and certifications to boost your resume. These hands-on sessions will transform you into a cybersecurity powerhouse, giving you an edge over others in the field. We are committed to providing our members with exceptional resources and opportunities that set them apart.
summary: Join SCS today and unlock a world of cybersecurity possibilities. Together, we'll build a strong network, excel in competitions, and secure a promising future. Let's embark on this thrilling journey and become the cybersecurity champions of tomorrow!

name: Dungeons and Dragons Club at UGA
description:
Welcome to the Dungeons and Dragons Club at UGA! Dedicated to providing a platform for UGA students to both learn and play Dungeons and Dragons, we not only provide you with the tools to play but also offer a club-wide game in our West Marches setting available to all members. Anyone can join in our biweekly meetings on Wednesdays where we teach, discuss, and do D&D-related activities. Members can join in our club campaign with sessions that are held throughout the year (dates depending on DM running). We are happy to have you join our community!

This Discord server is how we coordinate club activities and how people participate in the club. Join it to get the full experience. Note: You must be registered on the Involvement Network and message an officer on the Discord in order to join this organization.

For more information feel free to message us.

We also have an Instagram and Twitter.
summary: D&D@UGA is the place for students to not only learn how to play the game Dungeons and Dragons, but also to find a community of players and enthusiasts to experience the game with. All experience levels welcome!

name: Women Pre-Law Students Association
description: The Women Pre-Law Students Association (WPSA) supports and empowers aspiring lawyers by providing resources, mentorship, and networking opportunities. The organization hosts events such as law school panels, LSAT prep sessions, and professional development workshops. WPSA also fosters a strong community that encourages academic success and personal growth in the legal field.
summary: WPSA promotes a driven atmosphere of pre-law students. We shall provide our members with resources, mentors, and professionals to guide their undergraduate to a career in law.

name: Saazish
description: Saazish is the premier competitive Bollywood fusion dance team at the University of Georgia. Saazish hopes to build a bridge between the American and South Asian communities of dance on and off-campus while maintaining an energetic and positive environment. Our goal is to inspire people from diverse cultures and ages, as well as educate each individual on the various forms of dance. Saazish strives to spread the dedication and commitment to dance through competitions, events, and other performances. We perform many dance styles such as hip-hop, bollywood-fusion, bhangra, and more.
summary: Saazish is the premier competitive Bollywood-Fusion Dance Team at the University of Georgia.

name: Beta Chi Theta National Fraternity, Inc.
description: Beta Chi Theta National Fraternity, Inc. (BXΘ) is a national South Asian fraternity with chapters at more than 25 universities and 1,700+ members. The six founding pillars ("principles") which support and give purpose to Beta Chi Theta are: Brotherhood, Tradition, South Asian Awareness, Service to Humanity, Academic Excellence, and Nationwide Networking. Our National Philanthropy is Men's Mental Health Awareness.
summary: Beta Chi Theta National Fraternity, Inc. (BXΘ) is a multicultural organization that abides by the six principles of Brotherhood, Tradition, South Asian Awareness, Service to Humanity, Academic Excellence, and Nationwide Networking.

name: Fair Fashion
description: Fair Fashion works to raise awareness in the UGA community about unethical labor and pollution/waste negligence within the fashion industry. The organization aims at educating others on how to consume fashion through more ethical & sustainable means, such as through thrifting and shopping small. We host monthly clothing swap events for students that provide accessible opportunities to keep clothing local. Find us on Instagram @fairfashionuga!
summary: Fair Fashion works to raise awareness in the UGA community about unethical labor and pollution/waste negligence within the fashion industry. The group wishes to educate others on how to consume fashion through a more ethical & sustainable means.

name: Turfgrass Management Club at UGA
description: The objective of the Turfgrass Management Club at UGA is to focus on the mentoring, networking, and overall education of Turfgrass Management students in the Department of Crop and Soil Sciences. Participation in the club offers students many opportunities to visit turfgrass facilities, attend educational conferences, and create long-term bonds and friendships. Students will be given opportunities to engage in community service and outreach projects locally and throughout Georgia. The actions and activities of this club help promote the growth and success of the Turfgrass Management program.
summary: The objective of the Turfgrass Management Club at UGA is to focus on the mentoring, networking, and overall education of Turfgrass Management students in the Department of Crop and Soil Sciences.

name: Pre- Counseling Club
description: The Pre-Counseling Club is dedicated to raising mental health awareness around campus. We strive to foster a safe environment where others can learn more about mental health and where open dialogue about mental health is encouraged. Through various activities, workshops, and informative sessions, we aim to equip our members with coping mechanisms, resilience, and self-compassion. We also offer QPR training each semester in the place of a meeting, these training sessions help build a more educated community in regards to suicide prevention. We aim to ignite a positive change in our communities' perception of mental health, fostering an atmosphere of empathy and acceptance. Our big event is the Out of the Darkness Walk where we partner with the American Foundation for Suicide Prevention. Additionally, this club offers information about pursuing counseling as a career, building a community of individuals who are interested in the same career, and networking with employers, current graduate students, faculty, and staff. This club is for anyone interested in going into a helping profession. This can include Mental Health, School Counseling, Marriage and Family Therapy, Social Work, etc.
summary: This club is for anyone interested in going into a helping profession. This can include Mental Health, School Counseling, Marriage and Family Therapy, Social Work, etc. At each meeting, we will have speakers about the different fields of counseling.

name: Graduate Scholars of Epidemiology and Biostatistics
description:
GSEB was organized by students and advisers in the department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics at the School of Public Health in order to support:

1) The students within the department, and

2) Any student interested in this field of study.

Through monthly meetings, service events, and social events, GSEB seeks to create a fun and inclusive community of students in the excellent academic environment of the college.
summary: Organization supporting the success and well-being of students within the department of Epidemiology and Biostatistics, and to create a forum for discussion for all students with a broad interest in the field.

name: Society of Professional Journalists at UGA
description:
The Society of Professional Journalists at UGA is a student chapter of the national chapter of SPJ which provides resources to aspiring journalists and media communicators. We hold monthly meetings and even attend the national conference every fall!

SPJ focuses on information sessions regarding First Amendment rights, the free flow of information and open government records. As a student-run organization, we act as a resource for student journalists by providing opportunities in the journalism field on current issues and trends in the industry.

SPJ at UGA accepts members from all years and majors. No membership fee is required.

Members should expect a variety of workshops, informational sessions and panels covering topics that are relevant to the journalism industry. Check out our events page to see previous events sponsored by SPJ.

Students interested in joining should contact SPJ and attend the next upcoming event.
summary: The Society of Professional Journalists at UGA is a student chapter of the national chapter of SPJ which provides resources to aspiring journalists and media communicators. We hold monthly meetings and even attend the national conference every fall!

name: Ocean Initiative
description:
Ocean Initiative is an organization led by conservation-minded students passionate about protecting marine life. Some of our foci include sustainable seafood, marine debris & eliminating single-use plastics, and climate change & ocean acidification. We want to help the UGA and Athens communities make connections to the ocean and coasts, even if the Atlantic may be 200 miles away. The marine environment is in peril-- largely because of human activities. In order to solve current conservation issues, we need changes to our mindset and daily choices. By providing education, service, and interactive experiences, we hope to teach people of all majors and backgrounds how we can ensure a healthy marine environment for generations to come.

One per month, we host guest lectures with marine scientists, ecologists, biologists, and graduate students at our meetings. We participate in outreach and educational events, plan interactive lessons for the community, and table on campus about our focus areas. We also perform campus and stream clean-ups on a regular basis.

Whether you are an aspiring marine scientist or just love the beach, there is a place for you in Ocean Initiative!
summary: We use a science and service-based approach to foster awareness of marine conservation issues. You don't need to live on the coast to be a better marine environmental steward--you can do your part right here in Athens!

name: Lambda Theta Phi Latin Fraternity, Inc.
description: We are the first and largest Latino interest based fraternity in the nation. We exemplify the meaning of brotherhood. We strive to affect positive change in our communities by dedicating our will to the service of others.
summary: We are a service/social multicultural fraternity. We strive to promote academic excellence, professionalism, and brotherhood. We are a nonexclusive and we welcome interested college males from all different backgrounds.

name: Kappa Alpha Psi
description: The Zeta Iota Chapter of Kappa Alpha Psi Fraternity, Inc. at the University of Georgia.
summary:
Greek Life Office
Roster for CRETS EMS

name: MAcc Student Association
description:
The purpose of MAccSA at UGA shall be:

  1. To provide a means for MAcc students at UGA to meet and interact.
  2. To provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of our general interests.
  3. To provide a place to participate in various interactions, and plan events tailored to our organizational interests.
summary: MAccSA is a social organization for MAcc students. We host a variety of events that give MAcc students an opportunity to socialize and network.

name: Vibha at UGA
description:
• Vibha's vision is to ensure that every underprivileged child attains his, or her right to health, education and opportunity.
  • Vibha educates, enables, and empowers individuals who wish to make a positive impact on the lives of underprivileged children.
  • Vibha brings together the combined skills and actions of volunteers, donors, social entrepreneurs and NGOs to a common platform to find sustainable solutions to the problems faced by underprivileged children.
summary: Vibha at UGA is a club that will work with the Vibha organization (a non-governmental organization) to raise awareness for the social equity of underprivileged children through advocacy, fundraising, and volunteering efforts.

name: Regenerative Bioscience Center Graduate Student Association
description:
The RBC GSA aims to unify the increasing number of RBC graduate students and aid in networking opportunities. The goal of this organization is to unite the graduate students throughout the Regenerative Bioscience Center by acting as their advocate. Our mission statement is as follows: 
-Represent and promote the academic and social aims of graduate students,
-Represent the graduate student community within the RBC, and
-Establish effective communication channels among graduates and professional students, other student organizations, and with the academic and administrative units of other groups and universities.
summary: The Regenerative Bioscience Center Graduate Student Association (RBC GSA) aims to unite RBC professional students through professional development, social, and community outreach events.

name: Shotgun Team at UGA
description:
The UGA Shotgun Team is an organization led by students who enjoy competing in clay target competitions. We shoot at the state, regional, and national levels. While we do shoot other disciplines, our main focus is sporting clays, trap, and skeet. We have practices at Athens Rifle Club every week and are involved in collegiate competitions throughout the year. Above all else, we strive to promote the safe handling of firearms. We also want to promote awareness for this sport that is growing in popularity. 

Join the GroupMe for more information: https://groupme.com/join_group/110384268/DBvLFkep
summary: We are a team that competes at the state, regional, and national levels of clay target sports. We focus on sporting clays, skeet, trap, and five-stand.

name: Board Game Club at UGA
description:
Our in-person meetings are twice a week with people all over campus who love board games. You can bring your own game, pick a game from the club's collection of over 100 games, or join in on a game picked by someone else. We hope this can be a place for you to meet new friends and have a great time playing board games after a week of classes! 

 

 

Please follow our social media for club updates and announcements!

Discord: Discord Link.

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/100441062/CMOj9W18.

Instagram: Our handle is uga.bgc!

 

2024 - 2025 MEETINGS:

Time: 6:00 - 11:00 P.M. on Mondays and 5:15 - 11:00 P.M. on Fridays!

Location: Dawson Hall 202 and 208.
summary: If you love board games, but don't have the time to organize a game, don't have the people to play, or just want to try different games you've never had the chance to play before -- then this is the club for you! All experience levels and games welcome!

name: Actuaries at UGA
description: Actuaries come from many different backgrounds. Therefore, this organization is open to all majors. Whether you are trying to figure out how to succeed as an actuary or are wondering if a career in actuarial science is right for you, this organization is here to help! Actuaries at UGA plans to host events where potential actuaries can connect with current actuaries, students who have already passed an exam, and other potential actuaries. Events to be hosted include Q & A panels, guest speaker presentations, case studies, and internships informational sessions.
summary: This organization is open to students of all majors considering a career in Actuarial Science. By hosting guest speakers, networking events and presentations, Actuaries at UGA aims to educate potential actuaries on how to succeed in the profession.

name: Pre-Optometry Club
description: This organization is a pre-professional organization specifically focused on the field of optometry. Our members are those on the pre-optometry track and want to pursue a career in optometry. However, all are welcome to join. Optometry is a growing specialty in health care, and our club is dedicated to promoting this profession as well as providing information to those who are interested. Typical meetings include inviting guest speakers from optometry schools around the country who typically give a seminar on what their individual schools look for in candidates. We also provide tips for those applying to optometry schools, taking the OAT, etc. If you know you want to be in the health care field, but don't know exactly what you want to do yet, come check us out!
summary: This organization is dedicated to informing students interested in the Pre-optometry track more about the profession as well as educating them on the optometry school application process. It also serves as a way for these students to socialize.

name: Philosophy Undergraduate Student Association
description: Philosophy Club looks to facilitate good discussion about philosophy! Philosophy can be intimidating or boring in a classroom, and so Philosophy Club looks to make it easier for people to interact with and learn from each other. We hope that taking suggestions and facilitating discussion on what students want to talk about will help everyone get involved, no matter major or previous experience with philosophy!
summary: An undergraduate student organization that sits in a circle at 6 pm every Tuesday in Peabody room 220 and discusses a topic. No philosophy knowledge or experience is required! The aim is to foster clear communication and an intellectual appreciation.

name: Japanese Conversation Club
description: The purpose shall be to assemble college students of the University of Georgia who enjoy and would like to engage in practicing the Japanese language with both native speakers as well as other learners alike.  Furthermore, anyone who has any interest in Japanese culture, such as anime and J-Pop, is welcome to our club where we will hold events discussing these art forms and introducing concepts previously exclusive to Japan to the students at UGA. It will also be used as a gateway for University of Georgia Students to interact and use functional Japanese.
summary: The purpose shall be to assemble college students of the University of Georgia who enjoy and would like to engage in practicing Japanese.  It will also be used as a gateway for University of Georgia Students to interact and use function Japanese.

name: Bengali Cultural Organization
description:
The Bengali Cultural Organization is a student group promoting unity and friendship among Bengali and non-Bengali students here at the University of Georgia. The association is meant for any student wanting to learn more about Bengali culture. Our goal is to help bring everyone together through cultural and social events. 

Our main mode of communication is through Groupme and Instagram. Email or DM us on Instagram to be added to the Groupme!
summary: Bengali Cultural Organization (BCO) serves UGA community members of Bengali origin and anyone who identifies with the region culturally or socially. It offers the opportunity for students to be exposed to the culture and learn more about it.

name: Campus Compass Mission
description: Campus Compass Mission (C2M) at UGA is a religious organization that encourages a vibrant and welcoming Christian community for all students at the University of Georgia.
summary: Campus Compass Mission (C2M) at UGA is a religious organization that encourages a vibrant and welcoming Christian community for all students at the University of Georgia.

name: National Lawyers Guild
description: The National Lawyers Guild is an association dedicated to the need for basic change in the structure of our political and economic system. We seek to unite the lawyers, law students, legal workers, and jailhouse lawyers of America in an organization that shall function as an effective political and social force in the service of the people, to the end that human rights shall be regarded as more sacred than property interests. Our aim is to bring together all those who regard adjustments to new conditions as more important than the veneration of precedent; who recognize the importance of safeguarding and extending the rights of workers, women, farmers, and minority groups upon whom the welfare of the entire nation depends; who seek actively to eliminate racism; who work to maintain and protect our civil rights and liberties in the face of persistent attacks upon them; and who look upon the law as an instrument for the protection of the people, rather than for their repression.
summary: NLG seeks to unite the lawyers, law students, legal workers, and jailhouse lawyers of America in an organization that is an effective political and social force in the service of the people and holds human rights more sacred than property interests.

name: Clarke County Mentors at UGA
description: Clarke County Mentors @ UGA serves as a support system for UGA students that serve as mentors for the Clarke County Mentor Program and raises funds for the Clarke County Mentor Program. We provide opportunities for mentors to connect with each other and resources to help mentors better connect with their mentees. Also, our organization provides opportunities for interested students to become mentors. By sharing fun activities and exposing a child to new experiences, mentors encourage positive choices, promote high self-esteem, support academic achievement and introduce their mentee to new ideas. Clarke Mentors stress the importance of dependable and supportive role models, and provide the space to support volunteers on making a valuable impact in their mentee's life.
summary: Clarke County Mentors provides support for students mentoring with the Clarke County Mentor Program. These volunteers serve as mentors to students in all of the Athens-Clarke County public schools, ranging from kindergarten to twelfth grade.

name: Alpha Phi
description: Alpha Phi International Fraternity is more than just a Greek organization – it’s a community of empowered women supporting one another for a lifetime. Our sisterhood stretches across the United States and Canada with 175 collegiate chapters and a global network of high-achieving alumnae. When you join Alpha Phi, you’re welcomed by a community of more than 250,000 members committed to lifelong friendships, leadership, academic excellence, personal growth and community involvement.
summary: CRETS Category: Only Greek organizations registered with the Greek Life Office and not with CSAI.

name: Nerd Culture Club
description:
Welcome to the Nerd Culture Club! We welcome all that are interested in playing games, watching films, and discussing media. We are a friendly group, with interests in media ranging from board games, video games, trading card games, anime, Japanese culture, American comic books & movies, and fantasy & sci-fi books. Your fandom is welcome here, and we would love to introduce you to ours! 

Our meetings last for two hours, and we will plan activities in advance at the previous meeting. Typically we will chose from activities such as playing board games (Red Dragon Inn, Risk, Monopoly, etc), video or phone-based games (Super Smash Bros, Mario Kart, Jackbox, etc), and/or watching an animated movie or television show. New activity suggestions are encouraged, as is bringing your own activities to share with the group.

We look forward to seeing you! Please email Amelia Durden at acd77279@uga.edu to join the club GroupMe or if you have any questions!
summary: NCC is a social group whose members get together to casually play and appreciate video games, board games, Japanese entertainment, comic books, trading card games, and much more.

name: Defender-Advocate Society
description: The Defender-Advocate Society is a group at UGA that provides free and comprehensive assistance to students facing disciplinary proceedings.
summary: The Defender-Advocate Society is a group at UGA that provides free and comprehensive assistance to students facing disciplinary proceedings.

name: Film Club
description: Film Club provides a platform for UGA students to socialize with other movie lovers through weekly participation in roundtable film discussions as well as other activities.
summary: Film Club's mission is to provide a platform for UGA students to socialize with other movie lovers as well as participate in film related activities, and of course, discuss, watch and attend movies together.

name: Global Health Union
description: Global Health Union works to promote campus-wide engagement in global health initiatives outside the classroom. We are here to facilitate interdisciplinary collaboration with undergraduate and graduate students, global health related clubs, and professors at UGA. To promote conversations over international healthcare, Global Health Union hosts forums, educational events, a research symposium, and a case competition each year.
summary: Our organization strives to facilitate campus-wide engagement and interdisciplinary collaboration in global health initiatives outside the classroom.

name: Best Buddies at UGA
description: Best Buddies is the world’s largest organization dedicated to ending the social, physical and economic isolation of the 200 million people with intellectual and developmental disabilities (IDD). Our programs empower the special abilities of people with IDD by helping them form meaningful friendships with their peers. Typically, our student members are paired with a member with IDD for fall and spring semester, but anyone wishing to be matched is welcome and encouraged to join at any time. As student members become matched with a buddy, it is expected that students connect with their buddies at least twice a month via email, phone calls, text messages, or social media. Buddy pairs are also required to spend meaningful time in person at least twice a month. Aside from friendship and volunteering, Best Buddies also has bi-annual fundraisers and philanthropic events
summary: Best Buddies is a nonprofit organization dedicated to establishing one-to-one friendships between people with intellectual and developmental disabilities and their peers.

name: University Hackathons
description:
What exactly is a hackathon?

Hackathons are all about groups of dedicated people coming together at a workspace to create something awesome. The 36 hour hackathon is free and open to anyone.

It includes mentors, free food, video game competitions, social mixers and more. There's even mixers at the event for anyone that still needs to find some teammates!

What do you make at a hackathon?

Hackathon creations usually revolve around technology. Hardware, programs, games, and apps are great choices, but these are only a few examples.

At a hackathon, you can make anything you want. From projectors created out of trash, to exciting new apps, to wacky inventions... the possibilities are endless!

Want to learn more?

Visit us online at www.ugahacks.com or email us at hello@ugahacks.com.
summary: University Hackathons is a 501 (c)(3) non-profit organization that hosts an annual 36 hour programming sleepover which takes place in Athens, Georgia at the University of Georgia.

name: Girls in Healthcare
description: The purpose of Girls in Healthcare at UGA is to provide a positive, supportive network for all students at UGA pursuing careers in healthcare, in hopes of fostering a new generation of intentional, humble, and service-minded healthcare professionals.
summary: While promoting and recognizing the importance of humility, intentionality, and passion in the pursuit of the healthcare profession, Girls in Healthcare strives to unite all pre-health students on campus through mentorship, service, and fellowship

name: American Society For Microbiology Student Chapter
description: ASM is an internationally recognized organization with student chapters all over the world. With your participation, you will be part of a small knit group at UGA, but also a much larger global organization. The goal of this organization is to provide passionate science students with an outlet to connect with others and foster their interest in microbiology. We organize social, educational, and professional meetings for students to attend.
summary: Your participation in the ASM Student Chapter is a great way to combine community involvement, professional development, and an interest in microbes. Join ASM to connect with other students, faculty, and researchers who also love microbiology.

name: Industry Pharmacists Organization
description: Professional pharmacy organization focused on increasing awareness of the opportunities available to students in the pharmaceutical industry.  IPhO will function to properly prepare students to apply for desired internships and fellowships. IPhO will educate, provide connections, and encourage students to pursue careers in the pharmaceutical industry.
summary: IPhO is a professional pharmacy organization focused on increasing awareness of the opportunities available to students in the pharmaceutical industry.

name: Phi Delta Epsilon
description: Our mission is to bring premedical students together and create a community where they can find help and support in achieving their goals as aspiring physicians. We will offer help for medical school in the form of CPR certification, mock interviews, MCAT practice, peer tutoring, guest lectures, and reminders of important dates like MCAT sign up and Medical School application deadlines. Phi Delta Epsilon will provide many leadership opportunities for members and give them the chance to work on skills including recognizing strengths, delegating, public speaking, and organizing events. All members will have a mandatory service hours requirement easily fulfilled by helping with our national philanthropy The Children's Miracle Network. Weekly meetings will be held to help organize activities and dates, as well as update members on new and emerging information on applying to medical school.
summary: Our mission is to bring premedical students together and create a community where they can find help and support in achieving their goals as aspiring physicians. We will do this by having guest lecturers, mock interviews, and MCAT help courses.

name: Women in Media
description: Women in Media at UGA strives to foster a diverse community for all students with interests in media. Our goal is to continuously support, guide and empower one another through our pre-professional organization, leaving a bold group of leaders ready to excel in their respective fields. We bring awareness to current issues that women face in the media industry such as gender pay-gap, sexual harassment, lack of women leaders and more. Women in Media is here to create a safe space for our members to gain the knowledge and support they need to change the narrative. Membership is open to all students, regardless of major, background, or identity.
summary: Women in Media strives to foster a community of students interested in diverse forms of media, including journalism, public relations, advertising, entertainment and media studies, film studies and marketing.

name: Chinese Language and History Learning Association
description: CLHLA opens up a unique window into one of the world’s most ancient civilizations by sharing her language and history.  The People’s Republic of China currently boasts the fastest growing economy in the world and is widely expected to become a major geopolitical force in the new millennium. As soon as you begin studying the Chinese language, you begin learning about Chinese history, cultural values, philosophical and religious beliefs, and aesthetic traditions. And the more proficient you become, the more you will be able to appreciate and understand China’s past and present.
summary: CLHLA advocates Chinese language and history to children and teens.

name: Young Democratic Socialists of America in Athens
description: The YDSA is a youth and student section of the broader Democratic Socialists of America, a national organization with recognized campus chapters in nearly every major university and thousands of members nationally. We work with many local campaigns to address concerns relevant to not only UGA students but all Athenians. We do mutual aid work with the community as well in order to take care of one another's needs. Through educational events, activism, and community service we aim to create a better Athens and learn how to stand up for ourselves and others.
summary: Young Democratic Socialists of America in Athens is a youth chapter of the broader Democratic Socialists of America organization. Our goal is to create a resilient, active, and informed community work supporting mutual aid, labor unions, and more.

name: College of Engineering Student Ambassadors
description: The Engineering Student Ambassadors work to build awareness of the UGA College of Engineering on campus and across the state. Ambassadors help promote pride in Bulldog Engineering by assisting with college and university-sponsored special events and speaking about academic programs and student opportunities in the college. You'll see Engineering Ambassadors at recognition ceremonies, summer orientation sessions, alumni events, campus visits with prospective students and at many other events.
summary: The Engineering Student Ambassadors work to build awareness of the UGA College of Engineering on campus and across the state. Ambassadors help promote pride in Bulldog Engineering.

name: Club Field Hockey
description: Field Hockey is a co-ed club sport at the University of Georgia. This team competes within the NFHL, competing regionally and nationally against other club teams. This team is highly competitive, practicing 3 times a week during the Fall Season at the UGA Club Sports Complex and participating in and hosting many different tournaments throughout the year. This program hopes to promote field hockey in the state of Georgia and to provide a fun, physically and mentally challenging opportunity for the students of UGA. Finished 2nd in the NATION at the NFHL 2022 Fall Championship tournament.
summary: Club Field Hockey is a competitive non-varsity field hockey team at the University of Georgia. This team competes regionally and nationally, and strives to promote growth of the sport in Georgia as well as the rest of the nation.

name: Sports Analytics Club
description: An organization that analyzes and discusses the current state of advanced analytics in a variety of professional sports and attempts to foster an environment that advances the knowledge of sports strategies.
summary: A non-profit organization that analyzes and discusses the current state of advanced analytics in a variety of professional sports and attempt to foster an environment that advances the knowledge of sports strategies.

name: Student Veterans Resource Center
description:
Founded in spring 2013, the Student Veterans Resource Center (SVRC) serves as a central location for information about the university’s services for student veterans while also offering support, advocacy and a convenient location for student veterans to gather throughout the day. The center is located on the fourth level of the Tate Student Center.

The center’s strength lies in its established relationships with almost every office at UGA and with many service organizations across the region. The SVRC markets these services while keeping student veterans informed through weekly communications.

SVRC provides a convenient point of contact for student veterans, a service desk staffed by veterans, an evolving network of partnerships working to improve services and programs, a comfortable lounge, and a community for student veterans. A range of diverse opportunities enables student veterans to meaningfully engage with the University and the surrounding community.
summary: The Student Veterans Resource Center (SVRC) serves as a central location for information about the university’s services for student veterans while also offering support, advocacy and a convenient location for student veterans to gather throughout the d

name: Undergraduate English Association
description:
The Undergraduate English Association at the University of Georgia is dedicated to the English majors, minors, and enthusiasts who study in Park Hall. Our mission revolves around the three C’s:

  • To foster a welcoming community and safe space in which English majors create lasting connections with like-minded individuals
  • To provide career counseling to English majors and career information about the marketability of an English degree
  • To celebrate the creativity of our English students through events focused on creative writing, poetry, literary appreciation, and more

We strive to host a variety of events that provide services to English majors. Some of these events allow undergraduates to meet professionals who attained an English degree but dispersed into different professional fields.
summary: The UEA is a club dedicated to creating a greater community for Undergraduate English majors and minors that fosters social, academic, and pre-professional development.

name: Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs
description: Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs
summary: Office of the Vice President for Student Affairs

name: Student Conduct
description:
As a department within the Division of Student Affairs, the mission of Student Conduct is to enhance the learning environment for members of the University of Georgia community. This is accomplished by:

  • Educating University of Georgia students and student organizations about their rights, responsibilities and expectations as members of a community.
  • Providing a fair and educational process for students and student organizations through which alleged violations of the University Code of Conduct are resolved.
  • Creating opportunities for student learning and development.
  • Offering outreach services to educate faculty, staff and other community partners about the role of our office and the Code of Conduct.
summary: As a department within the Division of Student Affairs, the mission of Student Conduct is to enhance the learning environment for members of the University of Georgia community.

name: University Housing
description:
A part of the Division of Student Affairs, University Housing is home to more than 8,300 students who live in nine residential communities comprised of 22 residence halls, University Village apartments, and Health Science Campus townhomes as well as four Greek Park houses.

University Housing connects to the university’s mission by providing comfortable, affordable and secure on-campus housing options in residential communities where the academic success and personal growth of residents are encouraged and supported. As a result, research indicates students who live on campus perform better academically than their off-campus peers.

In support of this mission, University Housing employs more than 700 full and part-time employees within three comprehensive units – Administrative Services and Communications, Facilities, and Residential Programs and Services. As a self-supporting auxiliary, University Housing operates a $50 million budget derived only from student rent and other non-state sources.

University Housing fosters a supportive and challenging inclusive environment through staff selection and training, community programming and contact with staff and students. Residents are part of a community that values and appreciates diversity and where individual rights and differences are recognized and respected.
summary: University Housing provides comfortable, affordable and secure on-campus housing options in residential communities where the academic success and personal growth of residents are encouraged and supported.

name: University Health Center
description:
The University Health Center exists to advance the well-being of students and other members of the University community and supports student success and resilience by providing: primary and specialty health care, education and prevention focused services, and research which contributes to health knowledge and skills.

We strive for excellence in services that are student-centered, respectful, accessible, and offer satisfaction and value.
summary: The University Health Center exists to advance the well-being of students and other members of the University community and supports student success and resilience.

name: Greek Life
description: Our tradition dates back more than 150 years. There are over 60 Greek Letter Organizations from 4 Councils (IFC/MGC/NPC/NPHC) representing Greek Life at UGA.
summary: Our tradition dates back more than 150 years. There are over 60 Greek Letter Organizations from 4 Councils (IFC/MGC/NPC/NPHC) representing Greek Life at UGA.

name: Disability Resource Center
description:
The Disability Resource Center's primary commitment is to assist the University of Georgia in educating and serving students with disabilities who qualify for admissions. While our mission is to ensure equal educational opportunities as required by the ADA and other legislation, the DRC strives to promote a welcoming academic, physical, and social environment for students with disabilities at UGA.

Disability Resource Center professional staff work directly with students to assess their individual disability-related needs and to develop appropriate plans for academic accommodations and services. In addition, the DRC houses three distinct services that have proven most helpful in accommodating a variety of student needs: the Test Accommodations Office for onsite classroom testing; the Assistive Technology Lab equipped with a variety of programs designed for people with disabilities; and Alternative Media Services to assist students with print or reading disabilities by converting printed material required for courses into accessible formats.
summary: The DRC strives to promote a welcoming academic, physical, and social environment for students with disabilities at UGA.

name: Armed Forces Association at UGA School of Law
description: To provide a platform for law students interested in the military, Department of Defense, national security, and foreign affairs—including those interested in potential service with the military or DoD—and veterans connected with the law school to learn about the intersection of the law and national security, as well as promote recruitment efforts through advice and assistance to those interested in pursuing a career in the military, Department of Defense, or in other national security fields.
summary: Serving veterans and other law students interested in the military to promote networking, law student support, and discourse about the intersection of law and national security.

name: Global Medical Missions Alliance
description:
Global Medical Missions Alliance (GMMA) is an organization with members who are Christian Healthcare Professionals striving to TEACH and CARE for the well-being of the WHOLE PERSON in Mind, Body, and Spirit.

GMMA members strive to reach out to the Next Generation to EQUIP them for World Missions, and to fulfill the Great Commission of Jesus Christ.

GMMA members are the future healthcare leaders representing all races and ethnicities to “ARISE & SHINE” for God’s Glory and Grace wherever they go, at home and throughout the world.
summary: Global Medical Missions Alliance (GMMA) is an organization with members who are Christian Healthcare Professionals striving to TEACH and CARE for the well-being of the WHOLE PERSON in Mind, Body, and Spirit.

name: Rho Chi Pharmacy Honor Society
description:
Mission:

The Rho Chi Society will achieve universal recognition of its members as lifelong intellectual leaders in pharmacy.  As a community of scholars, the Society will instill the desire to pursue intellectual excellence and critical inquiry to advance the profession.

The Rho Chi Society, Pharmacy’s academic honor society, encourages and recognizes excellence in intellectual achievement and fosters fellowship among its members.  Further, the Society encourages high standards of conduct and character, and advocates critical inquiry in all aspects of pharmacy.

 

Objective:

The fundamental objective of Rho Chi has always been to promote the advancement of the pharmaceutical sciences through the encouragement and recognition of sound scholarship.  High standards of intellectual and scholarly attainment have been demanded for election to membership; and such election, symbolized by the award of the Rho Chi Key, supplies a unique scholarship incentive.

Rho Chi seeks to promote scholarly fellowship in pharmacy by bringing professional students, graduate students, and faculty members together in fraternal and helpful association.  Regular chapter meetings, and other activities, emphasize the professional aspects of pharmacy and point the way to instructive study and research.  By such means, Rho Chi, like its sister honor societies, seeks to increase the awareness of the ethical and social responsibilities of the profession, and thereby, to enhance the prestige of the profession.
summary: An academic honor society for students of the University of Georgia's College of Pharmacy

name: Paper Airplanes
description: Paper Airplanes works to provide free, one-on-one tutoring for Syrian refugees and those adversely impacted by conflict. All UGA students, faculty, and community members are welcome to reach out for more information about how to get involved.
summary: Paper Airplanes works to provide free, one-on-one tutoring for Syrian refugees and those adversely impacted by conflict.

name: Holy Cross Lutheran Campus Ministry
description:
Holy Cross Lutheran Campus Ministry aims to connect with students at UGA so that they will know Jesus, grow as people, and go to share God’s love in the world.

We meet weekly for a meal in the Tate Student Center, worship each Sunday, provide opportunities to build relationships and learn in small groups, and serve the Athens community with a variety of outreach events.
summary: Holy Cross Lutheran Campus Ministry aims to connect with students at UGA so that they will know Jesus, grow as people, and go to share God’s love in the world.

name: Campus View College Ministry
description: The CVCM thrives through family and community. We stem from our home church Campus View Church of Christ, and we grow together through retreats, events, and the love of God. Be a part of our Adopt-A-Dawg program and find your "home away from home." The CVCM creates a family atmosphere as we grow in genuine relationships with each other in God and community. While we do spend time within the group of college students themselves, it is also a great way to grow intergenerational relationships with other members of the body.
summary: The CVCM thrives through family and community. We stem from our home church Campus View Church of Christ, and we grow together through retreats, events, and the love of God. Be a part of our Adopt-A-Dawg program and find your "home away from home."

name: Music Business Ambassadors
description: Music Business Ambassadors is an organization University of Georgia founded in 2018 by Music Business Certificate students. This student ran ambassadorship looks to give other students interested in the music business various opportunities to meet and interact with industry professionals within the music and entertainment industry along with representing the Music Business Certificate program throughout a wide array of campus events. Student in the ambassadorship will be privy to experiences such as networking events with industry professionals, mentorship opportunities, professional relationship building and job/internship opportunities.
summary: Music Business Ambassadors provides opportunities for students interested in music business to network, represent, and engage with MBUS resources through events and firsthand experience.

name: Physics Graduate Student Association
description: The experience of being a graduate student is both rewarding and stressful. Many students feel underprepared or overwhelmed by the responsibilities that they are entrusted with. Physics as a field of study has its own set of interesting and challenging career difficulties which can be tricky and frightening to navigate. Our goals focus around examining the state of the physics graduate students and developing/providing resources to help all of us grow as professionals and as students while increasing the quality of the overall graduate experience.
summary: An organization dedicated to improving the graduate experience of physics students by fostering a sense of community and engagement within the student body and the UGA at large.

name: Entertainment Media Industries Club
description: The Industry is the first Entertainment and Media Studies Club within the University of Georgia. Open to all majors and grades, The Industry is a club geared towards students who are looking to learn more about the entertainment business, production, script writing, and much more. We are designed to promote collaboration and networking for future storytellers in film and television.
summary: The Industry is a networking space for future filmmakers to collaborate on future projects, recruit crew, learn about set safety, and develop ideas for screenplays.

name: Kalakaar
description: Kalakaar is a South Asian, nationally competitive, co-ed acapella team. Founded in 2013, Kalakaar fuses Western and Eastern styles of music and welcomes members of all backgrounds.
summary: Kalakaar is a South Asian, nationally competitive, co-ed acapella team that fuses Western and Eastern styles of music. No experience is required to audition! All are welcome!

name: Genetic Education Network and Experience Society
description: G.E.N.E.S. was founded to bring together students and faculty interested in genetics in its broadest scope across the University. This includes business, law, and humanities majors as well as all science majors. We will host speakers in patent law, management of biotechnology companies, as well as physicians and scientists from government, academia, and industry. We also have a mentorship program, where we pair upperclassmen with new students. The mentorship program is meant to help guide students through academics and the application process for various professional schools.
summary: G.E.N.E.S. was founded to build connections between all students who are interested in the field of genetics. We host speakers from a wide variety of industries including medicine, academia, and law to help students make informed career decisions.

name: Women in Accounting
description: Women in Accounting is a student-led organization committed to empowering students pursuing careers in accounting by offering structured opportunities for professional development, mentorship, and inclusive networking. Through workshops, speaker panels, study groups, and community initiatives, we aim to equip women with the confidence, skills, and support system needed to thrive academically and lead in the business world. We foster meaningful dialogue around industry trends and challenges, promote accessibility within the profession, and create pathways for leadership through active membership and executive board involvement. Our goal is to build a sustainable, welcoming community where students of all backgrounds feel supported, inspired, and prepared to make an impact.
summary: Women in Accounting is a student-led organization that empowers students pursuing accounting careers through professional development, mentorship, and inclusive networking. We help build confidence, skills, and community for success in the business world

name: BrainIAC
description: BrainIAC, or Brain Injury Awareness Club, is a volunteer-led student organization to help spread awareness of acquired brain injuries. We provide support, resources, and education about concussion and proper management in order to better academic and functional outcomes following concussion, or mild traumatic brain injury (mTBI). We raise funds for individuals affected by brain injury and hold discussion-focused meetings surrounding current best practices in rehabilitation. This group is for anyone who has experienced brain injury or is interested in learning more about it and supporting the community!
summary: The goal of BrainIAC is to increase brain injury awareness while providing support and resources to help affected students cope with negative impacts such injuries may have on academics and quality of life.

name: Inter Belief Works
description: Inter Belief Works (IBW) seeks to unite students who identify themselves as secular, spiritual, and/or religious, under a diverse platform, where empathy for the “Other” is developed through leadership, dialogue, and voluntary community service.  Our goal is to support university's initiatives that support Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) on campus.  IBW would like to lead in enhancing religious and spiritual diversity on UGA's campus by holding weekly local volunteering events, and monthly educational seminars to learn about various traditions, cultures, and beliefs.
summary: To unite students who identify themselves as secular, spiritual, and/or religious, under a diverse platform, where empathy for the “Other” is developed through leadership, dialogue, and voluntary community service.

name: Black Business Student Association
description: The goal of the Black Business Student Association is to increase the number of underrepresented minorities within the Terry College of Business by using a data driven approach, fostering a strong sense of community, while identifying gaps within the matriculation of those from intended, current, and Alumni of the Terry College of Business. BBSA caters to every major within the Terry College of Business and focuses on promoting resources and opportunities available for Intended-Terry and current students in Terry. BBSA also has an emphasis on professional development opportunities that help students secure internship and full-time roles. In order to build community, BBSA hosts social opportunities to allow business students of similar interests to meet each other and develop connections with peers and alumni.
summary: It is a resource to African-Americans and/or people of color catering to both intended, current and Alumni of the Terry College of Business. We seek to encourage academic, personal and professional development in our members.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action
description: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at UGA believes in the fundamental right of every individual to manage their sexual health and well-being, reproduction, and access to comprehensive and affirming healthcare in settings that preserve and protect the essential privacy and rights of each individual and advocates public policies that guarantee these rights. PPGA strives to provide educational programs and events that highlight these values and share them with the UGA community.
summary: This club provides sexual and health education and resources to students. Encouraging education on reproduction and advocating for reproductive justice.

name: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc.
description:
Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Incorporated (AKA) is an international service organization that was founded on the campus of Howard University in Washington, D.C. in 1908. It is the oldest Greek-letter organization established by African-American college-educated women. For more information please visit www.aka1908.com

The Eta Xi Chapter of Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc was chartered on January 13, 1973. Since the chapter's formation, the ladies of Eta Xi have hosted several innovative and relevant programs for the University of Georgia and Athens-Clarke County communities to enjoy and benefit.
summary: Alpha Kappa Alpha Sorority, Inc., the first Greek-lettered organization established by Black college women, was founded on January 15, 1908 at Howard University in Washington, DC.

name: RISE
shortName: Rural Students Igniting Success in Education
description: RISE is a student-led organization designed to give rural students a sense of community at the University of Georgia and a “home away from home.” We are dedicated to providing our members with the tools they need to be successful. To fulfill this mission, we invite faculty members and business professionals to our monthly general body meetings to engage with students. We are also committed to providing information regarding scholarships, community service opportunities, and more! We are an established community for any and every rural Bulldog, and we would love to have you join us!
summary: RISE provides UGA's rural students with a sense of community on campus through mentorship, outreach, social events, and optional community service opportunities.

name: Women in Economics
description:
Women in Economics is a Terry student organization dedicated to providing academic, personal, and professional development events to students. We are committed to enhancing the growth of diversity in the major while instilling confidence in the professional goals of women on campus by helping students find a community of support, learn from each other, and build a network for success.

Please visit our website to see upcoming organization events as well as to fill out our Membership Application.
summary: Women in Economics is a Terry student organization dedicated to providing academic, personal, and professional development events to students.

name: BAPS Campus Fellowship
description: The purpose of the organization is to unite students on the UGA campus and provide opportunities to learn about the Hindu heritage and culture. It will foster group discussions, and community service projects. The topics in discussion will be both practical and pragmatic such as work life balance. In addition, it will foster ideas of how to keep a balanced life in college, while practicing the Hindu Faith.
summary: The purpose of the organization is to unite students on the UGA campus and provide opportunities to learn about the Hindu heritage. It will foster group discussions, and community service projects. It will discuss topics such as work-life balance

name: School of Law First-Generation Student Association
description:
The School of Law’s First-Generation Student Association (FGSA) aims to enhance the academic success, professional growth and personal development of First-Generation Students at the University of Georgia School of Law. We serve as an organizing force around the social, academic, emotional, and financial issues relevant to first-generation students both within and outside of the law school environment. We strive to foster a community of like-minded individuals facing similar difficulties.

Our goal is to help first-generation students learn how to navigate law school as well as foster relationships with fellow students, legal professionals and the community. FGSA encourages students to learn about and celebrate the different backgrounds individuals bring to law school and the legal profession. We aspire to enhance diversity and inclusion at the University of Georgia School of Law through the promotion of understanding among students and acts of service, outreach, and mentorship in the community surrounding the law school.

The FGSA is an inclusive organization that welcomes any student, regardless of their first-generation status, but that shares the same vision of support for first-generation students.
summary: The School of Law’s First-Generation Student Association (FGSA) aims to enhance the academic success, professional growth and personal development of First-Generation Students at the University of Georgia School of Law.

name: Linguistics Club
description: The primary focus of this organization is to provide a meeting place for any UGA students who are interested in discussing linguistics-related content and want to share ideas pertaining to the field of linguistics or any of its subfields with other interested students. Linguistics Club also serves as a tool for students to get involved in the larger linguistics community through planning or attending social and academic events, and to provide a network for students to use in gaining better understanding of a range of topics for completing course work in the major/minor classes.
summary: The undergraduate Linguistics Club is an organization comprised of students interested in the field of linguistics.

name: Undergraduate Pediatric Society
description: The mission of the Undergraduate Pediatric Society is to encourage undergraduate pre-health students with an interest in pediatrics. This will be a community that is open to all undergraduates at the University of Georgia, regardless of age or major choice. As a society, our hope is to foster a community that inspires an academic and feasible pursuit of pediatrics. This will be achieved by exposing pre-health students to experience in hospital pediatric wards, the nuances of a pediatrician's day to day life, networking with trained professionals, and communication with patients. Additionally, UPS is dedicated to uplifting driven volunteers by providing experiences to help organizations who benefit children in underserved areas. Our goal as a society is to equip every member with meaningful bedside manner and a greater understanding of their goals in the medical field.
summary: An inclusive society dedicated to encouraging undergraduate, pre-health students to network and explore pediatrics. Our goal is to equip every member with connections and resources while supporting their passions to enter the healthcare field.

name: Bhakti Yoga Club
description:
The purpose of Bhakti Yoga Club is to:

  1. Emphasize the importance of yoga and its holistic benefits through weekly student-led sessions focusing on the principles of Bhakti Yoga, highlighting its positive impacts both on and off the mat.
  2. Help students manage stress more effectively, deepen their self-awareness, and build a supportive community of like-minded individuals.
  3. Develop habits in students for a healthy and happy lifestyle by encouraging active practice of yoga principles on a daily basis.
summary: We hold free yoga classes every Wednesday 6-8pm to practice svadhyaya (self-study), asanas, and pranayama! We aim to balance stress, promote a connection to self with mindfulness activities, and learn about yoga through practice and instruction

name: Medical Business Association
description: Medical Business Association focuses on bringing students together interested in the intersection of both business and medicine with intentions to provide its members with a variety of perspectives, mentorship and the opportunity to expand their professional network. Our club is perfect for those interested in the healthcare system, health tech/finance, health admin, consulting, and more!
summary: Medical Business Association aims to bring together students interested in the intersection of both business and medicine with intentions to provide its members with a variety of perspectives, opportunities, and mentorship.

name: ACHE UGA Student Chapter
description:
The American College of Healthcare Executives (ACHE) is the professional society for those passionate about healthcare and committed to improving health for all. For over 85 years, ACHE’s mission has been to advance the members and healthcare leadership excellence.

With more than 48,000 members across the world, ACHE serves as a network of healthcare professionals dedicated to making health care safer, more accessible, and more affordable to all while advancing equity, quality, and efficiency in health.

As a member of the ACHE UGA Student Chapter, you will have access to fresh insights, diverse perspectives, and an abundance of opportunities for collaboration within healthcare from a variety of settings. We are committed to lifelong learning and to educate, engage, and inspire our members as we tackle the trends in health and the healthcare system.
summary: The American College of Healthcare Executives (ACHE) is a professional society comprised of healthcare leaders, who are committed to improving healthcare. As a member, you will have access to divers insights  and opportunities from healthcare leaders.

name: Period Project at UGA
description: Period Project at UGA is part of a nationwide movement to end period poverty and period stigma. We work with local homeless shelters and community centers to provide menstrual products and health education to those in need across Athens-Clarke County. Period Project at UGA is committed to eliminating the period taboo and expanding access to menstrual care through education, advocacy, and outreach initiatives on campus and in the community.
summary: Period Project at UGA is part of a movement to end period poverty and stigma. We provide menstrual products and health education to those in need while engaging in education, advocacy, and outreach initiatives on campus and across Athens-Clarke County.

name: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society
description: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society recognizes academic and professional excellence rooted in personal integrity, promotes a well-rounded education essential for success, provides opportunities for leadership development and promotes lifelong learning, and cultivates a community of dedicated high achievers. Members are Juniors and Seniors who are invited to join based on outstanding academic achievement, and must display stellar character to be initiated as members.
summary: Tau Beta Pi Engineering Honor Society is the only engineering honor society at the University of Georgia which seeks to recognize and promote the academic success of the College of Engineering's students.

name: Delta Kappa Epsilon
description: We are a chapter of the nationally-renowned Delta Kappa Epsilon fraternity. Initially founded in 1844 at Yale University, Delta Kappa Epsilon is one of the oldest and most prominent fraternities in the U.S. “Deke” is known for producing outstanding gentlemen, scholars, and jolly good fellows. Our brothers aim to follow in the footsteps of many of our nation's greatest leaders, including George H.W. Bush and Theodore Roosevelt, by serving our university community as Dekes. Our members also get to look forward to an exciting social calendar, a spring mountain formal, and plenty of opportunities to serve the Athens area community through our philanthropy and community service events.
summary: We are the Beta Delta chapter of Delta Kappa Epsilon. Each day, our members strive to embody the founding principle of DKE: to be a gentleman, scholar, and jolly good fellow.

name: Dental Minority Dawgs
description: Dental Minority Dawgs is a free to join multicultural organization that exists as a safe space for pre-dental students to serve, learn, and teach others about their experiences as a pre-dental student. DMD is open to ALL students of ALL backgrounds. Our purpose is to increase the attendance of minority students in Dental School each year. Providing necessary information on prerequisite courses, the Dental Admissions Test, shadowing, letters of recommendation, etc., will better equip our D.M.D.s with proper knowledge before applying to Dental School, ultimately increasing their chances of success.
summary: Dental Minority Dawgs is a free to join multicultural organization that serves as a safe space for pre-dental students to serve, learn, and teach others about their experiences as a pre-dental student. DMD is open to ALL students of ALL backgrounds.

name: Young Farmers & Ranchers
description:
The purpose of the Young Farmers and Ranchers program is to bring together young adults (18-35) that are involved or interested in the agriculture industry while promoting leadership growth, educational outreach, diversity, and industry advocacy in our communities, state and nation.

In addition, the program will contribute to the overall effectiveness of the Georgia Farm Bureau by providing leadership, educational, and social opportunities, thereby preparing and enabling young adults to become more active members and to assume additional roles of leadership and responsibility within county, state or national Farm Bureau organizations.
summary: The University of Georgia Young Farmers & Ranchers is an organization with the purpose of developing young agricultural leaders through volunteering, advocating for the agriculture industry and hosting events that promote agriculture.

name: Girls Who Code
description: Girls Who Code is an organization where college students will go weekly to local elementary, middle, or high schools that we are partnered with in order to provide an enjoyable, supportive, and challenging environment for young girls, to serve as their role models, and to spark an interest in computer science along with providing a forum for discussion and appreciation of getting women and girls involved in STEM - specifically, technology and computing.
summary: Members visit local elementary, middle, and high schools weekly to create an enjoyable, supportive, and challenging environment for young girls and introduce them to the world of computer science and STEM fields.

name: Terry Involvement and Engagement Ambassadors
description:
The Terry Involvement and Engagement Ambassadors (TIEA) work to celebrate and promote the diversity within the Terry community. We plan events and activities that highlight different cultures, create meaningful connections, and foster an inclusive environment where all students feel welcomed and represented. Through community-focused events, we aim to bring people together and strengthen the sense of belonging throughout Terry.

Join the GroupMe!
summary: The Terry Involvement and Engagement Ambassadors (TIEA) work to celebrate and connect the diversity within the Terry community.

name: ManiCUREs for Alzheimer's
description:
ManiCUREs for Alzheimer's (M4A) focuses on raising money for the Fischer Center for Alzheimer's Research Foundation, an organization that works tirelessly to contribute to novel Alzheimer's research. Members of M4A will raise money by providing affordable manicures (regular and gel) for students at the University of Georgia. In order to become a member of M4A, individuals will be asked to complete training that covers simple manicure techniques; proficiency will be assessed by the executive board of M4A. Manicure events will be held in partnership with Greek Life, local Athens businesses, student organizations, and individuals.

 

If you have any questions, partnership inquiries, or interest in joining, please contact us via email or Instagram DM.
summary: The primary focus of this organization is to raise money for the Fischer Center for Alzheimer's Research Foundation. Our members provide affordable manicures to students and organizations across campus ranging from cultural clubs to sororities.

name: Lebanese Student Organization
description: Lebanese Student Organization at the University of Georgia is focused on gathering the Lebanese and non-Lebanese student body at UGA to help spread information and appreciation of Lebanese heritage, culture, and history. We will do this through discussion, taking part in Lebanese culture, such as dances and folk music, and other means of teaching about the Lebanese culture. Lebanese history and culture is one that has been incredibly important to ancient and modern history of both the East and West. We seek to help the student body at UGA learn about this influential culture. Furthermore, we seek to create a family of students where everyone has a backbone of people to help in times of stress. We want to create a little Lebanese enclave at UGA where students can retreat to feel relaxed and at home.
summary: The Lebanese Student Organization is focused on gathering the Lebanese students at UGA, and teaching them about their rich culture and heritage through many events. We also seek to teach the whole of the UGA student body about the Lebanese culture.

name: Medical Reserve Corps Students at UGA
description:
The Medical Reserve Corps (MRC) is a volunteer organization present in chapters across the nation. This organization builds awareness in the community about public health initiatives and how to prepare for, respond to, and recover from emergencies. The purpose of MRC includes engagement in strengthening public health, emergency response, and community resiliency in the county and on campus. The mission of the University of Georgia Medical Reserve Corps is to support the UGA Campus, Clarke County Emergency Management Agency (EMA), Public Health, and the local hospital's response plans where medical personnel support is needed.


Volunteering is the foundation of our organization. Our club participates in volunteer opportunities such as staffing first aid tents at community events and sewing homemade masks for local hospitals. Members of our organization include people from various majors and backgrounds (both medical & non-medical). All of our members receive various training such as CPR and First Aid. Our meetings occur twice a month on Tuesdays starting at 5:30pm. During our meetings, we learn and practice emergency preparedness and response skills such as triage protocol, search and rescue techniques, and how to set up points of distribution. We have had various professionals in emergency management, public health, and public service as special speakers at our meetings. In order to volunteer with our organization, please join our organization on the Involvement Network and our MRC Students at UGA GroupMe (https://groupme.com/join_group/71347863/awqGD4mW) to receive information about our meetings, visit https://www.servga.gov/, and visit our website for instructions on how to become a volunteer (https://prepare.uga.edu/mrc/).

Anyone wishing to become a member of the UGA MRC and to support its mission is welcome to join at any time! Just join our organization on the UGA Involvement Network and and our MRC Students at UGA GroupMe (https://groupme.com/join_group/71347863/awqGD4mW) to receive information about our meetings.


For more information about our club, please visit our website https://prepare.uga.edu/mrc/ or contact our officers via ugamedicalreservecorps@gmail.com.
summary: The mission of the University of Georgia Medical Reserve Corps is to support the UGA Campus, Clarke County Emergency Management Agency (EMA), Public Health, and the local hospital's response plans where medical personnel support is needed.

name: Environmental Health Science Graduate Student Association
description: The Environmental Health Science program offers a wide range of avenues toward careers in the fields and subfields it encompasses. This Graduate Student Association will aim to connect graduate students in the program with opportunities to expand their professional experiences. The EHS GSA will also plan monthly social outings for graduate students and faculty in the department and aim to plan recruitment events for potential incoming graduate students.
summary: Student-run organization aiming to enrich the graduate school experience offered by the Environmental Health Science department through avenues of scientific communication, scientific advocacy, and holding events.

name: Global Dental Brigades
description:
The University of Georgia is a chapter of Global Dental Brigades, an international movement of students and medical professionals working alongside local communities and staff to implement sustainable health systems. We work in remote, rural, and under-resourced communities in Honduras, Panama, Nicaragua and Ghana who would otherwise have limited to no access to health care. Each community receives a brigade every 3 to 4 months where hundreds of patients are provided access to dental & medical care and volunteers deliver public health workshops. Electronic patient records are collected for future visitations and to monitor overall community health trends. In conjunction with our Medical & Dental Programs, Global Brigades also supports communities with economic development, sanitation and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community’s health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring.

To learn more, please visit www.globalbrigades.org.
summary:
GDB is a secular, international student-run volunteer organization whose mission is to recruit volunteers and professionals to gather supplies and travel to developing countries to set up free dental clinics.

https://youtu.be/lcMTbysHEIg

name: Biotechnology Club
description:
The biotechnology club's ultimate goal is to connect students. To contextualize our mission, here's our stated constitutional purpose:

The purpose of the Biotechnology Club at UGA shall be:

  1. To connect biotechnology interested individuals to a network of likeminded individuals
  2. To foster interest and understanding in selected topics in biotechnology
  3. To provide insight into accomplishing biotechnology-based tasks achievable by at least undergraduates

Enrichment in the area of biotechnology learning can aid scientific creativity. Moreover, meeting other biotechnology students presents a challenge in a massive university. This club intends to assist in introducing STEM majors interested in similar STEM fields to one another. The club meetings will be a mix of activities, presentations, guest speakers, and discussions of papers relaying cutting-edge information.

Join our Discord!

https://discord.gg/Gk6duejeS5
summary: Biotechnology Club focuses on connecting students interested in biotechnology, but also introducing students who may not know much about biotechnology to the topic.

name: Institute for Healthcare Improvement Open School University of Georgia Chapter
description: The IHI Open School University of Georgia Chapter is a face-to-face, interpersonal organization for like-minded students, residents, and health care professionals to interact and gain critical skills to improve health care quality and patient safety.
summary: The IHI Open School University of Georgia Chapter supports IHI's mission to advance health care improvement and patient safety competencies in the next generation of health care professionals.

name: Government Finance Officers Association - University of Georgia Chapter
description:
The UGA Student Chapter of the Government Finance Officers Association is an organization dedicated to providing professional development to students interested in public finance. Our meetings consist of informational networking opportunities with current government financial officials to provide insight into the various options for employment in public finance.
