Please contact Rebekah Branch for information about our upcoming events.
summary: The UGA Student Chapter of GFOA is a professional organization for students interested in pursuing careers in public finance.

name: Juggling Club at UGA
description:
Welcome to the Juggling Club at University of Georgia! Founded in 2018, we love to promote the art and sport of juggling by meeting weekly to share tricks, socialize, teach newcomers, and most importantly- juggle!

 

Meetings are held every Monday at 4:30pm on North Campus behind UGA's Old College. Feel free to come and go as you please, and we'll nearly always be done by 6:00pm. Don't have juggling props? No problem! We've got dozens of extra props on hand that members can juggle with.

 

We're as social as we are talented, and welcome all skill levels. Our membership ranges from individuals with no juggling experience to routine performers, and more skilled members love giving free juggling lessons to beginners. All juggling disciplines are welcome here, from toss juggling, diablo, poi, devil sticks, fire staff, contact juggling, kendama, yo-yo, and any other circus genre that calls us home. We're excited to meet you, and juggle away!
summary: The juggling Club at UGA aims to promote the art and sport of juggling on campus. Don't know how to juggle? No problem! We love to teach newcomers and provide juggling props. For more advanced jugglers, we're known to put on shows on and off campus.

name: Lilly Branch Audubon Student Conservation Chapter
description: Lilly Branch Audubon Student Conservation Chapter, informally known as UGA Birding Club, is an Audubon Student Conservation Chapter dedicated to the conservation of birds and the habitats that support birds and other wildlife. The bird club provides a means for students to meet other members through a shared appreciation of birds and a place to participate in birding field trips, talks, and other related conservation events. We are also a service organization, participating in a group service project every year that ranges from pollinator garden maintenance to bird collision surveys.
summary: The LBAS is a conservation group at UGA that provides a means for students to meet other members through a shared appreciation of birds and a place to participate in birding field trips, talks, and other related conservation events

name: Middle Eastern Law Student Association
description:
Middle Eastern Law Student Association (MELSA) aspires to provide:

  1. a means for Middle Eastern and non-Middle Eastern students at Georgia Law to meet and interact.
  2. a forum for discussion and appreciation for our general interests such as assisting refugees abroad.
  3. a place to participate in various interactions, and plan philanthropic events tailored to our organizational interests.
summary: Middle Eastern Law Student Association (MELSA) aims to provide a means for Middle Eastern and non-Middle Eastern students at Georgia Law to meet and interact, in addition to providing a forum for discussion for our general interests.

name: Zoological and Exotic Medicine and the American Association of Wildlife Veterinarians
description: The ZEW Med Club is a group of students interested in furthering their interest in zoo, exotic, and wildlife medicine through specialized meetings, wet labs, and conference participation. We encompass the student chapters of AAWV, AAV, ARAV, AEMV, and AAZV, as well as shared participation in events with Wildlife Treatment Crew, Aquaponics, and UGA’s student chapter of the Wildlife Disease Association.
summary: The ZEW Med Club is a group of students based in the College of Veterinary Medicine interested in furthering their interest in zoo, exotic, and wildlife medicine through specialized meetings, wet labs, and conference participation.

name: Tate Student Center
description: The Tate Student Center, located in the heart of the University of Georgia campus, provides facilities, services, and programs for the University community. The Tate Student Center contains student organization and staff offices, an information desk, movie theater, food service areas, a print and copy service, business office, UGACard office, study lounge, TV lounges, and meeting, conference, and multipurpose rooms. Named for former Dean of Men William Tate, the Tate Student Center opened in 1983. It is open seven days a week when classes are in session.
summary: The Tate Student Center, located in the heart of the University of Georgia campus, provides facilities, services, and programs for the University community.

name: Center for Tropical and Emerging Global Diseases Graduate Student Association
description: The  Center for Tropical and Emerging Global Diseases Graduate Student Association (CTEGD GSA) aims to provide a means for graduate students within the CTEGD to meet and interact, provide a network for discussion and improvement of student’s professional development and career goals, and create a network of alumni from the CTEGD to help current students’ needs.
summary: The Center for Tropical and Emerging Global Diseases Graduate Student Association aims to provide a means for CTEGD graduate students to interact and to provide a network for discussion and improvement of student’s development and career goals.

name: National Hispanic Pharmacist Association
description: National Hispanic Pharmacist Association is a professional student chapter organization at the University of Georgia that focuses on the development of the student pharmacist's career by providing education on Hispanic minorities in the community. Community outreach and student education encompass the overall goals of NHPA to further a student pharmacist's professional development in order to ensure proper cultural exposure to a growing community that surrounds the profession.
summary:
The goals of NHPA are to define the future role of minority health professionals in the community,
develop a positive image of minority health professionals, and coordinate programs that will improve the healthcare of underserved Hispanic populations.

name: ACM-W girls.code()
description: girls.Code() is the official UGA ACM-W chapter committed to empowering women in Computer Science through an inclusive and opportunity-filled community. Through coding workshops, alumni panels, and career-building sessions, we create spaces where members can grow technically and professionally, enhance their programming skills, and thrive in tech. We welcome participation from all backgrounds and majors, and membership is open to all UGA students.
summary: Girls.Code() is the official UGA ACM-W chapter that fosters an inclusive and welcoming environment supporting women in Computer Science through professional development and community, and membership is open to all students.

name: American Statistical Association Student Chapter
description: There are three main tiers of our student chapter: professional development, outside learning, and service. There will be resume workshops, Q&A’s with professionals in various fields, information on internships and jobs, and opportunities to have your resume shared with potential employers. We will do some outside learning, but don’t be fooled—this will be fun. Learning to code, implementing our own studies, and participating in competitions are essential to applying the information we’ve learned, and doing it in an easy-going atmosphere with your peers takes the pressure off. Our organization is dedicated to advancing the understanding and appreciation of statistics.
summary: The ASA Student Chapter is an organization for students interested in statistics that will focus on professional development, outside learning, and service.

name: Student Care and Outreach
description: The mission of Student Care & Outreach is to coordinate care and assistance for all students, undergraduate and graduate, who experience complex, hardship, and/or unforeseen circumstances, by providing individualized assistance and tailored interventions.
summary: The mission of Student Care & Outreach is to coordinate care and assistance for all students, undergraduate and graduate, who experience complex, hardship, and/or unforeseen circumstances, by providing individualized assistance and tailored interventions

name: Terry Fintech Society
description: The purpose for Terry FinTech Society is to disseminate and further explore the emerging areas of FinTech in order to adequately prepare the next generation of innovators and problem-solvers. This purpose will be acheived through education, implementation, impact, and service.
summary: The purpose for Terry FinTech Society is to disseminate and further explore the emerging areas of FinTech in order to adequately prepare the next generation of innovators and problem-solvers.

name: Privacy and Technology Law Society
description: The Privacy and Technology Law Society (PTLS) aims to promote knowledge and awareness of information privacy and technology law. PTLS further aims to facilitate professional events and panels to allow students interested in this area of law to connect with professionals and experts in the field. Finally, PTLS seeks to build connections among a diverse range of students to promote professional skill development, foster community, and contribute to the University of Georgia School of Law in innovative ways.
summary: The Privacy and Technology Law Society (PTLS) aims to promote knowledge and awareness of information privacy and technology law and foster connections among students and privacy professionals.

name: Common Ground
description: Common Ground was created in order to provide a space for students to participate in respectful political debates with those who may not share their political ideologies. We believe that as the nation is currently seen as more divided than ever, it's important for us to set aside time to talk with people we disagree with and to see past our differences. Our founding members came together to talk about politics as a distraction from the coronavirus crisis, and we found that we have much more in common with our beliefs than we would have ever guessed. We have seen Democrats, Independents, Republicans, and others all come together and actually agree on political topics. Not only is it important to talk respectfully with those you disagree with in order to build bridges between different political ideologies, but we have also found that by debating these political topics, we have all become more solidified in our own beliefs and have a better understanding of why we believe the things we do. It is important to have your opinions challenged so that you can self-reflect and evaluate your own perspective and belief system. Our main goal is to close the divide that has been growing in this country by humanizing "the other side," whomever that may be.
summary: Common Ground is a club created for the purpose of showing students who don't share the same political ideologies that they have more in common than they might think. We participate in debates in order to find common ground between opposing ideologies.

name: Español en Medicina
description: A student run organization focused on interests in the Latinx community, Spanish language, and healthcare. We encourage pre-health students, as well as Spanish majors and minors to join and become part of a community of service and education.
summary: Latinx community, Spanish language, and healthcare

name: Sustainable Business Society
description: Sustainable Business Society is a student-led club with the mission of equipping members with tools to analyze and better understand the intersection between business and sustainability. Members will develop a deeper understanding of how businesses can thrive using the triple bottom line (people, profit, and planet) through speakers and the chance to work with local Athens businesses.
summary: Sustainable Business Society aims to equip members with tools to analyze and better understand the intersection between business and sustainability.

name: Doctors Without Borders
description: Doctors Without Borders / Médecins Sans Frontières (MSF) is an international, independent, medical humanitarian organization that delivers emergency aid to people affected by armed conflict, epidemics, natural disasters, and exclusion from healthcare. MSF offers assistance to people based on need, irrespective of race, religion, gender, or political affiliation. We help out the underserved community in Athens, raise money for issues around the world, and help students gain connections.
summary:
Doctors Without Borders / Médecins Sans Frontières is an international,
independent, medical humanitarian organization that delivers emergency aid to
people affected by armed conflict, epidemics, natural disasters, and exclusion from
healthcare.

name: Chi Epsilon Pi Atmospheric Sciences Honor Society
description: The organization description of ΧΕΠ at UGA is to recognize students for achieving academic excellence in the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program. The purpose of ΧΕΠ at UGA shall be to recognize students for achieving academic excellence in the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program. The honor society shall provide a forum for the discussion and appreciation of excellence in atmospheric sciences and plan events tailored to our organizational interests. In addition, the purpose is to take part in outreach activities aimed toward increasing chapter members’ exposure to atmospheric science-related activities and increase the chapter’s interaction with the local community. The Chi Epsilon Pi Atmospheric Sciences Honor Society also places a focus on hosting guest speakers alongside the community outreach efforts and the general body meetings. Membership to join the UGA Chi Epsilon Phi Honor Society is open to currently enrolled University of Georgia students, and University of Georgia Alumni who have achieved the required cumulative and major GPA requirement, who agree to abide by the requirements of this constitution. For induction into the honors society, active members need to be enrolled in the Atmospheric Sciences Program, have a cumulative GPA of 3.3 or greater, a major GPA of 3.5 or greater, and need to have taken at least 10 credits in the Atmospheric Sciences Program. Active Membership as currently defined is given to enrolled students at the University of Georgia. Non-active Alumni Membership is extended to Atmospheric Sciences Alumni who meet the cumulative and major GPA requirements before graduating.
summary: The organization description of ΧΕΠ at UGA is to recognize students for achieving academic excellence in the UGA Atmospheric Sciences Program through a forum for discussion, public outreach services, and guest speakers.

name: Ambassadors of the UGA Mentor Program
description:
Vision

Every UGA student has a mentor in their corner prior to going beyond the Arch.

Mission

Our mission is to strengthen the UGA community by fostering meaningful mentoring relationships between students and experienced Bulldogs through authentic connection, shared experience, and mutual support.

Overview of the Program

Each ambassador will take part in 1 committee (Social Media & Strategic Marketing; Mentee Recruitment and Engagement; Professional Development and Internal Relations; Mentor & Faculty Recruitment & Engagement). Committees will meet throughout the year to plan events and execute new initiatives. Throughout the year, our ambassadors have the opportunity to gain a sense of community; Leadership; Public Speaking skills; Presentation building; Event Planning skills; Professional Development Opportunities; Leaving a Legacy at UGA.
summary: Ambassadors of the UGA Mentor Program promote the UGA Mentor Program and support students to develop an appreciation for mentorship as a lifelong personal and professional development tool.

name: Amara
description: Amara is a dance team that specializes in classical Indian dance styles. We represent the University of Georgia in the Origins Classical dance circuit and compete in competitions around the nation. We aim to build a strong foundation by forming tight relationships between our members and promoting our university both on campus and at competitions. We aim to create a welcoming community within our organization while raising awareness about indian classical heritage.
summary: Amara is the premier, co-ed, competitive Indian Classical dance team.

name: Loch Johnson Society
description: The Loch Johnson Society is the University of Georgia's student-led, nonpartisan foreign policy journal. It aims to develop future world leaders and policy practitioners by providing a platform for discussions and analysis regarding international security and trade. To this end, our members create policy briefs, articles, weekly briefings, and other forms of media intended to highlight critical foreign affairs issues.
summary: The Loch Johnson Society is a student-led, non-partisan foreign policy group at UGA that aims to shape future world leaders and practitioners by engaging in dialogue surrounding international security and trade.

name: Engagement, Leadership, and Service
description:
The Department of Engagement, Leadership, and Service (ELS) seeks to inspire students to engage meaningfully, grow intellectually, and build character so they will create thriving communities.      

ELS believes student engagement is a pivotal part of an enriched collegiate experience. With ELS, students can join student organizations, participate in and lead service opportunities, engage with leadership trainings and content, coordinate and attend multiple events on campus, and so much more.

Looking for opportunities to be involved on campus or in the community? ELS is your entry point to hundreds of options.
summary: The Department of Engagement, Leadership, and Service (ELS) seeks to inspire students to engage meaningfully, grow intellectually, and build character so they will create thriving communities.

name: University Transitions
description:
The Department of University Transitions provides intentional support for the unique needs of students in, through, and out of UGA. We empower students with support for individual success by facilitating an enhanced sense of belonging.

University Transitions offers robust programs and connects students to Student Affairs and campus resources in order to help students effectively navigate times of transition and continue timely progression to graduation.
summary: The Department of University Transitions provides dedicated support for key times of student transition.

name: Aurora at UGA
description:
Aurora at UGA is a service organization that aims to bring back the joys of childhood to children with cancer and their siblings. We accomplish this mission through year-round programs designed to provide happiness to them during challenging times. Some of these programs include...

  • Attending Fun Days (events at which we volunteer and take the children on fun adventures to trampoline parks, the movies, corn mazes, parks, etc.)
  • Writing letters and making friendship bracelets for children with cancer and their siblings
  • Creating personalized goody bags to distribute at the hospitals
  • Fundraising
  • Other acts of service all with the purpose of spreading joy to these children

If you have any questions or would like more information about getting involved, feel free to email us at aurorabulldogs@gmail.com!

 

Click here to officially register!

 

Aurora at UGA is a subsidiary of the nonprofit Aurora Day Camp, a Sunrise Association member based in Atlanta, Georgia.

 

Our Website

Join our GroupMe!
summary: Aurora at UGA serves to bring back joy into the lives of children with cancer and their siblings through heartening programs designed to put a smile on their faces amid challenging times.

name: Thirst Project Club at UGA
description: This club supports a foundation called the Thirst Project that builds sustainable freshwater wells that completely change the lives of the community the well supports. 4,100 children die everyday due to a lack of access to clean drinking water, and every year waterborne diseases kill more people than AIDs, Malaria, and all world violence combined. Women and children in the communities we help walk 4-6 miles each day to gather water in 45lb jugs that hold only 5 gallons of water, which means that the children can’t get an education and the women cannot work. Moreover, the 5 gallons of water that likely contains typhoid fever, E. coli, and cholera is meant to support all their cooking, cleaning, hygienic, and drinking needs. For comparison, we as Americans use 150 gallons a day and flush 5 gallons of water every time we use the toilet. When a well is completed in a village, the child mortality rate drops 90% overnight, and disease rates drop upwards of 88%. Women are able to get jobs, children can get an education, and the village finally has crop security with a constant water source. Lastly, sanitation and hygiene improves, with water related deaths dropping 37.5% and 35% respectively. With $25 you can buy someone water for life; (as $12,000 funds a well). As people of not only UGA but the world, it is our duty to do as much as we can to help those who weren't born into the same standard of living we are accustomed to.
summary: We are a club dedicated to raising funds and awareness to aid the water crisis. Waterborne diseases kill more people each year than AIDs, malaria, and all world violence combined, so we believe it is our duty to help those in need and combat the crisis.

name: Black Felicity Student Association
description: The Black Felicity Student Association is a multi-cultural organization at UGA that will be focusing on political advocacy and strengthening relationships with the Athens community. Our main goal is to aid the well-being and advancement of people by providing resources for growth and development and ensuring self-sufficiency. The Black Felicity Student Association strives to provide political information to all because we believe that it is important to make comprehensible information accessible and give everyone a fair opportunity to vote with knowledge about all candidates, including those in local elections. We also aim to be another group of people focused on and fighting for the betterment of people at UGA, in the Athens community, and in life.
summary: The Black Felicity Student Association is an organization that mainly focuses on political advocacy and aides in the well-being and promotion of advancement for black people by providing resources for growth and development and ensuring self-sufficiency.

name: XChanged Life
description: XChanged Life is dedicated to enriching the experience of incoming exchange students at the University of Georgia. We achieve this by facilitating vibrant social interactions and fostering strong connections with permanent UGA students. Through engaging events and activities, such as ice skating, international dinner nights, and hiking trips, we aim to create memorable experiences and lasting friendships. Additionally, at the start of each semester, we organize a buddy system to ensure that UGA students and exchange students can forge deeper personal connections.
summary:
XChanged Life fosters a community for current exchange students and UGA students passionate about travel and cross-cultural communication.

Our mission is to cultivate a safe and inclusive environment where students can engage in cultural exchanges.

name: Special Pops Tennis
description: Special Pops tennis is an adaptive tennis program specifically designed to share the lifetime sport of tennis with children and adults with intellectual disabilities. Our organization works to increase the involvement of UGA students in order to facilitate the integration of people with intellectual disabilities into the community. By hosting weekly practices, pizza parties, movie nights and participating in nation-wide tournaments, SPTA hopes to foster friendships among students and those with intellectual disabilities. Email ju63654@uga.edu for more information about joining!
summary: Special Pops Tennis is a low commitment, fun program that hosts weekly tennis practices for people with special needs. Our goal is to integrate those with special needs in the community through sports. *NO TENNIS EXPERIENCE NEEDED*

name: Young Nonprofit Professionals Network at UGA
description:
Founded in July 1997 and incorporated in 2003, the YNPN activates emerging leaders to fulfill our vision of stronger communities supported by a diverse and powerful social sector. As of 2015, YNPN has over 40 chapters around the United States. YNPN UGA is one of the proud chapters in the United States. 

YNPN is building a network that:
● Activates the next generation of emerging leaders
● Creates a pipeline for diverse talent into the nonprofit sector
● Elevates the voices, ideas, and experiences of young nonprofit professionals
● Provides support and training to empower young professionals
● Brings innovative ideas to the sector and inspires big-picture thinking

YNPN vision: Stronger communities advanced by a diverse and powerful social sector.

Five pillars of leadership development:
1. PROVIDE ACCESS to training and skill development
2. CONNECT to individual coaching and support
3. PROMOTE SPACE to exercise those skills
4. GIVE ACCESS to networks
5. FACILITATE REFLECTION AND SPACE for building emotional intelligence
summary:
The YNPN - Athens chapter is dedicated to activate the next generation of emerging
leaders in the social sector who currently study at UGA. YNPN vision: Stronger communities advanced by a diverse and powerful social sector.

name: Office of Service-Learning
description: The UGA Office of Service-Learning (OSL) supports academic service-learning and community engagement initiatives designed to enhance students’ civic and academic learning, promote engaged research that is responsive to community needs, and contribute to the public good through mutually beneficial community-university partnerships. The OSL reports jointly to the Vice President for Public Service and Outreach and the Vice President for Instruction.
summary: The OSL was established in 2005 to promote and support the development of quality academic service-learning experiences in response to critical community needs.

name: CodeHub
description: Interested in exploring CS languages and topics? CodeHub is all about hands-on exploration! You can collaborate on personal projects and meet others interested in tech, data, statistical analysis, computer science, and more. We encourage students to join regardless of major and explore how coding and technology intersect with their interests.
summary: CodeHub provides a space to explore coding, connect students to others passionate about coding, and provide students with additional resources for learning about coding and computer science.

name: Farmers Market Friends
description: The purpose of this club is to raise awareness of and improve ease of access to the Athens Farmers Market and the Farm RX Program to the citizens of Athens-Clarke County. This is accomplished through fundraising (including, but not limited to, grant writing), incentivizing volunteering from UGA students, spreading information on campus & through social media, and providing useful tips and details on the benefits of healthy eating and the science involved in malnutrition, a common cause of many health issues experienced by low-income Athens residents. Farmers Market Friends hosts events and performs direct community outreach in order to accomplish the goal of making Athens a better place to live. This includes implementing "Farm to Campus" farmers markets, which accept Paw Points for produce and gives students the opportunity to run a food system. The club works directly with the farmers market and the coordinator of the Farm RX program in order to effectively fulfill their needs.
summary: Farmers Market Friends aims to improve ease of access to the Athens Farmers Market and the Farm RX Program for the citizens of Athens-Clarke County, as well as incentivize student engagement with local food systems by hosting Farm to Campus markets.

name: Project Sunshine
description: Project Sunshine is a national club, and Project Sunshine at UGA is the University of Georgia chapter! Project Sunshine serves pediatric patients, from newborn to 21 years old, facing all medical challenges. The programs Project Sunshine organizes encourages family members to participate, because we know that a child being sick affects the whole family. The goal of Project Sunshine is to organize events and activities for children in hospitals to help them adapt to their stay in the hospital and bring them happiness during their treatment. This is done through a plethora of events that not only promote healthy habits, but are also a lot of fun for everyone!
summary: Project Sunshine at UGA serves pediatric patients, from newborn to 21 years old, facing all medical challenges. The goal of Project Sunshine is to organize events and activities for children in hospitals to help them adapt to their stay in the hospital.

name: Brain Exercise Initiative at the University of Georgia
shortName: Brain Exercise Initiative at UGA and/or BEI at UGA
description:
We are a nonprofit, student-led organization that aims to improve the cognitive function of senior citizens that have Alzheimer's Disease, Dementia, and other related diseases.

Student volunteers play brain games with senior citizens, such as simple arithmetic, reading, and writing exercises, which studies show are mentally stimulating. The program the volunteers use is called a Learning Therapy. This program is based on a study done by a Japanese neuroscience team, which shows that reading aloud and doing simple arithmetic can improve cognitive function. The study shows that doing these exercises multiple times a week can improve the overall cognitive ability and day-to-day life of patients with Alzheimer's, Dementia, and other related diseases. 

Please email President, Nika Shlomi (ns02484@uga.edu) if you are interested in joining or may have any questions.
summary: Brain Exercise Initiative at the University of Georgia is a student body organization that aims to improve the cognitive function of senior citizens by pairing them with student volunteers who proctor brain-stimulating activities.

name: First Gen Dawgs
description: First Gen Dawgs is an organization that uplifts and creates a community within the first-generation population on campus. Our programs provide students with essential resources to succeed, develop professionally, and gain valuable experience and knowledge. First Gen Dawgs also fosters a supportive environment where students can build connections with peers who share similar backgrounds. Within this community, they’ll discover diversity, mentorship, and opportunities to grow their network and thrive both academically and personally.
summary: First Gen Dawgs is an organization for first-generational college undergraduates at the University of Georgia. This organization will create community, build students up professionally, and uplift the first-generational student population.

name: Black Student Pharmacist Organization
description:
The purpose of the The Black Student Pharmacist Organization (BSPO) aims to support and uplift underrepresented minority pharmacy students by fostering academic success and connection, while welcoming all students to engage, learn, and build community together:

  1. Informational/Education: organizing events and seminars which cater to the intrinsically unique nature of the Black experience. As many preventable diseases disproportionately affect Blacks, our goal is to create awareness and educate the Black community, from within the community. We aim to enhance the visibility of Black pharmacists within the Athens community, UGA campus, and beyond. We also strive to incorporate programming that pays special consideration to societal barriers that have historically limited access to higher education within this unique population.
  2. Academic: provide meaningful learning opportunities to all professional students that reflect on past and present contributions of Blacks within the field of pharmacy. The BSPO will educate individuals outside of the organization with focus groups, discussions, and dialogue about the cultural intricacies associated with the Black experience. Additionally, we aim to increase awareness among high school, undergraduate, and professional students about the significance of positive Black representation within the pharmacy profession.
  3. Social/Interpersonal: provide social outlets for interpersonal contact, cultural bonding, and emotional support to aid with the retention of underrepresented minority professional students within predominantly white institutions such as UGA. The BSPO will serve as one of many learning liaisons between students and the College of Pharmacy (COP) with regards to the Black student experience and opportunities to build partnerships that extend beyond the current curriculum.
  4. Community Service: provide resources (e.g. intellectual, social, psychoeducational, emotional, financial support and/or assistance) in an effort to give back to predominantly Black communities. Through this endeavor, we recognize our professional responsibility and interconnectedness as we strive to empower others within this shared community.
summary: The Black Student Pharmacist Organization (BSPO) aims to support and uplift underrepresented minority pharmacy students by fostering academic success and connection, while welcoming all students to engage, learn, and build community together.

name: Beyond the Bell Tutoring
description: Beyond the Bell is a non-profit tutoring organization that provides free after-school tutoring opportunities for students in Athens-Clarke County high schools, specifically in STEM subjects. Our organization comprises dedicated volunteer tutors who attend the University of Georgia and want to help improve the learning outcomes of high school students in their local community. Our mission is to provide excellent, free tutoring services for STEM classes to students in Athens-Clarke County high schools and encourage students to seek their best possible performance in all aspects of their education.
summary: Beyond the Bell is a UGA-led non-profit offering free STEM tutoring to Athens-Clarke high school students. Our volunteers help improve learning outcomes and inspire students to excel in their education.

name: Graduate Arts Community Council
description: GACC is a place for graduate students studying art to come together and work towards the further development of our graduate community and to organize as a body towards the collective interest. Our goal is to provide 1) a forum for critical discussion and appreciation of our collective interests and our specific research topics 2) a place to participate in various community-building, event-based, and research-oriented initiatives tailored to our community’s needs and 3) to support the development of community within the graduate arts programs, with an especial focus on the leadership and voices of MFA Studio Art program participants.
summary: GACC's purpose is to build community within the graduate art programs.

name: Cognitive Science Club
description: An interdisciplinary student organization exploring historical and contemporary theories of mind, and the implications of these theories across a wide range of disciplines, notably AI, philosophy, linguistics, psychology, and computer science. Our goal is to establish a collaborative and open forum for discussion in response to member-suggested readings and/or videos. All majors are welcome. Join our GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/104065235/bK4KWPVn
summary: An interdisciplinary student organization exploring historical and contemporary theories of mind.

name: Criminal Law Society
description: The Criminal Law Society was founded to provide all students at the University of Georgia School of Law with the opportunity to engage in informational meetings, panels, and networking events with practicing attorneys in order to give students interested in a criminal law career a chance to engage with the field beyond the constraints of the classroom.  The field of criminal law is ever-changing, and the Criminal Law Society aims to promote the professional and ethical growth of future lawyers who are mindful of the complex and difficult issues they will encounter every day as prosecutors and defenders.
summary: Educational and networking opportunities for students within the School of Law interested in the practice of criminal law

name: Recreational Sports
description:
The Department of Recreational Sports in the Division of Student Affairs promotes healthy lifestyle choices by providing development, growth, and education for the University of Georgia through the spirit of recreation.

Rec Sports is housed in the 440,000 square foot Ramsey Student Center on the east side of UGA's campus. Program areas include Aquatics, Competitive Sports, Fitness & Wellness, Outdoor Recreation and Strength & Conditioning. The Ramsey Center includes the Gabrielsen Natatorium's three pools, two strength & conditioning rooms and outlooks with cardio equipment, racquetball courts, the Outdoor Recreation Center, a climbing wall and bouldering wall, three main gyms and fitness studios. Other facilities like the Recreational Sports Complex and Challenge Course are also managed by Rec Sports.

Values:

Ram Fam: Everyone is welcome: come play!
Excellence: Be the best: exceed expectations.
Customer Service: We want to know who you are: everyone matters.
Spirit of Recreation: Providing activities for enjoyment: there is something for everyone.
summary: The Department of Recreational Sports in the Division of Student Affairs promotes healthy lifestyle choices by providing development, growth, and education for the University of Georgia through the spirit of recreation.

name: African Graduate Students' Forum at UGA
description:
Vision

To create a network of all graduate students that promotes African culture across the broader University community, through the fostering of ethnic socio-cultural and professional engagements.

Mission

To establish of a community catered towards African international students at the University of Georgia.

To create engaging social and professional activities for the balanced growth of members.

To facilitate a safe-space for dialogue of African and other inter-cultural matters.

To share African culture to the rest of the University community. Membership is open to everyone. 

To cater to well-being of new members, while they navigate the transition to a new cultural and educational landscape of the United States.
summary: The African Graduate Students' Forum (AGSF)  is a network of graduate students at UGA, established to promote African culture, and cater to the social and professional growth of its members. Membership is open to everyone.

name: PhD Consulting Club
description: PhD Consulting Club aims to provide a means for non-business Master's/Doctoral students at UGA to prepare themselves for careers in consulting and to create a platform for members to connect with UGA's network of consulting professionals. Our members take part in trainings, case analyses, and form teams to take part in case competitions. We welcome all of you to join us.
summary: PhD Consulting Club aims to provide a means for students at UGA and its affiliated community to prepare themselves for careers in consulting and to create a platform for members to connect with UGA's network of consulting professionals.

name: Capture!
description:
• Capture! provides a place for members to express creativity through photography. Our organization welcomes all students, regardless of experience level, major, year, or possession of a professional camera. By joining, members will have the opportunity to attend a variety of different meetings, ranging from workshops to develop photography skills to local photographers serving as guest speakers. Furthermore, Capture! supplies its members with a supportive community to share and discuss their photos in a fun and judgment-free environment.
  • New members please join our GroupMe for information on upcoming events! https://groupme.com/join_group/71508694/tTCZNzDw
summary: Capture! provides a place for members to express creativity through photography. Our organization welcomes all students, regardless of experience level, major, year, or possession of a professional camera.

name: Association of Black Psychologist Student Circle
description: Black/African Centered psychology is a dynamic manifestation of unifying African principles, values and traditions. It is the self- conscious "centering" of psychological analyses and applications in African realities, cultures, and epistemologies. Therefore, ABPsi Student Circle is organized to operate as a resource for undergraduate and graduate students in psychology or a related discipline with an interest in Black/African centered psychology. Student Circle members have access to scholarship opportunities, leadership and professional development and student support.
summary: ABPsi Student Circle members are undergraduate and graduate students in psychology or a related discipline. Student Circle members have access to scholarship opportunities, leadership and professional development and student support.

name: TAMID Group
description:
TAMID Group

Education

Participate in an interactive educational program that integrates business leadership principles with Israel’s economic landscape. 

Consulting

Members may choose to join student consulting teams, advising Israeli companies on the solutions to important business problems.

Investment Fund

Members can also join the Investment Fund program, in which teams conduct equity research and manage a stock portfolio.

Fellowship

Each summer, members have the opportunity to spend eight weeks in Israel on our capstone internship experience.
summary: TAMID is a comprehensive, multi-phased student program on college campuses aimed at improving consulting and financial skills through real-world engagements and pitch competitions.

name: Athens Student Investors Club
description: Athens Student Investors Club is an organization focused on the stock market, global economy, and current events. Our main priority, however, is being a place where students with common interest can network and discuss polarizing topics.
summary: Athens Student Investors Club is an organization focused on the stock market, global economy, and current events. Our main priority, however, is being a place where students with common interest can network and discuss polarizing topics.

name: Out in STEM
description: oSTEM (Out in STEM) is a student organization devoted to the professional, social, and academic development of its members. It is an entity for LGBTQIA+ STEM students and their allies in all STEM related fields, programs, and majors at UGA. More importantly, we help members reach their fullest potential while remaining authentic to their identity.
summary: A student organization that seeks to promote professional, social, and academic development for members by developing and facilitating a community for LGBT+ students and their allies in the STEM fields.

name: Athens Helping Hand
description: Helping Hands offers members meaningful ways to serve the community and grow as leaders. Throughout the year, members can take part in organized volunteer opportunities with local organizations, weekend service outings, and campus-based projects that address real community needs. Members also have the chance to propose and lead their own volunteer initiatives with support from the club. In addition to hands-on service, we offer experiences that promote leadership development, advocacy, and team building, including workshops, educational events, and end-of-year recognition to celebrate the impact of our collective efforts.
summary: Helping Hands is a student-led volunteer group connecting members with meaningful service opportunities. We aim to promote social responsibility, build community partnerships, and inspire lifelong engagement through hands-on service and advocacy.

name: Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant Association
description:
The primary purpose of the Pre-Anesthesiologist Assistant Association shall be to serve the needs of its members by the following:

  1. Foster programs and activities in efforts to develop:
    1. Leadership and character development
    2. Moral and ethical work practices
    3. Physical, mental, and social wellness
  2. Prepare students for a career in anesthesia through networking opportunities 
  3. Educate and expose members to shadowing experiences and possible career opportunities in healthcare
  4. Promote inter-organizational relationships with other members, professionals, and organizations
summary: The Pre-AA club will prepare students for a career in anesthesia through networking opportunities, exposing members to shadowing experiences, and preparing members for the application process and best practices for being accepted to a program.

name: Game Builders Club
description:
Welcome to the GBC!

 

    The Game Builders Club (GBC) is a student organization dedicated to helping students learn all there is to know about video game design. GBC is centered primarily around video game programming, art, music, and theory, and requires NO prior experience with any of these to participate. If you are interested in building your own games, creating video game music, modeling props, drawing sprites, or if you just love video games, you have come to the right place!

 

    Members join small groups and have one semester to make a game! We hold weekly meetings every Wednesday, room TBD covering various tutorials and progress updates on our games. The club is largely based around getting together with other students and collaborating to create the games YOU want to play, so come and make some friends and meet some people just as passionate about video game design as you are! Accepting new members for Fall 2025!

 

    If you are interested, please feel free to join our Discord. That is where we send information on future meetings and announcements, so make sure to join! See you there!

 

Game Breakout 1 Showcase
summary: Game Builders Club is an organization centered around video game development. Students of almost any background, art, music, programming, or even no design experience at all, are welcome to come learn, teach, and collaborate on video game projects.

name: Office of Sustainability
description: The UGA Office of Sustainability coordinates, communicates, and advances initiatives in teaching, research, service, student engagement, and campus operations. In alignment with UGA's Great Commitments to create healthier populations; stronger, more resilient communities; and a safer, more secure future for all; our sustainability initiatives provide meaningful experiential learning for students and valuable support for the communities we serve.
summary: The UGA Office of Sustainability coordinates, communicates, and advances initiatives in teaching, research, service, student engagement, and campus operations.

name: Wake Sports Club of UGA
description: Our organization is a group of individuals who enjoy wakesports such as wakeboarding, wake surfing, foil surfing, and anything else you can do behind a boat. We will work to participate in these activities as much as possible, for fun and to enhance our abilities in these sports.
summary: We are club who enjoy participating in wake sports and welcome those who would like to learn. Join this GroupMe link to see up to date info!: https://groupme.com/join_group/69669934/OP0kTKax

name: Eta Sigma Delta
description: In 1978, a group of students from the University of New Hampshire's Whittemore School of Business and Economics (now The Peter T. Paul College of Business and Economics) identified a need to recognize hospitality and tourism students for outstanding academic achievement, meritorious service and demonstrated professionalism. At that time, honor societies existed for other disciplines, but not for the field of study serving one of the world's largest industries. As a special project, the students researched and evaluated a variety of options for fulfilling this need. The end result was the establishment of the first chapter of Eta Sigma Delta International Hospitality Management Society. Today, there are over 90 active Eta Sigma Delta chapters throughout the world. The growth is testament to the dedication of both students and professionals in hospitality and tourism to the pursuit of academic, professional and personal excellence.The guidance and support of International CHRIE has made it possible for ESD to grow. It is the intent that this Society will lead hospitality and tourism educators and professionals to the highest levels of professionalism and achievement.
summary: ESD is an international honors society that recognizes hospitality and tourism students for outstanding academic achievement, leadership, and a commitment to service. Our chapter provides members with opportunities for professional development.

name: South Asian Law Students Association
description: 1. To provide a forum for discussion and advice on the experiences of being a minority student, as well as of Desi background, in the legal profession and in Georgia. We also aim to provide a place to participate in various interactions, and plan events tailored to our organizational interests, such as forming connections between professionals of similar backgrounds and their experiences
summary: We hope to provide a means for students from the South Asian region, or of South Asian descent, or of interest in South Asian culture, at UGA Law and other universities, to meet and interact, and for advice on being a minority in the legal profession.

name: Hearts for the Homeless Athens
description: Hearts for the Homeless Athens is a non profit organization that serves to provide one of the most vulnerable populations with free blood pressure screenings, education on the causes of high blood pressure, and clinical outreach programs. Through partnering with local food drives and community health clinics, H4H strives to reduce the prevalence of hypertension in the homeless community of Athens. Furthermore, Hearts for the Homeless seeks to educate its members about cardiovascular disease, patient interaction, and hidden difficulties in the homeless community through volunteer experience. This organization assists with shaping the minds of our members into compassionate leaders along with spreading awareness of cardiovascular disease to underprivileged communities in Athens.
summary: Hearts for the Homeless Athens seeks to contribute to the health and well-being of one of the most vulnerable populations in our nation through free blood pressure screening opportunities, general education, and outreach opportunities.

name: Senior Classical League of UGA
description: Senior Classical League of UGA, like our sister organization Eta Sigma Phi, seeks to foster interest in the ancient world. Unlike Eta Sigma Phi, whose membership is limited only to those students studying Ancient Greek and Latin, we are open to anyone with an interest in the ancient world and the study of the classics. This includes those interested history, art, culture, and literature in addition to the study of these ancient languages.
summary: Senior Classical League of UGA encourages and fosters interest in the ancient world, including culture, language, history, and art. We are open to anyone who is interested in the classics, regardless of major. We are the sister organization of Eta Sigma

name: Sports Statistics Club
description:
Sports Statistics Club's mission is to provide any and all students at UGA with the skills, resources and contacts needed to excel in the Sports Data Analytics and/or Sports Business Analytics fields while also gathering all sports enthusiasts at UGA. Each Semester we host meetings and have events such as:

1: Attending Atlanta Sports games with Guest Speakers from the organization's Data or Business Analytics team 

2: Guest Speakers 

3: Host Watch Parties

4: Coding Workshops

5: Play Wiffle Ball 

6: Hack-a-thons and other sports analytics competitions

7: Attend UGA sports games

WE ARE NOW APART OF THE SPORTS ANALYTICS CLUB AT UGA.
summary: Sports Statistics Club's mission is to provide any and all students at UGA with the skills, resources and contacts needed to excel in the Sports Data Analytics and/or Sports Business Analytics fields while also gathering all sports enthusiasts at UGA.

name: Native American Students Association
description: The purpose of the Native American Student Association (NASA) at UGA is to foster community among Native students, to support Native students in their academic, financial, and cultural pursuits, and to host various events throughout the school year. If you are a Native American student at UGA and you are interested in joining, please reach out ASAP to a contact listed on the NASA involvement page. We are so excited to have you! Membership is open to all currently enrolled University of Georgia students, faculty, and/or staff members, interested community members, and/or NASA alumni.
summary: The Native American Student Association (NASA) aims to foster community among Native students, support Native students in their academic, financial, and cultural pursuits, and to host various events throughout the school year. All are welcome!

name: Gift of Life Marrow Registry
description: Every 3 minutes an individual is diagnosed with blood cancer. The most effective treatment for this disease is often a blood stem cell or bone marrow transplant, and the most requested donor are aged 18-30 years old. The Gift of Life Marrow registry was founded to add as many people as possible to the national registry to maximize the chances of patients finding their life-saving match. As of 2021, Gift of Life has registered over 380,000 donors and has facilitated almost 4,000 transplants in over 128 hospital and cancer centers. The UGA chapter of gift of life plans on engaging our student body and greater Athens community to register as many new donors as possible, and hopefully find a match for the patients who need it most.
summary: The Gift of Life is a bone marrow and stem cell registry with several chapters in universities across the nation. Through hosting donor drives, information sessions, and community events, we aim to register as many members of our community as possible.

name: Choral Project
description: The UGA Choral Project is an auditioned choir that seeks to imitate the "project" model of choral singing. Our other choirs such as Hodgson Singers and University Chorus meet every week on the same schedule throughout the year to prepare for their concerts. Instead, Choral Project singers are expected to prepare for their concerts with a quick turnaround and largely on their own. This organization is for talented vocalists and sight readers looking to get insight into what the professional choral world is like. After the audition, singers will receive music to learn on their own before meeting intensely for a short period of time at the beginning of the semester and putting on a concert in the first week of classes.
summary: The UGA Choral Project is an auditioned choir that seeks to imitate the "project" model of choral singing, and provide our students a near-professional experience.

name: Pre-Dental & Pre-Medical Association
description:
Pre-Dental & Pre-Medical Association aims to create a club involving both Pre-Dental and Pre-Medical students who are deciding which path to take or who want general opportunities and networking to enhance resumes for applying to schools. This club would provide a plethora of resources to pre-health students such as unique volunteer and shadowing opportunities, guest speakers, mentor mentee program, resources for graduate schools' information sessions, contacts, and a community of UGA students all interested in pursuing dental or medical school. Pre-Dental & Pre-Medical Association has an emphasis on creating service drives and community service opportunities for UGA students to be able to aid the Athens community. Our goal is to create a community for pre-med and pre-dental students to enhance their experience at UGA, create connections within the healthcare field, and most importantly exhibit our motto 'lending a hand by linking a hand' by serving the Athens community.

Fill out this google form as well as request membership on this website if interested in membership!

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/1bffae1DL4mmPbeIXUNoQa7_3KsX1lgCElR3tzqNfTFc/viewform?edit_requested=true
summary: Pre-Dental & Pre-Medical Association offers pre-dental and pre-medical students with a plethora of resources, invaluable opportunities, and a supportive community in order to enrich the pre-health journey of UGA students.

name: Elevate Mentoring
description: Elevate's goal is to create a firm foundation for young, African American students and UGA students, especially BIPOC, through mentorship in order to foster academic and personal achievement and provide students with role models to look up to. We currently cater to young students in the Clarke-County area. Mentors in Elevate mentor African American students at Barrow Elementary School by spending at least 30 minutes with them once a week in lunch or at recess, playing games and spending time with them. Events are hosted throughout the year where all mentors and mentees come together for large group activities, which fosters community. All are welcome to join!
summary: Elevate's mission is to create a firm foundation for young, African American students through mentorship by UGA students in order to foster academic and personal achievement and provide students with role models to look up to.

name: Cancer Support Community at UGA
description: Cancer Support Community at UGA offers support to populations impacted by cancer while working on our initiative to spread awareness on cancer/cancer prevention. Cancer Support Community at UGA puts heavy emphasis on education, support, and fundraising to maximize our impact. Cancer Support Community at UGA is a team working towards bringing out more smiles and laughter into our world.
summary: As the largest professionally led nonprofit network of cancer support worldwide, the Cancer Support Community is dedicated to ensuring that all people impacted by cancer are empowered by knowledge, strengthened by action, and sustained by community.

name: AccessPoint
description: Our goal is to work alongside the University of Georgia and our community partners to ensure an equitable world for all people, despite ability. Students for Disability Advocacy focuses on three core areas: advocacy, development and mobilization. We advocate with and for people with disabilities, we plan developmental events and programming to raise funds and awareness for our campus and community partners, and we mobilize students at the University of Georgia to speak on the intersectionality of disability policy and advocacy.
summary: To work with UGA and the surrounding areas to promote inclusivity while advocating with and on behalf of  students and individuals with intellectual, physical, developmental, and mental disabilities in all Georgia communities.

name: Bug Bites!
description: Bug Bites! aim is to celebrate all life on 6 legs through learning, getting outdoors (via field/collecting trips), and networking events. Bug Dawgs will assist undergrad entomologists in refining their arthropod-related techniques and skills before they move forward in their professional careers, while having fun along the way. As the sister organization to the H.O. Lund Club, we partner with graduate students to assist with bug-related outreach events in the community as well as connect to mentors within UGA and entomology broadly. We also frequently partner with the UGA Insect Zoo!
summary: Bug Bites! is dedicated to learning about arthropods through collecting adventures and keeping insect collections. We partner with the Entomology Department to assist in outreach events and network with faculty and like-minded peers.

name: Hindu YUVA
description:
Hindu YUVA aims to provide a platform to preserve, practice, promote, and protect Hindu Dharma by bringing together Hindu youth across college campuses in the United States. Our goals include:

Creating opportunities for college communities (students, staff, and faculty members), to understand and practice Hindu Dharma, or the Hindu way of life.

Conducting regular programs with a focus on physical, mental, and spiritual development. We aim to develop character and leadership skills in our members by emphasizing values such as self-discipline, self-confidence, and a spirit of selfless service for the society.

Doing sewa (service) activities in and around campus to benefit the local community.

Raising awareness about issues affecting Hindus around the world.
summary: Hindu YUVA aims to provide a platform to preserve, practice, promote, and protect Dharma by bringing together college students and working professionals across the United States.

name: Women's Ice Hockey Club at UGA
description: Georgia Women's Hockey is a part of the Women's Ice Hockey Division of the Collegiate Hockey South conference. It was founded in 2021 with the mission of "growing the game" for women, particularly in the Southeast. By creating an encouraging environment for all women, Georgia Women's Hockey aims to empower women to grow in their hockey skills, try hockey for the first time, and become confident in the hockey players that they are- on and off the ice. The club welcomes any hard-working, determined, and passionate women regardless of their skill level.
summary: Georgia Women's Hockey is a competitive, inclusive team for all skill levels. Players can develop skills, compete, and join a passionate hockey community. Dedicated to growing women's hockey in the Southeast, the team fosters teamwork and leadership.

name: Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia
description:
The Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia aims to provide a platform for University of Georgia undergraduate students to publish scholarly writing on legal topics. Through the writing process, the Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia seeks to help students expand their understanding of various legal systems and theories, sharpen their analytical and argumentative skills, engage with both past and current legal questions, and connect with other members of the University of Georgia who share their interest in the law. These students work together to publish scholarly articles and other materials for the following purposes:

  1. providing the undergraduate legal community with relevant materials on timely legal topics,
  2. providing the larger community with a valuable educational tool to further their general knowledge of complex legal topics,
  3. enhancing the reputation of the University of Georgia by serving as an elite forum for the exchange of ideas and knowledge between professional ambitions and the school, and
  4. allowing the organization to hone its Members' analytical, writing, time management, and leadership skills.
summary: The Undergraduate Law Review at Georgia aims to provide a platform for University of Georgia undergraduate students to publish scholarly writing on legal topics, connect with like-minded students, and explore the legal field and profession.

name: American Choral Director's Student Chapter
description: Our organization helps to support and promote the several choral ensembles at the University of Georgia. We help with big campus choral events such as UGA Choral Day. We help promote a sense of togetherness amongst all of our choirs. We also provide a space for students who are interested in the world of choral conducting to learn and grow together.
summary: The Purpose of the UGACDA shall be to promote excellence in the field of choral music and to support and encourage the activities of the UGA Vocal and Choral ensembles.

name: Athens Area Puppy Raisers
description: Athens Area Puppy Raisers is a group of puppy raisers based in Athens, GA who raise future assistance dogs for the Guide Dog Foundation for the Blind and America's VetDogs. The Guide Dog Foundation for the Blind places guide dogs with individuals who are blind or have low vision to help them live without boundaries, and America's VetDogs places service dogs with veterans and first responders to help them live with dignity and independence. Both organizations place these incredible dogs at no cost to the handler. The Athens Area Puppy Raisers socialize and train these dogs until they are ready to continue their training at the organization headquarters in New York, and they are the largest group of puppy raisers on a college campus for these organizations.
summary: Athens Area Puppy Raisers is a group of puppy raisers based in Athens, GA who raise future assistance dogs for the Guide Dog Foundation for the Blind and America's VetDogs.

name: Girl Gains
description: We are a nation-wide weight lifting organization that aims empower members to feel strong and beautiful in a judgment-free community. Girl Gains was created to provide people of all backgrounds and fitness levels with community, resources and confidence. Everyone is welcome and encouraged to join our organization, no fitness level required.
summary: A global movement, inspiring women to dominate in and out of the weight room. Our mission is to empower members to feel strong and confident by sharing our core values of Knowledge, Body Positivity, Community, and Strength.

name: Ethiopian Eritrean Student Association
description: The Ethiopian/Eritrean Student Association provides a forum for Ethiopian and Eritrean students, along with those interested in our culture, to learn about our diverse cultures and traditions. Our organization seeks to share the rich and diverse cultures of Ethiopia and Eritrea with the larger UGA community. Our club provides a forum for students to interact and build their social and academic networks. Members will be able to form relationships and collaborate through meetings, mixers, and community outreach events.
summary: EESA provides a platform that can connect more members of the UGA Ethiopian-Eritrean community together and bridge the gap with other students on campus. EESA promotes unity while celebrating and showcasing our diversity.

name: Motorcycle Club of UGA®
description: A community of fellow two wheel enthusiasts who want to better their riding ability as well as maximize their motorcycle safety. We are a group of motorcyclists of all kinds that venture together through our motorcycle journeys. Together we take time in the classroom to learn different techniques for riding. We also always put safety first and instruct the best ways to maximize safety on two wheels.
summary: A community of fellow two wheel enthusiasts who want to better their riding ability as well as maximize their motorcycle safety. We are a group of motorcyclists of all kinds that venture together through our motorcycle journeys.

name: QStone
description: QStone is a social organization that strives to create a fun, inclusive, safe, and comfortable environment for Black, Indigenous, and POC LGBTQ+ students of the University of Georgia. This club is open to all students who wish to join. This includes two socials beginning each semester, biweekly meetings, cultural and celebration events, and study meets. The main goals are to provide spaces for BIPOC students to gather and discuss similarities and differences. Allow students to meet other students they relate to and those who are different from them. Along with a space to indulge in common interests, find support, have fun, and make friends! QStone is a student organization that is open to all students attending the University of Georgia.
summary: QStone is a social organization that strives to create a fun, inclusive, safe, and comfortable environment for Black, Indigenous, and POC LGBTQ+ students of the University of Georgia.

name: Art-Isans
description: Our goal is to create a fun and creative environment for students to come together and express common interests related to art, make art, and participate in art-related events. This includes art/craft-themed monthly meet-ups, monthly practice your skills nights, bring-a-friend events, and more. Along with a chance to learn new things and make new friends who love art as much as you do!
summary: Art-Isans is an art club open to anyone interested in art of any medium.

name: Tabletop Wargaming at UGA
description:
We are a club dedicated to fostering a community of people that are interested in the hobby of tabletop wargaming and its facets. We participate in several activities such as painting models, playing games, and socialization among players. It will serve as a way to make new friends to enjoy our hobbies with. Old and new members are encouraged! Grab a brush, throw some dice, and hang out with us!

Join our Discord! Club announcements and scheduling are held here: https://discord.gg/zRBkwZ9hNJ
summary: We are a club dedicated to fostering a community of people that are interested in the hobby of tabletop wargaming and its facets. This includes painting models, playing the games, and socialization among players.

name: Georgia Baseball Dugout Dawgs
description:
The Georgia Baseball Dugout Dawg organization assists in game day operations and makes every fan feel welcome at Foley Field. The Dugout Dawgs serve as bat handlers, promote the baseball team, and ultimately represent the Georgia Baseball organization! Some duties include:

- Greeting fans into Foley Field

- Assisting with game day operations

- Serving in the community

- Representing at all large events through Georgia Baseball
summary: The Dugout Dawg organization assists in game day operations and makes every fan feel welcome at Foley Field. The Dugout Dawgs serve as bat handlers, promote the baseball team, and ultimately represent the Georgia Baseball organization!

name: Effective Altruism at UGA
description:
We often say that we want to make the world a better place. But how many of us take this idea seriously?

 

When we go out to a restaurant, wouldn’t it have been better for us to donate that money instead? Providing a meal to someone who would otherwise starve?

 

And speaking of food, is it wrong for us to eat animals? Why do we think it’s wrong to eat some, but not others? And can our attitudes be justified?

 

And then there’s climate change. What should we do about it? More broadly, what are our obligations to future generations?

 

These are serious and undoubtedly challenging questions. If we want to live a good life, we all need to think deeply about them (and many others!).

 

If thinking about these questions interests you (or if you find yourself in need of some answers!) EAUGA is the right place for you.

 

We will discuss topics such as: global poverty, animal rights/welfare, future generations, existential risks, public policy, geopolitics, scientific developments, and much more!

 

Alongside discussion, we will provide opportunities to put our ideas into action, encouraging you to take steps toward a better, more fulfilling life. Whether it's volunteering, fundraising, or career guidance, we are happy to help you.

 

If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to send an email to us!

 

---

 

For a short and simple video explaining the code ideas of EA, we highly recommend watching this video from the ‘godfather’ of the EA movement: Peter Singer: The why and how of effective altruism

 

For more detailed information, please visit the official Effective Altruism website! Their introductory article is a great starting place!
summary:
Effective Altruism (EA) is a global philosophical movement that asks: how can we do the most good with our time, money, and resources?

At UGA, we try to answer this question, using reason and evidence to put our ideas into practice.

name: Athens Campus Ministry
description: Our goal at Athens Campus Ministry is to reach the Athens and UGA community to share the simple truth of the Gospel of Jesus Christ. We are not affiliated with, nor financially supported by, a church or denominational background, but most members are part of the Church of Christ. We do, however, utilize the facilities at the Clark- County Church of Christ occasionally. We hold weekly Bible studies, growth-based group activities, and plan service projects in the community. Come join us!
summary: We are a Christian campus ministry dedicated to serving students in the Athens area (primarily UGA students)! This organization is not financially sponsored by any church or denomination, but a majority of students are Church of Christ!

name: Transactional Law Club
description: The Transactional Law Club is an organization for law students who are curious about alternative career paths outside of litigation. Panels will include both current attorneys practicing in transactional law as well as older students who will provide advice to younger students interested in the field. Additionally, TLC seeks to provide a mentorship program for younger students who would like to be more informed about their options when considering different aspects of transactional law.
summary: The Transactional Law Club seeks to inform law students on transactional-related careers they might not have considered otherwise. TLC will host panels, general body meetings, and networking events to connect students interested in this career field.

name: Interdisciplinary Field People
description: We promote the Interdisciplinary Field Program and other conservation and outdoor literacy goals by connecting with other students and faculty interested and involved in the program.
summary: Promoting the Interdisciplinary Field Program and sharing in local adventures!

name: Chinese Conversation Club
description:
The goal of the Chinese Conversation Club is to provide a space for those learning Mandarin Chinese to practice their language skills, all while building community to support them during the rich and formative process of learning a new language. We will have monthly conversation tables to focus on language learning, as well as monthly food events to better get to know other club members! You do not have to be fluent in Chinese to join or be a part of these events, all that is needed is interest and the willingness to learn!

Note: Please join our GroupMe by clicking the Earth icon below!
summary: This club aims to provide a space for those learning Mandarin Chinese to practice their language skills, all while building community to support them during the rich and formative process of learning a new language.

name: Mental Health Counseling Student Association
description:
The Mental Health Counseling Student Association is a student organization for current students enrolled in the University of Georgia's Master of Professional Counseling program through the College of Education. We work to raise awareness about the mental health needs of students at UGA, and we share information about the mental health resources available to students on campus at UGA. Our mission is to be of service to the mental health needs of the students at UGA. We meet monthly with faculty members from the College of Education, doctoral students in the Counseling Psychology Ph.D. program, and/or counselors in the community to learn ways we can better serve the mental health needs of the students at UGA.

*must be enrolled in the MEd Professional (Mental Health) Counseling program to join
summary: The Mental Health Counseling Student Association is a student organization for current students enrolled in UGA's Master of Professional Counseling program*. We work to raise awareness of the mental health needs of students at UGA and share resources.

name: Partners for World Health
description: Partners for World Health at UGA aims to reduce medical waste through the collection and redistribution of high-quality medical supplies to individuals and communities in need, both locally and internationally. Founded on values of global health equity and environmental sustainability, PWH members engage in multidisciplinary discussion at meetings and volunteerism during our supply drives. By participating in this initiative, students will not only contribute to keeping medical products out of landfills but also deepen their understanding of global health issues. Interested members can reach out to the organization's primary contact. See you at our next meeting!
summary: Partners for World Health strives to facilitate the collection and distribution of high-quality medical supplies for the benefit of individuals and communities in need. Members promote efforts of global health equity and environmental sustainability.

name: Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers - UGA Chapter
description:
We are a local student organization intending to support the values and mission of the national organization, Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers.

Our chapter's goal is to provide support to Asian Engineering and STEM students at UGA through a warm and welcoming community to make them feel at home. We seek to connect students on a deep and meaningful level while providing support and guidance, both professionally and culturally. SASE is a respectful and open-minded community, welcoming all Asian identities, as well as students of other identities with interests for DEI and professional development, into membership.

Though we are under the College of Engineering, all STEM students can join!

 

We are here to support you!

More information on the national society for Society of Asian Scientists and Engineers can be found at SASE's website. SASE at UGA also intends to reflect the missions and goals of the national organization as best as possible.
summary: Our mission is to support to the Asian Engineering community at UGA by providing a welcoming environment that celebrates identity and community through professional avenues and fun cultural exchanges!

name: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Political Alliance (APIDAPA)
description: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Political Alliance (APIDAPA) is a non-partisan student-led advocacy organization that promotes civic engagement and policy advocacy among APIDA students on campus. With community education on diverse policy issues germane to the APIDA community (i.e. voting rights, immigration, anti-Asian hate) and advocacy initiatives, APIDAPA hopes to kindle political participation among APIDA students and equip students with tools to advocate for APIDA and other racial and ethnic minority communities.
summary: Asian Pacific Islander Desi American Political Alliance is a non-partisan, student-led organization that promotes civic engagement and policy advocacy among APIDA students on campus.

name: College of Agricultural & Environmental Sciences
description:
Agricultural and environmental sciences are only as important as the food you eat, the clothes you wear and the water and air around you.
