To improve the quality and guard the safety of life's necessities, the UGA College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences has gathered world-renowned scientists and exceptional students.

CAES is among the best and most established U.S. colleges of agriculture. In its classrooms, its Agricultural Experiment Stations and its statewide network of UGA Cooperative Extension offices, the faculty and staff of CAES are dedicated to discovering, teaching and delivering the science required for healthy living to flourish in Georgia.

CAES is one of 18 schools and colleges that make up the University of Georgia. Small, upper-level classes allow students to connect easily with professors. Students can study plants, animals, business, communication, teaching, food science and the environment. The college focuses on providing a safe food supply, clean environment and a better life for everyone. CAES has classrooms, laboratories and facilities on UGA campuses in Athens, Griffin and Tifton, Georgia.
summary: The College of Agricultural and Environmental Sciences is focused on the food, fiber and environmental issues that affect lives around the world every day.

name: Odum School of Ecology
description: We study the fundamental ecological processes that shape the world, inspire and train future generations, and create science-based solutions to environmental problems. Building on our legacy of innovation, we create change by harnessing diverse tools and perspectives in a collaborative community.
summary: We study the fundamental ecological processes that shape the world, inspire and train future generations, and create science-based solutions to environmental problems.

name: Transfer Student Club
description: The Transfer Student Club is a club for students at UGA who have transferred from another college or institution to come together and meet other people who are having similar experiences as them and make friends. Being a transfer student can be a very lonely and isolating position and our mission is to help provide a safe space and sense of routine for transfers by meeting weekly. We're all about community, open-mindedness, acceptance, and helping each other. So if you're a transfer student and you need help figuring out what you're doing or need people to hang out with then come hang out with us. We'd love to have you :)
summary: Hii! This is the Transfer Student Club and we're a club for transfer students to come together and make friends and help each other out as we undergo an untraditional student experience while at UGA. Being a transfer is hard so we help each other out :D

name: Comparative Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association
description: The Comparative Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association (CBS-GSA) at UGA provides students of the Comparative Biomedical Sciences (CBS) graduate program, housed within the College of Veterinary Medicine, the means to meet and interact. Since CBS is a college-wide, interdisciplinary, and flexible program, its graduate students offer unique skill sets across disciplines. The CBS-GSA provides a means to meet and interact with CBS graduate students throughout the program to collaborate, share skills, innovate, and socialize. In this way, and through collaboration with other organizations, graduate students are able to network and develop relationships with peers and potential mentors. We aim to foster and strengthen relationships within our community and simultaneously give back to the broader community.
summary: The Comparative Biomedical Sciences Graduate Student Association (CBS-GSA) at UGA provides students of the Comparative Biomedical Sciences graduate program, housed within the College of Veterinary Medicine, the means to meet and interact.

name: Bodybuilding UGA
description: Bodybuilding UGA provides a community for everyone interested in the growing sport of bodybuilding. Whether you are new to the sport, or an experienced competitor, all of our members work to grow and improve their physiques, as well as support each other.
summary: Bodybuilding UGA provides a community for both competitive and recreational bodybuilders.

name: Asian Pharmacy Student Organization
description: The Asian Pharmacy Student Organization is an organization that strives to work together to bridge the existing gaps in healthcare for the Asian community through outreach and service. Our mission is to provide accessible professional development to future healthcare professionals so that they have the skill set to meet the needs of different racial and ethnic groups in the community. The term “Asian” encompasses over 20 different ethnic groups and 20 million Asian Americans. APSO intends to disaggregate each ethnic group to provide patient education and information through medication counseling and vaccination information cards. APSO will give students a chance to actively participate in educating the community on best healthcare practices and medication usage, develop leadership and communication skills, and enhance their networks of connections. APSO will provide the opportunity for members to engage in semesterly immunization and point-of-care testing events in efforts to make healthcare more attainable for the community. The monthly meetings will be used to discuss upcoming workshops and projects that will help achieve our mission.
summary: This is a pharmacy organization that is open to pre-pharmacy and pharmacy students at the University of Georgia who are interested in reducing the health disparities in the community.

name: Corsairs Law Society
description: Corsairs Law Society is an organization of law students who have found jobs in business law outside of the state of Georgia. The members of Law Corsairs provide advice and mentorship to rising second and third-year law students who are interested in finding out-of-state jobs in business law. The members host informational meetings, provide one-on-one mentoring, and connect students with alumnae in their cities of interest.
summary: Corsairs Law Society is an organization of law students who have found jobs in business law outside of Georgia. The members of Law Corsairs provide advice to rising 2Ls and 3Ls who are interested in finding out-of-state jobs.

name: alpha Kappa Delta Phi International Sorority, Inc.
description: The sisterhood of alpha Kappa Delta Phi provides women with a sense of belonging that nurtures life-long friendships, develops self-empowered leaders, and strengthens communities through service, scholarship, and Asian-Awareness.
summary: We are an Asian-Interest Sorority at the University of Georgia, and the 64th chapter of alpha Kappa Delta Phi International Sorority, Inc.

name: Page Turners
description: A book club open to all UGA students who enjoy reading or are wanting to enjoy reading. Our goal is to provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of books of all fiction genres. This club will read one book every month.
summary: A book club open to all UGA students who enjoy reading or are wanting to enjoy reading. Our goal is to provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of books of all fiction genres. This club will read one book every month.

name: Girls that Game
description:
Girls that Game is a student organization with the goal of bringing together female gamers at UGA! People of all genders are welcome to join. Come game with us!

Click here to view our socials!
summary: A community for gamers at UGA!

name: Georgia MBA Veterans Club
description: The Georgia MBA Veterans Club will represent active duty, Veteran, and Reserve students within Terry's Full-Time MBA, Professional MBA, and Executive MBA programs. The MBA programs have always had a consistent flow of actively engaged military-affiliated students and other competitive MBA programs have a student organization for their MBA military students so we are following suit. The organization will host events where students can network with alumni, employers, and classmates. While formed to represent student veteran's and LEO, our organization is open to all UGA students.
summary: Georgia MBA Veterans Club is a student Veteran organization within the Terry College of Business Graduate school that will support initiatives within the Military student population for Full-Time, Professional, and Executive MBA's (FTMBA, PMBA, EMBA).

name: Athens Disc Dogs
description: The Athens Disc Dogs is geared to students of all backgrounds with a love for being outdoors. Disc golf is played like golf but with a frisbee or what we call a disc. Whether you are unfamiliar with the sport or been playing for many years, we are excited to grow the club and grow the sport.
summary: The Athens Disc Dogs is geared to students of all backgrounds with a love for being outdoors. Disc golf is played like golf, but with a frisbee. We welcome all to give it a try.

name: Morehead Honors College
description:
The Jere W. Morehead Honors College began in 1960 with a handful of classes and about 40 students. We now serve close to 2,900 undergraduates—including close to 692 incoming first-year students for fall 2024 and 269 from spring 2025. Honors students can choose any degree program at UGA, which has 170-plus majors and academic programs.

The Morehead Honors College is committed to providing the best for our students. Through small class sizes, internship programs, travel-study funding, research opportunities, faculty connections, scholarship support, early registration, and alumni networks, we open doors to the best the university has to offer.
summary: The Morehead Honors College seeks to inspire students not only to meet but to exceed their academic dreams, and to provide them the means to do so.

name: Athens Poker
description:
JOINING THE CLUB
It is extremely unlikely that requests for membership on the involvement network or emails to the club email will result in a timely response. We conduct all of our meetings, scheduling, RSVPs, etc., on Discord, a communication service. Follow this link to join our community: https://discord.gg/URp4V6qbdJ

Overview
Athens Poker is a collection of UGA students that meet once a week to play FREE Texas NLH tournaments/ring games together. We are dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding of the game, whether you are a complete beginner or an experienced player!

Format
In compliance with Georgia law, our games are strictly FREE.

We usually play ring games, however this is subject to change on a per-game basis. If we switch to a tournament format, we will provide an announcement in the Discord. We only play Texas No Limit Hold'Em.

We may hold online games on occasion.

Contact
We have a club discord here.

What We Expect From You
Please be respectful and considerate not only in your interactions with fellow AP members but in your play! Playing with strategies that detract from the enjoyment of others (e.g. going all-in constantly, playing effectively randomly, sizing bets absurdly large) will be met with warnings before action in extreme cases

It goes without saying, but toxicity, bigotry, and generally inappropriate remarks will not be tolerated.
summary: Athens Poker is a collection of UGA students that meet once a week to play FREE Texas NLH tournaments/ring games together. We are dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding of the game, whether you are a complete beginner or an experienced player!

name: If/When/How: Lawyering for Reproductive Justice University of Georgia School of Law Chapter
description:
If/When/How is a national nonprofit network of law students, professors, and legal professionals committed to fostering the next wave of legal experts for the reproductive justice movement. Mobilizing and mentoring new lawyers and scholars is a long-term strategy that will build capacity, vision, and leadership for a more successful reproductive justice movement. Law functions as both a catalyst to prompt the expansion of rights and a tool with which to hold governments and communities accountable for the delivery of liberty, equality, dignity, and fairness promised by those rights.

                                                           

Reproductive justice will exist when all people can exercise the rights and access the resources they need to thrive and to decide if, when, and how to create and sustain a family with dignity, free from discrimination, coercion, or violence. We dedicate ourselves to ensuring access to medically accurate, age-appropriate, culturally competent sex and sexuality education, as well as comprehensive, quality reproductive healthcare, including prenatal care, postnatal care, contraception, abortion services, and alternative reproductive technologies for all people, free from coercion, discrimination, and violence.

                                                           

Today, a growing number of law schools offer comprehensive courses in reproductive rights, law, and justice, but opportunities for professional training remain scarce. As individuals, each of us faces difficulties obtaining the education and training we need to become strong, successful advocates for reproductive justice. Together, we can change this situation and become a powerful, educated force that defends and expands reproductive rights in the United States and around the world.
summary: If/When/How is a national nonprofit network of law students, professors, and legal professionals committed to fostering the next wave of legal experts for the reproductive justice movement.

name: Perfect Pair
description:
Purpose: Perfect Pair is created in order to establish one-on-one connections between seniors in assisted living communities and college students. Student volunteers and senior participants will be paired based on similar interests, hobbies, and backgrounds and are provided with a wide array of activities that they can choose to do together. Pairs will be able to tailor their meetings to fit their individual goals and needs, allowing for more personalized and unique connections.

Mission: Through connecting generations, we aim to foster friendships and provide support to seniors in assisted living, as well as, college students who are looking for a meaningful volunteer experience. Perfect Pair advocates for seniors in these communities, encouraging them to re-explore some of their passions with their new companions.

 

____ 

Hello prospective volunteers!

Thank you for your interest in Perfect Pair, we are excited for you to join. To sign up as a volunteer, please fill out the forms below and follow the instructions. Signing up for Perfect Pair is at your own pace, but please keep in mind that you will not be considered to match with a resident until all necessary paperwork is filled out. Matching with a resident is a highly specific process, so please note that the earlier you fill out these forms, the earlier you will be entered into our database, and the higher the chance to match with a resident. Please don't hesitate to reach out with any questions or concerns (see attached email).

General Information:

  1. National Perfect Pair Website
  2. UGA Fundraising Account
  3. UGA Instagram
  4. Perfect Pair Linkedin Profile

 

To Fill Out:

Match Application: HERE

Resource Hub: HERE, password is est2020

One-Time $10 Dues: HERE

Join GroupMe: HERE 

 

We look forward to having you onboard,

Shruti Kapoor and the rest of the UGA Perfect Pair Executive Board

 

Shruti Kapoor (she/her)

Perfect Pair | President, University of Georgia

shruti.kapoor@uga.edu
summary: Perfect Pair is created in order to establish one-on-one connections between seniors in assisted living communities and college students. Student volunteers and senior participants will be paired based on similar interests, hobbies, and backgrounds.

name: Casual Sports Club at the University of Georgia
description: We aim to allow people who are not interested in competitive play to still enjoy and improve themselves amongst fellow peers. Our goal is for people to be able to host their own casual games at any time and for beginners to learn how to play a variety of different sports. Meetings are weekly and would be decided at the beginning of every semester but any member can DM the GroupMe at any point of the week to ask for a casual game (groups must be hosted on campus). We will not cover funding for locations that require an entrance fee.
summary: A noncompetitive sports club aimed to let students learn and play in sports with other interested players. Open to people of any experience. No tryouts needed. If you would like to be a trainer or a liaison, please fill out the form on our website.

name: Minorities in Communication Sciences and Disorders
description: M-CMSD strives to uplift and empower minority voices through community and advocacy to create diverse, inclusive, and culturally responsive spaces. We welcome all--minorities and allies! We are a safe and inclusive space within the College of Education's CMSD program, hoping to increase the amount of diversity within the fields of Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and support each other in our professional journeys!
summary: M-CMSD strives to uplift and empower minority voices through community and advocacy to create diverse, inclusive, and culturally responsive spaces. We welcome all--minorities and allies!

name: Junior Jewels: A Taylor Swift Club
description: The Junior Jewels have shared interests in the art and works of Taylor Swift. We host bi-weekly meetings and events throughout the school year to get members excited, involved, and connected with each other through our shared common interest, which is being a Swiftie. We welcome all Swifties, new and old, and aim to provide a loving and welcoming environment.
summary: We are a club that aims to bring together students to appreciate and admire the works of Taylor Swift. We host events that aim to bring together fellow Swifties and build connections throughout campus whilst also giving back to our local community.

name: American Medical Women's Association
description:
The American Medical Women’s Association is an organization functioning at the local,
national, and international level to advance women’s health and women in medicine through
advocacy, leadership, education, and service.
summary: Our mission is to advance women in medicine, advocate for equity, and ensure excellence in health care. We want to shape the future of women’s health.

name: Georgia Blacks in Business Graduate Association
description:
The University of Georgia Blacks in Business is an organization founded to create space for intellectual apolitical interactions and dialogue to facilitate conversations that incorporate all voices in the discussion of business on a local, national, and global stage. It is an inclusive organization to nurture and promote that our best and brightest ideas lie in our differences and the ability to leverage those differences for good

 

Mission:

Diversity. Equity. Inclusion. Community

 

Vision:

Ensuring the instruction, conversations, and representations in and throughout the Terry College of Business campus and network are reflective of the interconnected global world in which we live
summary: An organization founded to create space for intellectual apolitical interactions and dialogue to facilitate conversations that incorporate all voices in the discussion of business on a local, national, and global stage.

name: Get Outdoors
description: Get Outdoors is an outdoor adventure club that celebrates the connection between Black students and the outdoors. Students will be taken to national parks where they will learn some of the basics of outdoor safety, natural ecosystems, and “leave no trace,” practices. The Get Outdoors webinar event will feature successful black people in the field of environmentalism. Throughout the year, we will host various outdoor recreation activities like hiking, kayaking, rock climbing, and more. At the end of the year, there will be an overnight camping trip that will teach students the basics of setting up a tent, preparing meals outside, wilderness safety & first aid, acclimating to changing conditions, and various fun outdoor activities (dendrology, mycology, navigation, birding, etc.). All UGA students are welcome.
summary: Get Outdoors celebrates the connection between black students and the outdoors! Explore nature while building community and learning about black leaders in environmentalism. All UGA students are welcome.

name: Ethics Club @ UGA
description: Ethics Club @ UGA is a community dedicated to promoting civil discourse about contemporary ethical issues, building a strong team to compete in competitions, and providing students with a safe space to form meaningful bonds through discourse. We believe that discourse amongst individuals of differing opinions is an essential part of critically evaluating and formulating opinions. We hope to promote the formation of these relationships through a variety of open-ended events. In addition, we hope to extend an interest in ethics to students from all backgrounds.
summary: We are dedicated to promoting civil discourse about contemporary ethical issues, building a strong team to compete in competitions, and providing students with a safe space to form meaningful bonds through discourse.

name: Equal Education for Athens
description:
Equal Education for Athens is a student-run UGA organization that partners with a local nonprofit to provide free, online tutoring to K-12 students in need across Athens Clarke county schools. Tutors will be able to engage students primarily in 45 minute reading and math tutoring sessions via zoom. We are also expanding to assist with the college application process/essays, as well as additional subjects like music and engineering. Experience is not necessary and our team will support and guide you through the process. As an EEA tutor, you will develop a mentor-mentee relationship with your student or students and really make an impact on them. We also host tons of fun events and mixers for our tutors!

If you are interested in becoming a tutor for EEA, please fill out the tutor interest form on our instagram: @ugaeea. If you have any questions, please don’t hesitate to reach out at eea.uga@gmail.com.
summary: The purpose of Equal Education for Athens is to provide free one-on-one tutoring in Mathematics and Reading to primary and secondary school students within Athens-Clarke County

name: Phi Sigma Rho
description: Phi Sigma Rho is an organization with the vision to be the foremost social sorority for women in engineering and other scientific fields by fostering among its members lifelong friendship, scholarship, and encouragement. The purpose of the sisterhood is to provide women studying STEM the opportunity to meet and bond with other female STEM students in a communal forum that promotes appreciation and acceptance for a diversity of interests. We strive to provide a place to participate in meaningful interactions, support innovative ideas, and partake in events tailored to nurture creative problem solving and community outreach. The goal of the sisterhood is to uphold each other to high standards of personal integrity, respect, and character in pursuit of academic and professional excellence.
summary: Phi Sigma Rho is a social sorority that provides a means for women in STEM degree programs to interact and bond in sisterhood by upholding the values of friendship, scholarship, and encouragement.

name: Athens Fiber Arts Society
description: A community for those hoping to develop their skills in Fiber Arts, be it knitting, crocheting, embroidering, ect. We come together to craft our projects, encouraging one another to stick with our project goals and donating what we can to those in need. Our meetings are a place for us to get together and hang out, sometimes with a Work In Progress, and sometimes with homework, please feel free to join us!
summary: A community for those hoping to develop their skills in Fiber Arts, be it knitting, crocheting, embroidering, ect. We come together to craft our projects, encouraging one another to stick with our project goals and donating what we can to those in need.

name: Scholars of Finance
description:
Backed by a strong national team, Scholars of Finance is an organization reaching universities all around the country aimed inspiring character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Our mission is to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Our vision is to create a future where all finance leaders steward the world's capital to serve the greater good.

SOF meetings range from speakers, leadership development, value training, and social networking. Along with membership in the organization, SOF offers a wide array of training and learning resources to members.

TO APPLY PLEASE GO THROUGH OUR ONLINE WEBSITE AND APPLY TO THE UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA CHAPTER:

https://scholarsoffinance.org/membership/join-a-chapter/

ALL UPDATES/EVENTS WILL BE ON INSTAGRAM: @sof.uga
summary: National organization focused on ethical finance. Our goal is to inspire character and integrity in the finance leaders of tomorrow. Ultimately we hope this creates a future where all finance leaders steward the world's capital to serve the greater good.

name: Equity Research Society
description: ERS produces Equity Research reports and aims to provide students with relevant experience in the field.
summary: ERS produces Equity Research reports and aims to provide students with relevant experience in the field.

name: Popcorn Club
description:
Each week, we meet at MLC to watch a movie decided by YOU. Members can nominate movies they want to watch, and everyone can vote on which of those movies they wish to see. We employ a ranked-choice voting system to maximize the overall satisfaction with the movie choice. We meet in MLC's auditoriums, which have large screens, powerful speakers, and stadium-style seating.

Join our GroupMe for more info! You can join it at  join.movienightuga.com
summary: We host weekly movie nights at MLC! Members of the club can nominate a movie they want to see, and everyone may vote on which movie they want to watch! We also allow enough time after the movie to discus our thoughts on the film. Join us today!

name: Health for Homeless
description: Since the formation of our organization in Spring 2023, Health for Homeless has been dedicated to giving back to the Athens community. In collaboration with the Medical Business Association and the Minority Student Science Association, H4H participated in the ‘Warm Hearts, Full Plates Initiative’ to support those experiencing homelessness. With generous assistance from Costco and student-led fundraising, we were able to accumulate medical supplies and assemble ~60 first aid kits at our first event, ‘First Aid Kits for the Homeless.' As our club membership grows, we remain committed to addressing homelessness and enhancing our community through student-driven initiatives at The University of Georgia.
summary: Student-led organization aiming to provide over-the-counter medications, basic healthcare, and support for Athens’ homeless community. #CHARITYMOVESUSFORWARD⚕️

name: Ars Gratia Artis
description: Ars Gratia Artis is a UGA student-run straight-play group. AGA aims to provide students the opportunity to both attend and participate in quality performances of theatrical works and workshops. In addition, we strive to foster a creative outlet, as well as an experimental, learning environment, for all those involved.
summary: AGA aims to provide students the opportunity to both attend and participate in quality performances of theatrical works.

name: All Things Ministries
description: All Things Ministries is an evangelical, non-denominational ministry that hosts The ARCH Retreat each year. The ARCH Retreat is a 3-day, 2-night retreat at the end of the summer that exists to help incoming freshman and transfer students start strong on a solid foundation. ARCH strives to connect these students to other freshmen, transfers, upperclassmen, campus ministries, and local Athens churches in order to equip them to seek the Lord in college and for the rest of their adult lives. ARCH hopes each student leaves the retreat confident in where the Lord has placed them, connected to Christ-centered community, and equipped to make an impact during their college career and for the rest of their lives. The letters of "ARCH" each have their own meaning, standing for Abide, Rely, Connect, and Harvest. We aim to equip students to abide in the Lord, rely on Him, connect to His church, and labor together to reap a plentiful harvest in Athens.
summary: All Things Ministries hosts "The ARCH Retreat," a retreat that exists to help incoming freshman and transfer students connect to local churches, campus ministries, and other UGA students to equip them to follow Jesus faithfully throughout college.

name: Youth Movement Against Alzheimer's
description:
Alzheimer’s patients and their extremely stressed caregivers are not going to be the voting population that push our Congress members to fund research – that burden falls to us. The older generation will be the ones affected by the disease, but we are going to be the ones forced to find solutions to this tough healthcare situation. We cannot solve everything overnight, but our generation must make a genuine start. We are the Youth Movement Against Alzheimer’s, and we are creating a future worth remembering. 

https://ugaymaa.wixsite.com/uga-ymaa
summary: To promote understanding of Alzheimer’s among youth and young adults by providing opportunities to help those affected by the disease.The vision of YMAA is to create a culture where youth embrace aging and alleviate the impact of Alzheimer’s.

name: Society of Government Relations
description: Society of Government Relations provides a platform for professionals in government relations and lobbying to share insights and experiences with students at the University of Georgia. Speakers discuss their careers, answer questions, and offer guidance. Students can also network with potential employers in the field, making it an excellent opportunity for those interested in government relations. The organization's primary goal is to connect students with government relations professionals, allowing for competitive advantages in internship placements, and providing exclusive networking opportunities.
summary: Society of Government Relations connects UGA students with government relations professionals, offering insights, networking, and competitive internship advantages. Our goal is to provide exclusive opportunities for student-professional engagement.

name: Polish Student Association
description:
The Polish Student Association at the University of Georgia strives to create a space for students to learn more about Polish and Slavic culture. The club aims to educate the student body and build a community around Polish culture through club events and activities.

Students do not have to be Polish to join the club! Hope to see you at one of our meetings.
summary: The University of Georgia Polish Student Association strives to create a space for students to learn more about Polish and Slavic culture. All students are welcome! We welcome all backgrounds and nationalities.

name: Pickleball Club at UGA
description:
This club provides students with the opportunity to play and learn the game of pickleball.
Want to stay up to date on events, practices, and exciting news? Request to join our roster to be accepted to our GroupMe!
summary: This club provides students with the opportunity to play and learn the game of pickleball. Being a member of the pickleball club gives students access to paddles and balls provided by the club so that students can play and enjoy the game of pickleball.

name: Tau Beta Sigma
description: Tau Beta Sigma is a highly respected music organization that creates an equitable and inclusive environment where all band members grow through leadership, musicianship, and service. Through shared values across an engaged membership base, we are empowered to flourish beyond our collegiate careers in all areas musical, professional, and philanthropic. We are committed to fostering connections and creating a meaningful experience for our members, band directors, college communities, and the band world.
summary: The Kappa Gamma Chapter of Tau Beta Sigma National Honorary Band Sorority Incorporated

name: Sadhana
description: Sadhana, meaning realization in Sanskrit, is the University of Georgia’s first Indian classical music organization. As an organization, we hope to cultivate, preserve, and thrive in our culture through Carnatic and Hindustani music. Anyone with a passion for music is welcome to join, no music experience is required.
summary: Sadhana, meaning realization in Sanskrit, is the University of Georgia’s first Indian classical music organization. As an organization, we hope to cultivate, preserve, and thrive in our culture through Carnatic and Hindustani music.

name: Skateboarding Club
description: Skateboarding Club is a student-run organization that aims to bring together individuals who share an interest in any and all types of skateboarding. Our goal is to create a community of riders who can connect and enjoy these activities together. We organize events and activities throughout the year, such as group rides on the greenway, competitions, and sometimes lessons. Joining our club is a great way to meet other skaters here at UGA, improve your skills, and have fun! GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/90027296/hLtQqc3B
summary: Skateboarding Club is a student-run organization that unites people with interests in all forms of skating. GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/90027296/hLtQqc3B

name: Women's Flag Football Club
description:
Flag Football is one of the fastest growing sports in the country. With so many girls now given the opportunity to play in high school, we are looking to provide a platform to allow girls to continue to play in college. We aim to:

  1. Establish character, relationships, and a championship environment through dedicated student-athletes driven to maximize their potential in academics, athletics, and in the community.
  2. Compete and network with top flag football teams in the nation.
  3. Offer a fun, fair, and competitive atmosphere for UGA students.
summary: The Women's Flag Football Club is seeking to provide a platform for women empowerment by building interest and endorsing participation in the growing sport of flag football.

name: Boxing at UGA
description: Boxing Club serves to build confidence, integrity, and sportsmanship in all students, regardless of experience level. We are passionate about fostering a positive environment where UGA students can challenge themselves and feel confident in their ability to move forward and make an impact.
summary: Boxing Club serves to build confidence, integrity, and sportsmanship in all students, regardless of experience level. We are passionate about fostering a positive environment where UGA students can challenge themselves.

name: Women in dentistry
description: Women in Dentistry is an organization where women pre-dental students are given the opportunity to network, learn, and invaluable personal connections. Also, this organization places emphasize on community service and serving the medically underserved. In doing so, students gain hands on experience that allows them to expand their dental knowledge and be better prepared prior to matriculating into dental school.
summary: Women in Dentistry is an organization where women pre-dental students are given the opportunity to network, learn, and invaluable personal connections. Also, this organization places emphasize on community service and serving the medically underserved.

name: Puppies and Pilates
description: Puppies and Pilates is dedicated to finding homes for dogs in shelters. We host a Pilates workout session with dogs from local animal shelters to encourage club members to consider adopting or fostering. Additionally, Puppies and Pilates schedules a monthly group volunteer session at the shelters. Everyone is welcome, and no previous Pilates experience is necessary.
summary: Puppies and Pilates hosts a monlthy group Pilates workout session with puppies and dogs from local animal shelters.

name: March of Dimes at UGA
description:
Mission Statement:
"Our mission is to lead the fight for the health of all moms and babies. Our goals are to end the preventable maternal health risks and deaths, end preventable preterm birth and infant death, and close the health equity gap."
summary:
Mission Statement:
"Our mission is to lead the fight for the health of all moms and babies. Our goals are to end the preventable maternal health risks and deaths, end preventable preterm birth and infant death, and close the health equity gap."

name: Alpha Sigma Phi
description: Alpha Sigma Phi was founded upon the values of character at Yale University in 1845. The Kappa Phi chapter at the University of Georgia takes pride in our mission to better the man through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood, founded upon the values of character: silence, charity, purity, honor, and patriotism.
summary: Alpha Sigma Phi was founded upon the values of character at Yale University in 1845. The Kappa Phi chapter at the University of Georgia takes pride in our mission to better the man through the creation and perpetuation of brotherhood.

name: Alexander Hamilton Society at UGA
description: The Alexander Hamilton Society is a preprofessional international affairs club with the mission to launch students into foreign policy and national security careers. Our club hosts weekly debates, discussions, and trivia nights, while offering professional development programming for the next generation of American leaders. Each semester, we invite national security professionals from DC to speak with students on the state of international affairs. Former speakers include senior fellows at policy think tanks and heads of federal agencies. AHS meets every Wednesday, 6:30-7:30 pm, at UGA's Center for International Trade & Security (110 E Clayton St 6th Floor, Athens, GA 30601). Find us on Instagram @ahs_uga.
summary: The Alexander Hamilton Society is a preprofessional international affairs club with the mission to launch students into foreign policy and national security careers.

name: Mahjong at UGA
description:
Mahjong at UGA is a student-run organization at the University of Georgia focused on the game of Mahjong. The club aims to provide a welcoming environment for students, faculty, and staff to meet, interact, and play/appreciate the strategic game of Mahjong. Free from any form of discrimination, UGA Mahjong welcomes all students and extends voting privileges and officer positions to all members.

The club holds weekly meetings for members to play, learn strategies, and discuss the game. Looking forward, UGA Mahjong aims to increase its membership, participation, and visibility on campus, contributing to the overall cultural diversity and richness of the University of Georgia.
summary: This club aims to provide a welcoming environment for UGA students, faculty, and staff to meet, interact, and play/appreciate the strategic game of Mahjong

name: One Love
description:
One Love educates young people about unhealthy and healthy relationships, empowering them to identify and avoid abuse and to practice healthy love. The One Love Foundation was founded by the mother of Yeardley Love, a young woman whom was killed by her boyfriend in 2010. Yeardley was from Baltimore, MD and attended the University of Virginia where she was a member of the women's lacrosse team. 

One Love now has chapters around the country determined to educate young men and women about the signs of unhealthy relationships and how we can love one another in a healthy way.
summary: One Love Foundation is a national non-profit organization with the goal of ending relationship abuse. We empower young people with the tools and resources they need to see the signs of healthy and unhealthy relationships and bring life-saving prevention

name: Biomedical Physiology Society
description: The BIPH Society hosts a variety of events ranging from lectures, health oriented programming, and picnics. All of the BIPH Society Programming are hand selected and produced by advisors and students from the BIPH Program in the College of Veterinary Medicine. The organization also provides its general body members the chance to be involved in a peer mentor program, where the upperclassmen students guide and assist underclassmen within the program. The organization is open to all students, including Biomedical Physiology majors and minors, as well as students with similar interests.
summary: The Biomedical Physiology Society is an organization established to promote the community of students enrolled in the Biomedical Physiology Program and other students with similar interests.

name: Audiology Dogs
description: AuD Dogs serves to establish a professional interest among UGA students in the career path of audiology through monthly speakers and meetings. We strive to raise money for UGA's speech and hearing clinic as well as create an alumni network for students.
summary: AuD Dogs serves to establish a professional interest among UGA students in the career path of audiology, through monthly speakers! We strive to raise money for the speech and hearing clinic as well as create an alumni network for students!

name: Premier League Club
description:
Premier League Club is the official English Premier League club of UGA. The club is open to any student at UGA who has any passion or interest in the League and the various teams involved in the elite tournament. From those who have just been introduced or have been following the league for years, this club is open for all to share their love of The Beautiful Game.

Join our GroupMe for announcements and general information about the club itself: https://groupme.com/join_group/94031589/V6v4Vzog
summary: The Premier League Club’s mission is to build community among students of the University through conversation, interaction, and analysis of the English Premier League and the respective parts associated with the league.

name: Campus Outreach
description: • Campus Outreach exists to serve students in their spiritual growth and development on the college campus for the sake of family, community, and global transformation. Our aim is to establish and equip students for a lifetime of walking deeply with God, and being a spiritual leader in society.
summary: Campus Outreach exists to serve students in their spiritual growth and development on the college campus for the sake of family, community, and global transformation.

name: Public Speaking Student Association
description: • We are dedicated to giving helping students gain confidence in public speaking. Whether for a collaborative meeting or work or a toast a wedding, public speaking is an important life skill. We provide a safe place to practice public speaking through exercises and opportunities to speak. We also provide opportunities for members to sign up and practice their presentations for class on us, to provide constructive feedback.
summary: We are dedicated to giving helping students gain confidence in public speaking. We provide a safe place to practice public speaking through exercises and opportunities to speak. You can also come practice your presentations for class with us!

name: Office of Inclusive Excellence
description:
The mission of the Office of Inclusive Excellence is to provide institutional leadership to the focused effort to enhance and maintain a diverse and inclusive environment for learning, teaching, research and service at the University of Georgia. Our goals include:

  • Enhancing diversity awareness and education through training and learning opportunities for faculty, students and staff throughout the university.
  • Promoting awareness of UGA’s diversity efforts by supporting diversity related events across the UGA campuses.
  • Establishing and defining strategic partnerships between the Office of Inclusive Excellence and other units.
  • Identifying and obtaining additional resources that will enhance and support institutional diversity endeavors.
summary: We are committed to a vision of leadership in embracing diversity and inclusion and our staff prioritizes initiatives that serve the diversity goals and academic mission of the university.

name: University Libraries
description:
Our purpose is to advance the University’s mission by providing the best possible access to recorded knowledge, actively contribute to the success of students and faculty through teaching and research services provided in physical and virtual environments that enhance learning and intellectual creativity, and exemplify the University’s strategic priority to serve the citizens of Georgia and beyond.

The Libraries provide their services at nine locations on the Athens campus, among them the Main Library, Zell B. Miller Learning Center, Science Library, Russell Special Collections Building, and Health Sciences Carnegie Library, which receive more than three million visits per year. They offer extensive hours, including 24/7 operation at the Miller Learning Center.

We provide a vast array of electronic and print resources. A good place to start is our multi-search tool on the Libraries homepage.  Librarians are available to help you in person or via our online chat service.
summary: The Libraries provide their services at nine locations on the Athens campus, among them the Main Library, Zell B. Miller Learning Center, Science Library, Russell Special Collections Building, and Health Sciences Carnegie Library.

name: Revive Book Club
description: Revive Book Club will be a space for students who are interested in exploring literature by Black authors across all genres. Our goal is to create a community of readers who engage in thought-provoking discussions. We will strive to foster a deeper appreciation of the literary contributions of Black authors among our participants.
summary: Revive Book Club is an organization dedicated to exploring literature by Black authors across all genres and fostering a sense of community among members.

name: Tamil Sangam
description: Hello! The Tamil Sangam is a student organization that seeks to spread knowledge and awareness of Tamil culture. This organization achieves this through discussions and presentations of literature, language, arts, history, and celebration of significant cultural events/holidays. Fun events are held including a language night, movie night, and a music night! We hope you join!
summary: This organization aims to spread knowledge and awareness of Tamil culture, including elements of the language, literature, arts, and history.

name: Environmental Economics and Management Club
description: Environmental Economics and Management Club began Fall 2023 to grow a community of like-minded individuals and interested students, and we plan on holding helping fellow Environmental Economics and Management Majors and others academically and professionally. We plan on holding meetings with recent alumni of the major along with field experts to get a better insight on the job market, and we also plan on having small talks and presentations on pressing Environmental Economics topics.
summary: Environmental Economics and Management Club will hold events to build a network with fellow Environmental Economics and Management Majors, and it will also work to foster academic and career development.

name: Physiology and Pharmacology Graduate Student Association
description: The Physiology and Pharmacology Graduate Student Association is a casual organization for graduate students interested in physiology and pharmacology. Primary obligations include social events and networking across programs.
summary: The Physiology and Pharmacology Graduate Student Association is a casual organization for graduate students interested in physiology and pharmacology. Primary obligations include social events and networking across programs.

name: College of Family and Consumer Sciences
description: The College of Family and Consumer Sciences (FACS) is committed to student success, holistic development and inclusive perspective taking. FACS develops leaders who focus on the science and art of helping individuals live well in a complex society. FACS students have access to internships, scholarships, service learning, study away programs, research, career development and student organizations.
summary: The UGA College of Family and Consumer Sciences is a close-knit college known for its student-first approach. We focus on helping people thrive.

name: Career Center
description:
The Career Center can help you with…

  • exploring majors and careers
  • developing or reviewing your resume, CV, or cover letter
  • job and internship search
  • networking
  • interview preparation
  • graduate school preparation
  • salary negotiation
summary: The Career Center provides a wide variety of services to UGA students and alumni, including Major & Career Exploration, Resume & Cover Letter Critiques, Job & Internship Searching, and much more.

name: Regenerative Bioscience Undergraduate Society
description: The Regenerative Bioscience Undergraduate Society (RBUS) aims to cultivate a community of students interested in the field of Regenerative Bioscience and equip these students with resources to develop their professional and interpersonal skills. We hope to accomplish this by giving members opportunities to serve the Athens area, provide access to mentorship opportunities between the RBC faculty and graduate students, and present current regenerative bioscience/medicine topics at monthly meetings through active activities that are engaging and fun. In addition we strive to help our members obtain hands-on pre-professional experience in and out of UGA.
summary: The Regenerative Bioscience Undergraduate Society (RBUS) aims to cultivate a community of students interested in the field of Regenerative Bioscience and equip these students with resources to develop their professional and interpersonal skills.

name: Turning Point USA at UGA
description: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and constitutional rights through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion. This is done with the use of provided academic resources which include: weekly activism pamphlets, short videos, free of charge contributor speeches, and in person support by TPUSA staff.
summary: Turning Point USA’s mission is to educate students about the importance of fiscal responsibility, free markets, and constitutional rights through innovative campus activism and non-partisan, thought-provoking discussion.

name: Odum School Undergraduate Mentor Program
description: The Odum School of Ecology (OSE) prides itself on its community engagement and connections between undergraduates, graduate students, faculty, staff, and alumni. OSUMP aims to support and maintain this community by pairing students who are new to the OSE community with their more senior peers and providing structured mentoring opportunities and social events throughout the academic year.
summary: The Odum School Undergraduate Mentor Program (OSUMP) pairs undergraduate students who are new to the Odum School with more senior ecology undergraduate majors to provide advice, mentoring, and community connections.

name: Arts Council
description: The members of the UGA Arts Council represent the performing, visual and literary arts, as well as interdisciplinary academic units. They work to foster an awareness and appreciation of the arts and an environment conducive to artistic innovation. Individually and collectively, the member units of the Arts Council promote excellence in the arts through academic programs, performances, exhibitions, scholarship and outreach activities to advance the university's goals of serving students, the state and the nation.
summary: The Arts Council works to foster an awareness and appreciation of the arts and an environment conducive to artistic innovation.

name: UGA Athletic Association
description: UGA Fan Engagement is the arm of UGA Athletics that is in responsible for creatively engaging fans with regards to attendance, awareness, and atmosphere.
summary: UGA Fan Engagement is the arm of UGA Athletics that is in responsible for creatively engaging fans with regards to attendance, awareness, and atmosphere.

name: LGBTQ+ STUDENTS IN ECOLOGY
description:
Our organization is centered around 5 main axes:

- create a sense of community within the LGBTQ+ student body working in fields related or adjacent to ecology

- develop mentorship relationships between LGBTQ+ graduate and undergraduate students to promote visibility, representation, but also access to safe spaces where to gain research experience

- create opportunities for LGBTQ+ youth to develop their leadership skills in a welcoming space. Each member is welcome to propose their own ideas for events or activities that the organization should be part of or organize, such as (but not limited to) workshops, educational events, but also invitation of guest speakers during ecology seminars.

- provide a safe space for students who are still questioning or have limited access to resources related to their identity.

- Work hand in hand with the Odum school of Ecology Diversity, Equity and Inclusion committee to bring up LGBTQ+ needs and experiences, particularly in ecological research and education.

 

According to campus-wide surveys, the Odum School of Ecology is one of the department with the largest LGBTQ+ student community. Yet, the aftermaths of the pandemic have made it difficult for both graduate and undergraduate students to find a community on campus, which can be particularly isolating for minority students. Our goal is to resolve this issue and create connections between LGBTQ+ students (and allies). To do so, we will hold weekly coffee hours during which students will have the opportunity to meet and interact with other LGBTQ+ members, in a small and welcoming community. We believe that this will contribute to our organization becoming a safe and inclusive space, where students feel empowered to express their own vision for our future. Unlike most existing LGBTQ+ organizations, our community will include both undergraduate and graduate students, which will contribute to increasing the visibility of queer scientists but also provide safe working relationships, particularly when fieldwork is involved. This will also help create connections that will promote better, more empathetic, instruction practices, but also bring together people with different life experiences. This is important particularly when students are in the process of questioning or discovering their own identity, or finding models of healthy successful LGBTQ+ models. Finally, our members will also have the opportunity to use coffee hours to discuss issues associated with ecological research and instructions that LGBTQ+ people are more likely to face, and propose solutions. For instance, many ecological researchers and instructors bring students to the field, in which housing may be divided according to a binary vision of gender, which can be difficult to navigate for gender non-conforming students. Similarly, fieldwork may not be safe for certain LGBTQ+ folks in certain areas. Our members will be provided with opportunities to express their own ideas based on their own experience, and to share them with the Diversity, Equity and Inclusion committee at the Odum School of Ecology, which will hopefully result in the adoption of best practices that were proposed for and by LGBTQ+ students.
summary: We contribute to creating a sense of community among LGBTQ+ undergraduate and graduate students working in the fields of ecology through weekly coffee hours. Our goal is to provide a safe space for members to network and make ecology a safe field for all

name: Volunteers in Pre-Health Organization
description: Volunteers in Pre-Health Organization (V-PHO) aims to serve the community as often as possible. We want to make a large impact in Athens-Clarke County by serving at homeless shelters, nursing homes, and animal shelters, and doing events such as canned food drives. We cater to the pre-health students because we understand the business of their schedules as pre-health majors, and we have the insight to help work around their anxiety about giving up time that could be spent studying. Students sometimes neglect their volunteer hours until the year of applying to their health professional school and are forced to rush their limited time and hard schedules with volunteering. This club is intended to help alleviate the stress of trying to find volunteer hours by finding several opportunities, monthly, where at least one event is available for every member between their tests and intense schedules. Aside from helping the community and gaining the necessary hours for applying to their desired schools, members will build skills to help them in their professional fields. They will learn how to interact with others, learn teamwork and leadership skills, learn how to speak with those in need of help, and most importantly, continue building their desire to help others.
summary: Our goal is to provide Pre-Health (Pre-Dental, Pre-Physician, Pre-PA, etc.) students with an opportunity to give their service to the community. Not only will they help better the county, they will gain the needed hours to further their desired education

name: Chinese American Student Association
description: In CASA, members can share their experiences of being Chinese-American or learning about Chinese-American culture in a community where Chinese and American cultures coexist and are celebrated together. Created to provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of Chinese culture, language, and history in conjunction with American experiences, CASA welcomes anyone who expresses an interest in learning about Chinese culture.
summary: This organization will provide a means for Chinese, American, and Chinese-American students at UGA to meet and interact and provide a forum for discussion and appreciation of Chinese culture, language and history in conjunction with American experiences.

name: Beach Volleyball Club
description:
The Beach Volleyball Club at UGA is a co-ed competitive sand/beach volleyball club. We aim to gather students with shared interests in working out, developing volleyball skills, and competing against other teams. No previous volleyball experience is required, but players will need to be evaluated prior to joining the team. Due payments are $100 for members to cover expenses. Click on the globe icon below to find our website and the link to fill out our interest form if you are interested in potentially becoming a member. Let us know if you have any questions and feel free to follow us on Insta (click the Instagram icon)!

Email: ugabeachvolleyball@gmail.com
summary: The Official Co-Ed, competitive, Beach Volleyball Club at the University of Georgia. We strive to develop students at the University of Georgia as well-rounded volleyball players and individuals.

name: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity
description: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at the University of Georgia is a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields. We are committed to providing our brothers with the tools necessary to succeed academically, professionally, and socially and in doing so create leaders for the future.
summary: Kappa Alpha Pi Pre-Law and Government Fraternity at UGA is a community of individuals interested in pursuing careers within the legal and government fields. We are committed to providing our brothers with the tools necessary to succeed in these fields.

name: Graduate School
description:
The Graduate School's Office of Student Success & Well-being provides holistic support to graduate students at all stages and on all campuses, including online students.  Innovative and timely programs focus on an array of topics ranging from professional well-being (time management, navigating imposter syndrome) to personal well-being (nutrition, financial literacy) to community building (cooking classes). The Office of Student Success & Well-being works to promote an inclusive environment in which all graduate students can thrive.

Spring 2025:

Graduate Peer Support Group Sessions:  Cultivating a Social Life in Grad School (January); Setting Boundaries (February); Women in Academia: Collaboration & Community (March); Working Through Burnout (April)

Culinary Passport Series:  Healthy Eating 101 (January), Baked Tonkatsu (February); Peri Peri Chicken (March)

Professional & personal well-being as well as financial education workshops are FREE and available upon request for graduate student organizations.
summary: UGA’s graduate students are at the forefront of critical breakthroughs in health and well-being, advancements in social justice, and promoting a safe and secure nation and world.

name: Bulldog Battalion
description: The Bulldog Battalion is a highly esteemed student group at UGA, focused on fostering fellowship for future leaders. The purpose of Bulldog Battalion is to provide support and improve morale among students with an interest in military history and community. The organization rewards students for their leadership, academic excellence, and physical fitness through team-building activities and encouraging comradeship. Besides aiding in creating future leaders, Bulldog Battalion emphasizes teamwork, professionalism, and camaraderie, creating a supportive and motivated community. Students benefit from a vast network of service-oriented members, alumni, and mentors forming lasting connections beyond college.
summary: The purpose of the Bulldog Battalion is to provide support and promote morale among students of Army ROTC. Bulldog Battalion will help create a sense of community and encourage camaraderie among students as they develop leadership skills.

name: Women's Network at UGA
description: The Women’s Network (TWN) was created for ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being lifted by their community. TWN offers opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world, network with high-profile speakers and meet peers around their campus and the country. TWN meets biweekly (twice a month) and holds speaker meetings, various networking events, resume, internship and LinkedIn workshops, alumni networking receptions and interactive activity sessions to develop relationships with fellow members. Our workshops and guest speakers help prepare you for your next steps and may open your eyes to paths you haven't even considered. All are welcome!
summary: The Women’s Network empowers ambitious women looking to grow professionally while being lifted by their community, offering opportunities to gain exposure to the professional world and network with high profile speakers and peers. All are welcome!

name: Women in Political Science and International Affairs
description: Women in Political Science and International Affairs is an organization for women to meet and discuss plans after and outside college. This is an opportunity to make friends, learn the interests and career goals of women studying this field, and help each other with future success. This organization encourages women to reach their full potential and support one another through this integral part of life in college. Career development for women in politics can be difficult as it is a very flexible interest. This organization aims to give women a resource for questions and discussions to aid career goals and overall political knowledge. This organization is open to all students.
summary: Meeting biweekly to provide a means for women majoring in Political Science and International Affairs to learn about career opportunities, interests, and discuss and give advice to aid in these goals. This organization is open to all students.

name: Minorities in Public Health
description: The mission of the MPH Club is to promote health equity within the Athens Clarke County community through community service, advocacy, education, and collaboration. We are committed to addressing public health disparities, with a particular focus on marginalized and underserved populations.
summary: MPH Club was created to help promote health equity within the Athens Clarke County community through community service, advocacy, education, and collaboration.

name: Film and Media Scoring Student Association
description: If you like film and media music, we have a place for you! Come join us and learn about writing music for film, games, and TV with many guest artists, and our own on-faculty film/media composer.
summary: If you like film music, we have a place for you! Come join us and learn about writing music for film, games, and TV with many guest artists and our own on faculty film composer.

name: Girls Club at Georgia
description: Our goal is to encourage and uplift those around us in the field of sports and to grow the diversity within the industry. This club is accessible to all people and all majors, making it represent all walks of interest. We will have opportunities for all to network and gain experience that will allow them to further their knowledge within the industry.
summary: Girls Club at Georgia is an organization for all women looking to make connections and gain experience in sports. Whether you are a fan, an athlete, or wanting to work within the industry, this is the perfect club for you.

name: Adil Institute at UGA
description: The Adil Institute University of Georgia Chapter strives to research, advocate for, and oversee the implementation of policies at the University of Georgia dedicated to providing a fair and equitable environment for Muslim Students. The Adil Institute will derive its Islamic foundations and identity from what is agreed upon amongst the righteous scholars and traditions of Orthodox Sunni Islam. The chosen leaders of this organization and its members will strive to uphold Islamic morality, commit to integrity, and achieve change only through policy action and other peaceful means. The Adil Institute welcomes all students, faculty, and staff Muslim or non-Muslim to create a more inclusive campus. Integrity, Aptitude, and Service define members of this organization and inspire the work they shall do. Our events will include but are not limited to General Body Meetings once a month, Volunteering once a month, and lectures once a month on a particular global issue affecting Muslims.
summary: The Adil Institute at the University of Georgia strives to research, advocate for, and oversee the implementation of policies at the University of Georgia dedicated to providing a fair and equitable environment for Muslim Students.

name: InterVarsity Christian Fellowship
description: The purpose of InterVarsity Christian Fellowship is to establish and advance at colleges and universities witnessing communities of students and faculty who follow Jesus as Savior and Lord: growing in love for God, God’s Word, God’s people of every ethnicity and culture, and God’s purposes in the world.
summary: In response to God’s love, grace, and gift of new life through Jesus, we are a community that equips students to know Jesus and follow him in his mission to transform our lives, renew our campus, and change the world.

name: ENGREEK
description: Being an engineering student in Greek Life comes with many challenges. Balancing a social and academic schedule, finding students with similar values and lifestyles as yourself, and finding ways to get involved are challenges that all engineering students in Greek Life face. ENGREEK is a club that brings together engineering students with common academic and social interests to provide community, useful resources, and real-world experience for its members.
summary: Engreek is a club that brings together engineering students with common academic and social interests to provide community, useful resources, and real-world experience for its members.

name: Women in Cybersecurity
description: WiCyS is dedicated to helping build a strong, gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce through the recruitment, retainment, and advancement of women in cybersecurity. This organization is dedicated to bringing together women in cybersecurity from academia, research and industry to share knowledge, experience, networking and mentoring. Membership is open to anyone and is not exclusive to women or female-identifying individuals.
summary: WiCyS is dedicated to helping build a strong, gender-diverse cybersecurity workforce through the recruitment, retainment, and advancement of women in cybersecurity.

name: Health Insight advocacy
description: Embracing Truths, Erasing Biases: Your Health, Our Commitment.
summary: Promoting a future where medical truths are embraced, discriminatory practices are eradicated, and faith in healthcare providers is restored. We stand for accuracy, equality, and trust in the realm of health. We believe in a world where everyone has acce

name: Pentecost Students and Associates
description:
Pentecost Students and Associates (PENSA) is a dedicated Christian organization committed to bringing people of all cultures to the saving knowledge of Jesus Christ. Our mission is to empower individuals with a deep understanding of sound Christian doctrine, foster discipleship, and encourage peer-to-peer mentoring to strengthen their spiritual lives.

At PENSA, we believe in the transformative power of faith and knowledge, and we are dedicated to equipping our members with the tools and guidance needed to lead purposeful lives per their Christian faith.

Our core objectives include:

Teaching Sound Christian Doctrine: We are passionate about providing our members with a comprehensive and well-grounded understanding of Christian doctrine to deepen their faith in God.

Discipleship: PENSA is committed to fostering a culture of discipleship among its members. We provide a platform for members to engage in meaningful peer-to-peer mentoring relationships, where they can learn from one another's experiences and grow together in faith. 

Our organization is committed to creating a welcoming and inclusive community where individuals can explore, learn, and apply Christian principles in their everyday lives. We invite individuals of all backgrounds and walks of life to join us on this journey of faith, discipleship, and service.
summary: PENSA will be committed to teaching sound Christian doctrine, discipleship, and peer-to-peer mentoring to strengthen the spiritual lives of members and totally equip them for service in the Body of Christ and society at large.

name: Catalyst
description:
Catalyst will allow STEM undergraduates to go through the entire peer-review process and gain valuable writing experience for submitting manuscripts later in their academic and professional careers. Approved student submissions will be published in a student-led journal and top submissions will be awarded travel grants to present their projects at conferences. This aims to further broadcast some of the incredible work undergraduate researchers perform at UGA. 

We will have graduate students serving as the senior editors to ensure the journal remains high-quality. As we progress, there will be room for undergraduates to move into editing and leadership roles within the group.

Dr. Todd Harrop of the Department of Chemistry will serve as the faculty advisor for Catalyst.
summary: Catalyst will allow students to go through the entire peer-review process and gain valuable writing experience through submitting manuscripts for publication in a student-led journal showcasing STEM undergraduates.

name: SOS Africa United
description: Our mission is to advocate for, send aid, and raise awareness to support impoverished communities in rural Africa. We work towards this goal through the use of fundraisers, meetings, and collaborative efforts to send support directly to Africa or to related non-profits. The main focus of this organization is on young school children and families who do not have access to basic necessities such as food, water, and education.
summary: Our mission is to help raise donations to support impoverished communities in rural Africa. We want to focus on young school children and families who do not have access to basic necessities and help them in any way we can.

name: Letters of Love Global
description: Letters of Love is a Non-Profit organization that provides emotional support for patients in Children’s Hospitals across the world. LOL provides emotional support to children battling cancer and other serious illnesses through handmade cards.
summary: Letters of Love is a Non-Profit organization that provides emotional support for patients in Children’s Hospitals across the world. LOL provides emotional support to children battling cancer and other serious illnesses through handmade cards.

name: Food Insecurity Club
description: Food Insecurity Club is a collective effort to make food and good cooking more accessible to college students and the less fortunate.
summary: A club focused on publishing affordable and delicious recipes for the creation of meals benefitting people facing food insecurity in Athens.

name: Health Link Society
description: Health Link Society is a national non-profit organization that oversees chapters at universities, with the primary objective of fostering collaboration among students to enhance public health initiatives within their local communities, while simultaneously promoting their own professional growth. Membership is not restricted to individuals within specific healthcare specialties, but rather encompasses those who possess an interest in pursuing a future in healthcare or engaging in community service. Current chapters hace made notable contributions, including organizing blood drives in partnership with the American Red Cross, participating in volunteer activities at children’s hospitals and homeless shelters, facilitating virtual shadowing opportunities, hosting seminars with admissions teams from professional schools, and engaging in various other impactful endeavors.
summary: This organization aims to connect pre-health students together and to professional growth opportunities by providing volunteer opportunities and monthly meetings.

name: Undergraduate Research Club
description: URC's goal is to improve the understanding and engagement in research. Our multidisciplinary club is open to all majors and students interested in research. We hold semi-monthly meetings that focus on different aspects of research, which start with a journal club discussion. We also offer facility lab tours and share research opportunities through a monthly Listserv. At the end of each semester, URC holds a showcase to highlight student and faculty research through short presentations. Through our meetings, we strive to increase undergraduate students understanding of research that students have.
summary: URC strives to provide a means for undergraduate students to learn about different aspects of research in academic and industrial capacities and to highlight opportunities for students to get involved in research at UGA and outside the university.

name: Between the Lines
description: Between the Lines is dedicated to raising awareness about the media censorship epidemic in America and its' public schools today. In our meetings, we will be discussing commonly banned media as well as relevant topics and news around the U.S. We will read one controversial or banned book a month, which will guide our discussion topics. Community service opportunities will be available through various times during each semester.
summary: Between the Lines is dedicated to raising awareness about the media censorship epidemic in America; through community building and involvement, discussion and awareness of controversial topics, and focusing on one "banned" book a month.

name: Office for Student Success and Achievement
description:
OSSA is your academic success team at UGA! We offer: 
- Free peer tutoring
- Free academic coaching
- Support for first-generation college students
- Academic success courses
- Help with public speaking and presentations
- TRIO programs
- And more
summary: The Office for Student Success and Achievement serves as a student success unit on campus. We provide a variety of services to help students transition into higher education and sustain their progress through the University’s unique academic environment.

name: Love and Money Center
description: The Love and Money Center at the University of Georgia brings together couple and family therapy and financial planning to advance relational and financial well-being. We provide clinical services, train the next generation of practitioners, conduct research, and connect with broader audiences through media, events, and community partnerships.
summary: The Love and Money Center at the University of Georgia brings together couple and family therapy and financial planning to advance relational and financial well-being.

name: Devs on Wall Street
description: Devs on Wall Street aims to equip emerging software engineers with specialized skills for a career in the finance sector. Our focus extends to preparing for both technical and behavioral interviews, conducting resume workshops, and organizing events with guest speakers.
summary: The purpose of Devs on Wall Street is to help aspiring software engineers gain specialized skills to enter the finance industry. We will be focusing on both technical and behavioral interview preparation, resume workshops and guest speaker events!

name: Backlight Student Film Festival of Athens
description: Founded in 2022, Backlight offers a variety of opportunities for students to develop their talent in the media entertainment industry through a student mixer, red carpet, film screening, industry panels, and an awards ceremony featuring notable figures. The festival aims to help celebrate and showcase the talent of these students, providing a platform for community recognition that illuminates the talent of young filmmakers and encourages student creation, communication, and collaboration.
summary: The Backlight Student Film Festival of Athens is an organization devoted to fostering innovation and creativity within the Georgia film community by promoting and highlighting the work of aspiring student filmmakers.

name: SustainAthens
description: With a mission to build a visible culture of sustainability on campus, SustainAthens is an environmental club that serves to bring together individuals to identify environmental needs on campus and to plan solutions on how to facilitate change and combat the climate crisis. The club will be focused on collaborating to implement member-driven sustainability projects while learning about the environment through current events, guest speakers, presentations, videos, are more. Additionally, Sustain will serve to build a community of green-minded individuals through fun social events and activities.
