If you want to get active in making our university more sustainable, learn about the environment through engaging meetings, or make new friends, this is the perfect club for you!
summary: Working to build the sustainable change we need for the future, SustainAthens fosters environmental awareness and action-oriented change by engaging with local community efforts, advocating for climate justice, and identifying solutions to climate change

name: Georgia Hurling Club
description:
Hurling has its roots in ancient Ireland. The sport can be briefly described as a field hockey/lacrosse hybrid. Check out these pages for an in-depth description/video:

https://www.ncgaa.org/hurling

https://playhurling.com/what-is-hurling/

Georgia Hurling Club was founded in 2022 and competes in the USGAA (United States Gaelic Athletic Association) with teams across the country. Competitions range from friendly scrimmages with local clubs to national tournaments across the USA. Beginners welcome! All but 2 of our current players were unaware of hurling before coming to UGA. Even if you're only slightly interested, join our GroupMe for updates/practice times: https://groupme.com/join_group/88972097/zZF9gd1J

We hold practices twice a week at the IM fields. Gear is available for new players to borrow. Please email us with any and all questions: ugahurling@gmail.com
summary: Hurling, "the fastest game on grass," is a 3,000-year-old sport of Irish origin. Georgia Hurling Club competes on the national collegiate level. Open to all experience levels.

name: Space Research Club
description: The Space Research Club serves as an organization centered around research around aerospace. It seeks to provide students with the opportunity to conduct cutting-edge research relevant to the world while providing students with the opportunities to grow. The group was created out of a need to provide UGA students with opportunities to explore the field of aerospace and feel supported to study topics related to the field as well. Consequently, we hope to use this research to better the world and the aerospace community. will always be open to all students and will provide an inclusive environment for anyone interested in joining.
summary: The Space Research Club serves as an organization to provide students with the opportunity to conduct research in aerospace.

name: Risefree
description:
Risefree is aligned with the objectives and goals of Rise, a national organization. We actively support Rise initiatives and its mission statement by engaging with university students on the importance of political advocacy work within our campus community. Our organizing efforts encompass various strategies, including but not limited to:

  • Building Strategic Partnerships: We collaborate with both state and student organizations to establish meaningful partnerships that enhance our advocacy efforts.
  • Innovative Recruitment Tactics: We employ creative methods, both in person and online, to attract attendees to our organizing events. This includes utilizing engaging content and outreach strategies.
  • Issue Advocacy and Conversations: We focus on issue advocacy and meaningful dialogues with students on campus . This involves activities such as canvassing crowds, setting up informational tables on campus, hosting events, and cultivating networks of students who share our enthusiasm for free college education.

By pursuing these avenues, Risefree aims to advance the cause of free college education and promote active engagement among university students in political advocacy
summary: Risefree is a student-led campaign organization advocating for college affordability and students' basic needs.

name: Georgia Museum of Natural History Student Association
description:
The Georgia Museum of Natural History is Georgia's state natural history museum. Since its founding, it has provided hundreds of valuable research publications and millions of dollars worth of contracts and grants aiding the University of Georgia's quest to understand the world through research. Unfortunately, not many students know about the museum on their campus. As a result, many students miss great research opportunities, and curators miss students to share their passion with.

That is why the Georgia Museum of Natural History Student Association was created. The club aims to act as an outreach group, notifying students of the wonders of the Georgia Museum of Natural History. Through museum-centered events, the club plans to increase recognition of the museum and connect students to potential pathways they previously had no realization of. The club's ultimate goal is to increase student volunteers and interns for the museum so Georgia's state natural history museum may progress and prosper.
summary: GMNH Club serves as a bridge between the Georgia Museum of Natural History and the student body of UGA. The goal of the club is to strengthen knowledge of the museum across campus, increase visitation, and attract student volunteers.

name: Magical Moments Foundation
description:
Children with facial differences often endure the pain of teasing, bullying, and isolation— pain that can leave emotional scars that last a lifetime. Children that face the world with a difference also endure the challenge of a more silent, but just as damaging, form of stigma: that of unconscious bias. And so, many children with differences grow up in a world that tells them that their dreams must be smaller than those of their peers.

 

Magical Moments is working to change all of that. We create magical experiences that restore hope and provide comfort, inspiration, and encouragement to children with facial differences that are the result of genetic disorders, congenital anomalies, cancer treatment, severe trauma and burns. We help children understand that they are not at all limited by their appearance, but only by the size of their dreams! Through the magical moments that we create, we not only make a difference in children’s lives, but we also promote awareness of the lifelong harm caused by appearance-related stigma and create more widespread acceptance of facial diversity within our communities.
summary: Magical Moments Foundation is a wish-granting organization for children with craniofacial disorders. We work to make a difference in these children’s lives, spread awareness of the lifelong harm caused by appearance-related stigma, and promote acceptance

name: Paddock Club at UGA
description: This is a club for people with an interest in Formula 1, racing, or cars in general. You don't have to be an expert, all interest levels are welcome! This is a social club for people with a shared interest.
summary: Club for people with an interest in Formula 1, racing, or cars.

name: Clean Athens Collective
description: Clean Athens Collective (CAC) strives to enhance and maintain the environmental health of Athens, GA by both performing trash cleanups and promoting green initiatives.
summary: Clean Athens Collective (CAC) strives to enhance and maintain the environmental health of Athens, GA by both performing trash cleanups and promoting green initiatives.

socialMedia.ExternalWebsite: No external website or social media pages yet.

name: Law and Political Economy Collective at Georgia
description:
The LPE Collective at Georgia is a student-led initiative. The aim of this collective is to critically examine the interplay between law, the economy, and the American political system so that we are properly equipped and prepared to be zealous advocates on behalf of our clients and communities. LPE meets on a regular basis to discuss articles and research pertaining to law and the political economy. Anyone is welcome to facilitate these discussions. We also seek out active partnerships with other groups on campus.

 

We welcome opportunities to connect and collaborate.
summary: LPE meets on a regular basis to discuss articles and research pertaining to law and the political economy. Any member is welcome to facilitate these discussions. We also seek out active partnerships with other groups on campus.

name: Club Management Association of America Student Chapter
description:
The Club Management Association of America (CMAA) is the oldest, most respected Association representing the club management profession. CMAA has 6,800 members who manage more than 3,000 country, city, athletic, faculty, yacht, town, and military clubs throughout the United States and abroad. The objective of the Association is to promote and advance friendly relations between persons connected with the management of clubs and other associations of similar character. In addition, CMAA encourages the education and professional advancement of its members and assists securing the utmost in efficient and successful operations. CMAA recognizes its responsibility to assist students in gaining a better understanding of the club management profession and in selecting a career in this sector of the hospitality industry. The first student chapter charters were granted in 1978 to Florida International University and the University of Wisconsin-Stout. The first meeting of CMAA’s Student Development/Scholarship Committee was held on February 12, 1978. Since that time, responsibility for student members has been assumed by the Chapter Relations Committee. The objectives of this committee were established at that time to do the following:

■ Inform college and university students about club management as a profession; increase their awareness of the career opportunities in club management; and serve as the liaison between practicing professionals and the academic community.

■ Provide students with employment opportunities within the club industry by means of part-time employment during the academic year, summer employment, work-study internships, and postgraduation placement.
summary: The CMAA's objective is to promote relationships between club management professionals and other similar professions; to encourage the education and advancement of members, and to provide the resources needed for efficient and successful club operations.

name: Playing Cards Club
description: Students will play and learn playing cards games while fostering a sense of community in a casual setting.
summary: The purpose of Playing Cards Club shall be creating a space where students can teach, play, and learn card games in a judgment free environment

name: Minorities in Business
description: Minorities in Business is an organization dedicated to empowering underrepresented voices in the business world. Through networking, professional development, and social events, we strive to create a supportive community where members can succeed.
summary: Minorities in Business is an organization dedicated to empowering underrepresented voices in the business world. Through networking, professional development, and social events, we strive to create a supportive community where members can succeed.

name: Kappa Theta Pi - Phi
description: Kappa Theta Pi - Phi Chapter at the University of Georgia is a professional co-ed fraternity dedicated to technology. We recruit ALL MAJORS and welcome anyone with at least 3 remaining semesters to join KTP. Our mission is to foster a dynamic community of students passionate about anything related to technology by promoting intellectual growth, professional development, technical innovation, and meaningful social engagement. We offer career guidance, facilitate strong connections among students, alumni, faculty, and industry partners, actively serve the community, and uphold a commitment to equality, inclusivity, and lifelong cooperation among our members. FOLLOW OUR INSTA @ugaktp to learn more about recruitment!!!
summary: Kappa Theta Pi Phi Chapter at UGA is a professional co-ed tech fraternity dedicated to professional growth, technical innovation, and building lasting connections through networking, mentorship, workshops, and engaging social events.

name: ISPOR UNIVERSITY OF GEORGIA STUDENT CHAPTER
description:
Welcome to the ISPOR (International Society for Pharmacoeconomics and Outcomes Research) University of Georgia Student Chapter, where passion for healthcare economics, outcomes research, and policy converge! Established within the vibrant academic community of the University of Georgia, our chapter serves as a dynamic hub for students interested in advancing their knowledge and engagement in the field of pharmacoeconomics and outcomes research.

At ISPOR UGA, we are committed to fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, professional development, and leadership opportunities for our members. Whether you're a student pursuing studies in pharmacy, health economics, public health, or related disciplines, our chapter provides a supportive environment where you can explore, learn, and grow.
summary: ISPOR student chapters provide a platform to support and develop future leaders in the field of health economics and outcomes research (HEOR). Provides an environment where students can share knowledge in HEOR.

name: Rhythm Gaming Association
description:
Welcome to the Rhythm Gaming Association (RGA) – where the pulsating beats of Guitar Hero and the harmonious chords of Rock Band bring us together! Established with a passion for rhythm-based video games, RGA is the ultimate hub for college students who share a love for virtual rock stardom and the thrill of hitting those notes just right.

Club Mission:
At RGA, our primary mission is to foster a vibrant and inclusive community centered around the joy of rhythm gaming. We believe that these games are not just about hitting the right notes, but also about connecting with fellow gamers, building friendships, and sharing unforgettable moments of musical triumph.

What We Do:
Every week, RGA members gather to jam out to their favorite tunes on Guitar Hero and Rock Band. Whether you're a seasoned pro or a complete beginner, there's a place for you in our club! We host friendly competitions, group play sessions, and even organize themed nights where we dive into specific genres or eras of music.

Who Can Join:
RGA welcomes players of every skill level – from those who can shred through a song on expert mode to those who are just picking up the guitar for the first time. Our club is all about learning, growing, and having a blast together, so don't be shy – come as you are and let's rock out!

Why Join RGA:

  • Build Community: Connect with like-minded individuals who share your passion for rhythm gaming and music.

  • Improve Skills: Challenge yourself to master new songs, techniques, and levels with the support of fellow members.

  • Have Fun: Let loose, enjoy some friendly competition, and create lasting memories with new friends.
summary: In RGA we meet to play games like Guitar Hero, Rock Band, Clone Hero, and Fortnite Festival. If you can play on expert or have never played at all, come out and join us.

name: Wellness Society
description: The University of Georgia Health and Wellness organization takes pride in being a safe space where college students can escape from the conventional college experience, and unite to better oneself. Together, let's thrive in mind and body throughout the transformative journey of higher education. Join us in shaping a college experience that values balance and the pursuit of overall health- both mental and physical.
summary: Welcome to Wellness Society, where we redefine the college experience by fostering a culture of self-care, resilience, and holistic health. This is your safe haven, where students come together to enhance their well-being and prioritize themselves.

name: Media and Entertainment Business Society
description: The goal of MEBS is to connect students both in and out of Terry with a shared interest in business within the entertainment industry. Whether you're interested in entertainment finance, talent management, marketing, distribution, or content development, MEBS offers a platform to engage with industry leaders, esteemed alumni, and fellow peers. Explore the diverse business landscape of entertainment and media and gain valuable insights and skills for success in the industry.
summary: UGA’s first student-led Terry College of Business Organization focused on exploring the diverse business and professional landscape within the entertainment and media industry.

name: Office of Global Engagement
description: The Office of Global Engagement provides experiential learning opportunities and resources for students to expand their resume and global experience through various programs – sending students to 88 countries and counting. Students can study, intern, research, and volunteer across the globe all while earning academic credit through our many programs. OGE puts on a Study AwayFair each Fall and Spring as well as a Study Abroad Open House with 80+ programs represented. Also, attend our information sessions all semester long, which cover partner study away programs, scholarships, National Student Exchange, independent experiences, the Global Education & Leadership Certificate, and more. In keeping with UGA’s mission, OGE is committed to highlighting the critical importance of internationalized curricula and engagement in an increasingly diverse and interdependent global society.
summary: The Office of Global Engagement provides experiential learning opportunities and resources for students to expand their resume and global experience through various programs – sending students to 88 countries and counting.

name: DevDogs
description:
Hey, we're DevDogs! We're a group of passionate students who want to better our community through code. We're also an official Google Developer Group on Campus!

Whether you're a UI designer, experienced developer, or impactful tech-enthusiast--we've got a place for you, all year round! Join us as we take some meaningful projects from concept to completion, developing full-stack applications that solve real problems. As a member, you'll join a network of awesome people, practice industry-standard tech, and learn how you can have an impact of your own.

Join the discussion and learn more with our Discord server below!
https://discord.gg/BdDdkNQhqp
summary: A community of student developers, designers, and tech enthusiasts taking impactful projects from concept to completion.

name: Rural Health Initiative
description:
The missions of RHI include:

  1. Promoting access to healthcare in underserved communities through rural health service, research initiatives, and fundraising
  2. Investing in the next generation by supporting younger students in their educational, professional, and personal development 
  3. Assisting pre-health students in building professional and clinical skills and gaining healthcare experience 
  4. Fostering a community of diverse, empathetic, and capable individuals who can share their experiences and resources with others
summary: The Rural Health Initiative at UGA is in collaboration with our non-profit organization, which is committed to increasing access to healthcare, empowering future healthcare providers, and creating sustainable, long-lasting change in the community.

name: Society of Environmental and Agronomic Developing Scientists
description: The purpose of Crop and Soil Science Student Society shall be to (1) provide support for all aspects of the undergraduate and graduate educational experience; (2) connect students to the faculty and administration; (3) foster a sense of community through organized activities which promote an environment of peer support; and (4) facilitate academic and professional growth through workshops, resources, and networking opportunities.
summary: An organization for anyone who is interested in Crop and Soil Sciences. We will hold monthly meetings as well as social events and workshops to foster academic and profession growth, student support, and community in our department.

name: Pre-Law Book Club
description: Pre-Law Book Club is a book club that chooses one collective law related book for the group to read over one semester. Meetings are held once or twice a month to discuss the book and major takeaways. The ultimate goal is to prepare our members better for law school. Our readings will establish better foundations on law jargon; some books we read may also talk about how to handle law school in general.
summary: Pre-Law Book Club is a book club that chooses one collective law related book for the group to read over one semester. Meetings are held once or twice a month to discuss the book and major takeaways. The ultimate goal is to prepare for law school.

name: College of Engineering
description: The College of Engineering at UGA is committed to empowering a new generation of engineers. The college is home to 8 undergraduate engineering degrees and collaborates with the Franklin College of Arts & Sciences to support students in the School of Computing. The college is home to over 20 student clubs and organizations that help you build teamwork and leadership skills, make new friends and professional connections, and give you the opportunity to pursue something your passionate about. The college hosts a number of events and activities aimed to increase your involvement and exposure to engineering fields including National Engineers Week, Homecoming Week, Welcome events, Fuel Up for Finals and more!
summary: The College of Engineering at UGA is committed to empowering a new generation of engineers.

name: Sales and Trading Society
description: This organization will cover all the basics within the Sales and Trading Division of an investment bank and will also be a guide for how the recruiting process works as well as potential exit opportunities. We will dive into what exactly sales is, what trading is, what people who work in those roles do on a daily basis, an introduction to different types of trading desks and the securities they deal with, concepts such as market making, how to network, resume workshops, lots of experienced guest speakers both UGA alumni and industry leaders from other institutions and lots more.
summary: An introductory organization educating UGA students on the Sales and Trading division of an Investment Bank. This club is open to students of all years however it is more catered toward / recommended for Freshman and Sophomores.

name: Pacific Unity Association
description: The purpose of the Pacific Unity Association is to promote unity among the members of the community who share a passion for the practices and preservation of the cultures of the Pacific Islands, or those who are interested in developing such attitudes, to spread social awareness and empowerment for the people of the Pacific Islands, and to encourage diversity and connection among its members and the Athens-Clarke County and University of Georgia communities.
summary: The Pacific Unity Association works to promote ideas around the people and culture of the Pacific Islands.

name: Dogs Inc - Athens
description:
Dogs Inc - Athens is a community of student volunteers supporting the mission of Dogs Inc (formally Southeastern Guide Dogs). Dogs Inc is the only organization in North America to provide guide and service dogs, online and in-person training, lifetime alumni support, premium dog food, and preventative veterinary care—all at no cost. Our student volunteer puppy raisers and sitters share their worlds with curious pups. In this, members raise hope for the people who need our dogs: individuals living with vision loss, veterans with disabilities, and children who need a best friend.

Learn more here: https://dogsinc.org/get-involved/raise-a-puppy/
summary: We are a community of student volunteers supporting the mission of Dogs Inc. Our volunteer puppy raisers and sitters work together to provide future working canines for individuals and children living with vision loss, and veterans with disabilities.

name: SheCanCode
description: This organization was established to provide guidance to women and non-binary people in tech. SheCanCode aims to create mentor-mentee relationships to foster an environment where students feel supported and empowered. And of course, you don't have to be a woman or non-binary person to join! A key principle of this club is inclusivity, which means no dues and no discrimination against any gender. Everyone is welcome to be a part of SheCanCode!
summary: Our organization establishes mentor-mentee relationships primarily for women and non-binary individuals in CS and DS. However, everyone is welcome to join! We aim to provide underrepresented students in tech with a supportive community.

name: Georgia Museum of Art
description: The Georgia Museum of Art is the University of Georgia’s fine art museum and the official state museum of art. Offering free admission for all, its 20+ galleries are open six days a week to provide inspiration.
summary: The Georgia Museum of Art is the University of Georgia’s fine art museum and the official state museum of art. Offering free admission for all, its 20+ galleries are open six days a week to provide inspiration.

name: Integrative Precision Agriculture Club
description: The purpose of the Integrative Precision Agriculture Club at the University of Georgia is to cultivate a vibrant community that champions the advancement and understanding of precision agriculture. Our goals are to foster an inclusive environment that not only encourages but thrives on the collaboration and exchange of ideas among students from diverse backgrounds, and to provide students exposure to career development and leadership opportunities, trainings and engagement with the community and stakeholders. By uniting through our shared passion for precision agriculture, we aim to create a supportive space where all members feel valued and empowered to contribute their unique insights. This collaboration is intended to enhance the collective knowledge and innovation within UGA, promoting a more comprehensive and forward-thinking approach to agriculture.
summary: The Integrative Precision Agriculture Club is dedicated to advancing the understanding and application of precision agriculture among young students.

name: Painters Society
description: Join Painters Society for a relaxed and intentional creative space where you can paint alongside a lively community. We will provide materials such as paints and brushes but feel free to bring your own as well. Meetings will consist of themes or activities that will allow you to explore different subject matters and techniques at your own pace. Meetings are inclusive and for everyone, regardless of background in art/skill level! We want you here.
summary: Let's paint! Cultivate your creative side alongside a supportive and inclusive community. Gain exposure, materials, and a community to relish in the freedom that painting can offer! We are open to everyone: painters of all skill levels!

name: National Residence Hall Honorary
description:
The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is a leadership-based organization comprised of exemplary students promoting service and recognition on campus. Serving under University Housing and as a sibling to the Residence Hall Association (RHA), UGA’s Georgia Alpha Chapter of NRHH strives to make an impact on our community, both on and off campus.  

Our members are students of any degree type, may or may not have direct involvement with University Housing, and live on or off campus.  NRHH members strive to leave a positive impact on the residential life experience at UGA and the broader community.

NRHH has various events and initiatives throughout the year dedicated towards exemplifying our core values: service, recognition, and leadership. The chapter is devoted to implementing our core values by promoting the interests and welfare of students, while also providing opportunities for personal growth and development.  

Interested in learning more or becoming a member? Fill out this form and NRHH@uga.edu will reach out with more information!
summary: The National Residence Hall Honorary (NRHH) is a leadership-based organization comprised of exemplary students advocating for campus service and recognition. NRHH serves under University Housing and as a sibling to the Residence Hall Association (RHA).

name: NEST Run Club
description: We’re a student-led run club built around intentional growth. We make running something students want to show up for, improving health, meeting people, and building real friendships in a welcoming, social environment that encourages consistency. We run most weeks on Saturday mornings on-campus, see our link-tree to get involved. You can access our instagram and group me for more information and the calendar. Most of our runs happen at Myers Quad or North Campus Chapel either on Saturday mornings, Friday or Sunday night runs. Our runs are around 2 to 3 miles, with some non-running events happen at the end of the semester.
summary: We’re a student-led run club built around intentional growth. We make running something students want to show up for, improving health, meeting people, and building real friendships in a welcoming, social environment that encourages consistency.

name: Campus Crochet
description: We knit and crochet items like stuffed animals and toys or anything we feel the children might like, and we also make cards for the children.
summary: We are making small stuffed crochet animals and cards for the pediatric department at Athens Piedmont hospital and will donate them each month.

name: Art Therapy Club
description: The purpose of the Art Therapy Club is for students to find their family here at UGA. Each month, the club hosts fun arts and crafts activities to help students relax, destress, and make new friends. Our activities range from making friendship bracelets to painting and everything in between! Each month will have something new for members to do! We try our best to foster positive relationships and integrate everyone into the club. It is a very casual club to hang out with friends and make art. This club is also welcoming to all, regardless of artistic ability or background. Student life at UGA can be very stressful, and we want to help give students an outlet to relax and help alleviate stress.
summary: The Art Therapy Club is a place for people to meet and casually make art with friends once a month.

name: Free Time Finance
description:
Free Time Finance is a student-run organization at the University of Georgia that fosters excitement and engagement with personal finance topics, especially regarding college living. The club provides weekly presentation on various topics in personal finance, creating a comfortable and reliable space to discuss experiences, opinions, and lessons learned through life in personal finance.

Free Time Finance welcomes all University of Georgia students, staff, faculty, and alumni alike, regardless of experience level in the world of personal finance. We as an organization aim to continue to expand our reach to more people to educate them on the importance of financial literacy.
summary: Free Time Finance is a student-run organization at the University of Georgia that fosters excitement and engagement with personal finance topics, especially regarding college living.

name: Parking Deck Dancers
description: Parking Deck Dancers is an organization that strives to create a fun and inclusive environment where members can learn new swing dancing moves, practice what they know, and build a community of dancers seeking to refine their skills. Whether you are beginner wanting to learn the basics of swing dancing or a more experienced dancing wanting to improve their current skills, our organization provides an opportunity for people of all levels to connect through swing dancing and line dancing.
summary: Parking Deck Dancers is a organization that hopes to bring people together who share a passion for swing dancing and line dancing. We offer weekly events every Tuesday where people come together to learn new swing dancing moves and tricks.

name: Citizens for Free Enterprise
description: CFFE is an organization that is working to help students and others understand the importance of free enterprise. By joining this club you will meet and work with those who are working to spread this mission.
summary: CFFE is an organization that is working to help students and others understand the importance of free enterprise. By joining this club you will meet and work with those who are working to spread this mission. As

name: Happy Little Accidents
description: 1. Happy Little Accidents is a painting club where members follow along with the tutorials of the late Bob Ross. Our primary goal is to give our members a relaxing environment to destress and creatively express themselves. We welcome all those who love to paint, want to try it out, or simply want a comfy place to pass the time.
summary: Happy Little Accidents is a club designed to unite artists and amateurs to destress from life's chaos. We will follow along with the painting tutorials of Bob Ross in a loose, fun format meant to brighten people's days instead of swamp them.

name: Athens Angel Helpers
description: Athens Angel Helpers was created to give back to others. We want to raise money for different non-profit organizations and host community service drives a few times over the semester. This organization will also allow others to make new friends, participate in student-led events, and try their hand at a leadership position!
summary: Athens Angel Helpers was created to give back. We raise funds for nonprofits, host service drives each semester, and create opportunities for students to make friends, lead events, and grow as leaders.

name: New Student Orientation
description: New Student Orientation at the University of Georgia seeks to prepare students for academic and social engagement as members of the UGA community. We are committed to providing students with a positive learning experience and we believe that in order to ensure a productive start to that journey, students must understand their role and expectations as a member of the UGA community. As such, our student-centered transition programs and staff provide various opportunities for students to learn and connect with campus partners, programs, and resources throughout UGA and to equip them to seek and connect to these resources for assistance. Further, we engage incoming students in various activities and programs that will help cultivate connections and help establish community at the University of Georgia.
summary: New Student Orientation at the University of Georgia seeks to prepare students for academic and social engagement as members of the UGA community.

name: History Graduate Student Association
description: The purpose of ​History Graduate Student Association​ shall be ​to provide social and professional development opportunities for master’s and doctoral students enrolled in the Department of History at the University of Georgia​.
summary: Organization representing the History Department graduate students at UGA.

name: A Pinch and a Dash
description: Welcomes to our baking and cooking club! A Pinch and a Dash is an organization dedicated to providing students with a community of like-minded individuals with an interest in cooking and baking. We seek to create an environment that will foster the growth of cooking and baking knowledge. Join us as we try out new recipes together!
summary: A Pinch and a Dash is an organization dedicated to providing students with a community of like-minded individuals with an interest in baking and cooking. We seek to create an environment that will foster the growth of baking and cooking knowledge.

name: Undergraduates at Law
description:
Undergraduates at Law is a student-led pre-law organization dedicated to making information about the legal profession accessible to all. Through hosting guest speakers, panel discussions, and Q&A sessions, we provide students with opportunities to learn directly from professionals and explore different career paths in law. Our goal is to give members the tools and insights they need to make informed decisions about their future.

As part of our mission, we also contribute to charitable causes, reflecting our commitment to making a positive impact beyond the classroom. Join us to connect with like-minded peers, gain valuable guidance, and prepare for your legal journey.

 

If you'd like to join, please join the GroupMe for more information!

 

GroupMe Link: https://groupme.com/join_group/102706725/PtCxbcU1

 

Fall 2025 Dues Link: https://ugapac.evenue.net/item/TATE2526/MEM.UAL
summary: Undergraduates at Law is a student-led organization dedicated to guiding and supporting undergraduates on their journey toward a legal career

name: Terry College of Business
description: Founded in 1912, the C. Herman and Mary Virginia Terry College of Business is steeped in a tradition of providing cutting-edge business education. Our challenging curriculum, exceptional faculty, and close-knit alumni network ensure Terry students are prepared to become leaders in their professions and communities.
summary: Founded in 1912, the C. Herman and Mary Virginia Terry College of Business is steeped in a tradition of providing cutting-edge business education.

name: Tribute, The
description: Each semester, The Tribute takes a vote to study at a certain director/filmmaker’s work and observe what exactly they do to make it their own. For the next 8-10 weeks, we take inspiration from them and go out to create something of our own. Involving a pre-production, production, and post-production process, members of The Tribute collaborate as a team to make something that gives homage and a proper 'tribute' to the filmmaker we elect to study. The Tribute creates a network of connections, fosters friendships with other like-minded creatives, and gives students interested in filmmaking a unique way to explore it in a professional manner.
summary: The Tribute is a student-led production club based upon the application of the age-old addage: “What is cinema?” The Tribute studies a filmmaker’s work and goes on to create a short based upon their specific style from pre-production to post-production.

name: Corporate Pre-Law Society
description:
Bridging the gap between undergraduate study and the corporate legal field.

The Corporate Pre-Law Society is a professional organization dedicated to empowering ambitious undergraduate students pursuing careers in corporate law. We provide a comprehensive platform for students to explore legal opportunities, develop essential professional skills, and build a competitive foundation for law school and beyond.

What We Offer

  • Real-World Legal Experience: Gain practical insights by participating in Mock Trials and conducting in-depth analysis of landmark cases.

  • Professional Networking: Build meaningful relationships with like-minded peers and gain exclusive access to guest speakers and industry experts.

  • Career Development: Attend specialized workshops designed to sharpen your professional edge and navigate the path to a legal career.

  • Community Impact: Engage in volunteer work directly related to the legal field, allowing you to give back while strengthening your resume.

Why Join CPLS?

  • No Membership Fees: We are committed to accessibility; there are no dues or financial obligations to join.

  • Biweekly Engagement: Consistent meetings held at the Miller Learning Center to ensure steady professional growth.

  • Strategic Advantage: Our Fall Application Cycle opens every August, providing a structured start to your academic year. We also open up Spring Applications every January for newcomers as well!
summary: CPLS empowers UGA students pursuing careers in corporate law through guest speakers, workshops, and networking opportunities with industry experts and lawyers. We offer a professional platform to build skills and connections with no membership fees.

name: Undergraduates in Psychology
description: The Undergraduates in Psychology club is dedicated to fostering a supportive and inclusive community for students majoring, minoring, or simply interested in psychology. Our mission is to provide a welcoming space where members can deepen their understanding of psychology, connect with like-minded peers, seek guidance, and access valuable resources. Through engaging events, discussions, and networking opportunities, we aim to inspire curiosity, promote academic and personal growth, and empower students to explore and pursue their passion for psychology.
summary: We strive to connect current undergraduate students pursuing a psychology major to others with similar interests as well as resources to succeed throughout their schooling process.

name: BadDogz
description:
BadDogz fuses traditional majorette flair with contemporary urban rhythms, creating an electrifying performance style that captivates audiences. With a dynamic blend of high-energy choreography, intricate formations, and stylish streetwear, this team redefines majorette dance for a modern audience.

Members showcase their talent through powerful routines that incorporate elements of hip-hop, jazz, and funk, all while celebrating community, empowerment, and creativity. Each performance bursts with personality, featuring vibrant colors, bold moves, and the undeniable spirit of teamwork.

BogDogz Majorettes are not just dancers—they’re trendsetters, inspiring the next generation with their passion and dedication. Join them as they take the stage and transform every performance into a celebration of culture and artistry!
summary: This is an organization dedicated to enriching the University of Georgia with urban dance culture. Our core values—community, leadership, and joy—drive our mission to spread happiness through dance, give back to the community, and promote leadership.

name: Field Naturalist Society
description:
The purpose of the Field Naturalist Society shall be to educate its members about the diverse flora, fauna, fungi, and ecosystems that make up our local environment. By promoting the observation and study of nature, FNS encourages members to learn about various species and their habitats through engaging in fieldwork. It fosters a community of like-minded individuals who share a passion for nature, facilitating collaboration and peer learning experiences. The Field Naturalist Society aims to connect naturalist students across the divided colleges of UGA; primarily from, but not limited to, Franklin College of Arts and Sciences, College of Agriculture and Environmental Sciences, Odum School of Ecology, and Warnell School of Forestry and Natural Resources. The society will organize regular events such as nature walks, guest lectures, Bioblitzs, bird watching, insect black lighting, and field trips to offer members organized hands-on experiences with the natural world. FNS encourages members to participate in science communication and biodiversity conservation-related projects within the community and to the public. Additionally, it helps members develop skills in identifying and understanding the natural history of different species, including all types of plants, animals, and fungi, enriching their knowledge and appreciation of the natural world.

Join the groupme: https://groupme.com/join_group/102827850/wdcfACwF

Follow us on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/fieldnaturalistsociety/
summary: The Field Naturalist Society encourages all nature enthusiasts to learn more about the diverse flora, fauna, and fungi of our local ecosystems. We hold regular nature ID walks, talks, & events to immerse ourselves in the natural world.

name: Malayalees of UGA
description:
The Malayalees of UGA is a student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to uniting students of Malayalee heritage and celebrating the rich cultural traditions of Kerala. Our purpose is to create a vibrant community where members can engage in cultural exchange, deepen their understanding of Malayalee customs, and share the unique heritage of Kerala with the broader university community.

Mission: Our mission is to provide a supportive environment that fosters cultural pride and connection among Malayalee students. We aim to organize events and activities that highlight Kerala’s diverse traditions, encourage cultural dialogue, and build a cohesive network of students who value and wish to promote their heritage.

Goals:

  • Unite Malayalee Students: Create a strong, inclusive community for students of Malayalee heritage to connect, share experiences, and support each other.
  • Foster Cultural Exchange: Promote understanding and appreciation of Kerala’s unique traditions through events, workshops, and educational activities.
  • Celebrate Kerala’s Heritage: Highlight and celebrate the cultural richness of Kerala by organizing festivals, cultural performances, and traditional celebrations.

Key Activities:

  • Cultural Celebrations: Host events such as Onam, Vishu, and other traditional Kerala festivals, featuring traditional food, music, dance, and activities.
  • Educational Workshops: Offer workshops on Malayalam language, traditional arts, and cultural practices to enhance members’ knowledge and engagement.
  • Social and Community Events: Organize social gatherings, movie nights, and community service projects to strengthen relationships within the group and engage with the wider UGA community.

Membership: Membership is open to all UGA students who have an interest in Malayalee culture and heritage. We welcome those who wish to participate in our events, contribute ideas, and be actively involved in our community.
summary: The purpose of the Malayalees of UGA shall be to unite students of Malayalee heritage, to foster an environment of cultural exchange, and to celebrate the unique traditions of Kerala.

name: Women Lead Right at UGA
description:
The purpose of Women Lead Right at UGA is to:

  1. Engage and amplify the civic and political impact of women on UGA’s campus.
  2. Engage, educate, train, and lead UGA’s women in grassroots advocacy.
  3. Offer a safe environment to discuss the concerns affecting women today.

We intend to host events and meetings to build a community for like-minded individuals to share ideas, relate with each other, and support each other. While this organization is primarily focused on empowering women, all students are welcome to join.
summary: Women Lead Right's mission is to engage and amplify the civic and political impact of Georgia’s women and all students. Through a collegiate chapter at UGA, we aim to foster conversation, share resources, educate members, and inspire political interest.

name: Cooks for a Cause at UGA
description: • Our club is unified by a shared love for cooking and a commitment to making an impact in the Athens community. We utilize our passion for cooking into preparing delicious meals and delivering them to homeless shelters in our community. Through every dish we create, we aim to provide comfort and care for those in need.
summary: Our club is unified by a shared love for cooking and a commitment to making an impact in the Athens community. We utilize our passion for cooking into preparing delicious meals and delivering them to homeless shelters in our community.

name: Five Points College Ministries
description:
Welcome to 5 Points Church! We are a Bible-believing church that aspires to be a Gospel outpost in the dynamic 5 Points neighborhood and surrounding Athens area, and we are dedicated to sharing the transformative love of Jesus in both word and deed. Five Points College Ministries strives to take our passion for Christian discipleship and God's heart for the nations, and apply it to the beautiful campus of UGA. We accomplish this through tabling events, Gospel-Sharing outreach events, and approaching new relationships with a heart of servitude and hospitality.

We would love for you to come join our family!
summary: Five Points College Ministries is an outpost for 5 Points Church on Milledge Ave. We strive to take our passion for Christian discipleship and God's heart for the nations, and apply it to see lives transformed on the beautiful campus of UGA!

name: Business Pre-Law Society
description: This club will give students interested in business and law the opportunity to meet others and network with those already in the field. The cross-section of law and business is not emphasized enough. Through this club, we hope to engage with students who have this interest or to allow students to recognize their interest in the combination of law and business, which they previously may not have been aware of.
summary: The goal of BPLS is to educate students in business, which is a non-traditional pre-law major, about law school, including the admissions process, life in law school, and what life in corporate law looks like.

name: School of Public and International Affairs
description: Inaugurated in 2001 as the University of Georgia’s fourteenth college, the School of Public and International Affairs (SPIA) was formed from the existing Department of Political Science in the Franklin College of Arts and Sciences. SPIA prepares undergraduate and graduate students for good citizenship and careers in public life and trains future generations of teachers and scholars in the fields of international affairs, political science, and public administration and policy.
summary: SPIA prepares undergraduate and graduate students for good citizenship and careers in public life and trains future generations of teachers and scholars in the fields of international affairs, political science, and public administration and policy.

name: Students United for America's ToothFairy
description: SUAT @ UGA is pre-dental undergraduate club at the University of Georgia and aims to provide critical oral health education to elementary schools in the Athens area. Members are taught SUAT's interactive lessons about various oral health topics while gaining teaching and volunteering experience. In addition to serving local communities, SUAT fosters a tight-knit community and essentials for professional development.
summary: SUAT’s mission is to educate and spread awareness about children's oral and dental health to elementary schools through proper education and community service.

name: Morgan's Message at UGA
description: Morgan Rodgers was a Duke lacrosse player who died by suicide in July of 2019, which prompted the beginning of Morgan's Message. Morgan's Message is an organization that aims to end the stigma surrounding mental health among athletes by building a community and a platform for advocacy. Our chapter at UGA will support our local community and work with athletic teams on campus to further this support system.
summary: Morgan's Message is a national organization dedicated to ending the stigma surrounding mental health among athletes. Our chapter at UGA aims to open up conversations about mental health and provide support to people in our community.

name: Prescription Fitness
description: Our club focuses on fostering a better community through educating optimal health, accessible nutrition, and physical/mental fitness and wellness. We aim to primarily work with marginalized communities.
summary: We are a pre-health club that focuses on fostering a better community through promoting optimal health, accessible nutrition, and physical/mental fitness and wellness. We primarily work with marginalized communities.

name: Dog House of Mouse
description: Dog House of Mouse is a student-led organization that celebrates all things Disney. Our mission is to create a welcoming and imaginative space for Disney fans to connect, share, and celebrate the magic that has shaped generations. We participate in Disney themed activities and meetings, where we dive into the legacy and cultural impact of Disney while fostering friendships and unforgettable experiences. Whether you grew up on Disney magic or are just starting to explore the world of Disney, you can find a home at the Dog House of Mouse. Join us as we explore how Disney's storying telling, innovation, and values continue to inspire and bring joy to audiences of all ages.
summary: Dog House of Mouse, brings together students who share a passion for all things Disney - from timeless animated classics to modern-day franchises, theme park experiences, and more. Activities include themed events, movie nights, trivia, discussions, etc.

name: Holiday Harvest
description: Our organization will connect with Churches, Greek Life, and grocery stores in the Athens area to get funding and canned food items. We will purchase turkeys and baskets to deliver the goods to homes around Athens. We will get names of families through Churches and through our own research.
summary: Holiday Harvest is a nonprofit organization that focuses on combatting hunger in the Athens area while fostering a sense of community and care, making sure that more families can share in the joy of holiday meals.

name: AI Society at Terry
description:
AI Society at Terry is a student-led organization dedicated to exploring and advancing the applications of artificial intelligence (AI) in business, technology, and society. This club aims to foster a community where students can collaborate on AI projects, attend workshops, and hear from industry leaders in the field of AI. Whether students are interested in learning the basics of AI, discussing ethical implications, or developing innovative AI solutions, the AI Society offers a platform for students to engage in meaningful discussions and hands-on experiences that bridge the gap between AI theory and real-world applications.

 

What We Offer:

- Speaker sessions with industry professionals

- Hands on workshops

- Professional development 

 

How To Get Involved?

Submit an application! Follow @aisocietyuga for updates on application dates and deadlines!

Application link: https://forms.gle/UuyrA851nWitXr2g8
summary: A student-led organization dedicated to exploring artificial intelligence through workshops, guest speakers, and collaborative projects, fostering professional development and community engagement among members passionate about AI.

name: Knox Martin Foundation at UGA
description: Our organization partners with the Knox Martin Foundation to address the underfunded and under-researched nature of brain cancer by supporting innovative research on glioblastoma (GBM), the most aggressive form of the disease. The foundation collaborates with doctors and researchers at the Duke Brain Tumor Center to advance treatment and understanding of GBM. Throughout the year, we host fundraising and educational events to engage members and make a lasting impact in the community.
summary: Our organization partners with The Knox Martin Foundation to raise awareness and funds for Glioblastoma brain cancer research at Duke University.

name: AthSketch
description:
1. The purpose of AthSketch shall be to foster the process of sketching on location. Additionally we aim to create a welcoming and supportive community of all creative individuals interested in Athens, Ga. 
  2. Each week we find a random location in Athens and we all sketch for about an hour and show our work to one another at the end.
  3. We also have a professor that gives all of the members demonstrations on things like building perspective, watercolor, and more.
summary: The purpose of AthSketch shall be to foster the process of sketching on location. Additionally we aim to create a welcoming and supportive community of all creative individuals interested in Athens, Ga.

socialMedia.ExternalWebsite: We don't have a website, Facebook and Instagram are our forms of communication.

name: Little Legal Minds
description:
(Little Legal Minds) LLM is a service-based organization dedicated to educating Athens youth on their basic legal rights and connecting them with accessible legal resources.

Our group aims to provide young students with a foundational understanding of essential legal principles, including key amendments, civil liberties, and personal rights, with a focus on how these concepts relate to their daily lives.

We plan to deliver this education through engaging presentations followed by interactive activities, allowing students to apply and reinforce what they’ve learned. Ideally, these sessions would be scheduled during after-school programs or in-class time, depending on what setting would be most impactful.
summary: LLM educates Athens youth on their legal rights and connects them to accessible resources. Through interactive sessions, students learn key legal principles and how they apply to daily life, offered in after-school or classroom settings.

name: Phi Delta Phi
description:
PDP is an international legal honor society. It was established in 1869. PDP celebrates academic excellence and community. This hall will consist of pre-law undergraduate students who are interested in community and guidance. 

Phi Delta Phi has hundreds of chapters throughout the world and the number increases yearly. Phi Delta Phi has initiated in excess of 200,000 members. Phi Delta Phi will collect dues, please contact uga.pdp@gmail.com for more information about dues and how to join. 

https://phideltaphi.org/about/
summary: Phi Delta Phi is an international legal honors society founded in 1869. In this honors society, we will focus on mentorship, community, teamwork, and leadership. This society will have resources for those who are pursuing the legal field in the future.

name: Mindful Sprouts
description: 1. The purpose of Mindful Sprouts is to connect students with opportunities to develop and implement nature-based education programs focused on sustainable agriculture, STEM learning, and environmental stewardship. Members will engage in hands-on volunteer projects, such as hosting workshops for children and families, and organizing educational outreach events. Through collaboration and mentorship from organization advisors, members will gain practical experience in program development, teaching strategies, and sustainability practices that create a lasting impact in the community.
summary: Mindful Sprouts connects students and faculty with opportunities to develop and implement nature-based education programs focused on environmental education, sustainable agriculture, STEM learning, and environmental stewardship.

name: Koun Khmer Student Association
description: “The KKSA aims to foster a vibrant community that supports Cambodian students and promotes awareness of Cambodian culture and history. We strive to empower our members through educational initiatives, cultural celebrations, and advocacy efforts while creating an inclusive environment that honors our heritage and addresses the challenges face by the Cambodian American community.”
summary: A cultural appreciation club for Cambodian (Khmer) culture that is open to everyone to learn what Cambodia is and what the culture is like!

name: College of Environment and Design
description:
o The College of Environment + Design (CED) at the University of Georgia is one of the top institutions in the U.S. for landscape architecture, environmental design, and historic preservation. We offer a blend of design education, environmental stewardship, and interdisciplinary collaboration, preparing students to shape a better world.

With nationally accredited programs in landscape architecture, historic preservation, and environmental planning, CED students tackle real-world challenges through studio projects, hands-on learning, and community design charrettes. Our faculty and students are also at the forefront of research in areas like climate resilience, environmental justice, and cultural landscapes, helping to create sustainable, innovative, and historically sensitive environments that enrich our communities.
summary: o The College of Environment + Design (CED) at the University of Georgia is one of the top institutions in the U.S. for landscape architecture, environmental design, and historic preservation.

name: Music Production Club
description: Music Production Club is open to beginners and advanced producers. We create an environment designed to foster creativity and collaboration. Meet with other eager producers to discuss new production techniques as well as create new music projects!
summary: Music Production Club is designed for learning and meeting other like-minded people who are passionate about music production, sound design, audio engineering, songwriting, and other production techniques!

name: PoliTalk Society
description: Politalk Society acts as a medium for young adults to be exposed to local government while learning more about their individual role in the democratic process. As a non-partisan political educational forum, we want members to obtain political information through a non-biased lens. At our meetings we conduct activities, hear lectures from local government leaders and attend community events.
summary: A non-partisan political educational forum for young adults to better understand their impact on government policy, discuss opinions on current political issues and learn how to engage with local government processes.

name: Hack Pack, The
description:
The Hack Pack is UGA’s premier practical and competitive cybersecurity club, dedicated to developing real-world hacking skills through hands-on training, research, and high-intensity competitions. Whether you're just starting in cybersecurity or looking to take your skills to the next level, The Hack Pack offers resources, mentorship, and opportunities to sharpen your craft.
 

Learn more at our website: https://thehackpack.org

Join our Discord to become a member: http://discord.thehackpack.org
summary: The Hack Pack is UGA's practical and competitive cybersecurity club that sharpens real-world hacking skills through hands-on training, research, and competitions. We cater to all skill levels from new to veteran hackers!

name: Women In Consulting
description:
Women in Consulting (WIC) at UGA is a community-driven organization dedicated to empowering women to succeed in the consulting industry. By fostering inclusivity and providing access to resources, mentorship, and skill-building opportunities, we aim to create a supportive network for students from all backgrounds.

WIC is committed to:

  • Inclusivity: Welcoming aspiring consultants regardless of experience level or prior involvement in competitive clubs.
  • Empowerment: Equipping members with the tools and confidence to excel through workshops, case prep, and networking opportunities.
  • Representation: Highlighting the achievements of women in consulting and promoting diversity in the industry.
  • Community: Building a strong network of students and professionals passionate about consulting and collaboration.

Our mission is to bridge the gap between ambition and opportunity, ensuring that every woman at UGA has the chance to thrive in consulting.
summary: Women in Consulting (WIC) at UGA is a community-driven organization dedicated to empowering women to succeed in the consulting industry. By fostering mentorship and inclusivity, we aim to create a supportive network for students from all backgrounds.

name: Club Figure Skating
description:
Students will have access to ice time and instructional training by a coach, with aid from the president and vice president of the club. This will be a comfortable environment for members to have fun and learn new skills without the pressure of unfamiliarity in the activity. Our goal as an organization is to develop skills and bring skaters to events that will expose them to the figure skating world. Members can also choose to compete through the club at competitions, ranging from beginner to advanced levels, and will represent the Club Figure Skating program in the US Figure Skating collegiate program. In addition to on ice activities, we strive to support members in any way so that we can provide a comfortable space on and off the ice for students. As an organization, we hope to have fun and provide a space for any, and all people (even the ones who have never skated before)! 

Please bring any questions to the Club President at ugaclubfigureskating@gmail.com
summary: Club Figure Skating is a club activity available to the students of the University of Georgia at all levels. This club will provide students with instructional training on how to skate, and allow them the opportunity to showcase their skills.

name: R3 Studio
description: R3 Studio aims to promote sustainability, wellness, and community engagement by transforming recycled materials into art pieces and innovative creations. By embracing the practice of "reduce, reuse, and recycle," we inspire creativity, encourage self-expression, and build meaningful connections between individuals and the community.
summary: R3 Studio is a creative, sustainability-driven organization that engages students in recycling awareness, innovative artistic practices, and wellness initiatives, inspiring them to incorporate eco-conscious habits into their daily lives.

name: Reconnect
description: Reconnect is a National organization seeking to bring phone-free social spaces to college campuses across the country! Today, Reconnect is rapidly spreading through Gen Z and beyond as a network of peer-led clubs to hang out, make new friends, and do fun stuff without phones. Reconnect creates fully present, uninterrupted social spaces where connection naturally thrives. And everyone's invited.
summary:
We seek to create fully present, uninterrupted, social spaces where connection naturally
thrives at the University of Georgia by removing the barriers of technology.

name: Wigs For Kids
description: Wigs For Kids is a student-led organization that is dedicated to supporting children experiencing medically-induced hair loss. We will fulfill this role through fundraising, tabling, collaborating with other clubs, and hosting campus-wide events. Through this organization, we plan to spread awareness of the importance of the cause. We are a new organization, just getting on our feet, so we plan on expanding our involvement and events as the year goes on.
summary: Wigs For Kids is a student-led organization that is dedicated to supporting children experiencing medically-induced hair loss. We will fulfill this role through fundraising, tabling, collaborating with other clubs, and hosting campus-wide events.

name: Melanin in Motion
description: At Melanin in Motion, our mission is to break down barriers to health by providing education, resources, and support that address the unique challenges faced by minorities in achieving physical and mental well-being. Through community-driven initiatives, personalized fitness programs, and holistic wellness approaches, we strive to foster a space for undergraduate students where every individual can thrive, build confidence, and take control of their health—because wellness should be for everyone.
summary: Melanin in Motion empowers undergraduate students in minority communities with inclusive, culturally responsive fitness and wellness programs.

name: South Asian and Asian Business Association
description:
Mission Statement

To create a robust network that connects individuals regardless of backround, businesses, and organizations, fostering professional growth, leadership development, and internship opportunities for the Asian American Community. 

Core Objectives

  1. Networking Opportunities
  • Host regular networking events, both virtual and in-person, to connect Asian American professionals across industries.
  • Partner with corporations, startups, and nonprofits to provide access to exclusive networking opportunities.
  1. Corporate Partnerships
  • Collaborate with companies and startups to provide internship opportunities, and scholarships.
  • Host events to connect Asian American professionals with students.
  1. Community Outreach
  • Partner with Asian American cultural organizations and student groups to engage professionals with students.

Long-Term Vision

To become the go to platform for Asian Americans seeking to thrive in the business world, while also serving as a bridge for corporations and organizations to engage with this dynamic and growing community.
summary: To establish an organization that serves as the primary connection for all and any individuals, regardless of background, who appreciate and celebrate Asian Culture and have a desire to grow professionally in business. We welcome anyone to attend a GBM!

name: Smile Dogs Dental
description: Our purpose will be practiced by visiting local elementary schools and providing basic oral hygiene procedures and oral hygiene kits. The organization will also visit senior living homes to educate residents on proper oral hygiene techniques such as dry mouth, gum disease, and denture care. Additionally, volunteers will engage in interactive sessions to promote preventative care and overall oral health awareness.
summary: The purpose of Smile Dogs Dental shall be to promote healthier oral health and educate the community of Athens, Georgia, on the importance of oral health.

name: LGBTQ+ and Ally Future Educators
description:
All LGBTQ+ identifying or LGBTQ+ ally education majors, regardless of subject discipline, can expect to find support in LGBTQ+ and Ally Future Educators. This organization seeks to strengthen the community of LGBTQ+ and ally students within the Mary Frances Early College of Education through numerous social-oriented events and meetings that will help facilitate familiarity between members. Members will also have opportunities to support one another, share/receive information through peer-led educational presentations, reach out into the local community, and connect with numerous professional educators. We're always seeking to give back to our members and to those around us!

To get involved, join our GroupMe and attend meetings! No minimum meeting attendance is required for membership.
summary: This organization aims to create community, provide support, and enhance professional as well as academic development for all students within the Mary Frances Early College of Education who identify as LGBTQ+ or LGBTQ+ allies.

name: Immigration Law Society
description: The Immigration Law Society offers a platform for students to engage with immigration law and policy. The ILS provides an opportunity for networking with students and immigration attorneys, and fosters an  an environment of learning, collaboration, and professional growth. Members gain invaluable insights into immigration law, access to the latest legal developments, and a chance to deepen their understanding of critical issues impacting communities worldwide. The Immigration Law Society is committed to empowering students with the knowledge, connections, and resources to become future leaders in the field of immigration law.
summary: The Immigration Law Society provides an excellent opportunity for students to connect with immigration attorneys, learn more about immigration law and current policies, network with other students, and stay informed with up-to-date immigration news.

name: Traders@UGA
description: Traders@UGA is a project-based student organization at the University of Georgia focused on empowering students to develop practical skills in trading, investing, and quantitative finance. Our members work on collaborative projects that explore real-world applications of market strategies, algorithmic trading, portfolio management, and financial modeling. Through hands-on initiatives, workshops, and industry mentorship, we provide opportunities to build tangible experience and a deep understanding of financial markets. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced trader, Traders@UGA is the perfect community to grow, network, and prepare for careers in finance.
summary: Traders@UGA is a project-based group at UGA where students gain hands-on experience in quant trading and quant finance.

name: Mixed Martial Arts Club at UGA
description: Mixed Martial Arts (MMA) is one of the most rapidly growing sports in recent history. Beginning less than 40 years ago, MMA has already made its mark on the world stage as the preferred spectacle combat sport of our generation. Furthermore, with a year-round "season" and multiple different big promotions, fans never fall short for things to talk about. MMA @ UGA seeks to provide a space for those interested to come discuss the history, the present, and the future of this sport.
summary: MMA @ UGA is an interest club and intramural sport for students interested in the all-encompassing combat sport of mixed martial arts.

name: Vertically Integrated Projects for Research Society
description: The purpose of the Vertically-Integrated Projects for Research Society is to build community and foster academic achievement among students enrolled in the Vertically-Integrated Projects for Research Program along with other students who have similar interests. The organization is established to serve the University of Georgia community with an undergraduate organization that can promote community service work, social outreach, and the engagement of students interested in research of all disciplines.
summary: VIPRS at UGA fosters community and academic growth among students in the VIP Research Program and other students with similar interests. We hold research discussions to promote outreach, collaboration, and engagement in research across disciplines.

name: Meditation with the Monks
description: The mission of the Meditation with the Monks Club is to foster a community where students can experience the transformative benefits of meditation and explore the timeless teachings of Vedic knowledge. Through the practice of meditation, the study of the Bhagavad Gita, and immersion in Vedic culture, we aim to help individuals cultivate inner peace, purpose, and a deeper understanding of life.
summary: The mission of the Meditation with the Monks Club is to foster a community where students can experience the transformative benefits of meditation and explore the timeless teachings of Vedic knowledge.

name: Business Brigades
description: Business Brigades is a chapter of one of the Global Brigades subdivisions forming an international movement of university students and professionals to build economic opportunities in the developing world. We work alongside promising entrepreneurs and experienced year-round staff to create financial opportunities in remote, rural, and under resourced areas of Honduras, Ghana, Belize, Greece, and Panama. We work with communities to build community-owned banks to provide access to loans, savings accounts and financial literacy for community members. Volunteers assist in consulting micro-enterprises, assessing loan capacity payments and providing financial literacy workshops to families. In conjunction with our Business Program, the overall organization also supports communities with healthcare, sanitation and clean water projects, and uniquely implements these programs in a holistic model to meet a community's health and economic goals. Our model systematically builds community ownership and collaboratively executes programs with the end goal of sustainably evolving to a relationship of impact monitoring. To learn more, please visit Global Brigades Consulting.
summary: Business Brigades is an international movement providing economic opportunities in under-resourced areas in Ghana, Belize, Greece, and Panama by consulting micro-enterprises, assessing loan capacity payments, and providing financial literacy education.

name: Wellness in Pre-Health
description: The purpose of Wellness in Pre-Health is to provide a support group for Pre-Health(Pre-Med, Pre-PA, etc) students at UGA. The organization will provide students with a medium to share their struggles in a judgment-free environment with peers who are experiencing similar adversity along the Pre-Health path. This medium will allow for expression to any extent regarding any issue, and students will have opportunities to share both anonymously and amongst peers. Wellness in Pre-Health will allow students to receive interdisciplinary guidance regarding mental self-care in any facet of Pre-Health, ranging from specific classes to long-term career plans. Many Pre-Health students struggle with managing their schedules, lifestyles, and plans for the future, and it is important that these individuals have an outlet to both address their problems and build a support group. The organization achieves its goals through a variety of means, including guest speakers, group discussions/seminars, workshops, Q&A sessions, surveys, and other events.
summary:
Wellness in Pre-Health is an organization that strives to guide Pre-Health students in handling mental 
struggles that arise from their pursuits through group seminars, guest speakers, Q&A sessions,
workshops, and more.

name: Bike UGA
description: Bike UGA is a student-led organization dedicated to fostering an inclusive community for cyclists of all experience levels at the University of Georgia. Our mission is to make biking a more practical, sustainable, and enjoyable way to navigate campus life. Through open discussions, collaborative projects, and advocacy, we aim to improve biking infrastructure and accessibility, while inspiring a culture of cycling at UGA.
summary: Bike UGA is dedicated to building an inclusive and supportive community for cyclists of all levels at UGA.

name: NASA Technology Transfer University Program
description: The UGA T2U Program connects students with NASA’s cutting-edge technology, research opportunities, and career pathways, empowering them to develop solutions for real-world challenges. By fostering cross-disciplinary collaboration and industry engagement, the organization ensures that every student at UGA has the opportunity to contribute to NASA’s mission of advancing science, technology, and space exploration.
summary: The NASA Technology Transfer University (T2U) Program at the University of Georgia is dedicated to integrating NASA-developed technologies into academic learning, fostering innovation, entrepreneurship, and workforce development.

name: Forensic Science Society
description: The purpose of the Forensic Science Society shall be to educate students in forensic sciences, integrate different departments on campus, and connect students with other programs and resources. There will be fun field trips, cool labs, and guest speakers to educate students in the field. There will be different opportunities for different parts of forensics, so if people are unsure of what they enjoy, they can get a feel for different jobs and fields. The club is not limited to people who want a career in forensics, but is open to everyone who wants to experience or learn about it.
summary: The purpose of the Forensic Science Society shall be to educate students in forensic sciences, integrate different departments on campus, and connect students with other programs and resources. We will have guest speakers, fun labs, and field trips.

name: Journal of Medicine for All
description: Journal of Medicine for All is an organization dedicated to fostering dialogue and advancing knowledge in clinical medicine, medical ethics, and public health. Our mission is to provide accessible, high-quality publications, including newsletters and journals, that highlight the latest advancements in medicine, and explore the complexities and various perspectives of medical ethics.
