We aim to inspire, engage, and inform a diverse audience in the University community through our work by bridging the gaps in communication and knowledge. Through our journals, we strive to cultivate the highest standards of integrity in medicine.
summary: The Journal of Medicine for All fosters dialogue and advances knowledge in clinical medicine, medical ethics, and public health. We provide accessible, high-quality publications to inform, inspire, and engage the community while upholding integrity.

name: Music Appreciation Club
description: The Music Appreciation Club is a place for all lovers of music to connect. It is a place dedicated for students to explore, discuss, and discover different kinds of genres, eras, and types of music. Club members will engage in weekly discussion on music they decide. Club members will also have the opportunity to attend listening parties, in which members listen to an album together during new releases. There will also be opportunities for members to attend live concerts at local venues.
summary: The Music Appreciation Club is a space dedicated for students to discuss and discover all kinds of music. We hold weekly meetings where we discuss music chosen by the members, along with special events such as listening parties and field trips to venues.

name: National Society of Black Women in Medicine
description: NSBWM is committed to supporting current and future healthcare professionals & scientists in efforts to combat educational and professional racial inequities by promoting attainability through educational resources, unity, and mentorship.
summary: The University of Georgia chapter of the National Society of Black Women in Medicine aims to increase the recruitment & retention of Black women pursuing careers in medicine, while simultaneously promoting the advancement of those already established.

name: CineVoice
description: CineVoice will use filmmaking, readings, and analysis to fully dive into the filmmaking industry for creators of color. We will discuss the different cultures and societal structures that truly go into making a film and further amplify the voices of those who are not heard enough in the industry!
summary: CineVoice is an organization that will focus on amplifying the voices of filmmakers of color from all backgrounds and skill levels.

name: Science in Film
description: This club is a fun way to use what we have learned in science class and apply our knowledge. We will watch a sci-fi film, and then talk about whether or not any of that is practical, or just fiction.
summary: We watch interesting science fiction films and then discuss the legitimacy of the scientific experiments/logic

name: First Generation Law Society
description:
The First Generation Law Society is an undergraduate student organization that is committed to educating prospective first generation law students by providing networking opportunities, resources, and mentorship efforts.

https://groupme.com/join_group/109938674/G2YmdsZo

https://www.instagram.com/ugafirstgenlaw?igsh=cGIxZXlwcWZ0Mnk0&utm_source=qr
summary: The First Generation Law Society is an undergraduate student organization that is committed to educating prospective first generation law students by providing networking opportunities, resources, and mentorship efforts.

name: Asian Medicine and Health Association
description: The Asian Medicine and Health Association (AMHA) is an organization dedicated to connecting how Asian cultures interconnect with healthcare, and will have numerous initiatives stimulating and advancing the development of Pre-Health students towards their career goals, while preparing them to face future patients of diverse backgrounds with knowledge and empathy. AMHA will have a focus on promoting cultural awareness and appreciation for traditional Asian medicine techniques, supporting need-based populations in Georgia through volunteer initiatives with a focus on underserved, Asian-demographic areas, and cultivating community and connection with members of all racial and ethnic backgrounds.
summary: The Asian Medicine and Health Association at the University of Georgia will bridge the connection between Asian cultures and healthcare through various initiatives.

name: Latino Medical Student Association PLUS
description:
The Latino Medical Student Association PLUS (LMSA+) Chapter is dedicated to uplifting and empowering pre-health students by fostering a strong foundation in service, mentorship, and education. Through a wide range of initiatives and programs, LMSA+ creates opportunities for aspiring physicians to engage directly with their communities, gain valuable guidance from mentors in the medical field, and develop the skills necessary for academic and professional success.

The chapter places a strong emphasis on advocating for health equity and addressing the unique challenges faced by Hispanic and Latina/o/x populations across the United States. By equipping students with the knowledge, resources, and support networks they need, LMSA+ helps cultivate culturally competent future healthcare leaders who are committed to reducing disparities and improving health outcomes in underserved communities.

Whether through community outreach events, educational workshops, networking opportunities, or collaboration with healthcare professionals, the LMSA+ Chapter serves as a vital platform for students to grow, lead, and make a meaningful impact on the future of healthcare for Hispanic and Latina/o/x individuals nationwide.

Our LinkTree: https://linktr.ee/lmsaplusuga
summary: The Latino Medical Student Association PLUS Chapter empowers pre-medical students through service, mentorship, and education to advocate for improved health outcomes in Hispanic and Latina/o/x communities across the United States.

name: Hopelessly Yellow at Georgia
description: Originally founded at the University of Michigan in 2021, Hopelessly Yellow is a digital brand promoting mental wellness and positivity through writing, design, music, and media. Now at the University of Georgia, Hopelessly Yellow strives to foster a passionate community for all. More than just a club, Hopelessly Yellow embodies a blissful feeling, a fresh mindset, and a golden lifestyle.
summary: Hopelessly Yellow is a digital brand promoting mental wellness and positivity through writing, design, music, and media. HY strives to foster a passionate community for all, embodying a blissful feeling, a fresh mindset, and a golden lifestyle.

name: Xtension
description:
Xtension is a mentorship program dedicated to fostering organic relationships between minority first-year students and well-versed upperclassmen. By creating lasting friendships and providing a relaxed yet stimulating environment, Xtension aims to help students establish their social, professional, and academic networks within the university.

Xtension envisions a collegiate environment where all students feel supported, connected, and equipped to thrive, promoting inclusivity and mentorship as the foundation of the college experience. Its mission is to facilitate the development of meaningful peer relationships and empower students to maximize their institutional knowledge and connections, equipping them with the tools to succeed as minority students in diverse spaces.

By joining Xtension, you’ll develop leadership skills, confidence, and a strong network of like-minded individuals striving for excellence. More than just a student organization, Xtension fosters lifelong friendships, career connections, and mentorship relationships that extend beyond graduation.

If you're looking for an authentic, empowering, and inspiring community, Xtension is here for you.
summary: Xtension at UGA empowers African American students through mentorship, academic support, and community, fostering growth, success, and belonging.

name: Palestinian Student Life
description:
Palestinian Student Life is an up-and-coming student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to celebrating Palestinian culture, heritage, and community. Our mission is to create a welcoming space where students can connect, share stories, and engage with the rich traditions and history of Palestine.

Through cultural events, educational discussions, and community-building activities, we aim to amplify Palestinian voices on campus while fostering understanding, solidarity, and pride. Whether you're Palestinian or simply curious to learn more, our club offers a space for meaningful connection and cultural exchange.

Our club is open to all students—regardless of background—who are interested in learning more about Palestine. Whether you’re looking to connect with others or explore your roots, Palestinian Student Life is your home away from home.

Join us as we grow, celebrate, and stand together in community.
summary:
Palestinian Student Life is an up-and-coming space to celebrate Palestinian culture, heritage, and community at the University of Georgia. 

Join us as we grow, celebrate, and stand together in community.

name: CURE Childhood Cancer Club at UGA
description:
https://linktr.ee/curechildcanceruga

Our club is dedicated to supporting Cure Childhood Cancer, a nonprofit committed to funding research and providing support to children and families affected by childhood cancer. Through awareness campaigns, fundraising events, and community engagement, we aim to make a meaningful impact in the fight against childhood cancer. By uniting students with a shared passion for advocacy and compassion, we strive to bring hope, resources, and real change to those who need it most.
summary: Our club is dedicated to supporting Cure Childhood Cancer, a nonprofit committed to funding research and providing support to children and families affected by childhood cancer through awareness campaigns, fundraising events, and community engagement.

name: Autoimmune Student Association
description: The Autoimmune Student Association is a student organization where students struggling with Autoimmune disease or other related issues can come together to support one another.
summary: The Autoimmune Student Association is a student organization where students struggling with Autoimmune disease or other related issues can come together to support one another.

name: Alternative Investment Society
description: The Alternative Investment Society (AIS) will introduce students to the world of alternative assets including private equity, venture capital, real estate, hedge funds, crypto, and unique assets. We’ll cover what these investments are, how they work, and what careers in these fields look like. The club will offer educational meetings, resume workshops, interview prep, and competitions. Members will gain access to guest speakers, mentorship, and networking events, with a focus on helping students break into highly competitive finance roles outside of traditional paths.
summary: The Alternative Investment Society empowers undergraduates to explore private equity, venture capital, real estate, hedge funds, crypto, and unique assets through education, competitions, and networking with top industry professionals.

name: Corazón Consciente
description: while Hispanic people are 50% less likely to develop cardiovascular disease during their lifetime compared to other communities, an overwhelming 53,7% of Hispanic men and 43% of Hispanic women were diagnosed with some form of cardiovascular disease (CVD) in 2020. the goal of the organization is to foster understanding, education, and a safe environment that enables to Hispanic community to better advocate for themselves in the worlds of CVD, and to know when they should seek medical attention. In days world, many young people suffer from CVD and have no clue about it until it is too late, this organization aims to address that issue.
summary: to foster cardiovascular disease awareness on campus and in the community, will providing resources and information to the Hispanic community to effectively address the rising numbers of cardiovascular disease within the Hispanic community.

name: ArtRx
description:
The purpose of ArtRx is to unite UGA pre-health students to relax and enjoy art while providing good deeds for their communities. We aim to:

  1. Create a relaxed environment for pre-health students to enjoy themselves and improve skills in visual perception, empathic communication, and reflection through art.
  2. Teach pre-health students various art forms as relaxation methods and promote education in all fields.
  3. Connect to a variety of members of the community through volunteering and providing them with relaxation and fun activities.
summary: The purpose of ArtRx is to unite UGA pre-health students to relax and enjoy art while providing good deeds for their communities. Through our meetings, we hope to bridge the gap between pre-health and art and explore the intersection of the two.

name: Franklin College of Arts and Sciences
description: The heart of UGA’s liberal arts learning environment, the Franklin College of Arts and Sciences strives to create a strong sense of community and pride amongst its more than 11,000 students across 80+ degree programs. Our student programming focuses on providing interdisciplinary interaction amongst majors, enhancing academic excellence and promoting career readiness.
summary: The heart of UGA’s liberal arts learning environment, the Franklin College of Arts and Sciences strives to create a strong sense of community and pride amongst its more than 11,000 students across 80+ degree programs.

name: Wealth Management Society
description: WMS connects students with industry professionals through speaker events, case studies, and portfolio simulations. Focused on career development and technical skills, it fosters a collaborative environment for learning financial strategies and wealth management.
summary: The Wealth Management Society (WMS) at UGA prepares students for careers in wealth management through workshops, networking, and hands-on experiences. Members gain insights into financial strategies, investment management, and client relations.

name: Women of Color Advancing Peace, Security, and Conflict Transformation
description:
Women of Color Advancing Peace, Security, and Conflict Transformation (WCAPS) at the University of Georgia is dedicated to amplifying the voices and experiences of women and gender-diverse people of color in fields related to international affairs, public policy, human rights, and social justice. As a chapter of the national WCAPS network, we aim to cultivate a supportive and dynamic community for students who are passionate about driving change locally and globally.

Our organization focuses on:

  • Mentorship and Leadership Development: Creating structured mentorship opportunities that connect students with professionals and alumni in peace, security, and policy-related fields.
  • Community Outreach and Volunteering: Partnering with local organizations to engage in meaningful service that supports marginalized communities in Athens and surrounding areas.
  • Conferences and Professional Exposure: Attending and organizing events, panels, and conferences to expand our members’ access to professional development and global conversations.
  • Collaborative Programming: Working with other student organizations to host intersectional discussions, joint initiatives, and events that bridge diverse topics and communities.
  • Wellness and Empowerment: Hosting retreats and wellness-centered activities to prioritize self-care, healing, and sustainable activism.
  • Historical and Cultural Awareness: Examining often-overlooked narratives in history and policy, with a focus on underrepresented communities and global contexts.
summary: WCAPS is a student-led organization committed to empowering women and gender-diverse students of color through engagement with peace, security, and social justice. While centering these voices, we welcome everyone to be part of our community.

name: Food and Agricultural Society
description: The Food and Agriculture Law Society (FALS) brings together students, professionals, and advocates passionate about the intersection of law, food, and agriculture. Our mission is to explore legal issues affecting the food industry, agricultural practices.
summary: The Food and Agriculture Law Society (FALS) brings together students, professionals, and advocates passionate about the intersection of law, food, and agriculture. Our mission is to explore legal issues affecting the food industry, agricultural practices

name: Health and Longevity
description: A club dedicated to dialogue about topics oriented around health, longevity, biohacking, biology, health optimization, current social media trends and views pertaining to health, and lifestyle implementation of any of the above. The club also aims to create an inclusive environment that cultivates a love and appreciation for a sustainable and healthy lifestyle conducive to longevity and higher quality of life.
summary: A community designed to foster well-being through discussion of health and longevity research, current social media trends and views on the topic, and pragmatic implementation of practices to aid in health and longevity.

name: Students of Agronomy, Soils, and Environmental Sciences Club
description: Our goal is to build a community of undergraduate students, primarily but not limited to the Department of Crop and Soil Science, who are interested in agronomy, soils, and environmental science. It will also be a pathway for CRSS students to join the national SASES organization and participate in their events and competitions.
summary:
. 
This will be both a social and professional development club aimed at building a community of students who are passionate about agronomy, soils, and environmental science and to get members involved with national SASES events and competitions

name: Growing Ramos Association Cultivating Inclusion and Academic Support
description: GRACIAS at UGA, an organization at the University of Georgia, is dedicated to advancing the Latino community in Athens through educational support. Events hosted at public schools and community centers provide opportunities for UGA’s students to volunteer both on and off campus, fostering meaningful connections with the local community. These events facilitate connections between students, college peers, and alumni, offering guidance on college options, applications, financial aid, and scholarships. Mentors provide personalized advice, assisting students in navigating the college landscape and completing essential tasks like the FAFSA. Moreover, the chapter aims to create a bigger sense of belonging and inclusion, improving the experience of current college students while diminishing impostor syndrome. With GRACIAS, a community where educational opportunities are accessible to all is envisioned, ensuring every student can thrive. Join in making a difference in the lives of Latino students and families in Athens.
summary: GRACIAS at UGA supports the Latino community in Athens through mentorship, college prep, & volunteer events. It fosters inclusion, combats impostor syndrome, & helps students with applications, FAFSA, & scholarships to ensure equal access to education.

name: Rev & Ride
description: Rev & Ride is an inclusive community dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding and appreciation for cars. We strive to engage individuals from all genders, majors, backgrounds, and levels of knowledge, providing a welcoming space for enthusiasts to explore, discuss, and learn about the diverse world of automobiles. Rev & Ride hosts technical demos, automotive debates/conversations, race watch parties, and more!
summary: Rev & Ride is an inclusive community dedicated to fostering a deeper understanding and appreciation for cars. We strive to engage individuals from all levels of knowledge, providing a space for enthusiasts to learn and explore the world of automobiles!

name: Climate Collective at UGA
description: Climate Collective at UGA is climate change and sustainability focused on-campus organisation which will provide opportunities to collaborate on interdisciplinary projects related to climate policy, research, and sustainability, have a biweekly newsletter and guest speakers, and inform students of relevant professional opportunities within and beyond UGA.
summary: Climate Collective at UGA is climate change and sustainability focused on-campus organisation which aims to bring together students and faculty from various departments to create a community that engages with these topics.

name: Pre-Professional Student Ambassadors
description: Pre-Professional Student Ambassadors (PPSAs) work directly with the Pre-Professional Advising Office (PPAO) to provide support and guidance to pre-health and pre-law students at UGA.
summary: Pre-Professional Student Ambassadors (PPSA) work directly with the Pre-Professional Advising Office (PPAO) to establish and build an inclusive community for pre-health and pre-law students at UGA.

name: Aspiring Educators at UGA
description: Aspiring Educators at UGA is a pre-professional organization seeking to provide opportunities and resources to enhance the education of students pursuing a teaching degree.
summary: Aspiring Educators at UGA serves as the connection between UGA students and the local, state, and national levels of the National Educators Association. Through membership, we provide professional development opportunities and resources to students.

name: Building Leaders Advancing Career Knowledge Union
description: Building Leaders Advancing Career Knowledge (B.L.A.C.K.) Union is a student-led professional organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to empowering underrepresented students across all majors. Through a focus on leadership development, career readiness, and personal growth, B.L.A.C.K. Union provides a platform for students to build meaningful connections, access mentorship, and develop the skills necessary to excel in any industry. The organization hosts workshops, speaker panels, service initiatives, and networking events that promote professionalism, celebrate multicultural excellence, and cultivate a community of ambitious, forward-thinking leaders committed to success and impact beyond the classroom.
summary: Building Leaders Advancing Career Knowledge Union is a professional organization that empowers underrepresented students of all majors through leadership development, networking, mentorship, and events that celebrate professionalism and excellence.

name: Finance Competition Society
description: Throughout the school year, we will host two competitions related to the field of finance and containing elements of Investment Banking, Consulting, Accounting, and many more fields. Members will be grouped together to collaborate on these competitions and work to deliver a strong result for themselves and the school.
summary: Our organization aims to further the achievement of our members in the world of finance through global competitions. One club-sponsored competition will take place each semester, with trainings, resources, and guidance leading up to the competition.

name: Georgia Connect: High School Community Alliances
description: Georgia Connect brings UGA students together with high schoolers across the state through volunteering, service, and mentorship. Our goal is to give students ways to get involved in projects that support their schools and local communities. A lot of high school students don’t know what resources are available to them, so we step in to offer guidance and support from UGA peers who’ve been in their shoes. At the same time, UGA students get hands-on leadership experience and the chance to make a meaningful impact. By working with communities all over Georgia, we hope to create positive, lasting change together.
summary: Connecting UGA students with high school students across Georgia through volunteer work, community service, and mentorship.

name: Journaling Club
description:
The purpose of Journaling Club shall be to bring together students who share a

common interest in journaling. All are welcome! Whether a student has been

journaling for years or wants a place to begin, this club serves to be a space for

members to take time away from their busy schedules, build community, and

catch up their journals. Our main goal is always to prioritize the mental health of

our members by giving them a place to de-stress, creatively express themselves,

and collaborate with fellow journalers and students .
summary: A space for members to socialize and build community, while also being able to catch up on your journal!

name: Guftugu: Urdu Language Club
description: The Urdu Language Club aims to bolster exposure to the language we know as Urdu, and spur excitement among new and experienced students alike. Our club will include everything from basic grammar sessions, guided readings of poetry and prose, conversation drills, movie nights, lectures, and more!
summary: We are a group of UGA students with a passion for all things relating to the Urdu language. Heritage speakers, enthusiasts, linguists, complete beginners, and anyone in between are welcome!

name: Neuroscience Graduate Student Association
description: The Neuroscience Graduate Student Association (NGSA)’s mission is to operate as a liaison between graduate students from various home departments, the Biomedical and Health Sciences Institute, the Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Program, and associated University of Georgia administration. The NSGA aims to encourage collaboration and communication among faculty, researchers, and graduate students by facilitating social events and mentoring opportunities. These social events will also provide graduate students with opportunities to network and establish professional connections with researchers from a variety of scientific disciplines. Overall, the NGSA seeks to act as a voice and support system for graduate students looking to enhance or focus their studies in the field of Neuroscience.
summary: The Neuroscience Graduate Student Association’s mission is to foster community among current Neuroscience graduate students, and to operate as a liaison between graduate students and faculty/administration of the Interdisciplinary Neuroscience Program.

name: Airsoft at UGA
description:
The Airsoft Club at the University of Georgia strives to provide a fun and safe way for students to participate in Airsoft events and competitions. Whether you're looking to compete at a high level or enjoy casual games, our club offers training sessions and workshops to help members improve their skills and knowledge of the sport regardless of prior experience. Joining the Airsoft Club is a great way to meet new people, stay physically active, and be part of a supportive community that shares a passion for Airsoft.

Join our GroupMe for more:

https://groupme.com/join_group/106942859/3RVrTweR
summary: The University of Georgia Airsoft Club offers a safe and supportive environment for students to participate in Airsoft events and competitions and meet other students interested in the sport.

name: Christian Business Society
description: The Christian Business Society (CBS) at the University of Georgia exists to unite Christian students within the Terry College of Business, creating a supportive and faith-centered community. CBS fosters spiritual growth, meaningful relationships, and a commitment to service in the Athens community. Members learn how to integrate Christian values into their professional lives while engaging with business leaders who exemplify servant leadership in their workplaces, families, and broader communities. Through regular meetings, speaker events, and service opportunities, CBS equips students to honor God in both their careers and personal lives.
summary: Connecting Christian business students at UGA to grow spiritually, build lasting relationships, serve the community, and learn from leaders who exemplify Christ-centered servant leadership in business, family, and beyond.

name: Muslim Pre-Health Society
description: The Muslim Pre-Health Society at UGA aims to create a welcoming space for Muslim students interested in healthcare careers. We offer mentorship, academic and professional development, faith-centered dialogue, and collaborative events to foster both personal and career growth. Members benefit from peer panels, volunteering opportunities, guest speakers, and joint events with other pre-health organizations on campus. Our mission is to build a supportive network rooted in Islamic values and healthcare excellence, while remaining open and inclusive to all students at UGA.
summary: The Muslim Pre-Health Society at UGA provides mentorship, professional development, and a welcoming space for all students pursuing healthcare careers.

name: Finance Advisory Board
description: The goal of the Finance Advisory Board is to ensure the Finance program at the University of Georgia is constantly improving and improvising to supply qualified and motivated students that meet the needs and desires of employers in the Finance industry.
summary: The Finance Advisory Board is a board of passionate and motivated students who want to continue to improve the Finance program at the University of Georgia.

name: Madhatter Knits Chapter
description:
The chapter will help serve as a way to create and collect knitted hats for premature babies and distribute these to various hospitals and places. This organization aims to give back to the community by hosting events and raising awareness.

https://www.madhatterknits.org/
summary: The chapter will help serve as a way to create and collect knitted hats for premature babies and distribute these to various hospitals and places. This organization aims to give back to the community by hosting events and raising awareness.

name: South Asian Student Association at UGA
description: SASA at UGA is a student-led organization that brings together individuals interested in South Asian culture to build community, celebrate diversity, and promote cultural awareness on campus. Through events like cultural showcases, socials, service projects, and educational discussions, SASA highlights the traditions, stories, and voices of countries such as India, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Bhutan, the Maldives, and Afghanistan. Whether you're South Asian or simply interested in learning more, SASA is a welcoming space for anyone looking to connect, engage, and celebrate culture.
summary: SASA at UGA unites students to celebrate South Asian cultures through events, service, and community. Open to all, we highlight the diversity of countries like India, Pakistan, Nepal, and more while promoting inclusion and cultural awareness on campus.

name: VT Seva
description: Increasing awareness on the blind children in Nethravidyala, India through outreach events and also engaging students in local community service events to help the Athens community and beyond.
summary: Community service-oriented organization raising awareness primarily for the education of blind children, along with other community-related events

name: Crump Humanism in Medicine Exposition Scholars
description: Crump Humanism in Medicine Exposition Scholars (CHIMES) is a student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to promoting empathy, equity, and patient-centered care in medicine. Through workshops, speaker events, and reflective projects, CHIMES fosters discussion on physician wellness, social determinants of health, and diversity in healthcare. The club provides a space for future healthcare professionals to explore the human side of medicine and advocate for compassionate, inclusive care.
summary: Crump Humanism in Medicine Exposition Scholars (CHIMES) is a student organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to promoting empathy, equity, and patient-centered care in medicine.

name: Pre-Law Rising
description: Pre-Law Rising is a student organization dedicated to supporting individuals pursuing a career in law. We aim to show aspiring lawyers what a career in law really looks like. We're here for students who want to learn about the daily work, real challenges, and diverse career paths that exist within the legal world. Our goal is to foster a low-pressure environment where members can explore a career in law. Whether envisioning a future in the courtroom or exploring alternative paths beyond traditional legal careers, this is a space to discover what fits you best.
summary: Pre-Law Rising supports students interested in legal careers by providing insight into the realities of practicing law and its many paths. We're committed to a low-pressure space for first-generation students and anyone curious about the legal field.

name: Alpha Xi Delta
description: Alpha Xi Delta is a national women's fraternity founded on April 17, 1893, at Lombard College in Galesburg, Illinois. As one of the oldest women’s fraternities in the United States, Alpha Xi Delta is dedicated to empowering women to realize their potential. Together, we strive to become better versions of ourselves by growing in courage, graciousness, and peace. We emphasize leadership, academic excellence, personal development, and community involvement. Our national philanthropic focus is the Kindly Hearts Initiative, which supports kids and teens experiencing foster care and homelessness. More information about our organization can be found on our external website.
summary: Alpha Xi Delta is a Panhellenic sorority at the University of Georgia, dedicated to inspiring women to realize their potential.

name: Bridge: The Black Transfer Collective
description:
The Bride: Black Transfer Collective (Bridge) is a student-led organization created to support and uplift transfer students who identify with the Black experience. We’re here to help students build community, find resources, and feel a true sense of belonging as they transition into campus life.

BTSA offers a space to connect with others who understand the unique journey of transferring — from adjusting socially and academically, to navigating new systems and opportunities. Through events, mentorship, and open conversations, we aim to empower members to thrive and leave their mark on campus.

While our focus is on affirming and celebrating the Black transfer experience, we welcome all students who support our mission and values. Everyone has a role to play in building a more inclusive and connected campus community.
summary: Bridge is a student-led organization made to support and uplift transfer students who identify with the Black experience. We’re here to help students build community, find resources, and feel a true sense of belonging as they transition into campus life.

name: Limitless
description: We are a nonprofit focused on raising funds, awareness, and breaking the stigma around disabilities. Through a unique mentorship with the kids of ESP Athens, a freshman leadership program, a fundraising committee, and a volunteer program, we aim to promote inclusivity and equal opportunities for all.
summary: We are a nonprofit focused on raising funds, awareness, and breaking the stigma around disabilities. Through a unique mentorship with the kids of ESP Athens and a freshman leadership program, we aim to promote inclusivity and equal opportunities for all.

name: K-Pop Club
description:
K-Pop Club is a safe space for those interested in Korean pop music and Korean dramas to connect with like-minded individuals and engage in a variety of related activities. We will have photocard trading, decorating, concert recaps, listening parties, and other fun activities. Whether you consider yourself a casual listener/fan or an enthusiast, we welcome everyone to enjoy and celebrate their interests together!

GroupMe: https://groupme.com/join_group/108534657/wjlGCPOU
summary: K-Pop Club is a safe space for those interested in Korean pop music and dramas to connect with like-minded individuals and engage in a variety of activities. Whether you consider yourself a casual listener/fan or an enthusiast, all are welcome!

name: Medieval and Early Modern Society
description: The Medieval and Early Modern Society is an interdisciplinary society aimed primarily at Graduate Students in any area of pre-modern study (though membership is open to undergraduates, faculty, staff, and any member of UGA with an interest in pre-modern studies). The aim of the society is to connect UGA pre-modern scholars, share resources and news in pre-modern studies, and organize reading groups, colloquia, and social events.
summary: The Medieval and Early Modern Society (MEMS) is an interdisciplinary society at scholars with interest in any pre-modern area.

name: BCI Research Organization
description: BCI Research Organization conducts and writes research papers, projects, and grant proposals for the purpose of initiating more BCI (Brain-Computer Interface) research efforts and projects at UGA. The organization is meant to connect students and faculty from multiple departments and disciplines to work towards establishing a robust and productive BCI research environment at UGA.
summary: BCIRO writes and conducts research on applications for BCI (Brain-Computer Interface).

name: Crafted for Care
description: Crafted for Care is a student-led organization committed to helping pre-dental students strengthen their manual dexterity skills, an essential component of both dental school applications and future success in the dental field. We foster an encouraging environment for all aspiring dental professionals and other interested students, serving as a resource for dental school guidance, peer collaboration, hands-on practice to build dexterity, and community engagement.
summary: Crafted for Care is a student-led organization committed to advancing the manual dexterity of aspiring dental professionals and other interested students, serving as a resource for dental school guidance, peer collaboration, and community engagement.

name: Girls in STEM
description: The purpose of Girls in STEM is to empower young women and all students to explore and pursue future educations and careers in science, technology, engineering and mathematics. To challenge gender disparities in STEM, GIS fosters a supportive and connected community through peer led education, outreach and service. GIS was originally founded in 2015 at a Texas high school with the mission of creating an open and welcoming community for all students internationally. Our chapter’s Founding Creator and President, Alyssa Hitzel, established the University of Georgia’s chapter to serve as the first collegiate chapter, as well as the first chapter in the state of Georgia of the international organization. Hitzel founded GIS at UGA, aligning with our founder's original purpose, to inspire and motivate ALL students on UGA’s campus and in local Athens schools, and to encourage pioneering, innovation, collaboration, and research regardless of gender, sexual orientation, race, age, etc. As the first collegiate chapter, we partner with local schools and community centers in Athens to provide mentorship, host interactive STEM workshops and encourage students, especially young women interested in STEM, to envision themselves in these promising fields.
summary: Serving as the first collegiate chapter, and first chapter in the state of Georgia, Girls in STEM at UGA empowers ALL students in the local Athens area to pursue STEM educations and careers with the help of GIS’ mentorship, hands-on workshops, outreach.

name: Future Smiles Initiative
description: The purpose of Future Smiles Initiative is to educate and empower communities in the Athens area by promoting essential knowledge of dental hygiene, teaching the long-term effects of poor oral care on the body, and encouraging preventive practices that support lifelong oral health. Our mission is especially focused on serving populations in Athens that are affected by lower socioeconomic status, where access to dental care and oral health education is often limited. We aim to visit local schools, community centers, and homeless shelters to provide engaging, age-appropriate lessons and hands-on demonstrations that help individuals of all ages understand how to properly care for their teeth. By reaching these underserved communities, we hope to address the knowledge gaps and resource barriers that contribute to preventable oral diseases that can have serious impacts on overall health, including heart disease, diabetes complications, and systemic infections. Through early intervention, education, and community outreach, Future Smiles Initiative seeks to promote equity in oral health and inspire a generation empowered to take charge of their well-being. *Not affiliated with the non-profit organization*
summary: The purpose of Future Smiles Initiative is to empower Athens areas by teaching dental hygiene, effects of poor oral care, and preventive practices through outreach in schools, homeless shelters, and community centers to promote lifelong oral health.

name: SODA: Student Organ Donation Advocates
description:
After personally experiencing the power of organ, eye, and tissue donation, Sara Royf realized that students, like her, had an important role to play in reducing the organ shortage. So, she founded the first SODA chapter at Washington University in St. Louis in 2014. Sara and the other original student leaders overcame a lot of challenges when leading this first chapter–establishing a mission, creating a brand, raising money, connecting with partners, and encouraging peers to become organ, eye, and tissue donors was a lot of work.

When students from other campuses expressed interest in saving and healing lives, Sara wanted to make advocacy fast and easy for them by expanding SODA to provide mentorship, supplies, event funding, how-to guides, and other resources to the new student leaders. The second and third SODA chapters were founded in 2017.

In 2019, SODA was incorporated as a 501(c)(3) organization. To support the growing organization, we hired our first employee, Nicole Nidea, to lead the SODA National Team, build partnerships, guide strategy, and more in 2021. In 2022, SODA hired Engagement Manager, Sophia Renner, to provide student and local partner support. SODA is also supported by a Board of Directors, led by Erin Kupferman as Board President, that is made up of people with connections to donation/transplantation and a team of part-time support.

Today, SODA supports students who want to make a difference by helping them host education and registration events and lead organ, eye, and tissue donation advocacy chapters on their high school, college, and graduate school campuses. These students' efforts save lives, build leadership skills, and create positive ripple effects throughout their communities.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nm477Lrrplc

At SODA, we believe students represent a promising segment of the population to become educated about the critical need for organ, eye,  and tissue donation and to register as organ, eye, and tissue donors.

We reduce barriers for high school, college, and graduate school students so they can mobilize organ, eye, and tissue donation ambassadors and their peers, spark a passion for organ donation that can last a lifetime, and fulfill an unmet need for student-focused organ, eye, and tissue donation education and registration efforts across the United States.

To support our events and chapters, SODA works with Community Outreach Teams at 35 Organ Procurement Organizations (OPOs).
summary: SODA: Student Organ Donation Advocates is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization that inspires and supports student-led organ, eye, and tissue donation education and registration efforts on high school, college, and graduate school campuses.

name: SAVE Promise Club
description: SAVE Promise Club is an extension of the Sandy Hook Promise organization. The mission of this club is getting college students involved on campus with advocacy for school safety and mental health in schools. We work towards creating a welcoming environments for all students on campus.
summary: SAVE Promise Club is an extension of the Sandy Hook Promise organization. The mission of this club is getting college students involved on campus with advocacy for school safety and mental health in schools.

name: AI at UGA
description:
AI@UGA: Applied Artificial Intelligence & Industry Collaboration

AI@UGA is a student-led organization at the University of Georgia dedicated to applied artificial intelligence, machine learning, and software engineering. We bridge the gap between students and industry by connecting motivated undergraduate and graduate members with real-world technical projects in collaboration with companies, city governments, and research teams.

What We Do:

  • Build hands-on experience by working on projects in machine learning, software engineering, and data science.

  • Collaborate with industry partners on real-world applications, from 3D scene reconstruction to satellite image analysis and AI-driven chatbots.

  • Gain exposure to professional development workflows, including agile project management, version control, testing, and documentation.

Opportunities for Members:

  • Work on interdisciplinary teams with software engineers, data scientists, and project managers.

  • Develop technical skills in web/mobile development, backend/cloud infrastructure, and AI/ML (Python, PyTorch, TensorFlow).

  • Engage in research and contribute to publications in scientific journals.

Why Join?
AI@UGA offers a unique opportunity to apply your classroom knowledge to impactful, real-world problems while collaborating directly with industry professionals. Members go through a structured selection process, ensuring teams are composed of driven, technically skilled individuals.

Interested?
Join us to gain hands-on experience, build your portfolio, and network with industry leaders in AI.
summary: Connects ML students with industry projects for hands-on experience, real-world programming, and research. Students collaborate with companies, build portfolios, and gain leadership as project managers.

name: Pi Delta Phi National French Honor Society
description:
Pi Delta Phi is the National French Honor Society for college and university undergraduate and graduate students. Founded at the University of California, Berkeley in 1906, Pi Delta Phi is the oldest national academic honor society for a foreign language in the United States. The aims of the Society are to recognize outstanding scholarship in the French language and Francophone literatures, to increase the knowledge and appreciation of Americans for the cultural contributions of the French-speaking world, and to encourage and to stimulate French and francophone cultural activities. There are currently more than 400 chapters and over 75,000 members across the United States.

In order to be eligible for nomination, undergraduate students must be sophomores, must have completed at least one semester of a 300-level or higher course in French, have a current minimum GPA of 3.0 in French 3.0 overall, and rank in the top 35% of their class. National one-time dues for a lifetime membership are also required, for which students receive the Pi Delta Phi pin, certificate, and one set of graduation honor cords in blue, white and red.
summary: Pi Delta Phi is the national French honor society in the U.S., recognizing academic excellence in French language, literature, and culture. It promotes French studies and cultural exchange among students.

name: Classic Productions
description:
Classic Productions' mission is to learn and create a short film by the end of each spring semester. This club has a position for students interested in writing, acting, social media, and more! During fall semester, members will learn info an how to create a short film and spring semester we will use our creativity and apply what we have learned to create and promote our project! Join the GroupMe for more information.

https://groupme.com/join_group/108794045/GhwNQOK5
summary: Classic Productions' mission is to learn and create a short film by the end of each spring semester. This club has a position for students interested in writing, acting, social media, and more! Join the GroupMe for more information.

name: Dogs Team Handball
description:
Dogs Team Handball is the official collegiate handball club at the University of Georgia, dedicated to training, competing, and growing the sport of handball at the university level. Our mission is to develop a competitive team that proudly represents our school at the Collegiate Handball Nationals, while also creating opportunities for players to advance to national-level training camps and even the U.S. National Team.

 

Backed by two members of the Handball Futures Board, the Collegiate Nationals Coordinator, and a former Olympian and alumni coach, our team is built on excellence, inclusivity, and school spirit. Whether you’re a seasoned athlete or a newcomer to the sport, Dawgs Team Handball welcomes all students with the drive to compete, improve, and represent the DAWGS on the national stage.
summary: Team Handball Club is an opportunity for the students of the University of Georgia to learn, participate, and compete in the sport of team handball at the intercollegiate level.Experiencing the value of teamwork, sportsmanship, and competition.

name: Hands of Hope
description: Our club will hold service projects in our community. Our goal is to creating a long-lasting positive impact on Athens. We are a body made up of students seeking healthcare jobs, and believe it is important to give back. We will organize events such as flower bouquet making and drop off at hospitals and canned food drive. We value our commitment to our community and hope our impact can continue.
summary: An organization focused on serving our community and helping neighbors through service and compassion.

name: Peabody Student Collective
description: This organization works in coordination with the Peabody Awards to explore the Peabody Archives, develop programming for students and educate students on the history and mission of the awards.
summary: Inspiring and informing students through the Peabody Awards, with programming made by and for students throughout the year.

name: Connection Church of Athens
description: At Connection Church of Athens, we remain committed to our mission of providing young adults with the mentors they need to answer questions and the friends who will share their beliefs, in order to encourage them in their faith, and help them grow as leaders in their communities. We meet once a week as a group with our college pastor and share a meal and Bible study together. Everyone is welcome and encouraged to come.
summary: We are a church that focuses on Bible oriented teachings with a purpose of equipping students with the knowledge and confidence to spread the gospel. Our college ministry brings students together in fellowship by sharing meals and studying scripture.

name: Accounting Society
description:
The Accounting Society is a student-led organization in the Terry College of Business that brings together freshman and sophomores who want to explore the world of accounting and finance. Whether you’re a business student or just curious about the fundamentals, our society provides opportunities to learn essential concepts, connect with accounting firms, and engage with Terry professors and peers interested in professional growth.

 

Why Join?

 

  • Develop leadership skills by serving on the Accounting Society Executive Board

  • Learn the fundamentals of accounting and finance through hands-on workshops and case studies

  • Network with professionals from top accounting and finance firms through guest speaker events and recruiting sessions

  • Connect with Terry faculty in a setting outside of the classroom

  • Gain valuable resources for internships, CPA preparation, and career development

Join our GroupMe!:

 

GroupMe
summary: The Accounting Society at UGA connects students with professional development and networking opportunities to prepare them for successful careers in accounting. Through workshops, guest speakers, and peer support, we help members excel professionally.

name: Digital Music Production
description: DMP aims to connect UGA students that share a passion for making music through DAWs. The organization will provide in-person meetings for connection, collaboration, and creation. We will also work to provide events for student to play their music live, and provide resources for students wanting to improve or expand their skills. This includes access to instruments, software, recording spaces, and teaching resources.
summary: We provide a community for UGA students with a passion for creating music through digital audio workspaces and similar technology to connect, collaborate, and share their works!

name: Sandwich Project at UGA, The
description: • The Sandwich Project at UGA is a student-led volunteer organization committed to alleviating food insecurity in the Athens community by preparing and distributing fresh, nutritious sandwiches. In partnership with the broader Sandwich Project initiative, our chapter seeks to educate students about hunger and homelessness while fostering service, empathy, and leadership among members.
summary: The Sandwich Project at UGA is a student-led volunteer organization operating as a part of The Sandwich Project in Atlanta. We aim to alleviate food insecurity in the Athens community by preparing and distributing fresh, nutritious sandwiches.

name: Polaris Society
description: Polaris Society is a semester long pre-professional program in UGA’s SPIA and Franklin communities that connects ambitious students with mentorship, career exploration, and professional development opportunities in government, law, policy, and international affairs.
summary: Polaris Society equips freshmen and sophomores interested in careers in government, policy, and international affairs with the professional skills, network, and mentorship to gain access to opportunities that are typically out of reach until junior year.

name: Taiwanese Arts and Performance Association
description: The Taiwanese Arts and Performance Association (TAPA) works to showcase the unique aspects of Taiwanese culture through the teaching and performance of its fine arts. Our drum team, Formosa Rhythms, has already performed at on-campus events such as APIDA Night Market and AASA's Lunar New Year Festival. Our yo-yo coordinators have 7 years of performance and choreography experience. In addition to performance opportunities, TAPA also hosts history and culture nights as a chance to interact with and learn more about Taiwanese fine arts from a different perspective!
summary: The Taiwanese Arts and Performance Association (TAPA) is committed to distinguishing Taiwanese culture through the teaching and performance of various Taiwanese fine arts. Join us to learn about Taiwanese drums, yo-yos, and history over yummy meals!

name: Latin American Literature Club
description: We meet once a month to discuss either novellas or short stories that explore various facets of Latin American culture, ranging from identity, politics, to ways of life.
summary: Discussion based meetings where we explore Latin American culture through various literary works.

name: World Literature Society
description: World Literature Society seeks to understand and promote cultures from around the world using literature as our outlet. We will study history, language, and philosophy through the lens of literature, with a special emphasis on translated works. Our primary goal is to build a community centered on broadening perspectives and deepening our awareness of the world around us. We aim to do this by hosting academic events such as guest lectures and social events such as culture spotlight nights.
summary: World Literature Society seeks to appreciate and explore literature not originally written in English from various cultures. In addition to book club meetings, we will host various social events intended to foster a community of readers from all majors

name: General Media Club
description: 1. The General Media Club aims to explore obscure and avant-garde literature, art, music, and film by studying their message and exploring the boundaries they push to develop the media literacy of its members.
summary: We explore obscure and/or avant-garde methods of expression in media (literature, music, film, etc.). This club is perfect for people looking to expand their taste.

name: HealthLink Initiative, The
description: We are a 501c(3) organization founded by students from both the University of Georgia and Georgia Institute of Technology and are dedicated to connecting uninsured, undocumented, and underserved communities of Georgia with accessible healthcare services.
summary: The HealthLink Initiative is dedicated to connecting uninsured, undocumented, and underserved communities of Georgia with accessible healthcare services.

name: Russian Literature Club
description:
1. The purpose of the Russian Literature Club shall be to provide a structured and inclusive environment for the study, discussion, and appreciation of Russian literature from the 19th, 20th, and 21st  centuries. The organization shall seek to promote cultural and intellectual engagement through the reading and analysis of literary works, both classic and contemporary, from the Russian tradition.
  2. The Russian Literature Club is committed to fostering a safe, respectful, and beginner-friendly learning environment in which students of all academic backgrounds may participate. It shall encourage open dialogue, mutual respect, and academic curiosity, while supporting members in the development of critical thinking, cultural literacy, and a deeper understanding of Russian literature within a global context.
summary: Russian Literature Club is dedicated to creating a beginner friendly environment for the discussion and appreciation of works of Russian literature from the 19th to 21st century. All majors are welcome and encouraged to join.

name: Joy Mission
description: The Joy Mission at the University of Georgia seeks to support students in Athens Clarke County grieving the loss of a parent or guardian by providing a safe, uplifting, and empowering community. Originally founded at Lassiter High School as a branch of the non-profit organization The Joy Mission, the club saw great success in creating space for students to connect through shared experiences, open conversations, and meaningful support. That success inspired its expansion to UGA.  The organization focuses on fostering resilience, emotional wellness, and purpose-driven living. Through weekly meetings, campus-wide events, and peer-led discussions, the Joy Mission encourages members to support one another, process grief in healthy ways, and rebuild their lives with hope and confidence.  A central pillar of our organization is outreach: UGA students will have the opportunity to serve as mentors for children in the Athens-Clarke County School District who are also navigating parental loss. By bridging the university and local community, we hope to offer a model of strength, empathy, and continuity across life stages.  The Joy Mission is built on the belief that while grief changes us, it can also unite us—and together, we can build joy from even the hardest of experiences.
summary: Joy Mission seeks to support students in Athens Clarke County grieving the loss of a parent or guardian by providing a safe, uplifting, and empowering community. This is done through conversations, meetings, and discussions with the youth.

name: Christian Conservatives
description:
The silent majority is tired of being silent. It's time for us to stand up and make a change! And we want your help to do it.

 
Our Organization is focused on bringing good to our community- a little at a time. With acts of service, such as food drives, toy donations, and many others, we try to make a world of a difference to those that need it most. Through civil political discourse and group prayer sessions, we aim to bring Enlightenment and awareness to other individuals in our campus community.
 
One person can't change the world, but changing the world only starts with one person. It's up to us to save our generation and our Country!
 

The purpose of Christian Conservatives ("the Organization") is to join those of Christian faith and Conservative ideology to form a joint movement to advocate for local change. The Organization aims to facilitate change within the local community through acts of service, civil political discourse, and group prayer. The Organization intends to spread awareness of political events, and share a perspective rooted in the Goodness of God.
summary: The silent majority is done being silent. It's time to get up and make a change! Our Organization is intent on bringing good to our community- a little at a time. With acts of service, civil political discourse and group prayer, we work to save America!

name: We Raise the Bar
description: We Raise the Bar is a nonprofit and RSO dedicated to fostering DEI within the legal field by empowering underrepresented students to pursue their dreams of becoming legal professionals. Our mission is to inspire the next generation of lawyers by providing access to networking opportunities, skill-building workshops, LSAT preparation, and valuable insights into the legal profession. We are committed to cultivating a community where students from diverse backgrounds can thrive academically, professionally, and personally.
summary: Student-led 501(c)(3) nonprofit empowering minority & underprivileged youth to explore careers in law. We bring students to bridge learning gaps in the community, as well as encourage local youth to pursue higher education through fundraising & tutoring.

name: Spanish Language & Culture Club
description: The Spanish Language & Culture Club is a welcoming community of all language levels that is passionate about learning and improving our Spanish speaking skills while exploring the diversity and history of Spanish culture and Spanish-speaking countries. From food and film to dance and music, we’ll enjoy all that Spanish culture has to offer.
summary: A community of all language levels that is passionate about learning and improving our Spanish speaking skills while exploring the diversity and history of Spanish culture and Spanish-speaking countries.

name: Black Youth Empowerment Network UGA Chapter
description:
Black Youth Empowerment Network, University of Georgia Chapter (BYEN UGA) is a student-led organization dedicated to cultivating confident, community-centered Black leaders through exposure, education, and empowerment. We provide a space where students can grow personally and professionally, connect with peers and mentors, and take action on the issues that matter to them most. Through interactive workshops, service initiatives, creative expression, and leadership development, BYEN exists to equip Black students with the tools and support they need to thrive on campus and beyond.

While our focus is on the empowerment of Black students, UGA BYEN welcomes all UGA students regardless of race, background, gender identity, or orientation who are committed to our mission and values. Through civic engagement, cultural programming, mentorship, financial literacy workshops, and more, we foster student-led impact and community building on campus and beyond.
summary: The University of Georgia's chapter of Black Youth Empowerment Network (BYEN) is a youth-led organization dedicated to uplifting and empowering Black youth through leadership development, entrepreneurship, and creative expression.

name: MedSearch at UGA
description: MedSearch at UGA (MSUGA) is dedicated to helping students explore and prepare for careers in healthcare through shadowing, internships, mentorship, and educational workshops. In addition, the organization is committed to addressing healthcare access disparities by providing immigrant and underserved youth with resources, guidance, and opportunities to connect with local healthcare services. MSUGA empowers members to gain hands-on experience, develop professional skills, and make a meaningful impact in both their own futures and the health of the community.
summary: MedSearch at UGA (MSUGA) helps students explore healthcare career opportunities through shadowing, internships, and mentorship, while supporting immigrant and underserved youth in gaining access to healthcare resources and guidance.

name: Hope Haven at UGA
description: Hope Haven at UGA prepares, empowers, and connects adults with developmental disabilities by providing services, resources, and inclusive opportunities in the Athens-Clarke community. We engage UGA students through volunteer opportunities, service learning, and advocacy, fostering independence and meaningful community involvement.
summary: Empowering and connecting adults with developmental disabilities in Athens through student engagement, service, support, and fundraising.

name: Club Dodgeball
description:
The objects and purpose of the Club are:

  • The introduction, teaching, promotion, encouragement, understanding, and

extension of the game of dodgeball in all its aspects, within the University

and within the community.

  • To ensure that the playing of the game of dodgeball within the said area is

carried out observing fair play according to the Laws of the Game and a

sporting spirit.
summary:
The objects and purpose of the Club is the introduction, teaching, promotion, encouragement, understanding, and
extension of the game of dodgeball in all its aspects, within the University
and within the community.

name: Orion Society
description: The Orion Society’s mission is to create an engaging and dynamic learning environment that empowers highly motivated students to develop and refine their financial skills. Through hands-on experience, members gain exposure to financial valuation techniques, work collaboratively with peers, and take part in building and presenting professional sell-side pitch decks. By fostering both technical knowledge and teamwork, the society aims to prepare its members for success in future careers in finance and related fields.
summary: The mission of Orion Society is to provide motivated students the opportunity to participate in an active learning environment where members learn financial valuation and collaborate to build and pitch sell-side decks.

name: Collegiate Sportsmen and Women's Coalition at the University of Georgia
description:
The primary goal of the Collegiate Sportsmen and Women’s Coalition(Coalition) is to attract and educate students from diverse interests and backgrounds about what it means to be a sportsperson (with a focus on hunting,angling,recreational shooting, and trapping),the importance of policy initiatives that affect sportsmen and women,the role of sportsmen and women in conservation and conservation funding,and how to effectively engage in the policy process. Through the Coalition, the Congressional Sportsmen’s Foundation (CSF) will help educate the outdoor sporting community's next generation of conservation leaders.  Through the Coalition students will have opportunities to work directly with state legislators and fish and wildlife agency staff as they consider legislation and regulations pertaining to a wide-array of conservation-related matters. 

The Coalition will also provide networking opportunities in the conservation policy arena. Members of the Coalition will have opportunities to network with legislators, policy staff, state agencies, and industry and conservation partners. Many of the legislators and policy staff that the Coalition will interact with are already directly affiliated with their state’s respective Legislative Sportsmen's Caucus under the umbrella of CSF’s National Assembly of Sportsmen's Caucuses. Coalition members will have access to a vast array of informational resources on timely policy issues pertinent to sportsmen and conservation and will be able to learn about the importance of sportsmen and women engaging in the political and regulatory process. This will also include learning about a variety of approaches to advocacy efforts, and CSF’s staff will facilitate hands-on opportunities to put that learning to work in the policy arena.

Although the purpose and focus of the Coalition is certainly on policy education and engagement, the Coalition is also an opportunity to celebrate our sporting heritage and welcome others into the fold. Coalition members and leaders have the autonomy to host a wide variety of social, conservation,or philanthropic activities, including those that promote active participation in hunting, angling, recreational shooting, and trapping.
summary: The primary goal of the CSWC is to attract and educate students from diverse interests and backgrounds about what it means to be a sportsman, how policy initiatives affect sportsmen and women, and how to effectively engage in the policy process.

name: The Fontaine Center
summary: The Fontaine Center advances the well-being of students and the UGA community by leading in health promotion and wellness programs.

name: AhlulBayt Student Association
description: The AhlulBayt Student Association (ABSA) at the University of Georgia is a student-led organization committed to creating a welcoming environment for all students interested in learning about the teachings and legacy of the AhlulBayt. ABSA seeks to promote spiritual growth, cultural understanding, and interfaith dialogue through educational programs, discussions, and community service initiatives. By fostering an inclusive space, we aim to strengthen unity among students, celebrate diversity, and contribute positively to the UGA community.
summary: ABSA is a student-led cultural and religious organization dedicated to fostering community, dialogue, and service inspired by the teachings of the AhlulBayt at UGA.

name: School of Social Work Ambassadors
description: The SSW Student Ambassador program is designed to promote the Social Work programs at UGA and in the community to prospective students and represent the School of Social Work at various recruitment and promotional events (in-person and virtually). The program addresses the benefits of becoming a professional social worker and the numerous job options social workers have once they complete the degree. SSW Ambassadors will benefit from networking and leadership opportunities.
summary: SSW Ambassadors are student leaders who promote the UGA School of Social Work through outreach, recruitment, and service. Members support events, welcome prospective students, and build community while developing leadership and professional skills.

name: Athens After Class
description:
Athens After Class is a student-founded organization at the University of Georgia that reimagines how learning and dialogue can extend beyond the classroom. Our mission is to create a vibrant platform where students, professors, and Athens community members come together to share ideas, stories, and perspectives in an informal and welcoming environment.

Every two weeks, we host an event featuring one student and one professor who pair up to give a talk or conversation-style lecture. These events are not confined to campus lecture halls; instead, they take place in collaboration with Athens community spaces such as cafés, bookstores, libraries, and cultural venues. This format makes intellectual exchange accessible and engaging, blurring the line between the university and the city around it.

The student and professor pairings are intentionally interdisciplinary, highlighting both scholarly expertise and personal passion. A student in biology might team up with a philosophy professor to discuss ethics in medicine. A political science major could share the stage with a local historian to explore civic engagement in Athens. These talks are designed to be interactive, thought provoking, and fun, sparking conversations that continue long after the event.

Athens After Class also provides meaningful leadership opportunities for students. Our executive board includes officers in finance, operations, marketing, and professional relations, each developing skills in event planning, organizational management, and community outreach. Faculty gain a platform to connect with students and share aspects of their work or interests that may not typically appear in the classroom. The Athens community benefits from dynamic events that enrich cultural life and strengthen bonds with UGA.

Ultimately, Athens After Class is more than a lecture series; it is a bridge. By combining academic rigor with the accessibility of community venues, we cultivate a culture of curiosity, dialogue, and connection. Our vision is to make Athens a hub where ideas are exchanged freely, learning is social, and people from all walks of life come together to explore what it means to think, create, and live in community.
summary: Athens After Class pairs UGA students and professors for biweekly talks in Athens community spaces, creating lively and accessible events that foster curiosity, dialogue, and stronger ties between campus and city.

name: Black Women in Law
description: Black Women in Law (BWL) is committed to empowering, supporting, and advancing aspiring lawyers within the legal field. The organization serves to provide a community where our members can connect through mentorship, networking, and professional development opportunities in regard to their academic and career goals. Our mission is to foster an inclusive and empowering environment that encourages all interested students to excel in legal studies and beyond. Our members engage in a variety of activities including general body meetings, community service, social events, and workshops that focus on legal education, LSAT preparation, and overall leadership development. Through collaboration, education, and mentorship, Black Women in Law hopes to equip its members with the confidence, ability, and connections needed to thrive as future legal professionals in an inequitable field.
summary: Black Women in Law (BWL) empowers and supports students interested in the legal field through mentorship, networking, and professional development. Open to all UGA students, BWL fosters leadership, legal education, and community engagement.

name: Young Physicians Initiative - Athens Chapter
description: Our organization is committed to strengthening connections between pre-medical students and medical students. To this end, we will host various events, including interactive panels, workshops, and case studies, all of which will be open to all students. This organization is the Athens chapter for a much larger organization founded by Dr. Heval Kelli, and we have personal connections with medical students and residents across various schools, including the medical school in Athens. Through these connections, we will provide resources and contacts that can help aspiring physicians achieve their goals. Membership in YPI Athens is open to everyone.
summary: Our organization is committed to strengthening connections between pre-medical students and medical students. To this end, we will host various events, including interactive panels, workshops, and case studies, all of which will be open to all students.

name: Women's Basketball Club
description: The Women's Basketball Club will create a safe space for all women who are interested in playing basketball at a fun level where practices and tournoments will occur. This organization is open to all students at UGA and is perfect for anyone wanting to share their love and talent for basketball with other athletes, or begineers who want to better their skills to compete at a higher level. Practices will be weekly, and tournoments will occur multiple times in a school year. There will also be social events held within the club to foster a safe and welcoming community for female athletes. This club will create a competitive and comfortable community for all members!
summary: We are an organization for all women who enjoy playing basketball to come together with a common purpose of having fun and competing in games and tournoments. Membership is open to all students enrolled at the University of Georgia.

name: Risk Management and Financial Planning Club
description: The Risk Management and Financial Planning Club is dedicated to connecting students interested in the fields of insurance, finance, and risk management. Our mission is to provide members with meaningful networking opportunities, professional development resources, and valuable insights from industry professionals. Through events such as career workshops, resume reviews, and guest speaker sessions featuring representatives from local companies, we aim to prepare students for success in careers related to financial services, insurance, and risk management. The club also fosters peer-to-peer collaboration, creating a community where members can share knowledge, explore career paths, and develop skills that extend beyond the classroom.
summary: connect students interested in the fields of insurance, finance, and risk management. Our mission is to provide members with meaningful networking opportunities, professional development resources, and valuable insights from industry professionals.

name: Exploring Healthcare Professions
description: EHP targets students who know they want to pursue a career in healthcare, but are unsure what specific path is best for them. Our organization aims to educate students on the differences between healthcare professions and their undergrad requirements. So many students come to UGA with a particular pre-health track in mind, but end up switching their concentration two or even three times. Our general body meetings will unpack the reality of these careers (PA, MD, NP, Dental, PT, AA, etc…) and their respective prerequisite courses.
summary: EHP targets students who know they want to pursue a career in healthcare, but are unsure what specific path is best for them. Our organization aims to educate students on the differences between healthcare professions and their undergrad requirements.

name: Environmental Law Organization
description: The Environmental Law Organization aims to introduce undergraduates to key principles and institutions of environmental law, encourage critical analysis of statutes, cases, and regulatory frameworks, provide experiential learning through debates and case analysis, foster awareness of how law intersects with environmental justice, policy, and global governance and to build a community of students interested in legal reasoning and environmental advocacy. Open to only undergraduate UGA students.
summary: The Environmental Law Organization offers undergraduate students the ability to participate and learn about Environmental Law, as well as gain experience in mock debates and case analysis. Open to only undergraduate UGA students.

name: Aspiring Psychologists' Association
description: Aspiring Psychologists' Association is a group of highly ambitious students dedicated to a future in psychology. Through unique resources, leadership opportunities, and mentor programs, APA provides its members with the professional skills to achieve their post-graduate goals.
