summary: An organization dedicated to providing professional development and resources to undergraduates who intend to pursue graduate psychology degrees.

name: Environmental Review
description: Environmental Review creates the opportunity for members of all majors to write about environmental topics on local, state, and national levels; copyedit pieces; and publish them for a wider audience. Our goal is to spread awareness of environmental issues and enhance community knowledge about our planet. All UGA students that care about our environment are welcome to come write about it!
summary: Environmental Review at UGA creates the opportunity for members of all majors to write about environmental topics on local, state, and national levels; copyedit pieces; and publish them for a wider audience and to spread awareness.

name: IfYoureReadingThis at UGA
description: IfYoureReadingThis at UGA is a mental health nonprofit that empowers students to build and tap into their support networks by amplifying the faces and stories of the people who want to connect. Our mission is to create community around mental health, encourage radical empathy, and use peer support to bridge gaps in mental health resources.
summary: IfYoureReadingThis UGA is a mental health nonprofit that empowers students to build and tap into their support networks by amplifying the faces and voices of the people. Our mission is to create community around mental health an encourage radical empathy

name: Arch Political
description: Arch Political is a bipartisan news source at the University of Georgia dedicated to mending the divide between the two-party system by presenting both sides of every issue with honesty, balance, and respect.
summary: A bipartisan news source at the University of Georgia, mending the divide between the two-party system in our nation through balanced, ‘both-side’ reporting.

name: Maternal and Child Health Student Organization
description:
MCHSO will:

  • Connect students with an interest in maternal and child health (MCH)
  • Act as a unifying force for UGA students, in addition to providing a social and academic support network.
  • Foster a relationship between multidisciplinary students and professionals in the field of MCH.
  • Contribute to the Athens and MCH community through service projects.
  • Help students explore their career goals and interests by offering information on MCH careers, networking opportunities, and career development
summary: The mission of MCHSO is to advance maternal and child health by providing training and leadership opportunities, as well as creating a community for future maternal and child health leaders to connect and network.

name: Graduate Women in Business Association
description: Terry Graduate Women in Business Association (GWBA) is a student-led organization for MBA students at the Terry College of Business that focuses on promoting confidence, leadership, and professional development through networking, mentorship, and events. It provides opportunities for members to connect with peers and accomplished alumni, and it organizes events to address career challenges. Membership is extended to all students (not exclusive to women or female-identifying students).
summary: Terry Graduate Women in Business Association (GWBA) is a student-led organization for MBA students at the Terry College of Business that focuses on promoting confidence, leadership, and professional development. Membership is extended to all students.

name: Queer Pre-Health Club
description: Queer Pre-Health Club is a student organization who allows membership for all students and is dedicated to LGBTQIA+ visibility in healthcare. Along with the benefits of joining a pre-medical professional society, including volunteering events and guest speakers, we also hope to create a community full of students encouraging one another. We strive to provide a safe space where people support each other in both their academic and personal journeys.
summary: Queer Pre-Health Club is a student organization who allows membership for all students and is dedicated to LGBTQIA+ visibility in healthcare. We strive to provide a safe space where people support each other in both their academic and personal journeys.

name: Banking Club
description: Our mission is to equip students with a comprehensive understanding of the banking industry and its diverse career paths. Through professional development workshops, networking opportunities, and hands-on learning experiences, we prepare our members to excel in competitive financial environments. The Banking Club serves as a bridge between academic knowledge and real-world application, fostering connections with industry professionals and empowering students to pursue successful careers in banking.
summary: Our mission is to provide students with the skills and knowledge required to get a career in banking or prepare them to succeed in networking. We provide access to success through professional development, networking, and hands-on experience.

name: Graduate Consulting Club at Terry
description: The Graduate Consulting Club at Terry is dedicated to developing the consulting capabilities of MBA students at the University of Georgia’s Terry College of Business. We provide members with opportunities to build analytical, problem-solving, and presentation skills through workshops, case interview prep, and real-world consulting projects. The club also connects students with alumni and firm representatives through networking events, mentorship programs, and speaker sessions—helping bridge the gap between classroom learning and consulting careers.
summary: The Graduate Consulting Club at Terry equips MBA students with skills, resources, and professional connections to pursue careers in consulting through training sessions, case competitions, and networking with industry professionals.

name: Mariachi Bajo el Arco
description:
About

Mariachi Bajo el Arco is UGA's first mariachi band dedicated to showcasing the vibrance of Mexican culture through mariachi music in UGA and Athens. 

Anyone interested in singing or playing violin, trumpet, guitarrón, or vihuela is invited to join! 

Join our GroupMe! 

https://groupme.com/join_group/109757251/VVdeDkaT

 

Performance

We will likely be open to requested performances and showcases by Spring 2026. Feel free to contact us about performing at an event!
summary: Mariachi Bajo el Arco is UGA's first mariachi band dedicated to showcasing the vibrance of Mexican culture through mariachi music in UGA and Athens. Anyone interested in singing or playing violin, trumpet, guitarrón, or vihuela is invited to join!

name: Love Like Leah
description: Love Like Leah was established in 2023 following the untimely death of then rising sophomore at UGA, Leah Szecepanski. Leah suffered from Long QT Syndrome (LQTS) a genetic heart condition that affects 1 in 2,000 people. In Leah's case she had a genetic mutation of LQTS which she did not inherit from either of her parents and she was a healthy and active 19-year-old girl when she unexpectedly passed from the silent killer. Our goal is to raise awareness for LQTS and to not allow Leah to be forgotten. We seek to build a community of people who knew Leah, resonate with Leah's story, and want to help us carry on the legacy of our friend!
summary: Love Like Leah is an organization dedicated to raising funds and awareness for the 501c3 of the same name, established in honor of the late Leah Szecepanski who would've graduated from the University of Georgia in May of 2026.

name: Franklin CREATE!
description: Franklin CREATE! is a bold new initiative designed to build entrepreneurial confidence among arts and sciences students and prepare them to launch successful startups. Working in close partnership with the UGA Entrepreneurship Program and the UGA Innovation District, Franklin CREATE! extends the university's startup infrastructure into the Franklin College of Arts and Sciences, creating new pathways within the university’s vibrant innovation ecosystem for students to move from idea to launch.
summary: Franklin CREATE! is a bold new initiative designed to build entrepreneurial confidence among arts and sciences students and prepare them to launch successful startups.

name: Club Darts at UGA
description: Club Darts at UGA is a student-run organization dedicated to bringing together University of Georgia students who share an interest in the sport of darts. Whether you’re a beginner playing darts for the first time or an experienced player looking to improve your game, our club provides a welcoming and inclusive environment for players of all skill levels. Members will have access to our equipment, regular practice sessions, and opportunities to participate in both casual play and organized competitive matches based on interest and experience. If you’re interested in joining Club Darts at UGA or would like more information, please contact us or join our GroupMe to stay up to date on meetings and events.
summary: Club Darts at UGA aims to bring together students of all skill levels to play, practice, and compete in darts. We provide the equipment, have regular meetups, and opportunities for both casual and competitive play in a welcoming environment.

name: Muslim Tech Collaborative
description: We connect Muslim students and professionals in tech to learn together, support one another, and build meaningful impact for the Ummah through hands-on projects, mentorship, and workshops. Our space is open to all levels, with a focus on real-world problem-solving.
summary: This club unites Muslim students and professionals in tech to build skills, community, and impact for the Ummah through hands-on projects, mentorship, and workshops. We lower barriers, welcome all levels, and solve real-world problems.

name: Tomorrow's Advisory Group
description: 1. The Tomorrow's Advisory Group aims to support student organizations in developing effective financial management practices and promoting fiscal responsibility within the UGA community. Through consultation, education, and collaboration, the group assists organizations in budgeting, fundraising strategy, and transparent allocation of resources. Our purpose is to empower student leaders with the financial knowledge and tools necessary to sustain their initiatives, enhance organizational impact, and ensure long-term success. Through continued effort and collaboration, we will become the Advisors of Tomorrow.
summary: The Tomorrow's Advisory Group aims to support student organizations in developing effective financial, leadership, and management practices by promoting fiscal responsibility within the UGA community.

name: Electroincs Foundation Club
description:
The Electronics Foundation Club is a student organization affiliated with the Global Electronics Association. We focus on how electronics are physically built, with an emphasis on microfabrication, manufacturing, and industry standards.

Members learn how these standards are used in real companies, explore career paths in electronics fabrication, and gain insight into certifications and skills that are valued in the industry. The club helps students connect what they learn in class to real world applications and future job opportunities.
summary: The Electronics Foundation Club is a student organization affiliated with the Global Electronics Association that focuses on how electronics are physically built, including microfabrication, manufacturing, and industry standards. Members learn how these

name: Bite Melanoma
description:
Bite Melanoma is a student-led organization dedicated to raising awareness about skin cancer, particularly melanoma, through education, prevention, and early detection initiatives. The organization aims to empower students with knowledge about sun safety, skin cancer risk factors, and the importance of regular skin checks.

Bite Melanoma will host educational events, campus outreach efforts, and community partnerships, including an annual skin check event in collaboration with Georgia Skin Cancer, to provide accessible preventive care and promote early detection. Through advocacy, service, and awareness-based programming, the organization seeks to reduce stigma surrounding skin health and encourage proactive, lifelong habits that support melanoma prevention and overall well-being.
summary: Bite Melanoma raises awareness of skin cancer and melanoma through education, prevention initiatives, and partnerships with medical professionals to promote sun safety and early detection on campus.

name: ArchFurs
description:
ArchFurs is an organization made to build awareness of and about furry culture, through volunteering, meetups, and other events. We have a focus on artistic events, games, and other sorts of outings.

we coordinate events through our discord, so join us, if you'd like: 

https://discord.gg/UMCnGvS6xH
summary: ArchFurs is a safe space for those in or interested in the furry fandom. This club is dedicated to volunteering, community building, and one's artistic endeavors. Whether you're a long time furry member or a curious outsider, we welcome you!

name: Computer-Aided Drug Design (CADD) Club
description:
The purpose of the Computer-Aided Drug Design Club is to foster student interest, education, and professional development in computational chemistry, cheminformatics, molecular modeling, bioinformatics, and computer-aided drug discovery and design (CADD). The organization seeks to:

  • Provide hands-on training and workshops in molecular modeling, docking, molecular dynamics, QSAR, AI/ML-based drug discovery, and related computational tools
  • Promote interdisciplinary collaboration among students in chemistry, biochemistry, biology, pharmacy, computer science, data science, and related fields
  • Support research skill development, career exploration, and networking opportunities in pharmaceutical sciences, biotechnology, and computational research
summary: The purpose of the Computer-Aided Drug Design Club is to foster student interest, education, and professional development in computational chemistry, cheminformatics, molecular modeling, bioinformatics, and computer-aided drug discovery and design (CADD)

name: Christians In Psychology
description: • Christians in Psychology (CIP) exists to support and connect Christians who plan to enter the field of Psychology. CIP seeks to join together the minds of those preparing to thoughtfully navigate integrating and/or maintaining their biblical beliefs within their professional practice. We aspire to encourage spiritual growth, genuine connections, and a deeper understanding of the harmony between Christianity and Psychology. Through monthly meetings, guest speakers, and engaging educational presentations, CIP seeks to build a supportive community that equips members to move confidently into their future careers while remaining grounded in their faith.
summary: CIP seeks to join together Christians who are entering the field of Psychology and are preparing to navigate integrating their faith into their professional work.

name: Euchre Theory Club
description: The Euchre Theory Club aims to create an inclusive space for students at the University of Georgia to learn, enjoy, and play the card game Euchre. There is a strong focus on strategic thinking, learning, game theory, and, most importantly, having fun!
summary: The Euchre Theory Club aims to create an inclusive space for students at the University of Georgia to learn, enjoy, and play the card game Euchre.

name: Dexterity Dogs
description: Dexterity Dawgs is a student-led organization that focuses on building manual dexterity, developing fine motor control, and hand-eye coordination through fun, creative, hands-on crafts and activities. Our organization is open to all students who want to engage in an environment to work with their hands, not just Pre-Professional students who are interested in building skills for their future career. Instead of traditional drills and tools that are very technical and intimidating, we use everyday crafts like cookie decorating, rock painting, and other hands-on tasks that make it approachable and fun! Our club creates a welcoming, stress-free, relaxed space for members to get creative, hangout with friends, and also learn about ways to get connected with our community here at UGA and in Athens. We will have a community service initiative that will help us give back to the community through our crafts as well! We hope to partner with local organizations like senior homes, hospice care, and arthritis centers in Athens for volunteering opportunities and through goodie bags and gifts that we will create.
summary: Dexterity Dogs focuses on building manual dexterity through fun, everyday crafts that people don't always realize are improving fine motor skills, precision, and hand-eye coordination. We aim to engage with the Athens community too through service!

name: Voices of Equity at UGA
description: Voices of Equity at UGA is dedicated to advancing health equity through education, advocacy, and community engagement by providing members with volunteering and service opportunities. The organization seeks to deepen understanding of systemic health disparities and the social determinants that shape health outcomes, encouraging thoughtful dialogue that centers diverse perspectives and lived experiences. Through service initiatives, advocacy efforts, and collaboration with campus and community partners, members are empowered to translate awareness into meaningful local and national action. Voices of Equity UGA is committed to cultivating an inclusive campus community grounded in accessibility, representation, and sustained, community-driven change while actively supporting the broader mission and initiatives of the national organization.
summary: Voices of Equity at UGA is dedicated to advancing health equity through education, advocacy, and community engagement by providing members with volunteering and service opportunities.

name: Large Language Models Group
description:
Our organization is rooted in the mission of Innovation for the Benefit of All. We work toward developing transformative Artificial Intelligence that pairs technical excellence with real-world social impact. Guided by the principle of Large Language Models Group, we emphasize ethics, inclusivity, and humanity in how AI is designed, deployed, and evaluated.

Our research and activities take an interdisciplinary approach, drawing inspiration from the complexity of biological intelligence and ideas spanning frontier science. A major focus is Large Language Models (LLMs) & Universal Applications—architecting and deploying LLMs that can be personalized “for everything,” including:

  • accelerating scientific discovery,

  • improving and personalizing education,

  • preserving and enhancing human experience,

  • and building tools that strengthen communities and society.

We welcome students who are excited about responsible AI, applied impact, and learning through collaboration.
summary: Large Language Models Group advancing transformative, ethical and inclusive AI. We build and deploy large language models for universal applications—accelerating science, personalizing education, and improving society through interdisciplinary research.

name: Black Professionals in International Affairs
description:
As a collegiate chapter of Black Professionals in International Affairs, the purpose of BPIA Collegiate Chapters is to enhance, promote, and increase Black representation, pedagogy, scholarships, and perspectives in the field of international affairs through research, education, professional development, community, and mentorship on college campuses across the United States.

BPIA Collegiate Chapters have a goal of affirming, valuing, and amplifying the voices and concerns of Black students, although all students are welcome to be members of BPIA, in the field of international affairs, as well as strengthening and encouraging one another in what can be an alienating field. This organization aims to provide a supportive, affirming environment for fostering an intellectual community and facilitating the development of African and African diaspora studies and thought.

Membership is open to all UGA students, faculty, and staff members!

Membership:

  1. Membership shall be limited to currently enrolled University of Georgia students, faculty, and/or staff members.
  2. Dues will be 20 dollars a semester, which includes membership in both UGA’s collegiate chapter and the national chapter of BPIA.
  3. In addition to voting privileges, membership privileges include, but are not limited to: free admission to our annual events and the opportunity to socialize with students with similar interests.
summary: BPIA's goal is to affirm, value, and amplify the voices and concerns of students in the field of international affairs, as well as strengthen and encourage one another in what can be an alienating field. Membership is open to all UGA students!

name: Mind's Eye Club
description: Mind's Eye Club is an organization that promotes community, friendship, support, and goal-setting. The organization will bring people together through meetings and group activities.
summary: The purpose of Mind’s Eye Club is to bring students together to clarify, visualize, and pursue their personal, academic, and professional goals via creative expression and community support. Meetings will be themed monthly to guide students' aspirations.

name: Recess Club
description: Recess Club is a student organization dedicated to creating intentional, play-based experiences that promote joy, creativity, and meaningful connection among students. The club is built around the simple idea that college students still need their time to play. Rooted in the idea of embracing childhood nostalgia, the club offers opportunities for members to reconnect with the simplicity and fun of childhood school activities in a welcoming, low-pressure environment. In the middle of busy schedules and constant deadlines, we want to offer a place where students can slow down, laugh, and just enjoy being part of a community. By fostering genuine connection and moments of lightheartedness, the Recess Club supports holistic student well-being and encourages a balanced college experience where joy and community are valued alongside achievement.
summary: The Recess Club creates simple, play-based experiences that promote play, creativity, and connection. Inspired by childhood nostalgic activities, we provide welcoming spaces where students can relax, build community, and support their overall well-being.

name: LLM Innovation Group
description: The LLM Innovation Group is an initiative to bring computer science into a multi-disciplinary world.
summary: Filling in the gap between computer science and other non-technical majors using Large Language Models

name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at the University of Georgia
description: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at the University of Georgia (SEDS @ UGA) is an interdisciplinary student organization focused on connecting students interested in space with professional development, networking, and national opportunities. Open to all majors, the organization supports student engagement in space science, industry, and policy through events, mentorship, and participation in SEDS national programs and conferences.
summary: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space at the University of Georgia (SEDS @ UGA) is an interdisciplinary student organization focused on connecting students interested in space with professional development, networking, and national opport

name: Student LSAT Preparation Society
description: An undergraduate LSAT Preparation Society that builds a supportive community for aspiring law students through weekly study sessions, strategy workshops, practice exams, and peer mentorship—helping members boost scores and confidence. Open to all majors.
summary: An undergraduate LSAT Preparation Society that builds a supportive community for aspiring law students through weekly study sessions, strategy workshops, practice exams, and peer mentorship—helping members boost scores and confidence. Open to all majors.

name: Bloom Women's Fellowship
description:
Bloom Women’s Fellowship is a Christian organization dedicated to supporting and encouraging aspiring educators within the UGA community. The fellowship exists to cultivate spiritual growth, professional development, and meaningful relationships among education students who feel called to serve in the field of education. Rooted in faith and fellowship, Bloom exists to encourage, equip, and uplift students as they navigate the unique joys and challenges of the classroom.

 

Bloom provides a structured and supportive environment where members can engage in faith-based discussion, mentorship, and service initiatives. Through regular meetings, collaborative learning experiences, and community outreach, the organization seeks to strengthen members’ spiritual foundations while equipping them with the confidence, leadership skills, and ethical framework necessary for effective and compassionate teaching.

 

Bloom Women’s Fellowship is committed to fostering a welcoming and respectful environment that encourages personal growth, academic excellence, and servant leadership. The organization supports students in integrating their faith and vocation in a way that positively impacts their classrooms, schools, and broader communities. We believe that when teachers are spiritually nourished and supported, they are better able to pour into their students and impact their schools for God’s glory.
summary: A strong community of female pre-service teachers, intended education majors, and undergraduate, graduate, and professional education students that navigates the Christian faith as it relates to the teaching profession.

name: Mergers and Acquisitions Society at UGA
description:
The Mergers & Acquisitions Society is a student-led organization dedicated to developing members understanding of corporate transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, leveraged buyouts, and strategic restructurings. The society provides practical exposure to valuation, financial modeling, deal analysis, and transaction strategy through case studies, guest speakers, workshops, and current events. Members engage with real-world deal scenarios, collaborate on team-based analyses, and gain insight into careers in corporate development, consulting, and legal and advisory. The society welcomes students from all majors who are interested in corporate finance, strategy, and the mechanics behind major business transactions.

 

What We Offer:

- Speaker sessions with industry professionals

- Hands on workshops

- Professional development 

 

How To Get Involved?

Join the GroupMe:

https://groupme.com/join_group/112112727/reeawUk5
summary: A student-led organization dedicated to developing members understanding of corporate transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, leveraged buyouts, and strategic restructurings
